local: Tete Hagar na ŋkɔ Aƒetɔ, ame si ƒo nu kplii la be “Mawu, Nukpɔla Nènye” elabe ebia eɖokui be: “Menye Mawue mekpɔ vavã fifia gale agbe oa?”.

eng: The fourth generation will return here since the Amorites wrongdoing wont have reached its peak until then.
local: Dzidzime enelia me hafi tɔwòwo agatrɔ va afii, elabe Amoritɔwo ƒe nuvɔ̃ meva glo haɖe o.”.

eng: He said, Cursed be Canaan: the lowest servant he will be for his brothers.
local: egblɔ be: “Aɖi Kanaan ŋu kpékpekpe! Azu kluviwo ƒe kluvi na nɔviawo.”.

eng: To Eber were born two sons: The first was named Peleg, because during his lifetime the earth was divided. His brothers name was Joktan.
local: Ŋutsuvi evee nɔ Eber si. Gbãtɔ ŋkɔe nye Peleg, elabe eŋɔlie amegbetɔwo ma. Nɔvia ŋkɔe nye Yoktan.

eng: But Abram said to the king of Sodom, I promised the LORD, El Elyon, creator of heaven and earth.
local: Abram ɖo eŋu be: “Metà Aƒetɔ, Mawu Dziƒoʋĩtɔ, dziƒo kple anyigba Wɔla!.

eng: When Nahor was years old, he became the father of Terah.
local: Naxor xɔ ƒe bla eve vɔ asieke hafi dzi Terax.

eng: Therefore, that well is called Beer-lahai-roi; its the well between Kadesh and Bered.
local: Eya ta woyɔa vudo la be: Beerlaxairoi. Ele Kades kple Bared dome.

eng: After the sun had set and darkness had deepened, a smoking vessel with a fiery flame passed between the split-open animals.
local: Ɣe ɖo to, eye viviti do. Kpɔ ɖa, ze si le dzudzɔ tum kɔlikɔli kple akakati si le bibim flaflafla la va to lã siwo me wofe da ɖi la dome.

eng: He also said, Bless the LORD, the God of Shem; Canaan will be his servant.
local: Noa gagblɔ be: “Aƒetɔ, nye Mawu neyra Sem! Nena Kanaan nanye eƒe kluvi.

eng: Joktan fathered Almodad, Sheleph, Hazarmaveth, Jerah.
local: Yoktan dzi Almodad, Salep, Xaxar-mavet, Yarax.

eng: that I wouldnt take even a thread or a sandal strap from anything that was yours so that you couldnt say, Im the one who made Abram rich.
local: Ne maxɔ nane tso nu siwo katã nye tɔwò la me fĩi! Ɖetika loo, alo afɔkpaka teti hã nyemaxɔ le wò nuawo me, ne nàva gblɔ be, ‘Nyee wɔ Abram wòzu kesinɔtɔ’ o.

eng: After Terah was born, Nahor lived years; he had other sons and daughters.
local: Le Terax dzidzi megbe la, Naxor ganɔ agbe ƒe alafa ɖeka kple wui asieke, eye wòdzi viŋutsu bubuwo kple vinyɔnuwo.

eng: May God give space to Japheth; he will live in Shems tents, and Canaan will be his servant.
local: Mawu nena Yafet nadzi ɖe dzi. Nena eƒe dzidzimeviwo nanɔ Sem tɔwo gbɔ faa, eye Kanaan nanye Yafet ƒe kluvi.”.

eng: Hagar gave birth to a son for Abram, and Abram named him Ishmael.
local: Hagar dzi ŋutsuvi na Abram, eye Abram tsɔ ŋkɔ na ɖevia be Ismael.

eng: That day the LORD cut a covenant with Abram: To your descendants I give this land, from Egypts river to the great Euphrates.
local: Gbe ma gbe Aƒetɔ bla nu kple Abram be, “Metsɔ anyigba sia na wò dzidzimeviwo: Etso Egipte-tɔsisi gã la nu va se ɖe Frat-tɔsisi la nu ke.

eng: Hadoram, Uzal, Diklah.
local: Hadoram, Uzal, Dikla.

eng: The only exception is that the young men may keep whatever they have taken to eat, and the men who went with me—Aner, Eshcol, and Mamre—may keep their share.
local: Nye ŋutɔ nyemele naneke dim o, negbe nu si ɖekakpuiawo ɖu la ko. Ke medi be nàna Aner, Eskol kple Mamre, ame siwo va kpe ɖe ŋunye la, naxɔ woawo tɔ gome.”.

eng: When Terah was years old, he became the father of Abram, Nahor, and Haran.
local: Esi Terax xɔ ƒe bla adrẽ la, edzi Abram, Naxor kple Xaran.

eng: After the flood, Noah lived years.
local: Le tsiɖɔɖɔ la megbe la, Noa ganɔ agbe ƒe alafa etɔ̃ bla atɔ̃.

eng: together with the Kenites, the Kenizzites, the Kadmonites.
local: Kenitɔwo, Kenizitɔwo, Kadmonitɔwo.

eng: Obal, Abimael, Sheba.
local: Obal, Abimael, Seba.

eng: Abram was years old when Hagar gave birth to Ishmael for Abram.
local: Abram xɔ ƒe bla enyi vɔ ade esime Hagar dzi Ismael nɛ.

eng: These are Terahs descendants. Terah became the father of Abram, Nahor, and Haran. Haran became the father of Lot.
local: Terax ƒe dzidzimeviwoe nye esiawo: Terax dzi Abram, Naxor kple Xaran, eye Xaran dzi Lot.

eng: In all, Noah lived years; then he died.
local: Ale enɔ agbe ƒe alafa asieke bla atɔ̃ hafi ku.

eng: the Hittites, the Perizzites, the Rephaim.
local: Xititɔwo, Perizitɔwo, Refaimtɔwo.

eng: Ophir, Havilah, and Jobab. All of these were Joktans sons.
local: Ofir, Xavila kple Yobab. Ame siawo katã nye Yoktan viŋutsuwo.

eng: Haran died while with his father Terah in his native land, in Ur of the Chaldeans.
local: Ke Xaran tre kuku na tɔ́a Terax le wo de Ur le Kaldea-nyigba dzi.

eng: the Amorites, the Canaanites, the Girgashites, and the Jebusites.
local: Amoritɔwo, Kanaanitɔwo, Girgasitɔwo kple Yebusitɔwo ƒe anyigbae.”.

eng: Their settlements extended from Mesha by way of Sephar, the eastern mountains.
local: Anyigba si dzi wole la keke tso Mesa ɖo ta Safar si le tonyigba dzi le ɣedzeƒe.

eng: Abram and Nahor both married; Abrams wife was Sarai, and Nahors wife was Milcah the daughter of Haran, father of both Milcah and Iscah.
local: Abram kple Naxor ɖe srɔ̃. Abram srɔ̃ ŋkɔe nye Sarai. Naxor srɔ̃ ŋkɔe nye Milka, ame si nɔvie nye Yiska. Wo tɔ́ ŋkɔe nye Xaran.

eng: These are Shems sons according to their clans, their languages, their lands, and their nations.
local: Sem ƒe dzidzimeviwo nye esiawo ɖe woƒe ƒomewo, gbegbɔgblɔwo, anyigbawo kple woƒe dukɔwo nu.

eng: Sarai was unable to have children.
local: Ke Sarai tsi ko; medzi vi kpɔ o.

eng: These are the clans of Noahs sons according to their generations and their nations. From them the earths nations branched out after the flood.
local: Esiawoe nye Noa viŋutsuwo ƒe ƒomewo ɖe woƒe dzidzimewo kple dukɔwo nu. Woawo mee anyigba dzi dukɔwo dzɔ tso le tsiɖɔɖɔ la megbe.

eng: Terah took his son Abram, his grandson Lot , and his son Abrams wife, Sarai his daughter-in-law. They left Ur of the Chaldeans for the land of Canaan, and arriving at Haran, they settled there.
local: Terax ʋu kple via Abram kple tɔgbuiyɔvia Lot, ame si nye Xaran vi kple toyɔvia Sarai, via Abram srɔ̃ la, tso Ur le Kaldea, be woayi Kanaan-nyigba dzi. Ke esi woɖo Xaran la, wotsi afi ma.

eng: Terah lived years, and he died in Haran.
local: Terax nɔ agbe ƒe alafa eve kple atɔ̃ hafi ku le Xaran.

eng: When Abram was years old, the LORD appeared to Abram and said to him, I am El Shaddai. Walk with me and be trustworthy.
local: Esi Abram xɔ ƒe bla asieke vɔ asieke la, Aƒetɔ ɖe eɖokui fiae hegblɔ nɛ be: “Nyee nye Mawu Ŋusẽkatãtɔ. Zɔ le ŋkunye me, eye mokaka meganɔ ŋuwò o.

eng: I will make a covenant between us and I will give you many, many descendants.
local: Mabla nu kpli wò, eye mana wò dzidzimeviwo nasɔ gbɔ fũu.”.

eng: The LORD appeared to Abraham at the oaks of Mamre while he sat at the entrance of his tent in the days heat.
local: Aƒetɔ ɖe eɖokui fia Abraham le Mamre ƒe ati gã kɔkɔeawo gbɔ. Esime Abraham bɔbɔ nɔ eƒe agbadɔ ƒe mɔnu le ŋdɔme la.

eng: Abram fell on his face, and God said to him.
local: Tete Abram tsyɔ mo anyi, eye Mawu ƒo nu kplii be:.

eng: He looked up and suddenly saw three men standing near him. As soon as he saw them, he ran from his tent entrance to greet them and bowed deeply.
local: efɔ mo dzi. Kpɔ ɖa, ekpɔ ŋutsu etɔ̃ wole tsitre le eŋgɔ ɖa. Esi wòkpɔ wo la, eƒu du yi ɖakpe wo. Ebɔbɔ tsyɔ mo anyi.

eng: But me, my covenant is with you; you will be the ancestor of many nations.
local: “Kpɔ ɖa, nye nubabla li kpli wò. Àzu dukɔ geɖewo tɔgbui.

eng: He said, Sirs, if you would be so kind, dont just pass by your servant.
local: eye wògblɔ be: “Nye Aƒetɔ, meɖe kuku, migato ŋunye yi o. Minyo dɔme miatɔ ɖe gbɔnye ʋɛ.

eng: And because I have made you the ancestor of many nations, your name will no longer be Abram but Abraham.
local: Womagayɔ wò be Abram azɔ o, ke boŋ wò ŋkɔ zu Abraham, elabe meɖo wò dukɔ geɖewo tɔgbui.

eng: Let a little water be brought so you may wash your feet and refresh yourselves under the tree.
local: Mina maku tsi vi aɖe vɛ, ne miaklɔ afɔ, agbɔ ɖe eme vie le atia te.

eng: I will make you very fertile. I will produce nations from you, and kings will come from you.
local: Mana wò dzidzimeviwo nasɔ gbɔ fũu, eye dukɔ geɖewo kple fiawo ado tso mewò.

eng: Let me offer you a little bread so you will feel stronger, and after that you may leave your servant and go on your way—since you have visited your servant. They responded, Fine. Do just as you have said.
local: Matsɔ nuɖuɖu aɖe hã vɛ ne miade nu me, be wòado ŋusẽ mi hafi miagayi mɔzɔzɔa dzi, esi mieva to miaƒe dɔla gbɔ ta.” Woɖo eŋu nɛ be: “Wɔ nu si nèbe yeawɔ la faa.”.

eng: The two messengers entered Sodom in the evening. Lot, who was sitting at the gate of Sodom, saw them, got up to greet them, and bowed low.
local: Esi mawudɔla eveawo va ɖo Sodom le fiẽ me la, Lot bɔbɔ nɔ dua ƒe agbo nu. Esi wòkpɔ wo la, etsi tsitre yi ɖakpe wo. Ebɔbɔ de ta agu na wo.

eng: I will set up my covenant with you and your descendants after you in every generation as an enduring covenant. I will be your God and your descendants God after you.
local: Maɖo nye nubabla gɔme anyi ɖe nye kpli wò, wò dzidzimeviwo kple dzidzime bubu siwo ava dzɔ ɖe wo yome la, dome. Nye nubabla sia anɔ anyi tegbee. Manye wò Mawu kple wò dzidzimeviwo hã tɔ.

eng: So Abraham hurried to Sarah at his tent and said, Hurry! Knead three seahs of the finest flour and make some baked goods.
local: Tete Abraham ɖe abla yi Sara gbɔ le agbadɔa me, eye wògblɔ nɛ be: “Netsɔ, ku wɔ memee dzidzenu etɔ̃ lɔƒo, nàblu tsɔ tɔ tatalii.”.

eng: Abraham traveled from there toward the land of the arid southern plain, and he settled as an immigrant in Gerar, between Kadesh and Shur.
local: Abraham ʋu tso Mamre yi anyigbemekegbegbe lɔƒo, eye wòɖanɔ Kades kple Sur dome. Esi wònɔ Gerar abe amedzro ene la.

eng: He said, Come to your servants house, spend the night, and wash your feet. Then you can get up early and go on your way. But they said, No, we will spend the night in the town square.
local: eye wògblɔ na wo be: “Nye aƒetɔwo, miawoe zɔ. Taflatsɛ, miva míyi miaƒe dɔla ƒe aƒeme ne miaklɔ afɔ, atsi dɔ, eye etsɔ ŋdi kanya miagayi miaƒe mɔzɔzɔa dzi.” Ke woɖo eŋu be: “O, míatsi ablɔ me adɔ ko.”.

eng: I will give you and your descendants the land in which you are immigrants, the whole land of Canaan, as an enduring possession. And I will be their God.
local: Miele anyigba sia dzi fifia abe amedzrowo ene, ke matsɔe ana wò kple wò dzidzimeviwo. Kanaan-nyigba blibo la katã anye wò kple wò dzidzimeviwo tɔ tegbee, eye manye woƒe Mawu.”.

eng: Abraham ran to the cattle, took a healthy young calf, and gave it to a young servant, who prepared it quickly.
local: Abraham ƒu du yi eƒe nyiwo dome. Elé nyivi dami na eƒe dɔla wòwu enumake, hemee wòbi nyuie.

eng: Abraham said of his wife Sarah, Shes my sister. So King Abimelech of Gerar took her into his household.
local: egblɔ be ye nɔvinyɔnue nye Sara. Ale Abimelex, Gerar-fia, na wokplɔ Sara vɛ nɛ.

eng: The LORD was attentive to Sarah just as he had said, and the LORD carried out just what he had promised her.
local: Aƒetɔ ve Sara nu abe ale si wògblɔ ene, eye wòwɔ eƒe ŋugbedodo la dzi nɛ.

eng: He pleaded earnestly with them, so they went with him and entered his house. He made a big meal for them, even baking unleavened bread, and they ate.
local: Gake eyi dzi ɖe kuku na wo ʋuu va se ɖe esime wolɔ̃ yi ɖadze egbɔ. Lot na wotsɔ amɔwɔ maʋamaʋã tɔ tatali, eye wòɖo kplɔ̃ na wo woɖu nu.

eng: God said to Abraham, As for you, you must keep my covenant, you and your descendants in every generation.
local: Mawu yi edzi gblɔ na Abraham be: “Wò la, wɔ ɖe nu, si mebla kpli wò la dzi; wò ŋutɔ, viwòwo kple woƒe dzidzimeviwo katã nawɔ ɖe nubabla la dzi.

eng: Then Abraham took butter, milk, and the calf that had been prepared, put the food in front of them, and stood under the tree near them as they ate.
local: Etsɔ notsi kpekẽtɔ kple tsrolotɔ kpe ɖe nyilã meme la ŋu va ɖo wo kɔme. Azɔ eya ŋutɔ le tsitre ɖe wo gbɔ le atia te esime wole nua ɖum.

eng: After these events, God tested Abraham and said to him, Abraham! Abraham answered, Im here.
local: Esiawo megbe la, Mawu do Abraham kpɔ. Eyɔe be: “Abraham!”, eye wòtɔ be: “Nyee nye esi!”.

eng: But God appeared to Abimelech that night in a dream and said to him, You are as good as dead because of this woman you have taken. She is a married woman.
local: Ke gbe ɖeka, le zã me la, Mawu ɖe eɖokui fia Abimelex le drɔ̃eƒe gblɔ nɛ be: “Kpɔ ɖa, èle kuku ge le nyɔnu si nèxɔ la ta. Ame srɔ̃e!”.

eng: She became pregnant and gave birth to a son for Abraham when he was old, at the very time God had told him.
local: Ale Sara fɔ fu, eye wòdzi viŋutsu na Abraham le eƒe amegãkuku me le ɣeyiɣi, si Mawu gblɔ ɖi xoxo la dzi.

eng: Before they went to bed, the men of the city of Sodom—everyone from the youngest to the oldest—surrounded the house.
local: Hafi anyimlɔɣi naɖo la, Sodom-ŋutsuwo va ƒo xlã aƒea. Ŋutsu siwo katã le dua me ko, ɖeviwo kple ame tsitsiwo siaa va afi ma.

eng: This is my covenant that you and your descendants must keep: Circumcise every male.
local: Esiae nye nye nubabla si dzi nàwɔ ɖo, si anɔ nye kpli wò kpakple wò dzidzimeviwo dome. Woatso aʋa na ŋutsu ɖe sia ɖe le mia dome.

eng: God said, Take your son, your only son whom you love, Isaac, and go to the land of Moriah. Offer him up as an entirely burned offering there on one of the mountains that I will show you.
local: Mawu gblɔ nɛ be: “Kplɔ Isak, viwò ɖeka hɔ̃ la, si gbɔ melɔ̃a nu le o la, yi ɖe Moria-nyigba dzi, eye nàtsɔe mè nu le to aɖe si mafia wò la dzi.

eng: They said to him, Wheres your wife Sarah? And he said, Right here in the tent.
local: Azɔ wobiae be: “Afi ka srɔ̃wò Sara le?” Eɖo eŋu be: “Ele agbadɔme.”.

eng: Now Abimelech hadnt gone near her, and he said, Lord, will you really put an innocent nation to death?.
local: Ke Abimelex mede asi nyɔnua ŋu haɖe o; ale wògblɔ na Mawu be: “O Aƒetɔ, ame maɖifɔwo hãe nèbe yeawua?.

eng: Abraham named his son—the one Sarah bore him—Isaac.
local: Abraham tsɔ ŋkɔ na ɖevi si Sara dzi nɛ la be Isak.

eng: and called to Lot, Where are the men who arrived tonight? Bring them out to us so that we may have sex with them.
local: Wodo ɣli yɔ Lot, eye wobiae be: “Afi ka ŋutsu siwo va gbɔwò egbe fiẽ la le? Kplɔ wo vɛ ne míadɔ kpli wo.”.

eng: You must circumcise the flesh of your foreskins, and it will be a symbol of the covenant between us.
local: Mialã miaƒe aʋanuyi ɖa ne wòanye nubabla si le nye kpli miawo dome la ƒe dzesi.

eng: Then one of the men said, I will definitely return to you about this time next year. Then your wife Sarah will have a son! Sarah was listening at the tent door behind him.
local: Aƒetɔ be: “Ƒe si gbɔna ƒe ɣe alewo ɣi la, magatrɔ va, eye viŋutsuvi anɔ srɔ̃wò Sara si.” Ke Sara le emegbe le agbadɔa ƒe mɔnu le nya la sem.

eng: Sarah lived to be years old; this was how long she lived.
local: Azɔ Sara tsi xɔ ƒe alafa ɖeka bla eve vɔ adrẽ.

eng: Abraham got up early in the morning, harnessed his donkey, and took two of his young men with him, together with his son Isaac. He split the wood for the entirely burned offering, set out, and went to the place God had described to him.
local: Abraham fɔ ŋdi kanya, eye wòbla akpa na eƒe tedzi. Eyɔ eƒe dɔla eve kple Isak, eye wòdze nake ɖe numemevɔsa ŋu. Azɔ edze mɔ ɖo ta teƒe si Mawu fiae la.

eng: Didnt he say to me, Shes my sister, and didnt she—even she—say, Hes my brother? My intentions were pure, and I acted innocently when I did this.
local: Menye eya ŋutɔe gblɔ nam be ye nɔvinyɔnu ye oa? Sara ŋutɔ gɔ̃ hã gblɔ be ye nɔviŋutsu ye. Nye dzi mebu fɔm o, eye nyemeda vo o ɖe.”.

eng: Abraham circumcised his son Isaac when he was eight days old just as God had commanded him.
local: Esi ɖevia xɔ ŋkeke enyi la, Abraham tso aʋa nɛ abe ale si Mawu ɖo nɛ ene.

eng: Lot went out toward the entrance, closed the door behind him.
local: Lot do go yi wo gbɔ le agboa godo, eye wòdo agbo la.

eng: On the eighth day after birth, every male in every generation must be circumcised, including those who are not your own children: those born in your household and those purchased with silver from foreigners.
local: Woatso aʋa na mia dome ŋutsuvi ɖe sia ɖe si xɔ ŋkeke enyi le ƒome sia ƒome me, kluvi wònye woƒle le amedzro si o, woatso aʋa nɛ.

eng: Now Abraham and Sarah were both very old. Sarah was no longer menstruating.
local: Azɔ Abraham kple Sara tsi xɔ ƒè geɖee ŋutɔ ale be Sara megaɖoa asi anyi o.

eng: On the third day, Abraham looked up and saw the place at a distance.
local: Ŋkeke etɔ̃a gbe la, Abraham kpɔ teƒe la ɖa ʋĩi.

eng: She died in Kiriath-arba, that is, in Hebron, in the land of Canaan; and Abraham cried out in grief and wept for Sarah.
local: Eku le Xebron le Kanaan-nyigba dzi, eye Abraham fae ŋutɔŋutɔ.

eng: God said to him in the dream, I know that your intentions were pure when you did this. In fact, I kept you from sinning against me. Thats why I didnt allow you to touch her.
local: Mawu ɖo eŋu nɛ le drɔ̃eƒe be: “Nyateƒe, nye hã menyae be wò dzime kɔ le nu si nèwɔ la me, eya ta meɖo asi dziwò be nàgade asi eŋu ava wɔ vɔ̃ ɖe ŋunye o.”.

eng: Abraham was years old when his son Isaac was born.
local: Abraham xɔ ƒe alafa ɖeka esime wodzi Isak.

eng: and said, My brothers, dont do such an evil thing.
local: Egblɔ na wo be: “Nɔvinyewo, meɖe kuku na mi, migawɔ nuvɔ̃ɖi sia o.

eng: As the days went by and Abraham became older, the LORD blessed Abraham in every way.
local: Azɔ Abraham tsi xɔ ƒè geɖee, eye Aƒetɔ yrae le nu sia nu me.

eng: Be sure you circumcise those born in your household and those purchased with your silver. Your flesh will embody my covenant as an enduring covenant.
local: Woatso aʋa na aƒevi kple kluvi siaa, ne wòanye nye nubabla ƒe dzesi le miaƒe ŋutilã ŋu tegbee.

eng: So Sarah laughed to herself, thinking, Im no longer able to have children and my husbands old.
local: Tete Sara ko nu le eɖokui me gblɔ be: “Nye si ku nyagã alea, eye srɔ̃nye hã zu amegãɖeɖi la, ɖe nu agavivi nunyea?”.

eng: Abraham said to his servants, Stay here with the donkey. The boy and I will walk up there, worship, and then come back to you.
local: Tete Abraham gblɔ na eƒe dɔlawo be: “Miawo minɔ afii kple tedzia ne nye kple vinyea míayi ŋgɔ aɖasubɔ Mawu agbɔ.”.

eng: After he got up from embracing his deceased wife, he spoke with the Hittites.
local: Abraham tso le ame kuku la gbɔ yi ɖe Xitiviwo gbɔ ɖagblɔ na wo be:.

eng: Now return the mans wife. Hes a prophet; he will pray for you so you may live. But if you dont return her, know that you and everyone with you will die.
local: Azɔ gbugbɔ nyɔnua na srɔ̃a, elabe nye gbeƒãɖela ŋutsua nye, ne wòaɖe kuku ɖe tawò nàtsi agbe. Ke ne ègbe mètrɔe nɛ o la, ekema nànyae nyuie be wò kple aƒewò me tɔwo katã miele kuku ge.”.

eng: Sarah said, God has given me laughter. Everyone who hears about it will laugh with me.
local: Sara gblɔ be: “Mawu do nukokoe nam. Ame sia ame si ase nya sia la, aɖi kokoe nɛ.”.

eng: Ive got two daughters who are virgins. Let me bring them out to you, and you may do to them whatever you wish. But dont do anything to these men because they are now under the protection of my roof.
local: Mikpɔ ɖa, vinye ɖetugbi eve li; womenya ŋutsu o. Makplɔ wo vɛ na mi, ne miawɔ nu si dze mia ŋu la kpli wo. Ke ŋutsu siawo ya la, nye amedzrowo wonye. Migawɔ naneke ɖe wo ŋu o.”.

eng: Abraham said to the oldest servant of his household, who was in charge of everything he owned, Put your hand under my thigh.
local: Abraham gblɔ na eƒe dɔla tsitsitɔ si kpɔa eƒe nunɔamesiwo katã dzi la be: “Tsɔ asi de nye atame.

eng: Any uncircumcised male whose flesh of his foreskin remains uncircumcised will be cut off from his people. He has broken my covenant.
local: Ne ŋutsu aɖe li si womelã aʋanuyi nɛ o la, woneɖe bolobolotɔ ma ɖa le eƒe dukɔ dome, elabe etu nye nubabla la.”.

eng: The LORD said to Abraham, Why did Sarah laugh and say, Me give birth? At my age.
local: Tete Aƒetɔ bia Abraham be: “Nu ka ta Sara ko nu gblɔ be, ‘Nye si ku nyagã alea ɖe, magate ŋu adzi via?’.

eng: Abraham took the wood for the entirely burned offering and laid it on his son Isaac. He took the fire and the knife in his hand, and the two of them walked on together.
local: Abraham lé nake la ɖe ta na via Isak, eye eya ŋutɔ ɖe dzo, tsɔ hɛ ɖe asi, eye wo kple evea dze mɔ.

eng: Abimelech got up early in the morning and summoned all of his servants. When he told them everything that had happened, the men were terrified.
local: Abimelex fɔ ŋdi kanya, eye wòyɔ eƒe dɔlawo katã vɛ heto nya si dzɔ la na wo. Vɔvɔ̃ ɖo wo ale gbegbe.

eng: I am an immigrant and a temporary resident with you. Give me some property for a burial plot among you so that I can bury my deceased wife near me.
local: “Amedzroe menye le mia dome. Mina anyigbam ɖe mia dome ne maɖi nye ame kuku la ɖe edzi.”.

eng: She said, Who could have told Abraham that Sarah would nurse sons? But now Ive given birth to a son when he was old.
local: Azɔ egblɔ yi edzi be: “Ame kae bui kpɔ be yeagblɔ na Abraham be, ‘Sara hã ava na no vi?’ Ke, togbɔ be wòtsi, ku amegã hã la, nyee nye esi dzi vi nɛ.”.

eng: They said, Get out of the way! And they continued, Does this immigrant want to judge us? Now we will hurt you more than we will hurt them. They pushed Lot back and came close to breaking down the door.
local: Woblu ɖe Lot ta be: “He ɖa le mía ŋkume! Wò si nye amedzro le afi siaa, nànɔ ʋɔnu drɔ̃m mí alea! Ne mèkpɔ nyuie o la, nu si míatu kpli wò la, avɔ̃ɖi wu wo tɔ.” Ale wolũ ɖe Lot dzi, lée sesĩe be yewoagbã agboa.

eng: God said to Abraham, As for your wife Sarai, you will no longer call her Sarai. Her name will now be Sarah.
local: Mawu gagblɔ na Abraham be: “Mègayɔ srɔ̃wò be Sarai azɔ o. Sara anye eŋkɔ.

eng: By the LORD, God of heaven and earth, give me your word that you wont choose a wife for my son from the Canaanite women among whom I live.
local: ne natà Aƒetɔ, dziƒo kple anyigba ƒe Mawu la nam, be yemaɖe srɔ̃ na vinye tso Kanaantɔ siwo dome mele la vinyɔnuwo dome o.

eng: Isaac said to his father Abraham, My father? Abraham said, Im here, my son. Isaac said, Here is the fire and the wood, but where is the lamb for the entirely burned offering.
local: Esi woyina la, Isak yɔ tɔ́a be: “Tɔ́nye!” Etɔ be: “Nyee nye esi, vinye!” Isak biae be: “Kpɔ ɖa, dzoe nye esi; nake hãe nye esi. Meɖe kuku, alẽvi si míatsɔ amè nui la ya ɖe?”.

eng: Is anything too difficult for the LORD? When I return to you about this time next year, Sarah will have a son.
local: Ɖe nanewɔwɔ aglo Aƒetɔ a? Mebe ƒe si gbɔna ƒe ɣe alewo ɣi la, magatrɔ ava gbɔwò, eye viŋutsuvi anɔ srɔ̃wò Sara si.”.

eng: The Hittites responded to Abraham.
local: Woɖo eŋu nɛ be:.

eng: Then Abimelech summoned Abraham and said to him, What have you done to us? What sin did I commit against you that you have brought this terrible sin to me and my kingdom, by doing to me something that simply isnt done.
local: Tete Abimelex yɔ Abraham eye wòbiae be: “Nu kae nye esi nèwɔ ɖe mía ŋu? Nu vɔ̃ ka gɔ̃ mewɔ ɖe ŋuwò hafi nèhe dzɔgbevɔ̃e gã sia va nye kple nye fiaɖuƒe dzi? Womewɔa nu sia, si nèwɔ ɖe ŋunye la, gbeɖegbeɖe o.

eng: The men inside reached out and pulled Lot back into the house with them and slammed the door.
local: Ke ŋutsuawo do asi ɖa he Lot va wo ɖokui gbɔ le aƒea me, eye wotu agboa.

eng: The boy grew and stopped nursing. On the day he stopped nursing, Abraham prepared a huge banquet.
local: Isak va tsi. Gbe si gbe woɖee le no nu la, Abraham ɖo kplɔ̃ gã aɖe na amewo.

eng: Go to my land and my family and find a wife for my son Isaac there.
local: ke boŋ yeayi mía denyigba dzi, aɖaɖe srɔ̃ na vinye Isak tso nye ƒometɔwo dome.”.

eng: I will bless her and even give you a son from her. I will bless her so that she will become nations, and kings of peoples will come from her.
local: Mayrae, eye mana viŋutsui wòadzi na wò. Mayrae ne wòava zu dukɔwo kple dukɔ dziɖulawo dada.”.

eng: Abimelech said to Abraham, What were you thinking when you did this thing.
local: Ale ke nèbu hafi wɔe?”.

eng: Abraham said, The lamb for the entirely burned offering? God will see to it, my son. The two of them walked on together.
local: Abraham ɖo eŋu be: “Vinye, Mawu ŋutɔ ana alẽvi si míatsɔ amè nui.” Ale wo kple eve la gaɖo ta ŋgɔ.

eng: Sarah lied and said, I didnt laugh, because she was frightened. But he said, No, you laughed.
local: Ke Sara sẽ nu gblɔ be: “Nyemeko nu o.” Elabe ele vɔvɔ̃m. Ke egblɔ nɛ be: “Ao, èko nu ɖe!”.

eng: Listen to us, sir. You are an eminent man of God among us. Bury your dead in one of our own select burial sites. None of us will keep our own burial plots from you to bury your dead.
local: “Amegã, see ɖa na mí. Mawu ƒe amee nènye le mía dome. Àte ŋu aɖi wò ame kukua ɖe míaƒe ame veviwo ɖiƒe nyuitɔ faa. Mía dometɔ ɖe sia ɖe alɔ̃ faa be nàɖi wò amea ɖe yeƒe ameɖiƒe.”.

eng: Then the messengers blinded the men near the entrance of the house, from the youngest to the oldest, so that they groped around trying to find the entrance.
local: Tete ŋutsuawo na ame siwo katã le xexe le agboa nu, ɖevi kple tsitsia siaa ƒe ŋku gbã, eye wotsa di mɔa nu ʋuu do kpo.

eng: Sarah saw Hagars son laughing, the one Hagar the Egyptian had borne to Abraham.
local: Gbe ɖeka la, Sara kpɔ Egipte-nyɔnua, Hagar vi, Ismael, ame si wòdzi na Abraham la, wònɔ fefem kple via Isak.

eng: The servant said to him, What if the woman doesnt agree to come back with me to this land? Shouldnt I take your son back to the land you left.
local: Ke dɔla la gblɔ nɛ be: “Ɖewohĩ nyɔnua malɔ̃ be yeakplɔm ɖo ava anyigba sia dzi o. Ekema makplɔ viwò la gbugbɔ yi anyigba si dzi nèʋu tso la dzia?”.

eng: Abraham fell on his face and laughed. He said to himself, Can a -year-old man become a father, or Sarah, a -year-old woman, have a child?.
local: Abraham tsyɔ mo anyi, eye wòko nu hegblɔ le susu me be: “Ɖe ŋutsu si xɔ ƒe alafa ɖeka sɔŋ la ate ŋu adzi via? Ɖe Sara si xɔ ƒe bla asieke la ate ŋu adzi via?”.

eng: Abraham said, I thought to myself, No one reveres God here and they will kill me to get my wife.
local: Abraham ɖo eŋu be: “Nye ya metsɔe be mawuvɔ̃la aɖeke mele afi sia o, ale woawum axɔ srɔ̃nye.

eng: The men got up from there and went over to look down on Sodom. Abraham was walking along with them to send them off.
local: Le esia megbe la, ŋutsuawo dze mɔ, eye Abraham kplɔ wo ɖo ɖa. Wokpɔ Sodom le abu me ɖa.

eng: They arrived at the place God had described to him. Abraham built an altar there and arranged the wood on it. He tied up his son Isaac and laid him on the altar on top of the wood.
local: Esi woɖo teƒe si Mawu fiae la, Abraham ɖi vɔsamlekpui, eye wòɖo nake la ɖe edzi. Ebla Isak, kɔe da ɖe nakea dzi le mlekpuia dzi.

eng: Abraham rose, bowed to the local citizens the Hittites.
local: Abraham tsi tsitre eye wòbɔbɔ ta na Xitiviwo, ame siwo nye anyigbaa tɔwo la.

eng: The men said to Lot, Whos still with you here? Take away from this place your sons-in-law, your sons, your daughters, and everyone else you have in the city.
local: Tete ŋutsuawo gblɔ na Lot be: “Ame bubuwo le afi sia kpe ɖe ŋuwò abe viwò ŋutsuwo, viwò nyɔnuwo, toyɔviwòwo alo wò ƒometɔwo enea? Kplɔ wo dzoe le teƒe sia.

eng: So she said to Abraham, Send this servant away with her son! This servants son wont share the inheritance with my son Isaac.
local: Ale Sara gblɔ na Abraham be: “Nyà kosi sia kple via. Elabe vinye mele dome nyi ge kple kosi sia ƒe vi o.”.

eng: Abraham said to him, Be sure you dont take my son back there.
local: Abraham ɖo eŋu nɛ be: “Kpɔ nyuie be màgagbugbɔ vinye la ayi afi mae o!.

eng: To God Abraham said, If only you would accept Ishmael.
local: Ebia Mawu be: “Ale ke wɔ màna Ismael natsi agbe anɔ anyi le ŋkuwò me ko oa?”.

eng: She is, truthfully, my sister—my fathers daughter but not my mothers daughter—and shes now my wife.
local: Ke hã, le nyateƒe me la, nɔvinye nyɔnue, elabe tɔ́viwo míenye, ke menye dadaviwo o, eye meɖee.

eng: when the LORD said, Will I keep from Abraham what Im about to do?.
local: Aƒetɔ gblɔ be: “Ɖe maɣla nu si wɔ ge mele la ɖe Abrahama?.

eng: Then Abraham stretched out his hand and took the knife to kill his son as a sacrifice.
local: Azɔ Abraham do asi ɖa, tsɔ hɛ be yeawu ye viŋutsu la.

eng: and spoke with them: If you yourselves allow me to bury my dead near me, listen to me and ask Ephron, Zohars son.
local: eye wògblɔ na wo be: “Ne mielɔ̃ be maɖi nye amea ɖe afii la, ekema taflatsɛ, miɖe kuku na Efron, Zoxar vi nam.

eng: because we are about to destroy this place. The LORD has found the cries of injustice so serious that the LORD sent us to destroy it.
local: elabe míele dua tsrɔ̃ ge. Ɣlidodo le nu baɖa siwo wɔm dua me tɔwo le la ŋu va ɖo Aƒetɔ gbɔ, eye wòdɔ mí ɖa be míava tsrɔ̃ dua.”.

eng: This upset Abraham terribly because the boy was his son.
local: Ke nya la mesɔ na Abraham kura o, elabe Ismael hã nye via.

eng: The LORD, God of heaven—who took me from my fathers household and from my familys land, who spoke with me and who gave me his word, saying, I will give this land to your descendants—he will send his messenger in front of you, and you will find a wife for my son there.
local: Aƒetɔ, dziƒo ƒe Mawu la, ame si ɖem ɖa tso tɔ́nye ƒeme kple mía denyigba dzi, eye wòdo anyigba sia ŋugbe nam be: ‘Wò dzidzimeviwo matsɔ anyigba sia ana’ la, adɔ eƒe dɔla wòanɔ ŋgɔwò, nàɖaɖe srɔ̃ na vinye ŋutsu tso afi ma.

eng: But God said, No, your wife Sarah will give birth to a son for you, and you will name him Isaac. I will set up my covenant with him and with his descendants after him as an enduring covenant.
local: Ke Mawu gblɔ be: “Ao! Srɔ̃wò Sara le ŋutsuvi dzi ge na wò, eye nàyɔe be Isak. Mawɔ nye nubabla la dzi na eya kple eƒe dzidzimeviwo tegbee.

eng: When God led me away from my fathers household, I said to her, This is the loyalty I expect from you: in each place we visit, tell them, He is my brother.
local: Esi Mawu be maʋu le tɔ́nye ƒeme ayi dzronyigba dzi la, megblɔ na Sara be: ‘Medi be nàve nunye. Afi sia afi si míaɖo la, gblɔ na ame sia ame be ye nɔviŋutsue menye.’ ”.

eng: Abraham will certainly become a great populous nation, and all the earths nations will be blessed because of him.
local: Evɔ wònye eƒe dzidzimeviwoe ava zu dukɔ gã triakɔ aɖe, eye eyama mee woayra anyigba dzi dukɔwo katã le ɖe!.

eng: But the LORDs messenger called out to Abraham from heaven, Abraham? Abraham? Abraham said, Im here.
local: Ke Aƒetɔ ƒe dɔla yɔe tso dziƒo be: “Abraham! Abraham!” Etɔ be: “Nyee nye esi!”.

eng: to give me his own cave in Machpelah at the edge of his field. Let him give it to me for the full price, to be witnessed by you, as my own burial property.
local: Medi be wòadzra eƒe Maxpela-kpeto si le eƒe agble to la nam, le mia ŋkume afii, ale si wòdi be yeadzrae ko la, ne maƒlee atsɔ wɔ nye ameɖiƒee koŋ le mia dome.”.

eng: Lot went to speak to his sons-in-law, married to his daughters, and said, Get up and get out of this place because the LORD is about to destroy the city. But his sons-in-law thought he was joking.
local: Ale Lot do yi ŋutsu siwo bia via nyɔnuwo ta be yewoaɖe la gbɔ, eye wògblɔ na wo be: “Netsɔ! Miva mído le dua me, elabe Aƒetɔ le dua tsrɔ̃ ge!” Ke toyɔviawo ya bu eƒe nya la fefenyae.

eng: God said to Abraham, Dont be upset about the boy and your servant. Do everything Sarah tells you to do because your descendants will be traced through Isaac.
local: Tete Mawu gblɔ na Abraham be: “Mègana nya sia naɖe fu na wò le ɖevia kple wò kosi la ta o. Ke boŋ nya sia nya si Sara gblɔ na wò la nàɖo toe, elabe Isak me wò dzidzimeviwo ado tso.

eng: If the woman wont agree to come back with you, you will be free from this obligation to me. Only dont take my son back there.
local: Ke ne nyɔnua melɔ̃ be yeakplɔ wò ɖo ava o la, ekema menye wòe tu atam sia o; ɖeko mègakplɔ vinye la va yi afi mae gbeɖe o!”.

eng: As for Ishmael, I’ve heard your request. I will bless him and make him fertile and give him many, many descendants. He will be the ancestor of twelve tribal leaders, and I will make a great nation of him.
local: Ke le Ismael tɔ gome la, mese wò nyawo. Kpɔ ɖa, mayra eya hã; mana wòadzi viwo, eye eƒe dzidzimeviwo asɔ gbɔ fũu ŋutɔ. Ava nye tɔ́ na fia wui eve, eye mana eƒe dzidzimeviwo nanye dukɔ gã aɖe.

eng: I have formed a relationship with him so that he will instruct his children and his household after him. And they will keep to the LORDs path, being moral and just so that the LORD can do for Abraham everything he said he would.
local: Gawu la, nye Aƒetɔ, metiae be wòana viawo kple eƒe dzidzimeviwo naɖo tom, eye woawɔ nu dzɔdzɔe kple nu si le eteƒe, ne mawɔ nu si ŋugbe medo nɛ la dzi.”.

eng: Now Ephron was a native Hittite. So Ephron the Hittite responded to Abraham publicly in order that the Hittites and everyone at his citys gate could hear.
local: Efron ŋutɔ hã nɔ anyi ɖe Xitiviawo dome le agboa nu le afi ma, eye wòɖo nya la ŋu na Abraham le Xitiviawo kple ame siwo katã to agboa nu le gegem ɖe dua me la ŋkume be:.

eng: Abimelech took flocks, cattle, male servants, and female servants, and gave them to Abraham; and Abimelech returned his wife Sarah.
local: Tete Abimelex gbugbɔ Sara na Abraham, eye wòna alẽwo, nyiwo kple kluviwo hãe.

eng: The messenger said, Dont stretch out your hand against the young man, and dont do anything to him. I now know that you revere God and didnt hold back your son, your only son, from me.
local: Egblɔ nɛ be: “Mègaka asi ɖevia ŋu o; mègawɔ nu vevi aɖeke hãe o. Menyae azɔ be mawuvɔ̃la nènye, elabe mète viwò ɖeka hɔ̃ lam o.”.

eng: When dawn broke, the messengers urged Lot, Get up and take your wife and your two daughters who are here so that you are not swept away because of the evil in this city.
local: Le fɔŋli la, mawudɔlaawo ƒo du ɖe Lot te gblɔ be: “Netsɔ! Tso! Kplɔ srɔ̃wò kple viwò nyɔnu eve siwo le gbɔwò la, ne miado go le dua me, bene miagatsrɔ̃ le tohehe na dua me o.”.

eng: But I will make of your servants son a great nation too, because he is also your descendant.
local: Mana dukɔ nado tso kosi vi la hã me, elabe eya hã viwòe.”.

eng: So the servant put his hand under his master Abrahams thigh and gave him his word about this mission.
local: Ale dɔla la tsɔ asi de Abraham, eƒe aƒetɔ la, ƒe atame, eye wòka atam nɛ ɖe nya la dzi.

eng: But I will set up my covenant with Isaac, who will be born to Sarah at this time next year.
local: Ke mabla nu kple viwò Isak, ame si Sara adzi na wò ƒe si gbɔna ƒe ɣe alewo ɣi la.”.

eng: No, sir. Listen, I will give you the field, and I will give you the cave in it. In front of my peoples witnesses, I will give it to you. Bury your dead.
local: “Nye amegã, taflatsɛ see ɖa! Metsɔ nye agble la kple kpeto si le eme la na wò faa. Mele etsɔm na wò le mía detɔ siwo katã le afii la ŋkume. Ɖi wò ame la ɖe afi ma faa.”.

eng: Then the LORD said, The cries of injustice from Sodom and Gomorrah are countless, and their sin is very serious!.
local: Tete Aƒetɔ gblɔ na Abraham be: “Nutsotso ɖe Sodom kple Gomora duwo ŋu va glo, eye woƒe nuvɔ̃ wɔwɔ wu gbɔgblɔ.

eng: Abraham looked up and saw a single ram caught by its horns in the dense underbrush. Abraham went over, took the ram, and offered it as an entirely burned offering instead of his son.
local: Abraham trɔ kɔ. Kasia agbo aɖee nye ekem le emegbe, eƒe dzowo ku aveka. Eyi ɖalée, eye wòtsɔe sa vɔ lae ɖe via teƒe.

eng: Abimelech said, My land is here available to you. Live wherever you wish.
local: Egblɔ na Abraham be: “Tɔnyee nye anyigba sia katã. Nɔ afi sia afi si nyo ŋuwò la.”.

eng: He hesitated, but because the LORD intended to save him, the men grabbed him, his wife, and two daughters by the hand, took him out, and left him outside the city.
local: Ke esi Lot le tɔtrɔm to eme ko la, Aƒetɔ ve enu, eye ŋutsuawo lé eya, srɔ̃a kple via nyɔnu eveawo ƒe alɔnu, kplɔ wo va dua godo.

eng: Abraham got up early in the morning, took some bread and a flask of water, and gave it to Hagar. He put the boy in her shoulder sling and sent her away. She left and wandered through the desert near Beer-sheba.
local: Ale esi Abraham fɔ ŋdi kanya la, edi nuɖuɖu na Hagar. Eku tsi ɖe lãgbalẽgolo me wòyɔ, eye wòkɔ ɖevia kple nuawo ɖo abɔta nɛ hedo mɔe. Hagar dzo, eye wòyi ɖatsa le Beer-Seba gbegbe.

eng: The servant took ten of his masters camels and all of his masters best provisions, set out, and traveled to Nahors city in Aram-naharaim.
local: Dɔla la tia eƒe aƒetɔ la ƒe kposɔ ewo, tsɔ eƒe nu nyui vovovo aɖewo hã ɖe asi, eye wòdze mɔ yi ɖe du si me Naxor nɔ le Mesopotamia ƒe dzigbeme la me.

eng: When God finished speaking to him, God ascended, leaving Abraham alone.
local: Esi Mawu ƒo nu kple Abraham vɔ la, edzo yi dzi le egbɔ.

eng: Abraham bowed before the local citizens.
local: Abraham bɔbɔ ta le Xitiviawo ŋkume.

eng: I will go down now to examine the cries of injustice that have reached me. Have they really done all this? If not, I want to know.
local: Eya ta maɖi ayi, aɖakpɔ be nu siwo sem mele le wo ŋuti la le eme loo, alo womele eme o, ne manya.”.

eng: To Sarah, he said, Ive given your brother one thousand pieces of silver. It means that neither you nor anyone with you has done anything wrong. Everything has been set right.
local: Egblɔ na Sara be: “Kpɔ ɖa, metso afia na wò. Metsɔ klosaloga akpe ɖeka na nɔviwò be matsɔ afia ame siwo katã le ŋuwò, be ŋukpe aɖeke mele ŋuwò o, eye mèwɔ vɔ̃ aɖeke o.”.

eng: Abraham named that place the LORD sees. That is the reason people today say, On this mountain the LORD is seen.
local: Ale Abraham na ŋkɔ teƒe ma be: “Aƒetɔ ye naa nu.” Eya ta wogblɔna va se ɖe egbegbe be: “To dzie Aƒetɔ naa nu le.”.

eng: After getting them out, the men said, Save your lives! Dont look back! And dont stay in the valley. Escape to the mountains so that you are not swept away.
local: Esi wokplɔ wo do goe vɔ la, wogblɔ na Lot be: “Ekpo ɖe dziwò azɔ. Sí dzo, ne nàtsi agbe! Mègakpɔ megbe o; mègatɔ ɖe afi aɖeke hã le balia me o. Sí yi toawo dzi be nàgatsrɔ̃ o.”.

eng: Finally the water in the flask ran out, and she put the boy down under one of the desert shrubs.
local: Esi tsia vɔ la, Hagar tsɔ ɖevia mlɔ ati dodoe aɖe te.

eng: He had the camels kneel down outside the city at the well in the evening, when women come out to draw water.
local: Ena eƒe kposɔawo tsyɔ akɔ anyi ɖe vudo aɖe to le dua godo le fiẽ, le nyɔnuwo ƒe tɔmedeɣi.

eng: Abraham took his son Ishmael, all those born in his household, and all those purchased with his silver—that is, every male in Abrahams household—and he circumcised the flesh of their foreskins that same day, just as God had told him to do.
local: Gbe ma gbe ke la, Abraham lã aʋanuyi na Ismael kple ŋutsu ɖe sia ɖe si wodzi le eƒeme kpakple esiwo katã wòƒle kple ga la. Etso aʋa na wo abe ale si Mawu gblɔ nɛ la ene.

eng: and spoke to Ephron publicly in the presence of the local citizens: If only you would accept my offer. I will give you the price of the field. Take it from me so that I can bury my dead there.
local: eye wòƒo nu na Efron, dua me tɔwo katã see be: “Taflatsɛ, see ɖa nam. Na maxe fe ɖe anyigba la ta. Ga lae nye esi; xɔe, ne maɖi nye ame kukua ɖe edzi.”.

eng: Abraham prayed to God; and God restored Abimelech, his wife, and his women servants to health, and they were able to have children.
local: Tete Abraham do gbe ɖa na Mawu, eye Mawu yɔ dɔ Abimelex kple srɔ̃a kpakple eƒe kosiviwo ale be wodzi viwo.

eng: The LORDs messenger called out to Abraham from heaven a second time.
local: Aƒetɔ ƒe dɔla yɔ Abraham zi evelia tso dziƒo.

eng: The men turned away and walked toward Sodom, but Abraham remained standing in front of the LORD.
local: Ale ameawo trɔ ɖo ta Sodom. Abraham gale tsitre ɖe Aƒetɔ ŋkume ko.

eng: But Lot said to them, No, my lords, please.
local: Ke Lot ɖo eŋu na wo be: “Gbeɖe, Aƒetɔ, meganɔ nenema o.

eng: She walked away from him about as far as a bow shot and sat down, telling herself, I cant bear to see the boy die. She sat at a distance, cried out in grief, and wept.
local: elabe egblɔ na eɖokui be: “Nyemate ŋu ado dzi akpɔ ɖevia kuƒe o.” Ale wòdzo yi ɖabɔbɔ nɔ adzɔge abe kpedada ɖeka ƒe didime ene tso egbɔ. Azɔ ɖevia wó avi kple ɣli.

eng: He said, LORD, God of my master Abraham, make something good happen for me today and be loyal to my master Abraham.
local: Dɔla la do gbe ɖa be: “Aƒetɔ, nye aƒetɔ Abraham ƒe Mawu! Meɖe kuku, na nu si gbe meva la, nadze dzi nam egbe, eye nànyo dɔme na nye aƒetɔ Abraham.

eng: Abraham was years old when he circumcised the flesh of his foreskin.
local: Abraham xɔ ƒe bla asieke vɔ asieke hafi wotso aʋa nɛ.

eng: Ephron responded to Abraham.
local: Efron ɖo eŋu na Abraham be:.

eng: Because of the incident with Abrahams wife Sarah, the LORD had kept all of the women in Abimelechs household from having children.
local: Elabe le Sara, Abraham srɔ̃, ta la, Aƒetɔ xe nyɔnu siwo katã le Abimelex ƒeme la ƒe vidzidɔ nu.

eng: Abraham approached and said, Will you really sweep away the innocent with the guilty?.
local: Ete ɖe Aƒetɔ ŋu eye wòbiae be: “Ɖe nàtsrɔ̃ ame dzɔdzɔewo kple ame vɔ̃ɖiwo siaa nyateƒea?.

eng: and said, I give my word as the LORD that because you did this and didnt hold back your son, your only son.
local: eye wògblɔ be: “Metà ɖokuinye, esi nèwɔ nu sia, eye mète viwò ɖeka hɔ̃ lam o ta la.

eng: Youve done me a favor and have been so kind to save my life. But I cant escape to the mountains since the catastrophe might overtake me there and Id die.
local: Kpɔ ɖa, ènyo dɔme na nye, wò dɔla ŋutɔ, eye nèkpɔ nublanui nam, xɔm ɖe agbe; ke toawo gbɔ didi akpa. Ɖewohĩ ne wode asi dua tsɔtsrɔ̃ me la, nyemele toawo gbɔ ɖo ge hafi atsrɔ̃ o.

eng: God heard the boys cries, and Gods messenger called to Hagar from heaven and said to her, Hagar! Whats wrong? Dont be afraid. God has heard the boys cries over there.
local: Ke Mawu se ŋutsuvia ƒe gbe, eye mawudɔla yɔ Hagar tso dziƒo gblɔ nɛ be: “Hagar, nu kae le wɔwòm? Mègavɔ̃ o. Mawu se ŋutsuvia ƒe gbe le afi si wòle.

eng: I will stand here by the spring while the daughters of the men of the city come out to draw water.
local: Kpɔ ɖa, mele tsitre ɖe vudoa to fifia, eye dua me ɖetugbiwo gbɔna tsi du ge le afii.

eng: and his son Ishmael was years old when the flesh of his foreskin was circumcised.
local: eye via Ismael hã xɔ ƒe wui ene.

eng: Sir, what is four hundred shekels of silver between me and you for the land so that you can bury your dead.
local: “Amegã, ɖo to nàsee! Anyigba la axɔ klosaloga alafa ene. Ema manye nya le nye kple wò dome kura o. Tsɔe nàɖi wò amea ɖe edzi ko.”.

eng: What if there are fifty innocent people in the city? Will you really sweep it away and not save the place for the sake of the fifty innocent people in it?.
local: Ne ame dzɔdzɔe bla atɔ̃ le dua me ɖe, àtsrɔ̃ du bliboa? Màxa ɖe ame dzɔdzɔe bla atɔ̃ siawo ta atsɔ ke dua oa?.

eng: I will bless you richly and I will give you countless descendants, as many as the stars in the sky and as the grains of sand on the seashore. They will conquer their enemies cities.
local: mayra wò ale gbegbe, eye mana wò dzidzimeviwo nasɔ gbɔ abe dziƒoɣletiviwo kple ƒutake ene. Wò dzidzimeviwo axɔ woƒe futɔwo ƒe duwo.

eng: This city here is close enough to flee to, and its small. Its small, right? Let me escape there, and my life will be saved.
local: Taflatsɛ, du sue aɖee nye ekem ɖa. Eya tsɔ ɖe mía gbɔ. Melolo o; eya ta na masí yi afi ma bene matsi agbe.”.

eng: Get up, pick up the boy, and take him by the hand because I will make of him a great nation.
local: Tso nàyi ɖakɔe, eye nàlée ɖe asi, elabe mana eƒe dzidzimeviwo nazu dukɔ gã aɖe.”.

eng: That same day Abraham and his son Ishmael were circumcised.
local: Ŋkeke ɖeka dzie wotso aʋa na wo kple eve la.

eng: When I say to a young woman, Hand me your water jar so I can drink, and she says to me, Drink, and I will give your camels water too, may she be the one youve selected for your servant Isaac. In this way I will know that youve been loyal to my master.
local: Na ɖetugbi si magblɔ na be: ‘Meɖe kuku, dro wò tsize ɖi ne mano tsi’, eye wòagblɔ be: ‘Noe, eye mana wò kposɔwo hã woano’ la, nenye ame si dzi nètɔ asii na wò dɔla Isak. To esia me madze sii be ève nye aƒetɔ nu.”.

eng: Abraham accepted Ephrons offer and weighed out for Ephron the silver he requested publicly before the Hittites: four hundred shekels of silver at the current rate of exchange.
local: Abraham lɔ̃ ɖe nya la dzi. Eɖe klosaloga kpekpeme alafa ene, si Efron yɔ la, ɖe asitsalawo ƒe nudada nu le ɣe ma ɣi me, le Xitiviawo ŋkume hetsɔ nɛ.

eng: Its not like you to do this, killing the innocent with the guilty as if there were no difference. Its not like you! Will the judge of all the earth not act justly.
local: Nete ɖa xaa tso tso gbɔwò be nàwu ame dzɔdzɔe kple ame vɔ̃ɖi siaa ɖekae, eye ame dzɔdzɔe manɔ abe ame vɔ̃ɖi ene o. Nete ɖa xaa tso gbɔwò! Ɖe xexea me katã ƒe ʋɔnudrɔ̃la mawɔ nu dzɔdzɔe oa?”.

eng: He said to Lot, Ill do this for you as well; I wont overthrow the city that you have described.
local: Eɖo eŋu nɛ be: “Enyo, melɔ̃ ɖe wò nya sia hã dzi; nyemagbã du si nèwɔnɛ la o.

eng: Then God opened her eyes, and she saw a well. She went over, filled the water flask, and gave the boy a drink.
local: Tete Mawu ʋu Hagar ƒe ŋkuwo, eye wòkpɔ tsivudo aɖe le afi ma. Eyi ɖaku tsia ɖe lãgbalẽgolo la me wòyɔ. Ekɔe vɛ, eye wòna ɖe ɖevia wòno.

eng: All the nations of the earth will be blessed because of your descendants, because you obeyed me.
local: Esi nèwɔ nye gbe dzi ta la, woayra anyigba dzi dukɔwo katã to wò dzidzimeviwo dzi.”.

eng: All the men of his household, those born in his household and those purchased with silver from foreigners, were circumcised with him.
local: Wotsoe na ŋutsuwo katã le eƒeme; esiwo wodzi ɖe aƒea me kple esiwo wòƒle kple ga le amedzrowo si la siaa.

eng: Even before he finished speaking, Rebekah—daughter of Bethuel the son of Milcah wife of Nahor, Abrahams brother—was coming out with a water jar on her shoulder.
local: Ke subɔla la mekpɔ do gbea ɖa vɔ hafi ɖetugbi aɖe va do o. Eŋkɔe nye Rebeka. Enye Betuel, Abraham nɔviŋutsu Naxor viŋutsu kple srɔ̃a Milka ƒe vinyɔnuvi. Elé tɔmedeze ɖe abɔta va tsi du ge.

eng: So the field of Ephron in Machpelah near Mamre—the field and the cave in it, and all the trees within the fields boundaries—was officially transferred.
local: Ale Efron ƒe agble si le Maxpela, le Mamre ƒe ɣedzeƒe gome la va zu Abraham tɔ. Kpeto kple ati siwo katã le agblea me hã le eme.

eng: The LORD said, If I find fifty innocent people in the city of Sodom, I will save it because of them.
local: Aƒetɔ ɖo eŋu be: “Ne makpɔ ame dzɔdzɔe bla atɔ̃ le Sodom-du me la, maxa ɖe woawo ta atsɔ ke du bliboa.”.

eng: Hurry! Escape to it! I cant do anything until you get there. That is why the name of the city is Zoar.
local: Ke netsɔ! Sí yi afi ma kaba! Nyemate ŋu awɔ naneke o, va se ɖe esime nèɖo afi ma hafi.” Esia ta woyɔa du la be Zoar.

eng: God remained with the boy; he grew up, lived in the desert, and became an expert archer.
local: Mawu nɔ kple ɖevia, wòtsi. Enɔ gbedadaƒo, eye wòva zu aŋutrɔdala nyui aɖe.

eng: After Abraham returned to the young men, they got up and went to Beer-sheba where Abraham lived.
local: Ale Abraham trɔ va eƒe dɔlawo gbɔ, eye wodze mɔ yi Beer-Seba, afi si Abraham ɖanɔ.

eng: The young woman was very beautiful, old enough to be married, and hadnt known a man intimately. She went down to the spring, filled her water jar, and came back up.
local: Ɖetugbi la dze tugbe ŋutɔ, eye menya ŋutsu o. Eɖi ɖe vudoa me ɖaku tsia hedo go.

eng: to Abraham as his property in the presence of the Hittites and of everyone at his citys gate.
local: Xitiviawo kple ame bubu siwo katã va afi ma la kpɔ eteƒe be eva zu Abraham tɔ.

eng: Abraham responded, Since Ive already decided to speak with my Lord, even though Im just soil and ash.
local: Abraham yi edzi be: “Taflatsɛ, Aƒetɔ, tsɔe kem be medo dzi gale nu ƒom kpli wò. Anyi kple afi ko menye.

eng: As the sun rose over the earth, Lot arrived in Zoar.
local: Ɣe dze hafi Lot ɖo Zoar.

eng: He lived in the Paran desert, and his mother found him an Egyptian wife.
local: Enɔ Paran-gbegbe, eye nɔa ɖe srɔ̃ nɛ tso Egipte-nyigba dzi.

eng: After these events, Abraham was told: Milcah has now also given birth to sons for your brother Nahor.
local: Esiawo megbe la, Abraham se be Milka hã dzi viŋutsuwo na ye nɔvi Naxor.

eng: The servant ran to meet her and said, Give me a little sip of water from your jar.
local: Tete dɔla la ƒu du yi ɖakpee gblɔ nɛ be: “Meɖe kuku, na tsi vi aɖem mano!”.

eng: After this, Abraham buried his wife Sarah in the cave in the field of Machpelah near Mamre, that is, Hebron, in the land of Canaan.
local: Esia megbe la, Abraham ɖi srɔ̃a Sara ɖe kpeto la me le Maxpela, le Mamre ƒe ɣedzeƒe gome , le Kanaan-nyigba dzi.

eng: At that time Abimelech, and Phicol commander of his forces, said to Abraham, God is with you in everything that you do.
local: Ɣe ma ɣi la, Abimelex kple eƒe aʋakplɔla Pixol yi ɖagblɔ na Abraham be: “Mawu li kpli wò le nu sia nu si nèwɔna la me.

eng: what if there are five fewer innocent people than fifty? Will you destroy the whole city over just five? The LORD said, If I find forty-five there, I wont destroy it.
local: Ɖewohĩ ame atɔ̃ ato le ame dzɔdzɔe bla atɔ̃ me. Ɖe nàtsrɔ̃ du blibo la le ame atɔ̃ si to le eme la ta?” Aƒetɔ ɖo eŋu be: “Ne makpɔ ame dzɔdzɔe bla ene vɔ atɔ̃ le dua me la, nyematsrɔ̃e o.”.

eng: They are Uz his oldest son, Buz his brother, Kemuel the father of Aram.
local: Woƒe ŋkɔwoe nye: Uz, Naxor ƒe ŋgɔgbevi, Buz, Kemuel, ame si me Aramtɔwo dzɔ tso.

eng: and the LORD rained down burning asphalt from the skies onto Sodom and Gomorrah.
local: Tete Aƒetɔ na aŋɔkpe kple dzo dza tso dziƒo ɖe Sodom kple Gomora dzi.

eng: She said, Drink, sir. Then she quickly lowered the water jar with her hands and gave him some water to drink.
local: Egblɔ be: “Amegã, va no faa.” Edro ze la ɖi enumake, eye wòna tsi lae wòno.

eng: The field and the cave in it were officially transferred from the Hittites to Abraham as his burial property.
local: Ale agble kple kpeto si nye Xitiviwo tɔ tsã la va zu Abraham tɔ koŋ, eye wòzu eƒe ameɖiƒe.

eng: Chesed, Hazo, Pildash, Jidlaph, and Bethuel.
local: Kesed, Hoso, Pildoas, Yidlaf kple Betuel.

eng: So give me your word under God that you wont cheat me, my children, or my descendants. Just as I have treated you fairly, so you must treat me and the land in which you are an immigrant.
local: Eya ta ka atam nam le Mawu ŋkume afii, fifi laa be yemable nye ŋutɔ loo, vinyewo loo, alo nye dzidzimeviwo akpɔ o. Mewɔ nuteƒe na wò va yi, eya ta ka atam nam be ye hã yeawɔ nuteƒe nam kple anyigba si dzi yeva le fifia la hã.”.

eng: The LORD destroyed these cities, the entire valley, everyone who lived in the cities, and all of the fertile lands vegetation.
local: wofiã eye wòtsrɔ̃ nu siwo katã le balia me la keŋkeŋ. Dua me nɔlawo katã ku, eye numiemiewo tsrɔ̃ gbidii.

eng: Once again Abraham spoke, What if forty are there? The LORD said, For the sake of forty, I will do nothing.
local: Abraham gayi edzi be: “Ɖewohĩ ne wokpɔ ame bla ene ɖe?” Eɖo eŋu be: “Ne makpɔ ame bla ene le dua me la, nyematsrɔ̃e o.”.

eng: When she finished giving him a drink, she said, Ill draw some water for your camels too, till theyve had enough to drink.
local: Esi wòno vɔ la, ɖetugbi la gblɔ nɛ be: “Maku tsia ɖe vɛ na wò kposɔawo hã ne woano.”.

eng: Bethuel became the father of Rebekah. These are the eight Milcah bore for Nahor, Abrahams brother.
local: Betuel dzi Rebeka. Vi enyi siawoe Milka dzi na Naxor, Abraham nɔvi la.

eng: Abraham said, I give you my word.
local: Abraham gblɔ be: “Meka atam be mawɔ nuteƒe na mi.”.

eng: He said, Dont be angry with me, my Lord, but let me speak. What if thirty are there? The LORD said, I wont do it if I find thirty there.
local: Abraham gblɔ be: “Aƒetɔ, meɖe kuku, mègakpɔ dziku be megale nu ƒo ge o. Ɖewohĩ ame bla etɔ̃ ko woakpɔ.” Egblɔ be: “Ne makpɔ ame bla etɔ̃ le eme la, nyematsrɔ̃e o.”.

eng: When Lots wife looked back, she turned into a pillar of salt.
local: Ke Lot srɔ̃ si le eyome la trɔ kɔ kpɔ megbe, eye wòtrɔ zu dzekɔ.

eng: She emptied her water jar quickly into the watering trough, ran to the well again to draw water, and drew water for all of the camels.
local: Ewɔ kaba, trɔ tsia kɔ ɖe lãwo ƒe tsinonu me, eye wògaƒu du ɖi ɖe vudoa me ɖadu tsia gava trɔ ɖi na kposɔawo va se ɖe esime wo katã no tsi, wòɖi kɔ na wo.

eng: His secondary wifes name was Reumah, and she gave birth to Tebah, Gaham, Tahash, and Maacah.
local: Eƒe ahiãvi, si ŋkɔe nye Rehuma la, hã dzi Tebax, Gaham, Tahas kple Maxa nɛ.

eng: Then Abraham complained to Abimelech about a well that Abimelechs servants had seized.
local: Tete Abraham ƒo nu kple Abimelex le tsivudo si Abimelex ƒe dɔlawo xɔ le esi sesẽe la ŋuti.

eng: Abraham said, Since Ive already decided to speak with my Lord, what if twenty are there? The LORD said, I wont do it, for the sake of twenty.
local: Abraham gagblɔ be: “Aƒetɔ, meɖe kuku, tsɔe kem be medo dzi gale nu ƒom kpli wò. Ɖewohĩ ame bla eve ko woakpɔ.” Eɖo eŋu be: “Le ame bla eve ta la, nyematsrɔ̃e o.”.

eng: Abraham set out early for the place where he had stood with the LORD.
local: Evea gbe ƒe ŋdi kanya la, Abraham fɔ kaba yi teƒe si wòtsi tsitre ɖe Aƒetɔ ŋkume le va yi la.

eng: The man stood gazing at her, wondering silently if the LORD had made his trip successful or not.
local: Ŋutsu la li kpoo nɔ ekpɔm dũu, ne yeakpɔ ɖa be Aƒetɔ ana nu si gbe yeva la nadze edzi na ye mahã.

eng: Abimelech said, I dont know who has done this, and you didnt tell me. I didnt even hear about it until today.
local: Abimelex gblɔ be: “Nyemenya ame si wɔ nu sia o, mègblɔe nam kpɔ o, eye nyemese naneke tso eŋu kpɔ wu egbe o.”.

eng: Abraham said, Dont be angry with me, my Lord, but let me speak just once more. What if there are ten? And the LORD said, I will not destroy it because of those ten.
local: Abraham gblɔ be: “Aƒetɔ, meɖe kuku, mègakpɔ dziku o. Medi be magaƒo nu zi ɖeka pɛ ko. Ɖewohĩ ame ewo ko woakpɔ.” Eɖo eŋu be: “Le ame ewo ta la, nyematsrɔ̃ dua o.”.

eng: and looked out over Sodom and Gomorrah and over all the land of the valley. He saw the smoke from the land rise like the smoke from a kiln.
local: Ekpɔ Sodom kple Gomora kple teƒe siwo katã le balia me la ɖa, eye dzudzɔ le dzi dem tso anyigba la dzi, abe aŋɔɖaƒe ene.

eng: As soon as the camels had finished drinking, the man took out a gold ring, weighing a half shekel, and two gold bracelets for her arms, weighing ten shekels.
local: Esi kposɔawo no tsia vɔ la, ŋutsua ɖe sikasigɛ si ƒe kpekpeme anɔ gram ade kple sikalɔnuga eve siwo ƒe kpekpeme anɔ sika gram alafa ɖeka wui ene la, do na ɖetugbi la.

eng: When the LORD finished speaking with Abraham, he left; but Abraham stayed there in that place.
local: Ke esi Aƒetɔ ƒo nu kple Abraham vɔ la, edzo, eye Abraham hã trɔ yi aƒeme.

eng: Abraham took flocks and cattle, gave them to Abimelech, and the two of them drew up a treaty.
local: Azɔ Abraham na alẽwo kple nyiwo Abimelex, eye wo kple evea wobla nu.

eng: When God destroyed the cities in the valley, God remembered Abraham and sent Lot away from the disaster that overtook the cities in which Lot had lived.
local: Esi Mawu le du siwo le bali si me Lot nɔ tsrɔ̃ ge la, eƒe ŋku nɔ Abraham dzi eye wòɖe Lot tso tsɔtsrɔ̃ gã si va du siwo me wònɔ dzi la me.

eng: He said, Please tell me whose daughter you are. Is there room in your fathers house for us to spend the night.
local: Azɔ ebiae be: “Ame ka vi nènye? Míate ŋu akpɔ dzeƒe le tɔ́wò ƒemea?”.

eng: Abraham set aside, by themselves, seven female lambs from the flock.
local: Ke Abraham ɖe alẽnɔe adrẽ ɖe vovo.

eng: Since Lot had become fearful of living in Zoar, he and his two daughters headed up from Zoar and settled in the mountains where he and his two daughters lived in a cave.
local: Azɔ Lot va le Zoar nɔnɔ vɔ̃m, eya ta eya kple via nyɔnu eveawo ʋu le afi ma ɖanɔ agado me le tonyigba la dzi.

eng: She responded, Im the daughter of Bethuel, who is the son of Milcah and Nahor.
local: Eɖo eŋu nɛ be: “Betuel, Milka vi, si wòdzi na Naxor la vinyɔnu menye.”.

eng: So Abimelech said to Abraham, What are these seven lambs youve set apart.
local: Abimelex biae be: “Alẽnɔe adrẽ siawo, siwo nèɖe ɖe vovo la gɔme ɖe?”.

eng: The older daughter said to the younger, Our father is old, and there are no men in the land to sleep with us as is the custom everywhere.
local: Vinyɔnu tsitsitɔa gblɔ na ɖevitɔ be: “Mía tɔ́ tsi vɔ, eye ŋutsu aɖeke megale anyigba la dzi adɔ kpli mí abe ale si amegbetɔwo wɔnɛ la ene o.

eng: She continued, We have plenty of straw and feed for the camels, and a place to spend the night.
local: Egblɔ yi edzi be: “Aƒla kple gbe le mía si fũu, eye dzeƒe hã li.”.

eng: Abraham said, These seven lambs that you take from me will attest that I dug this well.
local: Abraham ɖo eŋu be: “Xɔ alẽnɔe adrẽ siawo. Woawoe nye kpeɖodzinu be wòe nye nye ɖasefo be nyee ɖe vudo sia.”.

eng: Come on, lets give our father wine to drink, lie down with him, and well have children from our father.
local: Kpɔ ɖa, na míana wain mía tɔ́ wòano amu, ale be míana wòado fu mí ne míadzi vi bene mía tɔ́ ƒe dzidzime nayi edzi.”.

eng: The man bowed down and praised the LORD.
local: Ŋutsua dze klo, eye wòdo gbe ɖa na Aƒetɔ.

eng: That night they served their father wine, and the older daughter went in and lay down with her father, without him noticing when she lay down or got up.
local: Ale gbe ma gbe ƒe zã me la, wona wain wo tɔ́ wòno, eye tsitsitɔ yi ɖamlɔ egbɔ. Ke wo tɔ́ menya be eva mlɔ ye gbɔ fɔ dzo o.

eng: Therefore, the name of that place is Beer-sheba because there they gave each other their word.
local: Esia tae woyɔa teƒe ma be Beer-Seba, elabe afi mae wo kple evea wobla nu le.

eng: Bless the LORD, God of my master Abraham, who hasnt given up his loyalty and his faithfulness to my master. The LORD has shown me the way to the household of my masters brother.
local: be: “Woakafu Aƒetɔ, nye aƒetɔ Abraham ƒe Mawu la. Ekpɔa eta, vea enu, eye wòwɔa nuteƒe nɛ ɖaa. Le gonye me la, Aƒetɔ fia mɔm, eye wòkplɔm tẽe va nye aƒetɔ ƒomeawo gbɔe.”.

eng: The next day the older daughter said to the younger, Since I lay down with our father last night, lets serve him wine tonight too, and you go in and lie down with him so that we will both have children from our father.
local: Esi ŋu ke la, tsitsitɔ gblɔ na ɖevitɔ be: “Étò ƒe zã me la, memlɔ tɔ́nye gbɔ. Míagana wòano wain egbe ƒe zã hã, ale be wò hã nàyi aɖamlɔ egbɔ bene míana eƒe dzidzime nayi edzi.”.

eng: After they drew up a treaty at Beer-sheba, Abimelech, and Phicol commander of his forces, returned to the land of the Philistines.
local: Esi wobla nu vɔ alea le Beer-Seba la, Abimelex kple eƒe aʋakplɔla Pixol dze mɔ gatrɔ yi ɖe Filistitɔwo ƒe anyigba dzi.

eng: The young woman ran and told her mothers household everything that had happened.
local: Ɖetugbia ƒu du yi aƒeme ɖagblɔ nya siawo na nɔa ƒemetɔwo.

eng: They served their father wine that night also, and the younger daughter lay down with him, without him knowing when she lay down or got up.
local: Ale esi zã gado la, wogana wain wo tɔ́ wòno mu, eye ɖevitɔ hã yi ɖamlɔ egbɔ. Ke wo tɔ́ menya be eva mlɔ ye gbɔ fɔ dzo o.

eng: Abraham planted a tamarisk tree in Beer-sheba, and he worshipped there in the name of the LORD, El Olam.
local: Abraham do atiwo ɖe Beer-Seba, eye wòsubɔ Aƒetɔ, Mawu Mavɔtɔ la le afi ma.

eng: Rebekah had a brother named Laban, and Laban ran to the man outside by the spring.
local: Azɔ nɔviŋutsu le Rebeka si; eŋkɔe nye Laban. Laban ƒu du yi ŋutsua gbɔ le vudoa to.

eng: Both of Lots daughters became pregnant by their father.
local: Ale wɔ Lot do fu eya ŋutɔ via nyɔnu eveawo.

eng: Abraham lived as an immigrant in the Philistines land for a long time.
local: Abraham nɔ Filistitɔwo ƒe anyigba dzi abe amedzro ene eteƒe didi.

eng: When he had seen the ring and the bracelets on his sisters arms, and when he had heard his sister Rebekah say, This is what the man said to me, he went to the man, who was still standing by the spring with his camels.
local: Do ŋgɔ la, Laban kpɔ ŋɔtigɛ kple alɔnuga le nɔvia nyɔnu ŋu, eye wòse nya siwo wòbe ŋutsua gblɔ na ye la hã. Eyi ɖatu ŋutsua wòle tsitre ɖe kposɔawo gbɔ le vudoa to.

eng: The older daughter gave birth to a son and named him Moab. He is the ancestor of todays Moabites.
local: Tsitsitɔ dzi ŋutsuvi, eye wòtsɔ ŋkɔ nɛ be Moab. Eya ƒe dzidzimeviwoe nye Moabitɔ siwo li egbegbe.

eng: Laban said, Come in, favored one of the LORD! Why are you standing outside? Ive prepared the house and a place for the camels.
local: Laban gblɔ nɛ be: “Wò ame si Aƒetɔ yra, va míyi aƒeme. Nu ka ta nètsi tsitre ɖe gbɔto le afii? Medzra xɔ me ɖo ɖi na wò. Teƒe li na wò kposɔawo hã.”.

eng: The younger daughter also gave birth to a son and named him Ben-ammi. He is the ancestor of todays Ammonites.
local: Ɖevitɔ hã dzi ŋutsuvi, eye wòtsɔ ŋkɔ nɛ be Ben-Ami. Eya ƒe dzidzimeviwoe nye Amontɔ siwo li egbegbe.

eng: So the man entered the house. Then Laban unbridled the camels, provided straw and feed for them and water to wash his feet and the feet of the men with him.
local: Esi wòkplɔ ŋutsua va aƒeme la, etu agbaawo le kposɔawo dzi, eye wòna gbe wo woɖu. Eku tsi vɛ na ŋutsua kple ame siwo katã kpe ɖe eŋuti la be woaklɔ afɔ.

eng: and set out a meal for him. But the man said, I wont eat until Ive said something. Laban replied, Say it.
local: Ke esi wotsɔ nuɖuɖu va ɖo ŋutsua kɔme la, egblɔ be: “Nyemaɖu nu o, va se ɖe esime megblɔ afɔ si dzi mele la na mi gbã.” Laban ɖo eŋu be: “Ƒo nu faa!”.

eng: The man said, I am Abrahams servant.
local: Egblɔ be: “Abraham ƒe dɔlae menye.

eng: The LORD has richly blessed my master, has made him a great man, and has given him flocks, cattle, silver, gold, men servants, women servants, camels, and donkeys.
local: Aƒetɔ yra nye aƒetɔ ŋutɔ, eye wòzu nunɔamesitɔ gã ale gbegbe. Ena alẽha kple nyiha, klosalo kple sika, kluviwo kple kosiwo, kposɔwo kple tedziwoe fũu.

eng: My masters wife Sarah gave birth to a son for my master in her old age, and hes given him everything he owns.
local: Nye aƒetɔ srɔ̃, Sara, togbɔ be eku nyagã hã, edzi viŋutsu nɛ, eye nye aƒetɔ tsɔ eƒe nunɔamesiwo katã na via.

eng: My master made me give him my word: Dont choose a wife for my son from the Canaanite women, in whose land Im living.
local: Nye aƒetɔ na meka atam nɛ be mawɔ ŋugbe si medo nɛ la dzi. Egblɔ nam be, ‘Mègaɖe srɔ̃ na vinye tso Kanaantɔ siwo dome mele la vinyɔnuwo dome o;.

eng: No, instead, go to my fathers household and to my relatives and choose a wife for my son.
local: ke boŋ yi ɖe ƒonyemeawo gbɔ, le tɔ́nye ƒeme, eye nàɖe srɔ̃ na vinye tso afi ma.

eng: I said to my master, What if the woman wont come back with me.
local: Azɔ mebia nye aƒetɔ be: ‘Ke ne nyɔnua melɔ̃ be yeakplɔm ɖo ava o ɖe, nu ka mawɔ?’.

eng: He said to me, The LORD, whom Ive traveled with everywhere, will send his messenger with you and make your trip successful; and you will choose a wife for my son from my relatives and from my fathers household.
local: Eɖo eŋu nam be: ‘Aƒetɔ, ame si meɖoa toe ɣe sia ɣi la, adɔ eƒe dɔla wòayi kpli wò, eye wòana nu si gbe nèyi la nadze dzi na wò, ale be nàɖe srɔ̃ na vinye tso ƒonyemeawo gbɔ le tɔ́nye ƒeme.

eng: If you go to my relatives, you will be free from your obligation to me. Even if they provide no one for you, you will be free from your obligation to me.
local: Evɔ ne èyi ƒonyemeawo gbɔ, eye womelɔ̃ tsɔ ɖetugbi aɖeke na wò o la, ekema menye wòe tu nye atam la o.’.

eng: Today I arrived at the spring, and I said, LORD, God of my master Abraham, if you wish to make the trip Im taking successful.
local: “Esi meva ɖo vudoa to egbea la, medo gbe ɖa be: ‘Aƒetɔ, nye aƒetɔ Abraham ƒe Mawu! Meɖe kuku, na be nu si wɔ ge meva la, nadze dzi nam.

eng: when Im standing by the spring and the young woman who comes out to draw water and to whom I say, Please give me a little drink of water from your jar.
local: Kpɔ ɖa, mele tsitre ɖe vudoa to. Nenye be ɖetugbi aɖe va tsi du ge la, mele ebia ge be wòana tsi vi aɖem mano.

eng: and she responds to me, Drink, and I will draw water for your camels too, may she be the woman the LORD has selected for my masters son.
local: Ne eɖo eŋu nam be: ‘Noe faa, eye mana wò kposɔawo hã woano’ la, ekema na be eya nanye nyɔnu si nètia da ɖi na nye aƒetɔ viŋutsu la be wòaɖe.’.

eng: Before I finished saying this to myself, Rebekah came out with her water jar on her shoulder and went down to the spring to draw water. And I said to her, Please give me something to drink.
local: Nyemekpɔ do gbea ɖa le tame vɔ o, kasia Rebeka va do. Elé ze ɖe abɔta, eye wòɖi ɖe vudoa me ɖaku tsia do va. Megblɔ nɛ be: ‘Meɖe kuku, na tsim mano!’.

eng: She immediately lowered her water jar and said, Drink, and I will give your camels something to drink too. So I drank and she also gave water to the camels.
local: Edro ze la ɖi enumake hegblɔ be: ‘Noe; mana wò kposɔawo hã.’ Esi meno tsia vɔ la, ena kposɔawo hã wono.

eng: Then I asked her, Whose daughter are you? And she said, The daughter of Bethuel, Nahors son whom Milcah bore him. I put a ring in her nose and bracelets on her arms.
local: Azɔ mebiae be: ‘Ame ka vi nènye?’ Eɖo eŋu nam be: ‘Betuel viŋutsu si Milka dzi na Naxor la vinyɔnue menye.’ Ale metsɔ ŋɔtigɛ la do nɛ hetsɔ alɔnuga la hã do ɖe alɔnu nɛ.

eng: I bowed and worshipped the LORD and blessed the LORD, the God of my master Abraham, who led me in the right direction to choose the granddaughter of my masters brother for his son.
local: Tete mebɔbɔ tsyɔ mo anyi hedo gbe ɖa na Aƒetɔ. Mekafu Aƒetɔ, nye aƒetɔ Abraham ƒe Mawu la, ame si kplɔm tẽe va nye aƒetɔ nɔviwo gbɔ tututu be maɖe srɔ̃ na via tso wo dome.

eng: Now if youre loyal and faithful to my master, tell me. If not, tell me so I will know where I stand either way.
local: Azɔ ne mielɔ̃ be yewoave nye aƒetɔ la nu, awɔ nuteƒe nɛ la, taflatsɛ, migblɔe nam. Ke ne mielɔ̃ o hã la, migblɔe nam bene manya nu si mawɔ.”.

eng: Laban and Bethuel both responded, This is all the LORDs doing. We have nothing to say about it.
local: Tete Laban kple Betuel woɖo eŋu be: “Esi wònye Aƒetɔ gbɔe nya sia tso ta la, míawo míate ŋu alɔ̃ alo agbe o.

eng: Here is Rebekah, right in front of you. Take her and go. She will be the wife of your masters son, just as the LORD said.
local: Kpɔ ɖa, Rebekae nye esi; kplɔe yii faa, ne wòazu wò aƒetɔ ƒe vi la srɔ̃ abe ale si Aƒetɔ la gblɔe ene.”.

eng: When Abrahams servant heard what they said, he bowed low before the LORD.
local: Esi Abraham ƒe dɔla la se nya sia la, etsyɔ mo anyi, eye wòdo gbe ɖa na Aƒetɔ.

eng: The servant brought out gold and silver jewelry and clothing and gave them to Rebekah. To her brother and to her mother he gave the finest gifts.
local: Azɔ la, dɔla la ɖe klosalo kple sika nuwo kple avɔ xɔasiwo ɖe go tsɔ na Rebeka. Etsɔ nunana xɔasi geɖewo hã na nɔvia ŋutsua kple nɔa.

eng: He and the men with him ate and drank and spent the night. When they got up in the morning, the servant said, See me off to my master.
local: Azɔ Abraham ƒe dɔla la kple ame siwo kpe ɖe eŋu la ɖu nu, no nu, eye wotsi afi ma dɔ. Esi wofɔ ŋdi la, ŋutsua gblɔ be: “Mido mɔm ne matrɔ yi nye aƒetɔ gbɔ.”.

eng: Her brother and mother said, Let the young woman stay with us not more than ten days, and after that she may go.
local: Ke Rebeka nɔviŋutsua kple nɔa gblɔ na ŋutsua be: “Na ɖetugbi la nanɔ mía gbɔ ʋɛ abe ŋkeke ewo ene, ema megbe la, ate ŋu ayi faa.”.

eng: Abraham married another wife, named Keturah.
local: Abraham ɖe srɔ̃ bubu si ŋkɔe nye Ketura.

eng: But he said to them, Dont delay me. The LORD has made my trip successful. See me off so that I can go to my master.
local: Ke eɖo eŋu na wo be: “Migazi mía dzi be míatsi anyi o. Esi Aƒetɔ na nu si wɔ ge meva la dze dzi nam ta la, mido mɔm ne matrɔ yi nye aƒetɔ gbɔ.”.

eng: The children she bore him were Zimran, Jokshan, Medan, Midian, Ishbak, and Shuah.
local: Eya dzi Simran, Yoksan, Medan, Midian, Yisbak kple Suax nɛ.

eng: They said, Summon the young woman, and lets ask her opinion.
local: Woɖo eŋu be: “Míayɔ ɖetugbi la ŋutɔ vɛ abiae se.”.

eng: Jokshan became the father of Sheba and Dedan. Dedans sons were Asshurim, Letushim, and Leummim.
local: Yoksan dzi Seba kple Dedan. Dedan ƒe dzidzimeviwoe nye: Asuritɔwo, Letusitɔwo kple Leumitɔwo.

eng: They called Rebekah and said to her, Will you go with this man? She said, I will go.
local: Ale woyɔ Rebeka vɛ hebiae be: “Àyi kple ŋutsu siaa?” Eɖo eŋu be: “Ẽ, mayi.”.

eng: When a famine gripped the land, a different one from the first famine that occurred in Abrahams time, Isaac set out toward Gerar and toward King Abimelech of the Philistines.
local: Dɔ gato le anyigba la dzi, abe ale si wòto le Abraham ŋɔli ene. Ale Isak ʋu yi ɖe Filistitɔwo ƒe fia Abimelex gbɔ le Gerar.

eng: Midians sons were Ephah, Epher, Enoch, Abida, and Eldaah. All of these were Keturahs sons.
local: Midian viŋutsuwoe nye Efa, Efer, Henox, Abida kple Edaa. Ame siawo katã nye Ketura ƒe dzidzimeviwo.

eng: So they sent off their sister Rebekah, her nurse, Abrahams servant, and his men.
local: Ale wodo mɔ wo nɔvi Rebeka kple nyɔnu si tsɔe wòtsi la, wodzo yi kple Abraham ƒe dɔla la kple eŋumewo.

eng: The LORD appeared to him and said, Dont go down to Egypt but settle temporarily in the land that I will show you.
local: Do ŋgɔ la, Aƒetɔ ɖe eɖokui fiae, gblɔ nɛ be: “Mègayi Egipte o; ke boŋ nɔ anyigba si mafia wò la dzi.

eng: Abraham gave everything he owned to Isaac.
local: Abraham tsɔ eƒe nunɔamesiwo katã na via Isak.

eng: And they blessed Rebekah, saying to her, May you, our sister, become thousands of ten thousand; may your children possess their enemies cities.
local: Woyra Rebeka gblɔ nɛ be: “Wò, mía nɔvinyɔnu, nàva zu ame akpe nanewo nɔ. Wò dzidzimeviwo neɖu woƒe futɔwo dzi; wonexɔ woƒe duwo woazu wo tɔ!”.

eng: Stay in this land as an immigrant, and I will be with you and bless you because I will give all of these lands to you and your descendants. I will keep my word, which I gave to your father Abraham.
local: Nɔ afi ma, eye manɔ kpli wò, ayra wò, elabe matsɔ anyigba blibo sia katã ana wò kple wò dzidzimeviwo. Mawɔ atam si meka na tɔ́wò Abraham la dzi godoo.

eng: To the sons of Abrahams secondary wives, Abraham gave gifts and, while he was still living, sent them away from his son Isaac to land in the east.
local: ke le eƒe agbenɔɣi la, Abraham na nu viŋutsu siwo eƒe ahiãviwo dzi nɛ la, eye wòdo mɔ wo ɖo ɖe anyigba si le ɣedzeƒe gome la dzi bene woaɖe ɖa le Isak ŋu.

eng: Rebekah and her young women got up, mounted the camels, and followed the man. So the servant took Rebekah and left.
local: Azɔ la, Rebeka kple eƒe nyɔnudɔlawo dzra ɖo, de kposɔwo dzi, eye wodze ŋutsua yome dzo. Ale ŋutsua kplɔ Rebeka dzoe.

eng: I will give you as many descendants as the stars in the sky, and I will give your descendants all of these lands. All of the nations of the earth will be blessed because of your descendants.
local: Mana wò dzidzimeviwo nasɔ gbɔ abe ɣletiviwo le dziƒo ene, eye matsɔ anyigba sia katã ana wò. Wò dzidzimeviwo dzi woato ayra anyigba dzi dukɔwo katã.

eng: Abraham lived to the age of .
local: Azɔ Abraham nɔ agbe ƒe alafa ɖeka bla adrẽ vɔ atɔ̃.

eng: When Isaac had grown old and his eyesight was failing, he summoned his older son Esau and said to him, My son? And Esau said, Im here.
local: Azɔ esi Isak tsi zu amegãɖeɖi, eƒe ŋkuwo dzi tsyɔ, eye megate ŋu le nu kpɔm o la, eyɔ via tsitsitɔ, Esau, vɛ hegblɔ nɛ be: “Vinye!” Etɔ be: “Nyee nye esi.”.

eng: Now Isaac had come from the region of Beer-lahai-roi and had settled in the arid southern plain.
local: Emegbe Isak va le Beerlaxairoi si le anyigbemekegbegbe.

eng: I will do this because Abraham obeyed me and kept my orders, my commandments, my statutes, and my instructions.
local: Mayra wò elabe Abraham ɖo tom, eye wòwɔ nye sededewo kple ɖoɖowo kpakple mɔnu siwo mefiae la dzi.”.

eng: He said, Im old and dont know when I will die.
local: Egblɔ be: “Kpɔ ɖa, metsi, eye nyemenya nye kugbe o.

eng: Abraham took his last breath and died after a good long life, a content old man, and he was placed with his ancestors.
local: Abraham ku esime wòtsi xɔ ƒè geɖee, eye wòzu amegãɖeɖi belibeli hafi yi tɔgbuiawo gbɔ.

eng: One evening, Isaac went out to inspect the pasture, and while staring he saw camels approaching.
local: Gbe ɖeka fiẽ la, Isak yi gbe dzi be yeaɖado gbe ɖa. Esi wòfɔ mo dzi la, ekpɔ kposɔwo gbɔna ɖa.

eng: So Isaac lived in Gerar.
local: Ale Isak nɔ Gerar.

eng: His sons Isaac and Ishmael buried him in the cave in Machpelah, which is in the field of Zohars son Ephron the Hittite, near Mamre.
local: Via ŋutsuwo, Isak kple Ismael, ɖii ɖe Maxpela-kpeto la me. Kpeto sia le agble si le Mamre ƒe ɣedzeƒe gome, eye wònye Xititɔ Efron, Zoxar vi ƒe anyigba kpɔ.

eng: So now, take your hunting gear, your bow and quiver of arrows, go out to the field, and hunt game for me.
local: Tsɔ wò adedanuwo, da kple dati, eye nàyi gbe me nàɖawu lã aɖe vɛ nam.

eng: So Isaac summoned Jacob, blessed him, and gave him these orders: Dont marry a Canaanite woman.
local: Isak yɔ Yakob vɛ, yrae, eye wòde se nɛ be: “Mègaɖe Kanaan-nyɔnuvi aɖeke o.

eng: Rebekah stared at Isaac. She got down from the camel.
local: Ke esi Rebeka hã fɔ kɔ dzi la, ekpɔ Isak ɖa. Rebeka wɔ kaba, ɖi le eƒe kposɔ dzi.

eng: When the men who lived there asked about his wife, he said, Shes my sister, because he was afraid to say, my wife, thinking, The men who live there will kill me for Rebekah because shes very beautiful.
local: Esi afi ma ŋutsuwo bia be ame kae nye srɔ̃a hã la, egblɔ na wo be ye nɔvinyɔnue. Egblɔ alea, elabe evɔ̃ be ne yegblɔ be ye srɔ̃e la, woawu ye le Rebeka ta, elabe edze tugbe ŋutɔ.

eng: Thus Abraham and his wife Sarah were both buried in the field Abraham had purchased from the Hittites.
local: Agble siae Abraham ƒle le Xititɔwo si va yi, eye afi mae woɖi, eya kple srɔ̃a Sara ɖo.

eng: Make me the delicious food that I love and bring it to me so I can eat. Then I can bless you before I die.
local: Nàtsɔ lã la wɔ nuɖuɖu si vivia nunye ŋutɔ la vɛ nam maɖu, ne mayra wò hafi maku.”.

eng: Get up and go to Paddan-aram, to the household of Bethuel, your mothers father, and once there, marry one of the daughters of Laban, your mothers brother.
local: Tso nàyi nɔwò tɔ́, Betuel, ƒeme le Padan-Aram ne nàɖe nyruiwò Laban vinyɔnuviwo dometɔ ɖeka.

eng: and said to the servant, Who is this man walking through the pasture to meet us? The servant said, Hes my master. So she took her headscarf and covered herself.
local: Ebia dɔla la be: “Ŋutsu kae nye ekem le gbea dzi, zɔ gbɔna ɖe mía gbɔ?” Dɔla la ɖo eŋu nɛ be: “Nye aƒetɔe.” Tete wòtsɔ motsyɔnu tsyɔ mo.

eng: After Isaac had lived there for some time, the Philistines King Abimelech looked out his window and saw Isaac laughing together with his wife Rebekah.
local: Esi wonɔ afi ma eteƒe didi la, gbe ɖeka Abimelex kpɔ Isak to fesre nu wònɔ fefem kple Rebeka srɔ̃tɔe.

eng: After Abrahams death, God blessed his son Isaac, and Isaac lived in Beer-lahai-roi.
local: Mawu yra Isak le tɔ́a Abraham ƒe ku megbe, eye wònɔ afi si woyɔna be “Agbe tɔ si le kpɔyem la ƒe Vudo.”.

eng: Rebekah was listening when Isaac spoke to his son Esau. When Esau went out to the field to hunt game to bring back.
local: Rebeka se nya si Isak gblɔ na Esau la. Ale esi Esau dzo yi adegbe be yeaɖawu lã vɛ la.

eng: God Almighty will bless you, make you fertile, and give you many descendants so that you will become a large group of peoples.
local: Mawu Ŋusẽkatãtɔ la neyra wò, eye wòana nàdzi fũu ale be nàva zu dukɔ geɖewo tɔ́.

eng: Jacob got to his feet and set out for the land of the easterners.
local: Yakob yi eƒe mɔzɔzɔ dzi, eye wòva ɖo ɣedzeƒenyigba dzi.

eng: The servant told Isaac everything that had happened.
local: Le esia megbe la, dɔla la gblɔ ale si wòde nua gbee la na Isak tsitotsito.

eng: So Abimelech summoned Isaac and said, Shes your wife, isnt she? How could you say, Shes my sister? Isaac responded, Because I thought that I might be killed because of her.
local: Ale Abimelex yɔ Isak vɛ, eye wòbiae be: “Srɔ̃wòe nye nyɔnuae! Nu ka ta nègblɔ be ye nɔvinyɔnue?” Isak ɖo eŋu nɛ be: “Mebu be ne megblɔ be srɔ̃nye wònye la, woawum.”.

eng: Rebekah said to her son Jacob, I just heard your father saying to your brother Esau.
local: Rebeka gblɔ na via Yakob be: “Mese tɔ́wò gblɔ na nɔviwò be:.

eng: These are the descendants of Ishmael, Abrahams son, whom Hagar the Egyptian, Sarahs servant, bore for Abraham.
local: Ismael, ame si Egipte-nyɔnu Hagar, Sara ƒe kosi, dzi na Abraham la.

eng: He saw a well in the field in front of him, near which three flocks of sheep were lying down. That well was their source for water because the flocks drank from that well. A huge stone covered the wells opening.
local: Kasia eva ke ɖe vudo aɖe ŋu le agbledeƒe aɖe. Alẽha etɔ̃ tsyɔ akɔ anyi ɖe vudoa gbɔ. Eya mee wokua tsi le naa alẽhaawo wonona. Kpe gã aɖe le vudoa nu.

eng: He will give you and your descendants Abrahams blessing so that you will own the land in which you are now immigrants, the land God gave to Abraham.
local: Neyra wò kple wò dzidzimeviwo abe ale si wòyra Abraham ene, eye wòatsɔ anyigba si dzi nènye amedzro le fifia, anyigba si wòtsɔ na Abraham la, ana wò.”.

eng: Isaac brought Rebekah into his mother Sarahs tent. He received Rebekah as his wife and loved her. So Isaac found comfort after his mothers death.
local: Azɔ Isak kplɔ Rebeka yi ɖe nɔa, Sara, ƒe agbadɔme. Eɖee wòzu srɔ̃a, eye wòlɔ̃e vevie. Ale Isak ƒe ŋku va ɖe ɖa le nɔa ƒe ku ŋu, eye eƒe dzi dze eme.

eng: Abimelech said, What are you trying to do to us? Before long, one of the people would have slept with your wife; and you would have made us guilty.
local: Abimelex gblɔ nɛ be: “Nu kae nye esi nèwɔ mí kloe? Esusɔ vie ŋunyemewo dometɔ ɖeka dɔ kple srɔ̃wò kloe, ne nàhe fɔbubu va mía dzi.”.

eng: Bring me some game and make me some delicious food so I can eat, and I will bless you in the LORDs presence before I die.
local: ‘Di adelã aɖe nàwɔ nuɖuɖu si vivia nunye vevie la vɛ nam maɖu ne mayra wò, le Aƒetɔ ŋkume hafi maku.’.

eng: These are the names of Ishmaels sons, by their names and according to their birth order: Nebaioth, Ishmaels oldest son; Kedar; Adbeel; Mibsam.
local: viwo ŋkɔwoe nye esiwo gbɔna ɖe woƒe tsitsime nu: Nebayot, Kedar, Adbeel, Mibsam.

eng: When all of the flocks were gathered there, the shepherds would roll the stone from the wells opening, water the sheep, and return the stone to its place at the wells opening.
local: Nenye be alẽhaawo katã va ƒo ƒu la, womlia kpea ɖa, eye wonaa tsi alẽawo. Emegbe la, wogamlia kpea tua enu.

eng: So Isaac sent Jacob off, and he traveled to Paddan-aram, to Laban son of Bethuel the Aramean and brother of Rebekah, Jacob and Esaus mother.
local: Ale Isak do mɔ Yakob ɖo ɖe Padan-Aram ɖe Aramtɔ Betuel vi Laban, ame si nye Yakob kple Esau dada nɔviŋutsu la gbɔ.

eng: Abimelech gave orders to all of the people, Anyone who touches this man or his wife will be put to death.
local: Ale Abimelex de se na eteviwo katã be: “Ame aɖeke megawɔ naneke ŋutsu sia kple srɔ̃a o. Ne ame aɖe ka asi ŋutsu sia kple srɔ̃a ŋu la, woawui kokoko.”.

eng: Now, my son, listen to me, to what Im telling you to do.
local: Azɔ vinye, se nya si gblɔ ge mala na wò la, eye nàwɔ edzi.

eng: Mishma; Dumah; Massa.
local: Misma, Duma, Masa.

eng: Jacob said to them, Where are you from, my brothers? They said, Were from Haran.
local: Yakob bia alẽkplɔlaawo be: “Nɔvinyewo, afi ka mietso?” Woɖo eŋu be: “Míetso Xaran.”.

eng: Esau understood that Isaac had blessed Jacob and sent him to Paddan-aram to marry a woman from there. He recognized that, when Isaac blessed Jacob, he had ordered him, Dont marry a Canaanite woman.
local: Esau va nya be Isak yra Yakob, eye wòɖoe ɖe Padan-Aram be wòayi aɖaɖe srɔ̃ le afi ma. Enya hã be Isak de se na Yakob vevie le eyraɣi be megaɖe Kanaan-vinyɔnu aɖeke o.

eng: Isaac planted grain in that land and reaped one hundred shearim that year because the LORD had blessed him.
local: Isak de agble ɖe anyigba la dzi, eye nu si wòŋe le ƒè ma me la de esi wòƒã la ƒe teƒe alafa ɖeka sɔŋ elabe Aƒetɔ yrae.

eng: Hadad; Tema; Jetur; Naphish; and Kedemah.
