local: Xadad, Tema, Yetur, Nafis kple Kedma.

eng: Go to the flock and get me two healthy young goats so I can prepare them as the delicious food your father loves.
local: Yi lãhawo dome nàlé gbɔ̃vi eve vɛ ne matsɔ wɔ nuɖuɖu si vivia tɔ́wò nu la nɛ.

eng: Then he said to them, Do you know Laban, Nahors grandson? They said, We know him.
local: Ebia wo be: “Mienya Naxor vi, Labana?” Woɖo eŋu be: “Ẽ, míenyae.”.

eng: and that Jacob had listened to his father and mother and gone to Paddan-aram.
local: Hekpe ɖe esia ŋu la, ede dzesii be Yakob ɖo to yewo dzilawo, eye wòdzo yi Padan-Aram.

eng: Isaac grew richer and richer until he was extremely wealthy.
local: Eva zu kesinɔtɔ gã ale gbegbe. Ɖeko eƒe nukpɔkpɔwo nɔ dzidzim ɖe dzi, ganɔ dzidzim ɖe dzi.

eng: These are Ishmaels sons. These are their names by their villages and their settlements: twelve tribal leaders according to their tribes.
local: Ame siawoe nye Ismael viŋutsuwo, eye esiawoe nye woƒe ŋkɔwo ɖe woƒe du suewo kple woƒe asaɖawo nu; amegã wui eve ɖe woƒe towo nu.

eng: You can bring it to your father, he will eat, and then he will bless you before he dies.
local: eye nàtsɔe ayi na tɔ́wò wòaɖu ne wòayra wò hafi aku.”.

eng: Esau realized that his father Isaac considered Canaanite women unacceptable.
local: Esi wòkpɔ dzesii be Kanaan-nyɔnuwo medze yewo tɔ́ Isak ŋu o la.

eng: He said to them, Is he well? They said, Hes fine. In fact, this is his daughter Rachel now, coming with the flock.
local: Egabia wo be: “Eli nyuiea?” Woɖo eŋu be: “Eli nyuie. Kpɔ ɖa, via Raxel gɔ̃ hãe nye ema kplɔ alẽwo gbɔna.”.

eng: He had livestock, both flocks and cattle, and many servants. As a result, the Philistines envied him.
local: Alẽhawo kple nyihawo sɔ gbɔ ɖe esi ale gbegbe, eye kluviwo hã nɔ esi fũu. Ale Filistitɔwo va ʋã ŋui.

eng: Ishmael lived to the age of . He took his last breath and died, and was placed with his ancestors.
local: Ismael tsi xɔ ƒe alafa ɖeka bla etɔ̃ vɔ adrẽ hafi ku.

eng: Jacob said to his mother Rebekah, My brother Esau is a hairy man, but I have smooth skin.
local: Ke Yakob gblɔ na nɔa Rebeka be: “Kpɔ ɖa, fu mele ŋunye o, evɔ nɔvinye Esau ya la, fúme wònye.

eng: So he went to Ishmael and married Mahalath daughter of Abrahams son Ishmael and sister of Nebaioth, in addition to his other wives.
local: eyi ɖe Abraham vi, Ismael gbɔ ɖaɖe via Mahalat si nye Nebayot nɔvinyɔnu kpe ɖe srɔ̃a bubuawo ŋu.

eng: He said to them, Its now only the middle of the day. Its not time yet to gather the animals. Water the flock, and then go, put them out to pasture.
local: Yakob gblɔ na wo be: “Kpɔ ɖa, ɣe gale dzi; migaƒo alẽawo nu ƒu haɖe o. Mina tsi wo woano ne miana woagayi aɖaɖu gbe.”.

eng: The Philistines closed up and filled with dirt all of the wells that his fathers servants had dug during his father Abrahams lifetime.
local: Woku anyi xe vudo siwo katã tɔ́a Abraham ƒe dɔlawo ɖe esime wònɔ agbe la keŋkeŋ.

eng: He established camps from Havilah to Shur, which is near Egypt on the road to Assyria. He died among all of his brothers.
local: Eƒe dzidzimeviwoe nɔ Xavila kple Sur dome le Egipte ƒe ɣedzeƒe gome ɖo ta Asiria. Woawo sa wo ɖokui ɖe aga tso Abraham ƒe dzidzimevi bubuawo gbɔ.

eng: When Rachel realized that she could bear Jacob no children, Rachel became jealous of her sister and said to Jacob, Give me children! If you dont, I may as well be dead.
local: Esi Raxel kpɔ be yemedzi vi aɖeke na Yakob o la, eʋã ŋu nɔvia Lea, eye wògblɔ na Yakob be: “Na madzi vi, ne menye nenem o la, maku.”.

eng: What if my father touches me and thinks Im making fun of him? I will be cursed instead of blessed.
local: Ɖewohĩ tɔ́nye ali asi ŋunye kpɔ, ale wòadze sim. Ekema abum ameflulae, eye mayram o, ke aƒo fi boŋ adem.”.

eng: Jacob left Beer-sheba and set out for Haran.
local: Yakob dze mɔ tso Beer-Seba ɖo ta Xaran.

eng: They said to him, We cant until all the herds are gathered, and then we roll the stone away from the wells opening and water the flock.
local: Woɖo eŋu be: “Míate ŋu awɔ nenema o, va se ɖe esime alẽhaawo katã naƒo ƒu, ekema míamli kpe la ɖa le vudoa nu, eye míana tsi wo woano.”.

eng: Abimelech said to Isaac, Move away from us because you have become too powerful among us.
local: Abimelex gblɔ na Isak be: “Dzo le mía dome, elabe èva kpɔ ŋusẽ wu mí.”.

eng: These are the descendants of Isaac, Abrahams son. Abraham became the father of Isaac.
local: Abraham vi Isak hã ƒe dzidzimeŋutinyae nye esi:.

eng: Jacob was angry at Rachel and said, Do you think Im God? God alone has kept you from giving birth.
local: Yakob do dɔmedzoe ɖe eŋu gblɔ be: “Mawue menye, axe wò vidzidɔ nua?”.

eng: His mother said to him, Your curse will be on me, my son. Just listen to me: go and get them for me.
local: Nɔa ɖo eŋu nɛ be: “Vinye, ne eƒo fi de wò la, neɖi ŋunye. Wò ya ɖo tom; yi nàlé lãawo vɛ ko!”.

eng: He reached a certain place and spent the night there. When the sun had set, he took one of the stones at that place and put it near his head. Then he lay down there.
local: Eva ɖo teƒe aɖe si zã do ɖee le, eye wòtsi afi ma dɔ. Etsɔ kpe aɖe le afi ma ziɔ, eye wòmlɔ anyi dɔ alɔ̃.

eng: While he was still talking to them, Rachel came with her fathers flock since she was its shepherd.
local: Yakob mekpɔ ɖe nu le eme hafi Raxel si nye alẽkplɔla la va ɖo kple tɔ́a ƒe alẽwo o.

eng: So Isaac moved away from there, camped in the valley of Gerar, and lived there.
local: Ale Isak dzo le afi ma. Ke eyi ɖatu agbadɔ ɖe Gerar-balia me, eye wònɔ afi ma.

eng: Jacob heard that Labans sons were saying, Jacob took everything our father owned and from it he produced all of this wealth.
local: Yakob se Laban viŋutsuwo le gbɔgblɔm be: “Yakob xɔ mía tɔ́ ƒe nuwo katã. Kesinɔnu siwo katã le esi la, mía tɔ́ tɔ me wotso.”.

eng: Isaac was years old when he married Rebekah the daughter of Bethuel the Aramean and the sister of Laban the Aramean, from Paddan-aram.
local: Isak xɔ ƒe bla ene, hafi ɖe Rebeka si nye Betuel vinyɔnu. Rebeka nɔviŋutsue nye Laban. Betuel nye Aramtɔ tso Mesopotamia.

eng: She said, Heres my servant Bilhah. Sleep with her, and she will give birth for me. Because of her, I will also have children.
local: Tete Raxel gblɔ be: “Nye subɔvi Bilha nye esi. Dɔ kplii ne wòadzi vi ɖe akɔ̀ nam, ne nye hã mazu vi nɔ to edzi.”.

eng: So he went and got them and brought them to his mother, and his mother made the delicious food that his father loved.
local: Ale Yakob yi ɖalé gbɔ̃viawo vɛ na nɔa, eye wòtsɔ wo ɖa nu si vivia wo tɔ́ nu ŋutɔ la.

eng: He dreamed and saw a raised staircase, its foundation on earth and its top touching the sky, and Gods messengers were ascending and descending on it.
local: Eku drɔ̃e kpɔ atsrɔe aɖe. Egɔme tɔ anyigba, ke eƒe nu gbɔ ɖatɔ dziƒo. Kpɔ ɖa, mawudɔlawo le eliam le dzi yim le ɖiɖim.

eng: When Jacob saw Rachel the daughter of Laban his uncle, and the flock of Laban, Jacob came up, rolled the stone from the wells opening, and watered the flock of his uncle Laban.
local: Esi Yakob kpɔ Raxel, nyruia Laban vinyɔnu kple nyruia Laban ƒe alẽha la, eyi vudoa gbɔ, mli kpea ɖa, eye wòna tsi alẽawo.

eng: Isaac dug out again the wells that were dug during the lifetime of his father Abraham. The Philistines had closed them up after Abrahams death. Isaac gave them the same names his father had given them.
local: Egbugbɔ vudo siwo woɖe le tɔ́a Abraham ŋɔli la ɖe, eye wògatsɔ ŋkɔ siwo tɔ́a Abraham na wo tsã la na wo. Vudo siawoe Filistitɔwo xe tsã le Abraham ƒe ku megbe.

eng: And Jacob saw that Laban no longer liked him as much as he used to.
local: Azɔ la, Yakob kpɔ be Laban megakɔa ŋkume ɖe ye abe tsã ene o.

eng: Isaac prayed to the LORD for his wife, since she was unable to have children. The LORD was moved by his prayer, and his wife Rebekah became pregnant.
local: Rebeka tsi ko eya ta Isak do gbe ɖa na Aƒetɔ vevie ɖe eta. Aƒetɔ se eƒe kukuɖeɖe la, eye Rebeka fɔ fu.

eng: So Rachel gave her servant Bilhah to Jacob as his wife, and he slept with her.
local: Ale Raxel tsɔ eƒe kosi, Bilha, na Yakob wòɖe, eye wòdɔ kplii.

eng: Rebekah took her older son Esaus favorite clothes that were in the house with her, and she put them on her younger son Jacob.
local: Tete Rebeka tsɔ via tsitsitɔ, Esau, ƒe atsyɔ̃ɖowu siwo le egbɔ le aƒea me la do na via ɖevitɔ Yakob.

eng: Suddenly the LORD was standing on it and saying, I am the LORD, the God of your father Abraham and the God of Isaac. I will give you and your descendants the land on which you are lying.
local: Tete Aƒetɔ le tsitre ɖe eŋgɔ gblɔ nɛ be: “Nyee nye Aƒetɔ, tɔgbuiwò Abraham kple tɔ́wò Isak ƒe Mawu la! Matsɔ anyigba si dzi nèmlɔ la ana wò kple wò dzidzimeviwo.

eng: Jacob kissed Rachel and wept aloud.
local: Azɔ egbugbɔ nu na Raxel, eye wòwó avi sesĩe.

eng: Isaacs servants dug wells in the valley and found a well there with fresh water.
local: Isak ƒe dɔlawo ɖe vudo ɖe balia me, eye woke ɖe tsidzidzi ŋu.

eng: Then the LORD said to Jacob, Go back to the land of your ancestors and to your relatives, and I will be with you.
local: Eya ta Aƒetɔ gblɔ na Yakob be: “Trɔ yi tɔgbuiwòwo ƒe anyigba dzi kple ƒowòmeawo gbɔ. Manɔ kpli wò.”.

eng: But the boys pushed against each other inside of her, and she said, If this is what its like, why did it happen to me? So she went to ask the LORD.
local: Ɖeviawo nɔ ʋiʋlim le eƒe dɔme. Ale ebia eɖokui be: “Nu ka tae nu sia ƒomevi le wɔyem?” eye wòyi ɖabia Aƒetɔ se.

eng: Bilhah became pregnant and gave birth to a son for Jacob.
local: Bilha fɔ fu, eye wòdzi ŋutsuvi na Yakob.

eng: On his arms and smooth neck she put the hide of young goats.
local: Etsɔ gbɔ̃viawo ƒe agbalẽ bla ɖe eƒe abɔ kple kɔme siwo zrɔ̃ tsinitsini la.

eng: Your descendants will become like the dust of the earth; you will spread out to the west, east, north, and south. Every family of earth will be blessed because of you and your descendants.
local: Wò dzidzimeviwo asɔ gbɔ abe ʋuʋudedi ene, eye miakaka ɖe xexeme ƒe ɣedzeƒe, ɣetoɖoƒe, dzigbeme kple anyigbeme katã. Wò kple wò dzidzimeviwo dzi mato ayra anyigba dzi dukɔwo katã.

eng: Jacob told Rachel that he was related to her father and that he was Rebekahs son. She then ran to tell her father.
local: Egblɔ na Raxel be tɔ́a ƒe ƒometɔe yenye. Yee nye Rebeka viŋutsu. Tete Raxel ƒu du yi ɖagblɔe na tɔ́a.

eng: Isaacs shepherds argued with Gerars shepherds, each claiming, This is our water. So Isaac named the well Esek because they quarreled with him.
local: Ke Gerartɔwo ƒe lãnyilawo wɔ dzre kple Isak ƒe lãnyilawo gblɔ be: “Míaƒe tsie.” Eya ta Isak na ŋkɔ vudoa be Esek elabe wowɔ dzre kplii ɖe vudoa ta.

eng: So Jacob sent for Rachel and Leah and summoned them into the field where his flock was.
local: Ale Yakob dɔ woɖayɔ Raxel kple Lea vɛ nɛ le gbe dzi, afi si wòle eƒe lãhawo gbɔ le, eye wògblɔ na wo be:.

eng: And the LORD said to her, Two nations are in your womb; two different peoples will emerge from your body. One people will be stronger than the other; the older will serve the younger.
local: Aƒetɔ ɖo eŋu nɛ be: “Dukɔ evee le wò dɔ me. Àdzi dukɔ eve siwo aʋli nu kple wo nɔewo. Ɖeka asesẽ wu nɔvia; tsitsitɔ asubɔ ɖevitɔ.”.

eng: and the delicious food and the bread she had made she put into her sons hands.
local: Etsɔ nuɖuɖu vivi si wòwɔ kple abolo si wòƒo la de Yakob si.

eng: Rachel said, God has judged in my favor, heard my voice, and given me a son. So she named him Dan.
local: Tete Raxel gblɔ be: “Mawu tso afia nam; ese nye gbedodoɖa, eye wòna viŋutsum. Ale wòna ŋkɔe be Dan.

eng: I am with you now, I will protect you everywhere you go, and I will bring you back to this land. I will not leave you until I have done everything that I have promised you.
local: Kpɔ ɖa, manɔ kpli wò; makpɔ tawò le afi sia afi si nàyi, eye makplɔ wò agbugbɔ va anyigba sia dzi. Nyemagblẽ wò ɖi akpɔ o, ke boŋ mawɔ ŋugbe si medo na wò la dzi godoo.”.

eng: When Laban heard about Jacob his sisters son, he ran to meet him. Laban embraced him, kissed him, and invited him into his house, where Jacob recounted to Laban everything that had happened.
local: Esi Laban se nyruiayɔvi Yakob ŋkɔ la, eƒu du yi ɖakpee, kpla asi kɔ nɛ, gbugbɔ nu nɛ, eye wòkplɔe va aƒemee. Yakob gblɔ nya siwo katã va dzɔ la nɛ.

eng: They dug another well and argued about it too, so he named it Sitnah.
local: Wogaɖe vudo bubu ɖe ema yome, eye wogawɔ dzre ɖe eya hã ta, eya ta wòna ŋkɔe be Fuléle.

eng: He said to them, I am aware that your father no longer likes me as much as he used to. But my fathers God has been with me.
local: “Mekpɔ be mia tɔ́ megakɔa ŋkume ɖem fifia abe tsã ene o; gake tɔ́nye ƒe Mawu la li kplim.

eng: When she reached the end of her pregnancy, she discovered that she had twins.
local: Rebeka ƒe vidziɣi ɖo, kasia evenɔviwoe le dɔa me.

eng: Rachels servant Bilhah became pregnant again and gave birth to a second son for Jacob.
local: Eyome Bilha gafɔ fu, eye wòdzi ŋutsuvi evelia na Yakob.

eng: Jacob went to his father and said, My father. And he said, Im here. Who are you, my son.
local: Tete Yakob yi tɔ́a gbɔ yɔe be: “Tɔ́nye”. Tɔ́a tɔ hebia be: “Metɔ! Wò vinye kae?”.

eng: Laban said to him, Yes, you are my flesh and blood. After Jacob had stayed with Laban for a month.
local: Laban gblɔ nɛ be: “Nyateƒe, nɔvinye kpamkpam nènye.” Yakob nɔ egbɔ ɣleti ɖeka.

eng: When Jacob woke from his sleep, he thought to himself, The LORD is definitely in this place, but I didnt know it.
local: Esi Yakob nyɔ la, egblɔ be: “Aƒetɔ la le afi sia pɛ! Eli gake nyemenya o.”.

eng: He left there and dug another well, but they didnt argue about it, so he named it Rehoboth and said, Now the LORD has made an open space for us and has made us fertile in the land.
local: Eʋu le afi ma ɖo ta ŋgɔ, eye wògaɖe vudo bubu. Womewɔ dzre ɖe esia ya ta o, ale wòna ŋkɔe be “Vovoƒe.” Egblɔ be: “Aƒetɔ na vovoƒe mí azɔ be míanɔ anyigba sia dzi, adzi ɖe dzi, akeke ta.”.

eng: You know that Ive worked for your father as hard as I could.
local: Mi ame evea mienya nyuie be nye ŋusẽ katã metsɔ wɔ dɔ na mia tɔ́.

eng: The first came out red all over, clothed with hair, and she named him Esau.
local: Gbãtɔ do. Ebiã hẽe, eye fu le eŋu kedzekedze, eya ta wona ŋkɔe be Esau.

eng: Rachel said, Ive competed fiercely with my sister, and now Ive won. So she named him Naphtali.
local: Raxel gblɔ be: “Meƒo koko na Mawu, te kame kple nɔvinye, eye meɖu dzi.” Ale wòtsɔ ŋkɔ nɛ be Naftali.

eng: Jacob said to his father, Im Esau your oldest son. Ive made what you asked me to. Sit up and eat some of the game so you can bless me.
local: Yakob ɖo eŋu be: “Nye viwò ŋgɔgbea, Esau ye. Mewɔ nu si nèbe mawɔ la. Fɔ nàɖu adelã la, ne nàyram.”.

eng: Laban said to Jacob, You shouldnt have to work for free just because you are my relative. Tell me what you would like to be paid.
local: Azɔ Laban gblɔ na Yakob be: “Togbɔ be nɔviwo míenye hã la, nyemadi be nàwɔ dɔ nam dzodzro o. Nenie maxe na wò? Gblɔe nam.”.

eng: He was terrified and thought, This sacred place is awesome. Its none other than Gods house and the entrance to heaven.
local: Vɔvɔ̃ ɖoe wògblɔ be: “Afi sia dzi ŋɔ loo! Mawu ƒe aƒe kokoko wònye, eye teƒe sia la dziƒogboe.”.

eng: Then he went up from Gerar to Beer-sheba.
local: Emegbe Isak ʋu le afi ma yi Beer-Seba.

eng: But your father cheated me and changed my payment ten times. Yet God didnt let him harm me.
local: Gake ebam, eye wòtrɔ nye fetu zigbɔzi ewo; ke Mawu meɖe mɔ nɛ wòwɔ nu vevim ya o.

eng: Immediately afterward, his brother came out gripping Esaus heel, and she named him Jacob. Isaac was years old when they were born.
local: Evelia hã va do, eye wòlé Esau ƒe afɔkpodzi ɖe asi, eya ta wona ŋkɔe be Yakob. Isak xɔ ƒe bla ade esime wodzi ɖeviawo.

eng: When Leah realized that she had stopped bearing children, she took her servant Zilpah and gave her to Jacob as his wife.
local: Esi Lea kpɔ be yemegale vi dzim o la, etsɔ eƒe subɔvi Silpa na Yakob wòɖe.

eng: Isaac said to his son, How could you find this so quickly, my son? He said, The LORD your God led me right to it.
local: Ke Isak gblɔ na via be: “Vinye, ale ke wɔ nèke ɖe eŋu kaba ale?” Yakob ɖo eŋu be: “Aƒetɔ, wò Mawu lae na lã la va kpem.”.

eng: Now Laban had two daughters: the older was named Leah and the younger Rachel.
local: Vinyɔnuvi eve le Laban si. Tsitsitɔ ŋkɔe nye Lea, eye ɖevitɔ ŋkɔe nye Raxel.

eng: After Jacob got up early in the morning, he took the stone that he had put near his head, set it up as a sacred pillar, and poured oil on the top of it.
local: Esi Yakob fɔ ŋdi kanya la, etsɔ kpe si wòziɔ la ɖo anyi abe dzesikpe ene, eye wòkɔ ami ɖe edzi.

eng: If he said, The speckled ones will be your payment, the whole flock gave birth to speckled young. And if he said, The striped ones will be your payment, the whole flock gave birth to striped young.
local: Ne mia tɔ́ gblɔ be: ‘Lã ŋɔtaŋɔtawo nanye wò fetu’ la, ekema lãha blibo la katã dzia vi ŋɔtaŋɔtawo sɔŋ. Ne egagblɔ be: ‘Lã siwo ƒe agbalẽ me fli to nanye wò fetu la, ekema lãha blibo la katã dzia vi siwo ƒe agbalẽ me fli to la.

eng: The LORD appeared to him that night and said, I am the God of your father Abraham. Dont be afraid because I am with you. I will bless you, and I will give you many children for my servant Abrahams sake.
local: Gbe si gbe wòʋu yi ƒe zã me la, Aƒetɔ ɖe eɖokui fiae gblɔ nɛ be: “Nyee nye tɔ́wò Abraham ƒe Mawu. Mègavɔ̃ o; meli kpli wò. Le nye dɔla Abraham ta la, mayra wò, eye mana wò dzidzimeviwo nasɔ gbɔ ɖe dzi.”.

eng: When the young men grew up, Esau became an outdoorsman who knew how to hunt, and Jacob became a quiet man who stayed at home.
local: Esi ɖeviawo va tsi la, Esau zu adela, eye wòlɔ̃a adegbedede. Ke Yakob ya nye ame fafa, eye wòlɔ̃a aƒeme nɔnɔ.

eng: Leahs servant Zilpah gave birth to a son for Jacob.
local: Silpa dzi ŋutsuvi na Yakob.

eng: Isaac said to Jacob, Come here and let me touch you, my son. Are you my son Esau or not.
local: Tete Isak gblɔ na Yakob be: “Te ɖe ŋunye ne mali asi ŋuwò kpɔ be vinye Esau tututue nènye mahã.”.

eng: Leah had delicate eyes, but Rachel had a beautiful figure and was good-looking.
local: Lea ƒe ŋkuwo le dɔ̃ɔ, ke Raxel ya dze tugbe, eye wònyakpɔ ŋutɔ.

eng: He named that sacred place Bethel, though Luz was the citys original name.
local: Azɔ ena ŋkɔ teƒe la be Betel. Woyɔa teƒe ma tsã be Luz.

eng: So Isaac built an altar there and worshipped in the LORDs name. Isaac pitched his tent there, and his servants dug a well.
local: Ale wòɖi vɔsamlekpui ɖe afi ma, eye wòsubɔ Aƒetɔ. Etu agbadɔ ɖi, eye eƒe dɔlawo gaɖe vudo bubu.

eng: God took away your fathers livestock and gave them to me.
local: Ale Mawu xɔ lãha blibo la katã le mia tɔ́ si hetsɔ nam.

eng: Isaac loved Esau because he enjoyed eating game, but Rebekah loved Jacob.
local: Isak lɔ̃ Esau ƒe nya elabe adelã vivia enu; ke Rebeka ya lɔ̃ Yakob.

eng: and Leah said, What good luck! So she named him Gad.
local: Lea gblɔ be: “Nye Mawu ɖi nam!” Ale wòna ŋkɔe be Gad.

eng: Jacob loved Rachel and said, I will work for you for seven years for Rachel, your younger daughter.
local: Raxel ƒe nu nyo Yakob ŋu, eya ta egblɔ na Laban be: “Medi be mawɔ dɔ na wò ƒe adrẽ ne nàtsɔ Raxel, viwò nyɔnu ɖevitɔ nam maɖe.”.

eng: So Jacob approached his father Isaac, and Isaac touched him and said, The voice is Jacobs voice, but the arms are Esaus arms.
local: Yakob te ɖe tɔ́a ŋu, eye esi wòle asi lim eŋu la, egblɔ be: “Gbea nye Yakob ƒe gbe; ke asiawo ya Esau ƒe asiwoe.”.

eng: Jacob made a solemn promise: If God is with me and protects me on this trip Im taking, and gives me bread to eat and clothes to wear.
local: Tete Yakob ɖe adzɔgbe na Aƒetɔ be: “Ne Mawu anɔ kplim, akpɔ tanye le afi si yim mele, eye wòana nuɖuɖu kple nudodom.

eng: But Abimelech set out toward him from Gerar, with Ahuzzath his ally and Phicol the commander of his forces.
local: Gbe ɖeka la, Abimelex kple eƒe aɖaŋuɖola, Ahusat kpakple eƒe aʋakplɔla, Pixol, wova Isak gbɔ be yewoakpɔe.

eng: When the flocks were mating, I looked up and saw in a dream that the male goats that mounted the flock were striped, speckled, and spotted.
local: Le lãwo ƒe asiyɔɣi la, meku drɔ̃e, eye mekpɔ lãtsuwo wole asi yɔm. Lãtsu siwo le asi yɔm la nye esiwo ƒe agbalẽ me fli to, esiwo tɔ le ŋɔtaŋɔta kple esiwo tɔ me yibɔ kple ɣiwo le la.

eng: Once when Jacob was boiling stew, Esau came in from the field hungry.
local: Gbe ɖeka la, Yakob nɔ nu ɖam. Esau tso adegbe gbɔ, eye dɔ nɔ ewum kutɔkutɔe.

eng: Leahs servant Zilpah gave birth to a second son for Jacob.
local: Eyome Silpa gadzi ŋutsuvi evelia na Yakob.

eng: Laban said, Id rather give her to you than to another man. Stay with me.
local: Laban ɖo eŋu nɛ be: “Enyo nam be matsɔe na wò wu be matsɔe na ŋutsu bubu. Nɔ gbɔnye.”.

eng: Isaac didnt recognize him because his arms were hairy like Esaus arms, so he blessed him.
local: Ale medze sii be Yakob ye o, elabe fu le eƒe asi kedzekedze abe nɔvia Esau tɔ ene. Isak di be yeayrae.

eng: and I return safely to my fathers household, then the LORD will be my God.
local: ana magatrɔ gbɔ va tɔ́nye ƒeme dedie la, ekema Aƒetɔ anye nye Mawu.

eng: Isaac said to him, Why have you come after me? You resented me and sent me away from you.
local: Isak bia wo be: “Nu ka tae mieva gbɔnye? Menye miawoe lé fum, nyàm le mia dome oa?”.

eng: In the dream, Gods messenger said to me, Jacob! and I said, Im here.
local: Mawudɔla aɖe yɔm le drɔ̃eƒea be: ‘Yakob!’ Metɔ be: ‘Nyee nye esi!’.

eng: and said to Jacob, Im starving! Let me devour some of this red stuff. Thats why his name is Edom.
local: Esau gblɔ na Yakob be: “Dɔ le wuyem, mebe maku. Na nuɖuɖu dzĩ ma ƒe ɖem maɖu.” Eya ta wotsɔ ŋkɔ nɛ be Edom.

eng: and Leah said, Im happy now because women call me happy. So she named him Asher.
local: Tete Lea gblɔ be: “Dzi dzɔm! Azɔ mia tɔ nyɔnuwo akafum ayɔm be ‘Dzidzɔkpɔla.” Ale wòtsɔ ŋkɔ nɛ be Aser.

eng: Jacob worked for Rachel for seven years, but it seemed like a few days because he loved her.
local: Ale Yakob subɔ nyruia ƒè adrẽ, ɖe Raxel ta; ke ɣeyiɣi la nɔ nɛ abe ŋkeke ʋɛ aɖe ko ene, elabe Raxel ƒe nu lé dzi nɛ ale gbegbe.

eng: Isaac said, Are you really my son Esau? And he said, I am.
local: ke egabiae be: “Vinye Esau, wò tututu yea?” Eɖo eŋu be: “Ẽ, nyee!”.

eng: They said, We now see that the LORD was with you. We propose that there be a formal agreement between us and that we draw up a treaty with you.
local: Woɖo eŋu be: “Fifia míekpɔ dze sii kɔtɛe be Aƒetɔ li kpli wò eya ta míebu be ele be mía kpli wò míaka atam na mía nɔewo. Míabla nu kpli wò.

eng: He said, Look up and watch all the striped, speckled, and spotted male goats mounting the flock. Ive seen everything that Laban is doing to you.
local: Egblɔ nam be: ‘Kpɔ ɖa, lãtsu siwo katã le asi yɔm la nye esiwo ƒe agbalẽ me fli to, alo wole ŋɔtaŋɔta alo esiwo ƒe agbalẽ me ɣi kple yibɔ le la. Mekpɔ nu siwo katã wɔm Laban le ɖe ŋuwò.

eng: This stone that Ive set up as a sacred pillar will be Gods house, and of everything you give me I will give a tenth back to you.
local: Metu kpe sia ɖe afii abe dzesi ene. Anye Mawu ƒe aƒe, eye matsɔ nu sia nu si nànam la, ƒe akpa ewolia ana wò.”.

eng: Jacob said, Sell me your birthright today.
local: Yakob ɖo eŋu be: “Dzra wò ŋgɔgbevinyenye nam gbã gbɔ.”.

eng: Jacob said to Laban, The time has come. Give me my wife so that I may sleep with her.
local: Ƒeawo va yi eye Yakob gblɔ na Laban be: “Nye ɣeyiɣia de, eya ta tsɔ srɔ̃nyea nam maɖe.”.

eng: During the wheat harvest, Reuben found some erotic herbs in the field and brought them to his mother Leah. Rachel said to Leah, Give me your sons erotic herbs.
local: Gbe ɖeka la, Ruben yi agble le luŋeɣi, eye wòtsɔ lɔlɔ̃gbitsa vɛ na nɔa Lea. Raxel gblɔ na Lea be: “Meɖe kuku, na viwò ƒe lɔlɔ̃gbitsa la ƒe ɖem.”.

eng: Isaac said, Bring some food here and let me eat some of my sons game so I can bless you. Jacob put it before him and he ate, and he brought him wine and he drank.
local: Tete Isak gblɔ be: “Tsɔ vinye ƒe adelã la vɛ maɖu ne mayra wò.” Yakob tsɔe vɛ nɛ wòɖu, eye wòna wain hãe wòno.

eng: you must not treat us badly since we havent harmed you and since we have treated you well at all times. Then we will send you away peacefully, for you are now blessed by the LORD.
local: bene nàgawɔ vɔ̃ aɖeke mí o, abe ale si míawo hã míewɔ vɔ̃ aɖeke wò va yi o la ene. Nu nyui wònye míewɔ na wò, be míena nèdzo le ŋutifafa me ale be Aƒetɔ yra wò.”.

eng: I am the God of Bethel, where you anointed a sacred pillar and where you made a solemn promise to me. Now, get up and leave this country and go back to the land of your relatives.
local: Nyee nye Mawu si ɖe eɖokui fia wò le Betel, afi si nètu ŋkuɖodzikpe ɖo, kɔ ami ɖe edzi, eye nèɖe adzɔgbe nam le la. Azɔ tso, ʋu le anyigba sia dzi, eye nàgatrɔ yi mia de.’ ”.

eng: Esau said, Since Im going to die anyway, what good is my birthright to me.
local: Esau gblɔ be: “Dɔ le wuyem, mebe makua, nu ka koŋ ŋgɔgbevinyenye awɔ nam?”.

eng: Leah replied, Isnt it enough that youve taken my husband? Now you want to take my sons erotic herbs too? Rachel said, For your sons erotic herbs, Jacob may sleep with you tonight.
local: Lea ɖo eŋu nɛ be: “Esi nèxɔ srɔ̃nye le asinye ɖe, mesɔ gbɔ na wò oa? Vinye ƒe agbitsa hãe nèbe yeagaxɔ le asinyea?” Raxel gblɔ be: “Ekema tsɔ viwò ƒe agbitsa la nam ne srɔ̃nyea nadɔ gbɔwò ɖe enu egbe ƒe zã.”.

eng: So Laban invited all the people of that place and prepared a banquet.
local: Ale Laban ɖo srɔ̃ɖekplɔ̃ eye wòkpe dua me ŋutsuwo katã vɛ.

eng: His father Isaac said to him, Come here and kiss me, my son.
local: Azɔ tɔ́a gblɔ nɛ be: “Te ɖe ŋunye azɔ, vinye, ne nàgbugbɔ nu nam!”.

eng: Isaac prepared a banquet for them, and they ate and drank.
local: Ale wòɖo kplɔ̃ na wo, eye woɖu nu, no nu nyuie.

eng: Rachel and Leah answered him, Is there any share or inheritance left for us in our fathers household?.
local: Raxel kple Lea ɖo eŋu na Yakob be: “Ɖe gome alo domenyinyi aɖe gale mía si le mía tɔ́ ƒeme afi siaa?.

eng: Jacob said, Give me your word today. And he did. He sold his birthright to Jacob.
local: Yakob be: “Ka atam nam gbã gbɔ.” Tete Esau ka atam na Yakob, eye wòtsɔ eƒe ŋgɔgbevinyenye dzra nɛ.

eng: When Jacob came back from the field in the evening, Leah went out to meet him and said, You must sleep with me because Ive paid for you with my sons erotic herbs. So he slept with her that night.
local: Esi Yakob gbɔ tso agble le fiẽ me la, Lea yi ɖakpee gblɔ nɛ be: “Egbe ya gbɔnye dɔ ge nàla, elabe metsɔ vinye ƒe agbitsa xe fe ɖe tawò.” Ale wòdɔ kplii gbe ma gbe ƒe zã.

eng: However, in the evening, he took his daughter Leah and brought her to Jacob, and he slept with her.
local: Ke esi zã do la, ekplɔ via Lea de xɔ na Yakob, eye wòdɔ kplii.

eng: So he came close and kissed him. When Isaac smelled the scent of his clothes, he blessed him, See, the scent of my son is like the scent of the field that the LORD has blessed.
local: Esi wòte ɖe eŋu gbugbɔ nu nɛ la, eʋẽ eƒe awu me se, ale wòyrae. Egblɔ be: “Ehɛ̃, vinye le ʋeʋẽm lílilĩ abe anyigba si Aƒetɔ yra la ene.

eng: They got up early in the morning, and they gave each other their word. Isaac sent them off, and they left peacefully.
local: Le ŋufɔke la, wofɔ ŋdi kanya, eye wobla nu kple wo nɔewo heka atam ƒu edzi. Isak do mɔ wo, wotrɔ dzo le ŋutifafa me.

eng: Doesnt he think of us as foreigners since he sold us and has even used up the payment he received for us?.
local: Menye ɖeko wòbua mí amedzrowoe oa? Etsɔ mí dzra, eye wòle ga si wòxɔ ɖe mía ta la ɖum va se ɖe fifi.

eng: God responded to Leah. She became pregnant and gave birth to a fifth son for Jacob.
local: Mawu se Lea ƒe gbedodoɖa, eye wòfɔ fu dzi ŋutsuvi atɔ̃lia na Yakob.

eng: So Jacob gave Esau bread and lentil stew. He ate, drank, got up, and left, showing just how little he thought of his birthright.
local: Azɔ Yakob na abolo kple ayidetsi la Esau. Eɖu nua, no tsi ɖe edzi, eye wòtso dzo. Aleae Esau wɔ, do vlo eƒe ŋgɔgbevinyenye nye esi.

eng: May God give you showers from the sky, olive oil from the earth, plenty of grain and new wine.
local: Mawu nena zãmu nadza tso dzi, nena wò anyigba nenyo. Nena bli kple wain nawɔ na wò fũu.

eng: That day Isaacs servants informed him about the well that they had been digging and said to him, We found water.
local: Gbe ma gbe ke la, Isak ƒe dɔlawo va gblɔ vudo si woɖe la ŋu nya nɛ. Wogblɔ nɛ be: “Míeke ɖe tsi ŋu!”.

eng: All of the wealth God took from our father belongs to us and our children. Now, do everything God told you to do.
local: Kesinɔnu siwo katã Mawu xɔ le mía tɔ́ si la nye mía kple mía viwo tɔ, eya ta nu sia nu si Mawu gblɔ na wò be nàwɔ la, wɔe.”.

eng: Laban had given his servant Zilpah to his daughter Leah as her servant.
local: Laban tsɔ eƒe kosi Silpa na Lea be wòanye eƒe subɔvi.

eng: Leah said, God gave me what I paid for, what I deserved for giving my servant to my husband. So she named him Issachar.
local: Lea gblɔ be: “Mawu xe fe nam elabe metsɔ nye subɔvi na srɔ̃nye wòɖe.” Ale wòna ŋkɔe be Isaxar.

eng: May the nations serve you, may peoples bow down to you. Be the most powerful man among your brothers, and may your mothers sons bow down to you. Those who curse you will be cursed, and those who bless you will be blessed.
local: Dukɔwo nesubɔ wò, eye ƒomewo nede ta agu na wò! Ɖu aƒetɔ ɖe nɔviwò ŋutsuwo dzi, eye dawò viwo nebɔbɔ ɖe tewò. Ame si ƒo fi de wò la, woaƒo fi adee, eye ame si yra wò la, woayra eyama.”.

eng: So Jacob got up, put his sons and wives on the camels.
local: Ale Yakob tso, eye wòtsɔ viawo kple srɔ̃awo ɖo kposɔwo dzi.

eng: He called it Shibah; therefore, the citys name has been Beer-sheba until today.
local: Ena ŋkɔ vudoa be Siba. Eya tae woyɔa du si wotso ɖe afi ma la be “Beer-Seba” va se ɖe egbe.

eng: Leah became pregnant again and gave birth to a sixth son for Jacob.
local: Eyome Lea gafɔ fu, eye wòdzi viŋutsu adelia na Yakob.

eng: In the morning, there she was—Leah! Jacob said to Laban, What have you done to me? Didnt I work for you to have Rachel? Why did you betray me.
local: Ŋu ke, kasia wòbe yeakpɔ la, Lea boŋ ye. Yakob yi ɖabia Laban be: “Nu kae nye esi nèwɔ ɖe ŋunye? Menye Raxel tae mesubɔ wò ɖo oa? Nu ka ta nèblem ale?”.

eng: After Isaac had finished blessing Jacob, and just as Jacob left his father Isaac, his brother Esau came back from his hunt.
local: Esi Isak yra Yakob vɔ, eye Yakob do dzo le egbɔ teti ko la, nɔvia Esau nye esi tso adegbe gbɔ va.

eng: When Esau was years old, he married Judith daughter of Beeri the Hittite, and Basemath daughter of Elon the Hittite.
local: Esi Esau xɔ ƒe bla ene la, eɖe Yudif, Xititɔ Beeri vinyɔnu. Eɖe Basmat si hã nye Xititɔ, Elon vinyɔnu hã.

eng: and set out with all of his livestock and all of his possessions that he had acquired in Paddan-aram in order to return to his father Isaac in the land of Canaan.
local: Azɔ ekplɔ eƒe lãhawo katã, eƒe lãha siwo katã su esi kple nyiha siwo katã wòwɔ dɔ kpɔ le Padan-Aram la, eye wòdze mɔ be yeayi ye tɔ́ Isak gbɔ le Kanaan-nyigba dzi.

eng: and she said, God has given me a wonderful gift. Now my husband will honor me since Ive borne him six sons. So she named him Zebulun.
local: Fifia Lea gblɔ be: “Mawu tsɔ nɔƒe xɔasi nam. Srɔ̃nye ade bubu ŋunye azɔ elabe medzi viŋutsu ade sɔŋ nɛ.” Ale wòna ŋkɔe be Zebulon.

eng: Laban said, Where we live, we dont give the younger woman before the oldest.
local: Laban ɖo eŋu nɛ be: “Le mía de kɔnu nu la, vinyɔnu ɖevitɔ meɖea srɔ̃ hafi tsitsitɔ ɖenɛ o.

eng: He too made some delicious food, brought it to his father, and said, Let my father sit up and eat from his sons game so that you may bless me.
local: Eya hã ɖa nu nyui aɖe vɛ na tɔ́a. Egblɔ nɛ be: “Tɔ́nye, meɖe kuku, fɔ nàɖu viwò ŋutsu ƒe adelã la ne nàyram.”.

eng: They made life very difficult for Isaac and Rebekah.
local: Wona agbenɔnɔ zu veve na Isak kple Rebeka.

eng: Now, while Laban was out shearing his sheep, Rachel stole the households divine images that belonged to her father.
local: Esi Laban yi fu ko ge na eƒe alẽwo la, Raxel fi tɔ́a ƒe aklama kpakpɛwo.

eng: After this, she gave birth to a daughter and named her Dinah.
local: Emegbe la, edzi nyɔnuvi, eye wòna ŋkɔe be Dina.

eng: Complete the celebratory week with this woman. Then I will give you this other woman too for your work, if you work for me seven more years.
local: Fifia na srɔ̃ɖekwasiɖa ɖeka sia nava yi, eye míatsɔ ɖevitɔ hã na wò nàɖe, nenye be nègawɔ dɔ nam ƒe adrẽ bubu ko.”.

eng: His father Isaac said to him, Who are you? And he said, Im your son, your oldest son, Esau.
local: Isak bia be: “Wò ame kae?” Eɖo eŋu be: “Nye viwò ŋgɔgbea, Esau ye.”.

eng: Moreover, Jacob deceived Laban the Aramean by not sending word to him that he was leaving.
local: Yakob flu Laban Aramtɔ la elabe meklãe be yele dzodzo ge o.

eng: Then God remembered Rachel, responded to her, and let her conceive.
local: Ke Mawu meŋlɔ Raxel be o. Eɖo eƒe gbedodoɖa ŋu nɛ, eye wòʋu eƒe vidzidɔ.

eng: Isaac was so shocked that he trembled violently. He said, Who was the hunter just here with game? He brought me food, and I ate all of it before you came. I blessed him, and he will stay blessed.
local: Vɔvɔ̃ lé Isak eye wònɔ dzodzom nyànyanya hebia be: “Ke ame kae de adegbe gbɔ tsɔ adelã vɛ nam? Ɖeko meɖu nua katã vɔ heyra amea ko nye esi nèva do. Eya tɔ ko yayra la nye. Ẽ, evɔ.”.

eng: So that is what Jacob did. He completed the celebratory week with this woman, and then Laban gave him his daughter Rachel as his wife.
local: Yakob lɔ̃ ɖe edzi. Laban lala srɔ̃ɖekwasiɖa la va yi, eye wòtsɔ Raxel hã nɛ wòɖe.

eng: So Jacob and his entire household left. He got up, crossed the river, and set out directly for the mountains of Gilead.
local: Ɖeko wòlɔ eƒe nuwo katã, sí dzo. Etso Frat-tɔsisia, eye wòɖo ta Gilead-tonyigba dzi.

eng: She became pregnant and gave birth to a son and said, God has taken away my shame.
local: Efɔ fu, eye wòdzi ŋutsuvi. Egblɔ be: “Mawu ɖe nye ŋukpe ɖa azɔ.

eng: When Esau heard what his father said, he let out a loud agonizing cry and wept bitterly. He said to his father, Bless me! Me too, my father.
local: Esi Esau se nya sia la, edo ɣli sesĩe kple vevesese be: “Oo! Tɔ́nye, yra nye hã!”.

eng: Three days later, Laban found out that Jacob had gone.
local: Ŋkeke etɔ̃ megbe la, wova gblɔ na Laban be Yakob sí dzo.

eng: Laban had given his servant Bilhah to his daughter Rachel as her servant.
local: Laban tsɔ eƒe kosi, Bilha na Raxel be wòanye eƒe subɔvi.

eng: She named him Joseph, saying to herself, May the LORD give me another son.
local: Aƒetɔ nena magadzi ŋutsuvi ɖeka akpee.” Eya ta ena ŋkɔe be Yosef.

eng: Isaac said, Your brother has already come deceitfully and has taken your blessing.
local: Isak ɖo eŋu be: “Nɔviwò wɔ aye va xɔ wò yayra la dzoe xoxo.”.

eng: so Laban took his brothers with him, chased Jacob for seven days, and caught up with him in the mountains of Gilead.
local: Laban kplɔ nɔviawo kpe ɖe eɖokui ŋu, eye woti Yakob kple etɔwo yome ŋkeke adrẽ sɔŋ hafi va tu wo le Gilead-tonyigba la dzi.

eng: Jacob slept with Rachel, and he loved Rachel more than Leah. He worked for Laban seven more years.
local: Azɔ Yakob dɔ kple Raxel hã, eye wòlɔ̃ eya sãa wu Lea. Tete wògawɔ dɔ na Laban ƒe adrẽ bubu.

eng: After Rachel gave birth to Joseph, Jacob said to Laban, Send me off so that I can go to my own place and my own country.
local: Le Yosef dzidzi megbe la, Yakob gblɔ na Laban be: “Medi be nàdo mɔm mayi aƒe.

eng: Esau said, Isnt this why hes called Jacob? Hes taken me twice now: he took my birthright, and now hes taken my blessing. He continued, Havent you saved a blessing for me.
local: Esau gblɔ be: “Yakob ko woayɔ nɛ vavã! Zi eveliae nye esi wòlé afɔkpodzi nam hebam. Exɔ nye ŋgɔgbevinyenye va yi, azɔ egaxɔ nye yayra hã.” Eyi edzi bia tɔ́a be: “Mèsusɔ yayra aɖeke ɖi nam oa?”.

eng: That night, God appeared to Laban the Aramean in a dream and said, Be careful and dont say anything hastily to Jacob one way or the other.
local: Gbe ma gbe ƒe zã me la, Mawu va Laban gbɔ le drɔ̃eƒe, eye wògblɔ nɛ be: “Kpɔ nyuie ne nàgagblɔ nya aɖeke ado ŋɔdzi na Yakob ne nàgblẽ nu ɖe edzi wu o.”.

eng: When the LORD saw that Leah was unloved, he opened her womb; but Rachel was unable to have children.
local: Esi Aƒetɔ kpɔ be Yakob lɔ̃ Raxel sãa wu Lea la, eʋu Lea ƒe vidzidɔ, ke exe Raxel ya tɔ.

eng: Give me my wives and children whom Ive worked for, and I will go. You know the work Ive done for you.
local: Ɖe asi le srɔ̃nye kple vinye siwo ta mewɔ dɔ na wò ɖo la ŋu nam, ne madzo. Wò ŋutɔ ènya ale si mesubɔ wòe.”.

eng: Isaac replied to Esau, Ive already made him more powerful than you, and Ive made all of his brothers his servants. Ive made him strong with grain and wine. What can I do for you, my son.
local: Isak ɖo eŋu be: “Kpɔ ɖa, meɖoe amegã ɖe dziwò, eye metsɔ nɔvia ŋutsuwo wɔ eƒe dɔlawoe. Metsɔ bli kple wain hã nɛ. Evɔ. Vinye, nu ka gɔ̃e li magawɔ na wò?”.

eng: Laban reached Jacob after Jacob had pitched his tent in the mountains. So Laban and his brothers also pitched theirs in the mountains of Gilead.
local: Laban kple etɔwo va tu Yakob wòtu eƒe agbadɔ ɖe to dzi. Laban kple nɔviawo hã tu wo tɔ ɖe tonyigba la dzi le Gilead.

eng: Leah became pregnant and gave birth to a son. She named him Reuben because she said, The LORD saw my harsh treatment, and now my husband will love me.
local: Lea dzi ŋutsuvi. Egblɔ be Aƒetɔ kpɔ nye nublanui eya ta srɔ̃nye alɔ̃m azɔ. Ale wòna ŋkɔ ɖevia be Ruben.

eng: Laban said to him, Do me this favor. Ive discovered by a divine sign that the LORD has blessed me because of you.
local: Laban gblɔ nɛ be: “Ne àɖe mɔ nam la, magblɔ be to afakaka me la, meke ɖe eŋu be tawòe Aƒetɔ yram ɖo.

eng: Esau said to his father, Do you really have only one blessing, Father? Bless me too, my father! And Esau wept loudly.
local: Esau ɖe kuku na tɔ́a vevie be: “Tɔ́nye, meɖe kuku, yayra ɖeka ma koe le asiwòa? Tɔ́nye, yra nye hã dzro!” Esau wó avi doo.

eng: Laban said to Jacob, What have you done? You have deceived me and taken off with my daughters as if they were prisoners of war.
local: Laban bia Yakob be: “Nu ka nye esi nèwɔ, flum, hekplɔ vinye nyɔnuwo dzoe abe aboyomewoe wonye ene?.

eng: She became pregnant again and gave birth to a son. She said, The LORD heard that I was unloved, so he gave me this son too, and she named him Simeon.
local: Eyome egafɔ fu, eye wòdzi ŋutsuvi. Egblɔ be: “Aƒetɔ se be wolé fum eya ta wòna megadzi ŋutsuvi.” Le esia ta ena ŋkɔe be Simeon.

eng: His father Isaac responded and said to him, Now, you will make a home far away from the olive groves of the earth, far away from the showers of the sky above.
local: Tete tɔ́a Isak gblɔ nɛ be: “Kpɔ ɖa, wò nɔƒe adidi tso anyigba wɔnu gbɔ, eye ànɔ adzɔge tso zãmu gbɔ.

eng: so name your price and I will pay it.
local: Nu ka nèdi be matsɔ xe fee na ye? Gblɔe nam mana wò.”.

eng: Why did you leave secretly, deceiving me, and not letting me know? I wouldve sent you off with a celebration, with songs and tambourines and harps.
local: Nu ka ta nèsí dzo xɛɛ le megbenye? Ne ɖe nèklãm la, anye ne aseyetsotso, hadzidzi, kasaŋku kple ʋuƒoƒo matsɔ ɖo wò ɖae.

eng: She became pregnant again and gave birth to a son. She said, Now, this time my husband will embrace me, since I have given birth to three sons for him. So she named him Levi.
local: Emegbe egafɔ fu dzi ŋutsuvi. Egblɔ be: “Azɔ ko srɔ̃nye alé ɖe ŋunye goŋgoŋ, elabe medzi ŋutsuvi etɔ̃ sɔŋ nɛ.” Ale wòna ŋkɔe be Levi.

eng: You will live by your sword; you will serve your brother. But when you grow restless, you will tear away his harness from your neck.
local: Wò aʋawɔyi dzi nànɔ agbe ɖo. Àsubɔ nɔviwò; evɔ ne èdze aglã ɖe eŋu la, àdo le eƒe ŋusẽ te.”.

eng: Jacob said to him, You know how Ive worked for you, and how well your livestock have done with me.
local: Yakob ɖo eŋu nɛ be: “Wò ŋutɔ ènya vi si meɖe na wò kple ale si wò lãha la va dzi ɖe edzi, esime mele edzi kpɔm la.

eng: You didnt even let me kiss my sons and my daughters good-bye. Now youve acted like a fool.
local: Mèna megbugbɔ nu na tɔgbuiyɔvinyewo kple vinye nyɔnuawo tsɔ do hedenyuie na wo teti hã o. Ewɔ bometsinu!.

eng: She became pregnant again and gave birth to a son. She said, This time I will praise the LORD. So she named him Judah. Then she stopped bearing children.
local: Lea gafɔ fu gadzi ŋutsuvi, eye wògblɔ be: “Azɔ la, makafu Aƒetɔ!” Ale wòna ŋkɔe be Yuda. Tete Lea tsò vi.

eng: While in my care, what little you had has multiplied a great deal. The LORD blessed you wherever I took your livestock. Now, when will I be able to work for my own household too.
local: Lãha sue aɖe koe nɔ asiwò hafi meva; ke azɔ la, edzi ɖe edzi fũu, eye Aƒetɔ yra wò to nye vava eme. Gbe ka gbe nye hã mava wɔ dɔ akpɔ nye aƒe dzi?”.

eng: Esau was furious at Jacob because his father had blessed him, and Esau said to himself, When the period of mourning for the death of my father is over, I will kill my brother.
local: Azɔ Esau lé fu Yakob elabe wo tɔ́ yra eya. Eɖo nugbe ɖe eŋu gblɔ le eɖokui me be: “Mía tɔ́ ƒe kuɣi le kpuie. Ne wole fu nyim nɛ ko la, mawu nɔvinye Yakob godoo.”.

eng: and I have the power to punish you. However, your fathers God told me yesterday, Be careful and dont say anything hastily to Jacob one way or the other.
local: Ŋusẽ le asinye be mawɔ nu vevi mi, gake le étò ƒe zã me la, tɔ́wò ƒe Mawu la ƒo nu nam be: ‘Kpɔ nyuie be magblɔ nya vɔ̃ alo nya nyui aɖeke na Yakob o.’.

eng: Laban said, What will I pay you? Jacob said, Dont pay me anything. If you will do this for me, I will take care of your flock again, and keep a portion.
local: Laban biae be: “Nu ka nèdi be matsɔ xe fe na ye?” Yakob ɖo eŋu be: “Nyemaxɔ naneke le asiwò o. Ke nya aɖe le asinye medi be magblɔ na wò. Ne àlɔ̃ awɔ esia nam ko la, manɔ anyi aganyi wò alẽawo, akpɔ wo dzi.

eng: Rebekah was told what her older son Esau was planning, so she summoned her younger son Jacob and said to him, Esau your brother is planning revenge. He plans to kill you.
local: Wogblɔ Esau ƒe nugbeɖoɖoa na Rebeka. Ale wòna woyɔ Yakob, via ɖevitɔ vɛ nɛ. Egblɔ nɛ be: “Kpɔ ɖa, nɔviwò Esau ɖo nugbe ɖe ŋuwò be yeabia hlɔ̃ awu wò.

eng: Youve rushed off now because you missed your fathers household so much, but why did you steal my gods.
local: Menya nyuie be aƒe le dzrowòm eya tae nèdzo ɖo. Ke nu ka ta nèfi nye mawuwo?”.

eng: I will go through the entire flock today, taking out all of the speckled and spotted sheep, all of the black male lambs, and all of the spotted and speckled female goats. That will be my price.
local: Eyae nye be egbe la, na matsa le wò lãha la katã me. Maɖe alẽ ɖe sia ɖe si ƒe agbalẽ me fli to kple esi le ŋɔtaŋɔta kple alẽvi yibɔwo kpakple gbɔ̃ siwo le ŋɔtaŋɔta kple esiwo ƒe agbalẽ me fli to la ɖe vovo. Woawoe maxɔ abe nye fetu ene.

eng: So now, my son, listen to me: Get up and escape to my brother Laban in Haran.
local: Eya ta, vinye, ɖo to nàwɔ nu si gblɔ ge mala na wò la. Netsɔ na wò nàsí yi ɖe nɔvinye Laban gbɔ le Xaran.

eng: Jacob responded to Laban, I was afraid and convinced myself that you would take your daughters away from me.
local: Yakob ɖo eŋu be: “Ɖe menɔ vɔvɔ̃m elabe mebu be ɖewohĩ àxɔ viwò nyɔnuawo le asinye.

eng: I will be completely honest with you: when you come to check on our agreement, every female goat with me that isnt speckled or spotted and every male lamb with me that isnt black will be considered stolen.
local: Azɔ tso fifia heyina ne èva be yeakpɔ nye nuteƒewɔwɔ la, ekema eme akɔ na wò ƒãa. Ne àkpɔ gbɔ̃ aɖe si mele ŋɔtaŋɔta o, alo esi ƒe agbalẽ me fli meto o, alo alẽvi aɖe si mele yibɔ o le tɔnyeawo dome ko la, ke ɖe mefii.”.

eng: Live with him for a short while until your brothers rage subsides.
local: Nɔ egbɔ va se ɖe esime nɔviwò ƒe dzi nafa.

eng: Whomever you find with your divine images wont live. Identify whatever I have that is yours, in front of your brothers, and take it. Jacob didnt know that Rachel had stolen them.
local: Ame si ƒe nuwo me nàkpɔ wò mawuawo le la, matsi agbe o. Ka nye nuwo me faa, nàtɔ asi nu si nye tɔwò la dzi le mía nɔviwo ŋkume afii, ne nàɖee dzoe.” Yakob menya be Raxel ye fi mawuawo o.

eng: Laban said, All right; lets do it.
local: Laban ɖo eŋu be: “Melɔ̃ ɖe edzi. Míawɔe nenema.”.

eng: until your brothers anger at you goes away and he forgets what you did to him. Then I will send for you and bring you back from there. Why should I suffer the loss of both of you on one day.
local: Ne edzudzɔ dzikudodo ɖe ŋuwò, eye wòŋlɔ nu si nèwɔ ɖe eŋu la be la, madɔ ame ɖa woava kplɔ wò tso afi ma vɛ. Nu ka ta mi ame evea miaku nam le ŋkeke ɖeka dzi?”.

eng: Laban went into Jacobs tent, Leahs tent, and her two servants tent and didnt find them. So he left Leahs tent and went into Rachels.
local: Ale Laban yi ɖaka nuwo me le Yakob, Lea kple kosi eveawo ƒe agbadɔ me, gake mekpɔ eƒe mawuawo o. Edo le Lea ƒe agbadɔ me ge ɖe Raxel tɔ me.

eng: However, on that very day Laban took out the striped and spotted male goats and all of the speckled and spotted female goats—any with some white in it—and all of the black male lambs, and gave them to his sons.
local: Gbe ma gbe ke la, Laban ɖe gbɔ̃tsu siwo ƒe agbalẽ me fli to, alo nu yibɔyibɔwo le la ɖe vovo. Nenema ke eɖe gbɔ̃nɔ siwo katã le ŋɔtaŋɔta alo esiwo ƒe agbalẽ me fli ɣi to la ɖe vovo. Azɔ egaɖe alẽvi yibɔwo hã ɖe vovo eye wòtsɔ lã siawo katã de asi na viawo.

eng: Rebekah then said to Isaac, I really loathe these Hittite women. If Jacob marries one of the Hittite women, like the women of this land, why should I go on living.
local: Rebeka gblɔ na Isak be: “Xiti-nyɔnu siwo Esau ɖe la na agbenɔnɔ ti kɔ nam. Ne Yakob hã aɖe Xiti-nyɔnuvi siawo ƒe ɖe la, ɖeko maku.”.

eng: Now Rachel had taken the divine images and put them into the camels saddlebag and sat on them. Laban felt around in the whole tent but couldnt find them.
local: Azɔ Raxel fi mawuawo tsɔ ɣla ɖe kposɔ ƒe akpa te hebɔbɔ nɔ edzi. Laban ka nu sia nu me le agbadɔa me, gake mekpɔ mawuawo o.

eng: He put a three-day trip between himself and Jacob, while Jacob was watching the rest of Labans flock.
local: Azɔ ena wokplɔ alẽha yeye sia dzoe, ŋkeke etɔ̃ mɔzɔzɔ ƒe didime, tso Yakob gbɔ. Ke Yakob nɔ Laban ƒe lãha mamlɛa dzi kpɔm.

eng: Rachel said to her father, Sir, dont be angry with me because I cant get up for you; Im having my period. He searched but couldnt find the divine images.
local: Raxel gblɔ na tɔ́a be: “Tɔ́nye, mègakpɔ dziku be nyemetso ɖe tsitrenu na wò o, elabe meɖo asi anyi.” Laban di mawuawo, gadi wo ʋuu, gake mekpɔ wo o.

eng: Then Jacob took new branches from poplar, almond, and plane trees; and he peeled white stripes on them, exposing the branches white color.
local: Yakob tsɔ logo, xe kple nyati mumuwo, eye wòfo tsro le akpa aɖewo ŋu, teƒe mawo fu ɣie, wòsusɔ tsro ɖe teƒe bubuawo.

eng: Jacob was angry and complained to Laban, What have I done wrong and whats my crime that youve tracked me down like this?.
local: Tete Yakob do dziku vevie ɖe Laban ŋuti. Ebia be: “Vo kae meda? Nu vɔ̃ ka gɔ̃e mewɔ si na be nèɖem ɖe nu alea gbegbe?.

eng: He set the branches that he had peeled near the watering troughs so that they were in front of the flock when they drank, because they often mated when they came to drink.
local: Azɔ etsɔ ati siawo ɖo lãawo ƒe tsinonu ŋgɔ. Ewɔ esia bene lãawo nanɔ ati siawo kpɔm ne wova le tsi nom, eye wole asi yɔm la.

eng: Youve now felt through all of my baggage, and what have you found from your households belongings? Put it in front of our relatives, and let them decide between us.
local: Togbɔ be nèka nye nuwo katã me hã, wò aƒemenu kae nèkpɔ? Tsɔe va da ɖe mí kple nɔviwòawo katã ŋkume, ne woabu fɔ, atso afia.

eng: Jacob went on his way, and Gods messengers approached him.
local: Yakob dzo yina, eye Mawu ƒe dɔlawo va do goe.

eng: For these twenty years Ive been with you, your female sheep and goats havent miscarried, and I havent eaten your flocks rams.
local: Menɔ gbɔwò ƒe bla eve sɔŋ; fu mege le wò alẽ alo wò gbɔ̃ aɖeke ƒo kpɔ o. Nyemelé agbo le wò alẽha la me ɖa ɖu kpɔ gɔ̃ hã o.

eng: When the flock mated in front of the branches, they gave birth to striped, speckled, and spotted young.
local: Ale lãawo nɔa atiawo kpɔm le asiyɔɣi, eya ta ne wodzi vi la, fli toa wo viawo ƒe agbalẽ me alo wonɔa ŋɔtaŋɔta alo woƒe agbalẽ me toa vovo.

eng: When Jacob saw them, he said, This is Gods camp, and he named that sacred place Mahanaim.
local: Esi wòkpɔ wo la, egblɔ be: “Mawu ƒe asaɖae nye esia!” Ale wòna ŋkɔ teƒea be Maxanaim.

eng: When animals were killed, I didnt bring them to you but took the loss myself. You demanded compensation from me for any animals poached during the day or night.
local: Ne lã lénu lé ɖe la, nye agbae; ɖe medinɛ ɖoa eteƒe. Ne wofi ɖe, ŋdɔ o, zã o, ɖe nènana media bubu ɖoa eteƒe na wò.

eng: Jacob sorted out the lambs, turning the flock to face the striped and black ones in Labans flock but keeping his flock separate, setting them apart from Labans flock.
local: Yakob ma alẽviawo ɖa tso lãha la gbɔ, eye wòna lãha la dzea ŋgɔ Laban ƒe lã siwo le ŋɔtaŋɔta kple esiwo le yibɔ la. Mena eƒe lãwo tsaka kple Laban tɔwo o.

eng: Jacob sent messengers ahead of him to his brother Esau, toward the land of Seir, the open country of Edom.
local: Yakob dɔ dɔlawo ɖo ɖe nɔvia Esau gbɔ le Seir-nyigba dzi le Edom-gbegbe.

eng: Whenever the strongest of the flock mated, Jacob put the branches in front of them near the watering troughs so that they mated near the branches.
local: Azɔ nenye be lã sesẽtɔwo le asi yɔm la, Yakob tsɔa ati siwo ŋu wòfo tsro le la ɖoa wo ŋgɔ le tsinonua gbɔ bene woafɔ fu adzi vi siwo ƒe agbalẽ me anɔ abe atiawo ene.

eng: He gave them these orders: Say this to my master Esau. This is the message of your servant Jacob: Ive lived as an immigrant with Laban, where Ive stayed till now.
local: Egblɔ na wo be: “Migblɔ na nye aƒetɔ Esau be: ‘Nye, wò dɔla Yakob mebe medzo ɖanɔ Laban gbɔ abe amedzro ene ʋuu va se ɖe fifi.

eng: The dry heat consumed me during the day, and the frost at night; I couldnt sleep.
local: Ale ŋdɔ ɖuam le ŋkeke me, vuvɔ wɔam le zã me, eye nyemekpɔa alɔ̃ dɔna gɔ̃ hã o.

eng: Ive now spent twenty years in your household. I worked for fourteen years for your two daughters and for six years for your flock, and you changed my pay ten times.
local: Ƒe bla eve siwo menɔ gbɔwò la, alea dzi ko menɔ. Mewɔ dɔ na wò ƒe wui ene ɖe viwò nyɔnu eveawo ta, eye megawɔ dɔ ƒe ade ɖe alẽawo ta. Gawu la, èɖɔli nye fetu zigbɔzi ewo sɔŋ.

eng: But he didnt put branches up for the weakest of the flock. So the weakest became Labans and the strongest Jacobs.
local: Ke meɖoa atiawo lã beliwo ya ŋgɔ o, eya ta lã beliawo ƒe viwo zua Laban tɔ, eye sesẽawo ƒe viwo zua Yakob tɔ.

eng: I own cattle, donkeys, flocks, men servants, and women servants. Im sending this message to my master now to ask that he be kind.
local: Nyiwo, tedziwo, alẽwo, kluviwo kple kosiwo va ɖo asinye fũu, eya ta mele ame dɔm ɖe wò, nye aƒetɔ, be nàve nunye.’ ”.

eng: The messengers returned to Jacob and said, We went out to your brother Esau, and hes coming to meet you with four hundred men.
local: Amedɔdɔawo trɔ va Yakob gbɔ gblɔ nɛ be: “Míede ɖakpɔ nɔviwò Esau, eye eya hã gbɔna be yeava kpe wò. Ekplɔ ame alafa ene ɖe ŋuti gbɔnae.”.

eng: The man Jacob became very, very rich: he owned large flocks, female and male servants, camels, and donkeys.
local: Ale Yakob va zu kesinɔtɔ gã aɖe. Alẽhawo, gbɔ̃wo, kluviwo, kosiwo, kposɔwo kple tedziwo ɖo esi fũu.

eng: If the God of my father—the God of Abraham and the awesome one of Isaac—hadnt been with me, youd have no doubt sent me away without anything. God saw my harsh treatment and my hard work and reprimanded you yesterday.
local: Ne menye Mawu si tɔgbuinye Abraham subɔna, Mawu si tɔ́nye Isak hã vɔ̃na la ɖe wòli kplim o la, anye ne asi ƒuƒlu ko nèdo mɔm. Ke Mawu kpɔ ale si mekpe fu, wɔ dɔ kple kutrikuku, eye wòtso afia nam étò ƒe zã me.”.

eng: Jacob was terrified and felt trapped, so he divided the people with him, and the flocks, cattle, and camels, into two camps.
local: Tete dzika tso Yakob ƒo, eye wòvɔ̃ ŋutɔŋutɔ. Ema eƒe amewo ɖe hatsotso eve me, eye wòma alẽawo, nyiawo kple kposɔawo hã ɖe akpa eve me.

eng: Laban responded and told Jacob, The daughters are my daughters, the children are my children, and the flocks are my flocks. Everything you see is mine. But what can I do now about my daughters and about their sons?.
local: Tete Laban ɖo eŋu na Yakob be: “Nyɔnu siawo la, vinyewoe, wo viwo hã nye tɔnye, eye lãhawo hã tɔnyewoe. Le nyateƒe me la, nu sia nu si le afii la, tɔnyee. Nu ka mate ŋu awɔ ɖe nye ŋutɔ vinye nyɔnuwo kple vi siwo wodzi la ŋu?.

eng: Come, lets make a treaty, you and me, and let something be our witness.
local: Na nye kple wò míabla nu, ne wòanye ɖaseɖiɖi le nye kple wò dome.”.

eng: He thought, If Esau meets the first camp and attacks it, at least one camp will be left to escape.
local: Egblɔ na eɖokui be: “Ne eva eme be Esau va dze hatsotso ɖeka dzi, ɖu edzi la, ekema hatsotso evelia ya así dzo.”.

eng: So Jacob took a stone, set it up as a sacred pillar.
local: Ale Yakob tsɔ kpe tu anyi be wòanye ŋkuɖodzinu.

eng: Jacob said, LORD, God of my father Abraham, God of my father Isaac, who said to me, Go back to your country and your relatives, and Ill make sure things go well for you.
local: Yakob gblɔ be: “Tɔgbuinye Abraham ƒe Mawu kple tɔ́nye Isak ƒe Mawu, Aƒetɔ, wòe gblɔ nam be: ‘Trɔ yi mia denyigba dzi kple ƒowòmeawo gbɔ, eye mawɔ nyui na wò.’.

eng: and said to his relatives, Gather stones. So they took stones, made a mound, and ate there near the mound.
local: Egblɔ na eƒe amewo be woafɔ kpe vɛ, eye wofɔ wo va li kɔ ɖi. Emegbe la, woɖu nu le kpekɔ la dzi.

eng: I dont deserve how loyal and truthful youve been to your servant. I went away across the Jordan with just my staff, but now Ive become two camps.
local: Èlɔ̃m vevie, eye nèwɔa nuteƒe na nye, wò dɔla ɣe sia ɣi, evɔ nyemedze hafi o. Atizɔti koe nɔ asinye metsɔ tsò Yordan-tɔsisi siae; ke azɔ la, megbɔ va kple asaɖa eve.

eng: Laban called it Jegar-sahadutha, but Jacob called it Galeed.
local: Laban tsɔ ŋkɔ nɛ be Yegar Saxaduta, ke Yakob ya tsɔ ŋkɔ nɛ be Galed.

eng: Save me from my brother Esau! Im afraid he will come and kill me, the mothers, and their children.
local: Meɖe kuku na wò, ɖem tso nɔvinye Esau si me. Mele evɔ̃m ŋutɔ. Ne menye nenema o la, ava sim, atsrɔ̃ vi nɔwo kple wo viwo gɔ̃ hã.

eng: Laban said, This mound is our witness today, and, therefore, he too named it Galeed.
local: Laban gblɔ be: “Kpekɔ sia nanye ŋkuɖodzikpe na nye kple wò tso egbe dzi,” eya ta wòna ŋkɔe be Galed.

eng: You were the one who told me, I will make sure things go well for you, and I will make your descendants like the sand of the sea, so many you wont be able to count them.
local: Evɔ wònye wòe gblɔ nam be: ‘Mana nu sia nu nadze edzi na wò nyuie; wò dzidzimeviwo asɔ gbɔ abe ƒutake si womate ŋu axlẽ o la ene.’ ”.

eng: He also named it Mizpah, because he said, The LORD will observe both of us when we are separated from each other.
local: Laban gagblɔ be: “Ne míeklã ɖa tso mía nɔewo gbɔ hã la, Aƒetɔ nenɔ mía ŋu dzɔm.” Ale wòna ŋkɔ teƒe ma be Mizpa.

eng: Jacob spent that night there. From what he had acquired, he set aside a gift for his brother Esau.
local: Wotsi afi ma dɔ ŋu ke, eye Yakob tsɔ nu siwo le esi la ƒe ɖe ɖo ɖe nɔvia Esau.

eng: If you treat my daughters badly and if you marry other women, though we arent there, know that God observed our witness.
local: Laban yi edzi be: “Ne èyi ɖale fu fiam vinye nyɔnuawo, alo yi ɖaɖe srɔ̃ kpe wo la, ɖo ŋku edzi be togbɔ be ame aɖeke meli o hã la, Mawu nye ɖasefo le nye kple wò dome.”.

eng: two hundred female goats and twenty male goats, two hundred ewes and twenty rams.
local: Eɖo gbɔ̃nɔ alafa eve, gbɔ̃tsu bla eve, alẽnɔ alafa eve kple agbo bla eve.

eng: Laban said to Jacob, Here is this mound and here is the sacred pillar that Ive set up for us.
local: Laban gagblɔ be: “Kpɔ kpekɔ kple ŋkuɖodzikpe sia si meɖo nye kple wò dome la ɖa.

eng: thirty nursing camels with their young, forty cows and ten bulls, and twenty female donkeys and ten male donkeys.
local: kposɔ vi nɔ siwo le no nam vi la, bla etɔ̃ kple wo viwo, nyinɔ bla eve kple nyitsu ewo, tedzinɔ bla eve kple tedzitsu ewo ɖee.

eng: This mound and the sacred pillar are witnesses that I wont travel beyond this mound and that you wont travel beyond this mound and this pillar to do harm.
local: Kpekɔ kple ŋkuɖodzikpe siawo zu ŋkuɖodzinu na mí ɖaa. Nyemadze to kpekɔ sia ŋu ava wɔ nu vevi wò o. Nenema ke wò hã màdze to kpekɔ sia kple ŋkuɖodzikpe sia ŋu ava wɔ nu vevim o.

eng: He separated these herds and gave them to his servants. He said to them, Go ahead of me and put some distance between each of the herds.
local: Ena dɔlaawo ma lã vovovoawo ɖa tso wo nɔewo gbɔ, eye wògblɔ na wo be: “Miva dze ŋgɔ nam, eye mina dometsotso nanɔ lãha vovovoawo dome.”.

eng: The God of Abraham and the God of Nahor will keep order between us. So Jacob gave his word in the name of the awesome one of his father Isaac.
local: Mawu si Abraham kple Naxor kple wo tɔgbuiwo subɔna la nanye ʋɔnudrɔ̃la le nye kpli wò dome.” Tete Yakob ka atam le tɔ́a Isak ƒe Mawu la ŋkɔ me, tsɔ ɖo kpe nubabla la dzi.

eng: He ordered the first group, When my brother Esau meets you and asks you, Who are you with? Where are you going? And whose herds are these in front of you.
local: Egblɔ na dɔla ŋgɔgbetɔ be: “Ne nɔvinye Esau va do go wò, eye wòbia wò be: ‘Ame ka vie nènye? Afi ka nèyina? Ame ka ƒe lãhawoe nye esiawo le ŋgɔwò?’ mahã la.

eng: Jacob offered a sacrifice on the mountain, and invited his relatives to a meal. They ate together and spent the night on the mountain.
local: Emegbe Yakob wu lã sa vɔe le to la dzi, eye wòyɔ etɔwo wova ɖu nu. Esi woɖu nua vɔ la, wotsi toa dzi dɔ.

eng: say, They are your servant Jacobs, a gift sent to my master Esau. And Jacob is actually right behind us.
local: ɖo eŋu nɛ be: ‘Wò dɔla Yakob tɔwoe. Nunana wònye wòɖo ɖe eƒe aƒetɔ Esau. Eya ŋutɔ le megbe gbɔna.’ ”.

eng: Laban got up early in the morning, kissed his sons and daughters, blessed them, and left to go back to his own place.
local: Le ŋufɔke ƒe ŋdi kanya la, Laban gbugbɔ nu na tɔgbuiyɔviawo kple via nyɔnuawo, eye wòyra wo. Emegbe etrɔ yi aƒe.

eng: He also ordered the second group, the third group, and everybody following the herds, Say exactly the same thing to Esau when you find him.
local: Egblɔ nya ma ke na ame evelia, etɔ̃lia kple bubu siwo katã le lãhaawo nu la be: “Ne miedo go Esau la, migblɔ nya si megblɔ la tututu.

eng: Jacob looked up and saw Esau approaching with four hundred men. Jacob divided the children among Leah, Rachel, and the two women servants.
local: Yakob fɔ mo dzi, eye wòkpɔ Esau kple eŋume alafa eneawo wogbɔna, ale wòma ɖeviawo na Lea, Raxel kple kosi eveawo.

eng: Say also, Your servant Jacob is right behind us. Jacob thought, I may be able to pacify Esau with the gift Im sending ahead. When I meet him, perhaps he will be kind to me.
local: Migblɔ be: ‘Wò dɔla Yakob ŋutɔ hã le megbe gbɔna.’ ” Elabe egblɔ le eɖokui me be: “Nunana siwo dze ŋgɔ la afa dzi nɛ gbã hafi nye ŋutɔ mava do ɖe eŋkume. Ɖewohĩ alɔ̃ axɔm.”.

eng: He put the servants and their children first, Leah and her children after them, and Rachel and Joseph last.
local: Etsɔ kosi eveawo kple wo viwo ɖo ŋgɔ, eyome la, Lea kple viawo va, eye Raxel kple Yosef wonɔ megbea.

eng: So Jacob sent the gift ahead of him, but he spent that night in the camp.
local: Ale nunanaawo dze ŋgɔ, ke eya ŋutɔ tsi asaɖa la me dɔ zã ma me.

eng: He himself went in front of them and bowed to the ground seven times as he was approaching his brother.
local: Eya ŋutɔ va le ŋgɔ na wo, eye wòle ta dem agu ʋuu zi adrẽ va se ɖe esime wògogo nɔvia.

eng: But Esau ran to meet him, threw his arms around his neck, kissed him, and they wept.
local: Ke Esau ƒu du va kpee, kpla asi kɔ nɛ, eye wògbugbɔ nu nɛ, eye wofa avi.

eng: Jacob got up during the night, took his two wives, his two women servants, and his eleven sons, and crossed the Jabbok Rivers shallow water.
local: Le zã ma me ke la, Yakob fɔ, eye wòkplɔ srɔ̃a eveawo, eƒe kosi eveawo kple via wuiɖekɛawo, eye wotsò Yabok-tɔsisi la.

eng: Dinah, the daughter whom Leah had borne to Jacob, went out to meet the women of that country.
local: Gbe ɖeka la, Dina, ame si nye Lea kple Yakob vinyɔnu la, ɖi tsa yi nyɔnu aɖewo gbɔ le nuto ma me.

eng: Esau looked up and saw the women and children and said, Who are these with you? Jacob said, The children that God generously gave your servant.
local: Esi Esau fɔ kɔ dzi kpɔ nyɔnuawo kple ɖeviawo la, ebia be: “Ame kawoe nye esiawo le ŋuwò?” Yakob ɖo eŋu be: “Vi siwo Mawu nyo dɔme, na nye wò dɔla woe.”.

eng: He took them and everything that belonged to him, and he helped them cross the river.
local: Ena wokplɔ wo tsò tɔ lae, eye nu siwo katã le esi la ɖo tɔa godo vɔ.

eng: When Shechem the son of the Hivite Hamor and the countrys prince saw her, he took her, slept with her, and humiliated her.
local: Esi Sixem, nuto ma me fia Xamor, Xivitɔ la ƒe viŋutsu kpɔe la, elée sesẽe, de asi eŋu, eye wògblẽe.

eng: The women servants and their children came forward and bowed down.
local: Tete kosiawo te va kple wo viwo, eye wode ta agu nɛ.

eng: But Jacob stayed apart by himself, and a man wrestled with him until dawn broke.
local: Ke eya ɖeɖe tsi megbe. Tete ŋutsu aɖe va te kame kplii ʋuu va se ɖe fɔŋli.

eng: He was drawn to Dinah, Jacobs daughter. He loved the young woman and tried to win her heart.
local: Dina, Yakob vinyɔnua, ƒe nu lé dzi na Sixem vevie. Elɔ̃ ɖetugbi la ŋutɔ, ale wòde asi enubeble me.

eng: Then Leah and her servants also came forward and bowed, and afterward Joseph and Rachel came forward and bowed.
local: Lea kple viawo hã te va, va de ta agu. Emegbe Raxel kple Yosef hã te va, eye wode ta agu nɛ.

eng: When the man saw that he couldnt defeat Jacob, he grabbed Jacobs thigh and tore a muscle in Jacobs thigh as he wrestled with him.
local: Esi ŋutsua kpɔ be yemete ŋu ɖu Yakob dzi o la, etɔ asi eƒe aligo, eye eƒe aligokpeƒe ɖɔli.

eng: Shechem said to his father Hamor, Get this girl for me as my wife.
local: Tete Sixem gblɔ na tɔ́a Xamor be: “Medi be nàɖe ɖetugbi sia nam.”.

eng: God said to Jacob, Get up, go to Bethel, and live there. Build an altar there to the God who appeared to you when you ran away from your brother Esau.
local: Mawu gblɔ na Yakob be: “Tso, nàʋu ayi Betel nàɖanɔ afi ma. Nàɖi vɔsamlekpui ɖe afi ma na Mawu, si ɖe eɖokui fia wò esime nèsí dzo yina le nɔviwò Esau nu.”.

eng: Esau said, Whats the meaning of this entire group of animals that I met? Jacob said, To ask for my masters kindness.
local: Esau biae be: “Lãha kawoe nye gbogbo siwo va kpem le mɔ dzi?” Yakob ɖo eŋu be: “Ɖe mebe matsɔ wo afa dzi na nye aƒetɔ.”.

eng: The man said, Let me go because the dawn is breaking. But Jacob said, I wont let you go until you bless me.
local: Ŋutsua gblɔ be: “Ɖe asi le ŋunye madzo; ŋu le kekem!” Ke Yakob ɖo eŋu nɛ be: “Nyemele asi ɖe ge le ŋuwò o; negbe ɖeko nàyram hafi.”.

eng: Now Jacob heard that Shechem defiled his daughter Dinah; but his sons were with the animals in the countryside, so he decided to keep quiet until they got back.
local: Yakob se be egblẽ Dina, ye vinyɔnuvia. Ke esi via ŋutsuwo nɔ gbe dzi le lãhawo gbɔ le ɣe ma ɣi ta la, ezi kpi va se ɖe esime wogbɔ.

eng: Jacob said to his household and to everyone who was with him, Get rid of the foreign gods you have with you. Clean yourselves and change your clothes.
local: Ale Yakob gblɔ na eƒemetɔwo kple ame siwo katã le egbɔ la be: “Mitsɔ mawu bubu siwo le mia si la ƒu gbe; mikɔ mia ɖokui ŋu, eye miɖɔli awu siwo le mia ŋu la.

eng: Esau said, I already have plenty, my brother. Keep whats yours.
local: Ke Esau ɖo eŋu be: “Nɔvinye, nu le asinye sɔ gbɔ. Wò nuwo nenɔ asiwò ko.”.

eng: He said to Jacob, Whats your name? and he said, Jacob.
local: Ŋutsua biae be: “Ŋkɔwò ɖe?” Eɖo eŋu be: “Yakob.”.

eng: Meanwhile, Hamor, Shechems father, went out to Jacob to speak with him.
local: Sixem tɔ́, Xamor, do yi Yakob gbɔ be yeaƒo nu kplii.

eng: Then lets rise and go up to Bethel so that I can build an altar there to the God who answered me when I was in trouble and who has been with me wherever Ive gone.
local: Mina míaʋu ayi Betel ne maɖaɖi vɔsamlekpui ɖe afi ma na Mawu si kpe ɖe ŋunye le nye xaxa me, eye wònɔ kplim le afi sia afi si mede la.”.

eng: Jacob said, No, please, do me the kindness of accepting my gift. Seeing your face is like seeing Gods face, since youve accepted me so warmly.
local: Yakob ɖo eŋu be: “Gbeɖe! Meɖe kuku, ne èdi be nye dzi nadze eme tututu la, ekema xɔ nye nunana la! Elabe esi mele kpɔwòm fifi laa la, ɖeko wòwɔ nam abe Mawu kpɔm mele ene. Ale si nève nunye wɔ dɔ ɖe dzinye ale gbegbe.

eng: Then he said, Your name wont be Jacob any longer, but Israel, because you struggled with God and with men and won.
local: Ŋutsua gblɔ nɛ be: “Womagayɔ wò be Yakob azɔ o; ke boŋ woayɔ wò be Israel elabe ète kame kple Mawu gate kame kple ame hã eye nèɖu dzi.”.

eng: Just then, Jacobs sons got back from the countryside. When they heard what had happened, they were deeply offended and very angry, because Shechem had disgraced Israel by sleeping with Jacobs daughter. Such things are simply not done.
local: Esi Yakob viŋutsuawo gbɔ tso gbea dzi va se nyaa la, dzi ku wo vevie. Wodo dɔmedzoe helihelĩ be Sixem ɖi gbɔ Israel, elabe ede asi Yakob vinyɔnu ŋuti; elabe medze be wòawɔ nu ma ƒomevi kura o.

eng: So they gave Jacob all of the foreign gods they had, as well as the rings in their ears, and Jacob buried them under the terebinth at Shechem.
local: Ale wotsɔ mawu bubu siwo katã le wo si kple woƒe togɛ siwo le woƒe to me la na Yakob, eye Yakob ɖi wo ɖe ati gã si le Sixem gbɔ la te.

eng: Take this present that Ive brought because God has been generous to me, and I have everything I need. So Jacob persuaded him, and he took it.
local: Meɖe kuku, xɔ nye nunana la nam ko, elabe Mawu yram mekpɔ nu geɖe.” Ale wòƒo nya ɖe Esau nu va se ɖe esime wòxɔ nunanaawo.

eng: Jacob also asked and said, Tell me your name. But he said, Why do you ask for my name? and he blessed Jacob there.
local: Yakob xɔ edzi be: “Meɖe kuku, yɔ wò ŋkɔ nam.” Ke ŋutsua ɖo eŋu be: “Nu ka ta nèdi be yeanya nye ŋkɔ?” Tete wòyra Yakob.

eng: Hamor said to them, My son Shechems heart is set on your daughter. Please let him marry her.
local: Xamor gblɔ na wo be: “Mia vi Dina ƒe nu lé dzi na vinye Sixem ŋutɔ, eya ta meɖe kuku, mitsɔe nɛ ne wòaɖe.

eng: When they set out, God made all of the surrounding cities fearful so that they didnt pursue Jacobs sons.
local: Esime wodze mɔ yina la, Mawu na vɔvɔ̃ ɖo du siwo ƒo xlã wo la ale gbegbe be wometi Yakob viawo yome o.

eng: Esau said, Lets break camp and set out, and Ill go with you.
local: Azɔ Esau gblɔ be: “Na míadze mɔ; manɔ ŋgɔ, ne miakplɔm ɖo.”.

eng: Jacob named the place Peniel, because Ive seen God face-to-face, and my life has been saved.
local: Yakob gblɔ be: “Mekpɔ Mawu ŋkume kple ŋkume, gake metsi agbe.” Ale wòtsɔ ŋkɔ na teƒe ma be Peniel.

eng: Arrange marriages with us: give us your daughters and take our daughters for yourselves.
local: ne srɔ̃ɖeɖe nanɔ mía dome: Mitsɔ miaƒe nyɔnuviwo na mí míaɖe, eye míawo hã míatsɔ mía tɔwo na mi miaɖe.

eng: But Jacob said to him, My master knows that the children arent strong and that I am responsible for the nursing flocks and cattle. If I push them hard for even one day, all of the flocks will die.
local: Yakob ɖo eŋu be: “Nye aƒetɔ, wò ŋutɔ ènya be ɖevi suewo, lã vi nɔwo kple wo vi nonowo hã le ŋunye. Ne míekplɔ wo kple du ŋkeke ɖeka teti hã la, wo katã atsrɔ̃.

eng: Jacob and all of the people with him arrived in Luz, otherwise known as Bethel, in the land of Canaan.
local: Yakob kple eŋumewo katã va ɖo Luz, si nye Betel, le Kanaan-nyigba dzi.

eng: The sun rose as Jacob passed Penuel, limping because of his thigh.
local: Ɣe dze esime wòdzo le afi ma yina. Enɔ tɔ̀tɔm ɖe ata ɖeka dzi.

eng: Live with us. The land is available to you: settle down, travel through it, and buy property in it.
local: Ekema mianɔ mía gbɔ; mianɔ anyigba la ƒe afi si dze mia ŋu, eye miatsa asi le mía dome aɖo nunɔamesiwo na mia ɖokui faa.”.

eng: My master, go on ahead of your servant, but Ive got to take it easy, going only as fast as the animals in front of me and the children are able to go, until I meet you in Seir.
local: Meɖe kuku, do ŋgɔ nam ne manɔ yowòme blewuu ale si lãha la kple ɖeviawo ate ŋu azɔe, va se ɖe esime matu wò le Seir.”.

eng: He built an altar there and named the place El-bethel, because God had revealed himself to him there when he ran away from his brother.
local: Eɖi vɔsamlekpui ɖe afi ma, eye wòyɔ teƒea be ‘Betel ƒe Mawu’ elabe afi mae Mawu ɖe eɖokui fiae le, esime wòsí le nɔvia Esau nu yina.

eng: Therefore, Israelites dont eat the tendon attached to the thigh muscle to this day, because he grabbed Jacobs thigh muscle at the tendon.
local: Esi wònye lãkusi sesẽ si le aligokpeƒee ame la tɔ asii na Yakob ta la, va se ɖe egbe Israelviwo meɖua lã ƒe akpa ma o.

eng: Shechem said to Dinahs father and brothers, If you approve of me, tell me what you want, and I will give it to you.
local: Sixem hã gblɔ na Dina tɔ́a kple nɔvia ŋutsuawo be: “Minyo dɔme nam ko eye nu sia nu si miabia la, mana faa.

eng: Rebekahs nurse Deborah died and was buried at Bethel under the oak, and Jacob named it Allon-bacuth.
local: Azɔ Debora, ame si kpɔ Rebeka dzi le ɖevime la, ku, eye woɖii ɖe logoti gã aɖe te le Betel ƒe anyigbeme. Ale wòna ŋkɔ afi ma be “Konyifalogoti.”.

eng: Esau said, Let me leave some of my people with you. But Jacob said, Why should you do this since my master has already been so kind to me.
local: Ale Esau xɔ edzi be: “Ekema magblẽ ŋunyeme aɖewo ɖe gbɔwò.” Gake Yakob gagblɔ be: “Ɖe wòhiã? Na makpɔ amenuveve le nye aƒetɔ ŋkume koe!”.

eng: Make the bride price and marriage gifts as large as you like, and I will pay whatever you tell me. Then let me marry the young woman.
local: Nyɔnutabianu kple srɔ̃ɖenu ale sinu hiã ko la, migblɔe nam. Mele klalo be mana ale si miedi, ne mianyatsɔ ɖetugbia nam maɖe ko.”.

eng: God appeared to Jacob again, while he was on his way back from Paddan-aram, and blessed him.
local: Esime Yakob gbɔ tso Padan-Aram la, Mawu gaɖe eɖokui fiae, eye wòyrae.

eng: That day Esau returned on the road to Seir.
local: Ale Esau dze mɔ gatrɔ ɖo ta Edom gbe ma gbe ke.

eng: Jacobs sons responded deviously to Shechem and his father Hamor because Shechem defiled their sister Dinah.
local: Yakob viŋutsuawo ɖo eŋu na Sixem kple tɔ́a Xamor ayetɔe elabe woɖi gbɔ wo nɔvi Dina.

eng: God said to him, Your name is Jacob, but your name will be Jacob no longer. No, your name will be Israel. And he named him Israel.
local: Mawu gblɔ nɛ be: “Ŋkɔwòe nye Yakob, ke tso fifia dzi heyina la, woayɔ wò be Israel.” Ale Israel zu ŋkɔ nɛ.

eng: but Jacob traveled to Succoth. He built a house for himself but made temporary shelters for his animals; therefore, he named the place Succoth.
local: Ke Yakob ɖo ta Sukot. Etso aƒe ɖe afi ma eye wòtu lãkpɔ na eƒe lãhawo hã ɖe afi ma. Eya tae wotsɔ ŋkɔ na afi ma be Sukot.

eng: They said to them, We cant do this, allowing our sisters to marry uncircumcised men, because its disgraceful to us.
local: Wogblɔ nɛ be: “Míate ŋu aɖe asi le mía nɔvinyɔnu ŋuti be wòaɖaɖe bolobolotɔ o, elabe enye ŋukpenanu na mí.

eng: These are the descendants of Esau, that is, Edom.
local: Esau si wogayɔna hã be Edom la ƒe dzidzimeviwo ŋutinyae nye esi:.

eng: Jacob arrived safely at the city of Shechem in the land of Canaan on his trip from Paddan-aram, and he camped in front of the city.
local: Esi Yakob trɔ gbɔ tso Padan-Aram la, eva ɖo Sixem-dua me le Kanaan-nyigba dzi dedie, eye wònɔ dua xa.

eng: God said to him, I am El Shaddai. Be fertile and multiply. A nation, even a large group of nations, will come from you; kings will descend from your own children.
local: Mawu gblɔ nɛ be: “Nyee nye Mawu Ŋusẽkatãtɔ. Mana nàdzi asɔ gbɔ fũu. Dukɔ, dukɔwo ado tso mewò, eye fiawo adzɔ tso wò dzidzimeviwo me.

eng: We can only agree to do this if you circumcise every male as we do.
local: Ne mielɔ̃ be yewoazu abe míawo ene, eye ŋutsu sia ŋutsu le mia dome natso aʋa la, eya ko míalɔ̃ ɖe nya sia dzi.

eng: Esau married Canaanite women: Adah the daughter of the Hittite Elon; Oholibamah the daughter of Anah son of the Hittite Zibeon.
local: Nyɔnu siwo Esau ɖe la nye Kanaantɔwo. Woawoe nye: Ada, Xititɔ Elon vinyɔnu; Oholibama, Ana vinyɔnu, ame si nye Xititɔ Zibeon tɔgbuiyɔvi.

eng: He bought the section of the field where he pitched his tent from the sons of Hamor, Shechems father, for one hundred qesitahs.
local: Eƒle anyigba si dzi wòtu eƒe agbadɔ ɖo la le Hemor, ame si tso Sixem-dua la viwo si klosaloga alafa ɖeka.

eng: The land I gave to Abraham and to Isaac, I give to you; and I will give the land to your descendants after you.
local: Anyigba si metsɔ na Abraham kple Isak la, matsɔe ana wò, eye matsɔe ana wò dzidzimevi siwo ava ɖe yowòme la hã.”.

eng: Then we will give our daughters to you, and we will take your daughters for ourselves. We will live with you and be one people.
local: Míatsɔ mía vinyɔnuwo na mi miaɖe, eye míawo hã míaɖe mia tɔwo. Míanɔ mia gbɔ, eye míazu dukɔ ɖeka.

eng: and Basemath the daughter of Ishmael and sister of Nebaioth.
local: kple Basmat, Ismael vinyɔnu, ame si nye Bebayot nɔvinyɔnu.

eng: Then he set up an altar there and named it El Elohe Israel.
local: Eɖi vɔsamlekpui ɖe afi ma, eye wòna ŋkɔe be: El-Elohe-Israel.

eng: Then God ascended, leaving him alone in the place where he spoke to him.
local: Azɔ Mawu dzo le egbɔ le afi si wòƒo nu kplii le la heyi dzi.

eng: But if you dont listen to us and become circumcised, we will take our daughter and leave.
local: Ke ne miexɔ míaƒe nya la hetso aʋa o la, ekema míaxɔ mía vinyɔnu la le mia si adzoe.”.

eng: Adah gave birth to Eliphaz for Esau, Basemath gave birth to Reuel.
local: Ada dzi Elifaz, eye Basmat dzi Reguel.

eng: So Jacob set up a sacred pillar, a stone pillar, at the place God spoke to him. He poured an offering of wine on it and then poured oil over it.
local: Tete Yakob tu ŋkuɖodzikpe ɖe afi si Mawu ƒo nu kplii le la tsɔ de dzesi teƒea. Ekɔ wain ɖe edzi, eye wòkɔ ami hã ɖe dzi.

eng: Their idea seemed like a good one to Hamor and Hamors son Shechem.
local: Nya la dze Xamor kple via Sixem ŋu.

eng: and Oholibamah gave birth to Jeush, Jalam, and Korah. These are Esaus sons born to him in the land of Canaan.
local: Oholibama dzi Yehus, Yalam kple Korax. Ŋutsuvi siawoe wodzi na Esau le Kanaan-nyigba dzi.

eng: Jacob named the place Bethel where God spoke to him.
local: Yakob na ŋkɔ teƒe ma si Mawu ƒo nu nɛ le la be Betel.

eng: The young man didnt waste any time doing this because he liked Jacobs daughter so much. He was more respected than anyone else in his fathers household.
local: Ɖekakpui la mehe nyaa kura o, elabe Yakob vinyɔnu la ƒe nu nyo eŋu ŋutɔ. Gawu la, ɖekakpui la nye ame si tɔ́a ƒometɔwo buna wu le ƒomea me.

eng: Esau took his wives, his sons, his daughters, and everyone in his household, and his livestock, all of his animals, and all of the property he had acquired in the land of Canaan; and he moved away from the land of Canaan and from his brother Jacob.
local: Esau kplɔ srɔ̃awo, via ŋutsuwo, via nyɔnuwo, eƒe kluvi siwo katã le eƒeme, eƒe lãhawo kple dɔwɔlãwo katã, eye wòƒo ƒu kesinɔnu siwo katã wòkpɔ le Kanaan-nyigba dzi la, eye wòʋu le nɔvia Yakob gbɔ yi Seir-nyigba dzi.

eng: Jacob lived in the land of Canaan where his father was an immigrant.
local: Yakob nɔ Kanaan-nyigba si dzi tɔ́a nye amedzro le la dzi.

eng: They left Bethel, and when they were still some distance from Ephrath, Rachel went into hard labor.
local: Azɔ Yakob kple etɔwo dze mɔ tso Betel yina. Esi wogogo Efrat la, Raxel de asi kuléle me, ke via dzidzi sesẽ nɛ.

eng: Hamor and his son Shechem went to their citys gate and spoke to the men of their city.
local: Xamor kple via Sixem va dua ƒe takpeƒe le du la ƒe agbo nu, eye woƒo nu na wo detɔwo. Wogblɔ be:.

eng: They had so many possessions that they couldnt live together. The land where they lived as immigrants couldnt support all of their livestock.
local: Eʋu elabe woƒe nunɔamesiwo sɔ gbɔ ale gbegbe be womagate ŋu anɔ wo nɔewo gbɔ o. Gawu la, woƒe lãhawo dzi ɖe edzi ale gbegbe be anyigba si dzi wonye amedzro le la megalolo na wo o.

eng: This is the account of Jacobs descendants. Joseph was years old and tended the flock with his brothers. While he was helping the sons of Bilhah and Zilpah, his fathers wives, Joseph told their father unflattering things about them.
local: Yakob ƒe dzidzimeviwo ŋutinyae nye esi: Esi Yosef nye ɖekakpuivi xɔ ƒe wui adrẽ la, eya kple tɔ́via, siwo nye tɔ́a srɔ̃ Bilha kple Silpa viŋutsuwo la nɔa alẽ kplɔm. Yosef nɔ nɔviawo ƒe nya vɔ̃ tom na wo tɔ́a.

eng: During her difficult labor, the midwife said to her, Dont be afraid. You have another son.
local: Esi via dzidzi nye dɔ nɛ la, vixela la gblɔ nɛ be: “Mègavɔ̃ o; ŋutsuvi dzi ge nègale.”.

eng: These men want peace with us. Let them live in the land and travel through it; theres plenty of land for them. We will marry their daughters and give them our daughters.
local: “Ame siawo la, ame fafawo wonye. Mina woanɔ mía dome, atso aƒe ɖe anyigba la dzi, eye woatsa asi le edzi faa. Mikpɔ anyigba la ɖa. Elolo le go sia go me; asu na mí kple wo siaa. Mina míaɖe wo vinyɔnuwo, eye míawo hã míatsɔ mía tɔwo ana wo woaɖe faa.

eng: So Esau, that is, Edom, lived in the mountains of Seir.
local: Ale Esau yi ɖanɔ Seir-tonyigba dzi. Esau kee nye Edom.

eng: Now Israel loved Joseph more than any of his other sons because he was born when Jacob was old. Jacob had made for him a long robe.
local: Azɔ la, Israel lɔ̃ Yosef wu via bubuawo katã, elabe enye eƒe tsitsime vi. Etɔ awu ʋlaya abɔ legbe nɛ.

eng: As her life faded away, just before she died, she named him Ben-oni, but his father named him Benjamin.
local: Nu té Raxel ŋu vɔ, eye wòkpɔ be yemele agbe tsi ge o. Ale wòna ŋkɔ ɖevia be Benoni Ke tɔ́a ya yɔe be Benyamin.

eng: But the men will agree to live with us and become one people only if we circumcise every male just as they do.
local: Ke wobe hafi yewoalɔ̃ anɔ mía gbɔ, eye míazu dukɔ ɖeka la, ele be mía dome ŋutsu sia ŋutsu natso aʋa abe yewo ene.

eng: These are the descendants of Esau, the ancestor of Edom, which lies in the mountains of Seir.
local: Esau nye Edomtɔwo ƒe tɔgbuitɔgbui si me wodzɔ tso. Wole Seir-tonyigba la dzi. Esau ƒe dzidzimeviwo ŋutinyae nye esi:.

eng: When his brothers saw that their father loved him more than any of his brothers, they hated him and couldnt even talk nicely to him.
local: Esi Yosef nɔviŋutsuawo kpɔ be yewo tɔ́ lɔ̃ Yosef wu yewo dometɔ ɖe sia ɖe la, wolé fui, eye womegaƒoa nu nɛ lɔlɔ̃tɔe azɔ o.

eng: Their livestock, their property, and all of their animals—wont they be ours? Lets agree with them and let them live with us.
local: Menye ɖeko woƒe lãhawo, nunɔamesiwo kple dɔwɔlãwo katã azu mía tɔ oa? Eya ta mina míalɔ̃ ɖe woƒe nya la dzi ko ne woanɔ mía gbɔ.”.

eng: Rachel died and was buried near the road to Ephrath, that is, Bethlehem.
local: Esi Raxel ku la, woɖii ɖe mɔ si ɖo ta Efrat, si woyɔna be Betlehem, la to.

eng: These are the names of Edoms sons: Eliphaz son of Esaus wife Adah, and Reuel son of Esaus wife Basemath.
local: Esau viŋutsuwo ŋkɔwoe nye esiawo: Esau srɔ̃ Ada viŋutsue nye Elifaz, eye Esau srɔ̃ Basmat viŋutsue nye Reguel.

eng: Jacob set up a pillar on her grave. Its the pillar on Rachels tomb thats still there today.
local: Yakob tu ŋkuɖodzikpe ɖe eƒe yɔdo dzi si woyɔna be Raxel ƒe yɔdodzikpe va se ɖe egbe.

eng: Joseph had a dream and told it to his brothers, which made them hate him even more.
local: Gbe ɖeka Yosef ku drɔ̃e aɖe, eye esi wòlĩi na nɔviawo la, wogalé fui wu tsã.

eng: Everyone at the city gate agreed with Hamor and his son Shechem, so every able-bodied male in the city was circumcised.
local: Ame siwo katã va takpeƒea la lɔ̃ ɖe Xamor kple via Sixem ƒe nya la dzi, eye dua me ŋutsuwo katã tso aʋa.

eng: Eliphazs sons were Teman, Omar, Zepho, Gatam, and Kenaz.
local: Elifaz viŋutsuwoe nye: Teman, Omar, Zefo, Gaetam kple Kenaz.
