eng: On the third day, when they were still in pain, two of Jacobs sons and Dinahs brothers Simeon and Levi took their swords, came into the city, which suspected nothing, and killed every male.
local: Tete le ŋkeke etɔ̃a gbe, esime abia nɔ wo vem vevie, eye woɖe ŋu ɖi la, Yakob viŋutsu eve, Simeon kple Levi, siwo nye Dina dadaviwo la, tsɔ yi, dze dua dzi hewu eme ŋutsuwo katã.

eng: Israel continued his trip and pitched his tent farther on near the tower of Eder.
local: Emegbe Israel yi eƒe mɔzɔzɔ dzi, eye wòva tu agbadɔ ɖe Eder gbetakpɔmɔ́ godo.

eng: He said to them, Listen to this dream I had.
local: Egblɔ na wo be: “Mise drɔ̃e si meku la ɖa.

eng: Timna was the secondary wife of Eliphaz, Esaus son, and she gave birth to Amalek for Eliphaz. These are the sons of Esaus wife Adah.
local: Esau viŋutsu, Elifaz, ƒe ahiãvie nye Timna. Eyae dzi Amalek na Elifaz. Esau srɔ̃, Ada, viŋutsuwoe nye esiawo.

eng: They killed Hamor and his son Shechem with their swords, took Dinah from Shechems household, and left.
local: Wowu Xamor kple via Sixem hã kple yi, eye wokplɔ Dina tso Sixem ƒeme dzoe.

eng: While Israel stayed in that place, Reuben went and slept with Bilhah his fathers secondary wife, and Israel heard about it. Jacob had twelve sons.
local: Esime Israel le nuto ma me la, Ruben de asi Bilha si nye tɔ́a ƒe ahiãvi ŋu, eye Israel see. Azɔ viŋutsuvi wui evee nɔ Yakob si.

eng: When we were binding stalks of grain in the field, my stalk got up and stood upright, while your stalks gathered around it and bowed down to my stalk.
local: Kpɔ ɖa, míele lu blam le agble me. Tete nye lu babla tsi tsitre hedzɔ ka. Kasia mia tɔwo va ƒo xlãe hele ta dem agu nɛ.”.

eng: These are Reuels sons: Nahath, Zerah, Shammah, and Mizzah. These are the sons of Esaus wife Basemath.
local: Reguel viŋutsuwoe nye: Naxat, Serax, Sama kple Misa. Woawoe nye Esau srɔ̃, Basmat, viŋutsuwo.

eng: When Jacobs other sons discovered the dead, they looted the city that had defiled their sister.
local: Yakob vi mamlɛawo hã va ɖe nu xɔasi siwo katã le ame kukuawo ŋu, eye woha dua le ale si wodo vlo wo nɔvinyɔnu la ta.

eng: The sons of Leah were Reuben, Jacobs oldest son, and Simeon, Levi, Judah, Issachar, and Zebulun.
local: Lea viŋutsuwoe nye: Ruben, ame si nye Yakob ƒe ŋgɔgbevi, Simeon, Levi, Yuda, Isaxar kple Zebulon.

eng: His brothers said to him, Will you really be our king and rule over us? So they hated him even more because of the dreams he told them.
local: Nɔviawo biae be: “Ɖe nègbɔna fia zu ge aɖu mía dzi nyateƒea?” Ale wogalé fui ɖe edzi wu tsã le eƒe drɔ̃eawo kple nyaawo ta.

eng: These are the sons of Esaus wife Oholibamah, the daughter of Anah, Zibeons son: she gave birth to Esau, Jeush, Jalam, and Korah.
local: Esau srɔ̃ Oholibama, Ana vinyɔnu, ame si nye Zibeon tɔgbuiyɔvi la, dzi viŋutsu etɔ̃ nɛ. Woawoe nye: Yehus, Yaelam kple Korax.

eng: At that time, Judah moved away from his brothers and settled near an Adullamite named Hirah.
local: Le ɣe ma ɣi me la, Yuda ʋu le nɔviawo gbɔ yi ɖanɔ Adulam-ŋutsu aɖe si ŋkɔe nye Xira, la gbɔ.

eng: They took their flocks, their cattle, and their donkeys, whether in the city or in the fields nearby.
local: Wokplɔ woƒe alẽwo, nyiwo kple tedziwo, eye wotsɔ nu sia nu si wokpɔ le dua me kple agblewo me la dzoe.

eng: The sons of Rachel were Joseph and Benjamin.
local: Raxel viŋutsuwoe nye Yosef kple Benyamin.

eng: Then Joseph had another dream and described it to his brothers: Ive just dreamed again, and this time the sun and the moon and eleven stars were bowing down to me.
local: Tete wògaku drɔ̃e bubu, eye wòlĩ eya hã na nɔviawo gblɔ be: “Kpɔ ɖa, megaku drɔ̃e bubu be ɣe, ɣleti kple ɣletivi wuiɖekɛ wode ta agu nam.”.

eng: These are the tribal chiefs from Esaus sons. The sons of Eliphaz, Esaus oldest son: Chief Teman, Chief Omar, Chief Zepho, Chief Kenaz.
local: To siwo dzɔ tso Esau me la woe nye esiwo gbɔna: Esau ƒe ŋgɔgbevi Elifaz ƒe dzidzimeviwoe nye: Teman, Omar, Zefo, Kenaz.

eng: There Judah saw the daughter of a Canaanite whose name was Shua, and he married her. After he slept with her.
local: Yuda kpɔ Kanaan-ŋutsu aɖe si woyɔna be Sua la ƒe vinyɔnu, eye wòɖee hedɔ kplii.

eng: They carried off their property, their children, and their wives. They looted the entire place.
local: Woha woƒe kesinɔnuwo katã, ɖe aboyo ɖeviwo kple nyɔnuwo siaa eye wotsɔ nu sia nu si wokpɔ le aƒeawo me la dzoe.

eng: The sons of Bilhah, Rachels servant, were Dan and Naphtali.
local: Raxel ƒe kosi Bilha viŋutsuwoe nye Dan kple Naftali.

eng: When he described it to his father and brothers, his father scolded him and said to him, What kind of dreams have you dreamed? Am I and your mother and your brothers supposed to come and bow down to the ground in front of you.
local: Yosef lĩ drɔ̃e la na tɔ́a kple nɔviawo. Ke tɔ́a ka mo nɛ gblɔ be: “Drɔ̃e ka ƒomevie nye esia nèku? Ɖe nye kple nɔwò, kpakple nɔviwòwo míava dze klo ade ta agu na wòa?”.

eng: Chief Korah, Chief Gatam, and Chief Amalek. These are the tribal chiefs of Eliphaz in the land of Edom; they are Adahs sons.
local: Korax, Gaetam kple Amalek. To siawo katã nye Esau srɔ̃ Ada ƒe dzidzimeviwo.

eng: she became pregnant and gave birth to a son, whom she named Er.
local: Nyɔnua fɔ fu dzi viŋutsu, eye Yuda na ŋkɔe be Er.

eng: Jacob said to Simeon and Levi, Youve put me in danger by making me offensive to those who live here in the land, to the Canaanites and the Perizzites. I have only a few men. They may join forces, attack me, and destroy me, me and my household.
local: Tete Yakob gblɔ na Simeon kple Levi be: “Miedem dzɔgbevɔ̃e me. Fifia Kanaantɔwo kple Perizitɔwo kpakple ame bubu siwo katã le anyigba sia dzi la além ɖe fu me. Mikpɔ ɖa, míesɔ gbɔ kura o. Ne woƒo ƒu, tso ɖe ŋunye la, woatsrɔ̃ nye kple ŋunyemewo katã.”.

eng: The sons of Zilpah, Leahs servant, were Gad and Asher. These were Jacobs sons born to him in Paddan-aram.
local: Lea ƒe kosi Silpa ƒe viŋutsuwo nye Gad kple Aser. Esiawoe nye viŋutsuvi siwo wodzi na Yakob le Padan-Aram.

eng: These are the sons of Reuel, Esaus son: Chief Nahath, Chief Zerah, Chief Shammah, and Chief Mizzah. These are the tribal chiefs of Reuel in the land of Edom; they are the sons of Esaus wife Basemath.
local: Esau vi Reguel ƒe dzidzimeviwoe nye: Naxat, Serax, Sama kple Misa. To siawo katã nye Esau srɔ̃ Basmat viwo.

eng: His brothers were jealous of him, but his father took careful note of the matter.
local: Tso esia dzi la, nɔviawo de asi ŋuʋaʋãe me, ke tɔ́a lé nya la ɖe tame.

eng: She became pregnant again, gave birth to a son, and named him Onan.
local: Eyome egafɔ fu dzi viŋutsu, eye nyɔnua tsɔ ŋkɔ nɛ be Onan.

eng: They said, But didnt he treat our sister like a prostitute.
local: Ke woɖo eŋu nɛ be: “Eya kpɔ mɔ awɔ mía nɔvinyɔnu abe gbolo enea?”.

eng: Jacob came to his father Isaac at Mamre, that is, Kiriath-arba. This is Hebron, where Abraham and Isaac lived as immigrants.
local: Yakob va tɔ́a Isak gbɔ le Mamre le Kiryat-Arba si yɔm wole egbe be Xebron, afi si Abraham kple Isak nɔ kpɔ la.

eng: These are the sons of Esaus wife Oholibamah: Chief Jeush, Chief Jalam, and Chief Korah. They are the tribal chiefs of Esaus wife Oholibamah the daughter of Anah.
local: Vi siwo Esau srɔ̃ Oholibama dzi nɛ la woe nye: Yehus, Yaelam kple Korax.

eng: Josephs brothers went to tend their fathers flocks near Shechem.
local: Gbe ɖeka Yosef nɔviwo yi wo tɔ́ ƒe alẽha kplɔƒe le Sixem.

eng: Then she gave birth to one more son and named him Shelah. She was in Chezib when she gave birth to him.
local: Emegbe egadzi viŋutsu bubu, eye nyɔnua na ŋkɔe be Sela. Nyɔnua nɔ Kesib hafi dzi ɖevi sia.

eng: At the age of years.
local: Isak nɔ agbe ƒe alafa ɖeka bla enyi.

eng: These are the sons of Esau, who is Edom, and these are their tribal chiefs.
local: To siawo katã dzɔ tso Esau si wogayɔna hã be Edom la me.

eng: Israel said to Joseph, Arent your brothers tending the sheep near Shechem? Come, Ill send you to them. And he said, Im ready.
local: Israel gblɔ na Yosef be: “Nɔviwòwo le alẽawo kplɔm le Sixem. Va madɔ wò ɖe wo gbɔ.” Yosef ɖo eŋu be: “Yoo, nyee nye esi.”.

eng: Judah married his oldest son Er to a woman named Tamar.
local: Yuda ɖe srɔ̃ na eƒe ŋgɔgbevi Er. Nyɔnua ŋkɔe nye Tamar.

eng: These are the sons of Seir, the Horite, who live in the land: Lotan, Shobal, Zibeon, Anah.
local: Seir, Xoritɔ, ame si nye Edom-nyigba la dzi nɔla tsã la ƒe dzidzimeviwoe nye esi: Lotan, Sobal, Zibeon, Ana.

eng: Isaac took his last breath and died. He was buried with his ancestors after a long, satisfying life. His sons Esau and Jacob buried him.
local: Isak ku esime wòtsi xɔ ƒè geɖee, eye wòzu amegãɖeɖi belibeli hafi yi tɔgbuiawo gbɔ. Via ŋutsuwo, Esau kple Yakob, ɖii.

eng: Jacob said to him, Go! Find out how your brothers are and how the flock is, and report back to me. So Jacob sent him from the Hebron Valley. When he approached Shechem.
local: Tɔ́a gblɔ nɛ be: “Yi nàɖakpɔ ɖa be nɔviwòwo kple lãhaawo li nyuie hã, ne nàva gblɔ nam.” Ale tɔ́a dɔe tso Xebron-bali me ɖo ɖe Sixem. Esi wòyi la.

eng: But the LORD considered Judahs oldest son Er immoral, and the LORD put him to death.
local: Ke Er, Yuda ƒe ŋgɔgbevi, nye ame vɔ̃ɖi le Aƒetɔ ŋkume, eya ta Aƒetɔ wui.

eng: Dishon, Ezer, and Dishan. These are the Horite tribal chiefs, Seirs sons, in the land of Edom.
local: Dison, Ezer kple Disan. Woawoe nye toawo ƒe tatɔwo.

eng: a man found him wandering in the field and asked him, What are you looking for.
local: eɖanɔ tsatsam le gbea dzi nɔ nɔviawo dim, eye ŋutsu aɖe kpɔe. Ŋutsua biae be: “Nu ka dim nèle?”.

eng: Judah said to Onan, Go to your brothers wife, do your duty as her brother-in-law, and provide children for your brother.
local: Tete Yuda gblɔ na Onan be: “Ɖe nɔviwò ƒe ahosi la, ne nàdo ƒome ɖi na nɔviwò la.”.

eng: Lotans sons are Hori and Heman, and Lotans sister was Timna.
local: Lotan viŋutsuwoe nye: Xori kple Heman. Nɔvia nyɔnue nye Timna.

eng: Onan knew the children wouldnt be his so when he slept with his brothers wife, he wasted his semen on the ground, so he wouldnt give his brother children.
local: Ke esi Onan nya be ɖeviawo manye ye tɔ o ta la, ne eya kple nɔvia srɔ̃ wole dɔdɔm, eye ŋutsunu gbɔna la, enana be wòkɔna ɖe anyigba, be nyɔnua magafɔ fu adzi vi ado ƒome ɖi na nɔvia o.

eng: Joseph said, Im looking for my brothers. Tell me, where are they tending the sheep.
local: Eɖo eŋu be: “Nɔvinyewo dim mele. Meɖe kuku, fia afi si wole lãawo kplɔm le lam.”.

eng: These are Shobals sons: Alvan, Manahath, Ebal, Shepho, and Onam.
local: Sobal viŋutsuwoe nye: Alvan, Manaxat, Ebal Sefo kple Onam.

eng: The LORD considered what he did as wrong and put him to death too.
local: Eƒe nuwɔna la medze Aƒetɔ ŋu kura o, eya ta ewu eya hã.

eng: The man said, They left here. I heard them saying, Lets go to Dothan. So Joseph went after his brothers and found them in Dothan.
local: Ŋutsua gblɔ nɛ be: “Wodzo, elabe mese wogblɔ be: ‘Mina míayi Dotan.’ ” Ale Yosef ti nɔviawo yome, eye wòva ke ɖe wo ŋu le Dotan.

eng: These are Zibeons sons: Aiah and Anah. Anah is the one who found water in the desert while pasturing his father Zibeons donkeys.
local: Zibeon viŋutsuwoe nye: Aya kple Ana. Ana siae fɔ tsi dzodzoe vudo esime wònɔ tɔ́a ƒe tedziwo gbɔ le gbedzi.

eng: Judah said to Tamar his daughter-in-law, Stay as a widow in your fathers household until my son Shelah grows up. He thought Shelah would die like his brothers had. So Tamar went and lived in her fathers household.
local: Azɔ Yuda gblɔ na toyɔvia Tamar be: “Trɔ yi tɔ́wò ƒeme, eye nànɔ anyi abe ahosi ene va se ɖe esime vinye Sela natsi.” Egblɔ esia elabe evɔ̃ be Sela hã ava ku abe nɔviawo ke ene. Ale Tamar trɔ yi ɖale tɔ́a ƒeme.

eng: They saw Joseph in the distance before he got close to them, and they plotted to kill him.
local: Wokpɔe le adzɔge ɖaa, eye hafi wòava ɖo wo gbɔ la, woɖo nugbe ɖe eŋu be yewoawui.

eng: These are Anahs children: Dishon and Anahs daughter Oholibamah.
local: Ana viwoe nye esiawo: Dison kple Ana vinyɔnu, Oholibama.

eng: After a long time, Judahs wife the daughter of Shua died. Then, after a period of mourning, he and his neighbor Hirah the Adullamite went up to Timnah, to those who were shearing his sheep.
local: Le ɣeyiɣi didi aɖe megbe la, Yuda srɔ̃, si nye Sua vinyɔnu la ku. Esi Yuda fae vɔ la, eya kple xɔlɔ̃a Xira, Adulamtɔ la, woyi ɖe Timnat be yewoaɖakpɔ ame siwo le fu kom na yeƒe alẽwo la ɖa.

eng: The brothers said to each other, Here comes the big dreamer.
local: Wogblɔ na wo nɔewo be: “Drɔ̃ekula lae nye esi gbɔna!.

eng: These are Dishons sons: Hemdan, Eshban, Ithran, and Cheran.
local: Dison viŋutsuwoe nye esiawo: Xemdan, Esban, Yitran kple Keran.

eng: Tamar was told, Your father-in-law is now on his way up to Timnah to shear his sheep.
local: Ame aɖe na nyanya Tamar be: “Kpɔ ɖa, towò gbɔna ɖe Timnat, fu ko ge na eƒe alẽwo.”.

eng: Come on now, lets kill him and throw him into one of the cisterns, and well say a wild animal devoured him. Then we will see what becomes of his dreams.
local: Mina míawui atsɔ ƒu gbe ɖe do me. Ɖeko míagblɔ be lã wɔadã aɖe lée. Ekema nu si eƒe drɔ̃eawo ava zu hã míakpɔe.”.

eng: These are Ezers sons: Bilhan, Zaavan, and Akan.
local: Ezer viŋutsuwoe nye: Bilhan, Savan kple Akan.

eng: So Tamar took off the clothing she wore as a widow, covered herself with a veil, put on makeup, and sat down at the entrance to Enaim on the road to Timnah, since she realized that although Shelah had already grown up, she hadnt been given to him as a wife.
local: Ale eɖe eƒe ahovɔ ɖi, tsɔ motsyɔnu tsyɔ ŋkume, eye wòbla eɖokui bubui va bɔbɔ nɔ anyi ɖe Enaim-gbo nu, le Timnat-mɔ dzi. Tamar wɔ esia elabe ekpɔ be Sela tsi aɖe ye, gake wometsɔ ye nɛ be wòaɖe o.

eng: When Joseph had been taken down to Egypt, Potiphar, Pharaohs chief officer, the commander of the royal guard and an Egyptian, purchased him from the Ishmaelites who had brought him down there.
local: Esi wokplɔ Yosef yi Egipte la, Egiptetɔ Potifar, si nye Egipte Fiagã ƒe aƒedzikpɔlawo kple eŋumewo ƒe amegã la ƒlee le Ismaeltɔawo si.

eng: When Reuben heard what they said, he saved him from them, telling them, Lets not take his life.
local: Ke esi Ruben se nyaa la, eɖoe be yeaxɔ nɛ. Ale egblɔ be: “Migana míaɖe eƒe agbe ɖa o!”.

eng: These are Dishans sons: Uz and Aran.
local: Disan viŋutsuwoe nye: Uz kple Aran.

eng: Judah saw her and thought she was a prostitute because she had covered her face.
local: Esi Tamar tsyɔ nu ŋkume ta la, Yuda bu xaa be gbolo aɖe ye.

eng: The LORD was with Joseph, and he became a successful man and served in his Egyptian masters household.
local: Aƒetɔ nɔ kple Yosef, eye nu sia nu dze dzi nɛ. Enɔ eƒe aƒetɔ Egiptetɔ la ƒeme.

eng: Reuben said to them, Dont spill his blood! Throw him into this desert cistern, but dont lay a hand on him. He intended to save Joseph from them and take him back to his father.
local: Egagblɔ be: “Migakɔ ʋu ɖi o. Mitsɔe da ɖe do sia me le gbedadaƒo afii boŋ. Migawɔ nu vevi aɖekee o.” Egblɔ esia bene yeate ŋu aɖee tso wo si me atsɔe aɖo ɖe wo tɔ́.

eng: These are the Horite tribal chiefs: Chiefs Lotan, Shobal, Zibeon, Anah.
local: Xoritɔwo ƒe amegãwoe nye esiawo: Lotan, Sobal, Zibeon, Ana.

eng: He turned to her beside the road and said, Let me sleep with you, because he didnt know she was his daughter-in-law. She said, What will you give me for sleeping with you.
local: Tete wòdze yi egbɔ le mɔa to hegblɔ nɛ be: “Va míyi, maɖamlɔ gbɔwò.” Menya be yeƒe toyɔvi lae o. Nyɔnua biae be: “Nu ka nànam hafi amlɔ gbɔnye?”.

eng: When Joseph reached his brothers, they stripped off Josephs long robe.
local: Esi Yosef va ɖo nɔviawo gbɔ la, wolée, ɖe eƒe awu ʋlaya la le eŋu, eƒe awu ʋlaya si didi, eye abɔ legbee li nɛ la.

eng: Dishon, Ezer, and Dishan. These are the Horite tribal chiefs, listed according to their chiefs in the land of Seir.
local: Dison, Ezer kple Disan. Esiawoe nye amegãwo le Seir-nyigba dzi le woƒe duawo nu.

eng: His master saw that the LORD was with him and that the LORD made everything he did successful.
local: Esi eƒe aƒetɔ la kpɔ be Aƒetɔ li kple Yosef, eye nu sia nu si wòwɔna dzea dzi nɛ la.

eng: He said, I will give you a kid goat from my flock. She said, Only if you give me some deposit, as security to guarantee that you will send it.
local: Yuda ɖo eŋu be: “Malé gbɔ̃tsu ɖeka le nye lãha me aɖo ɖe wò.” Nyɔnua gblɔ be: “Enyo, ekema tsɔ wò nane ɖo awɔba nam, va se ɖe esime nàɖoe ɖem.”.

eng: These are the kings who ruled in the land of Edom before a king ruled over the Israelites.
local: Hafi fiawo nava nɔ Edom-nyigba dzi la, fia siwo ŋkɔ gbɔna la ɖu fia le Edom-nyigba dzi:.

eng: took him, and threw him into the cistern, an empty cistern with no water in it.
local: eye wotsɔe da ɖe do aɖe me; ke doa me ɖi gbɔlo, tsi mele eme o.

eng: Potiphar thought highly of Joseph, and Joseph became his assistant; he appointed Joseph head of his household and put everything he had under Josephs supervision.
local: ekpɔ ŋudzedze le eŋu. Potifar wɔe eƒe subɔla kple eƒemenuwo dzikpɔlae, eye wòtsɔ eƒe nunɔamesiwo katã de esi.

eng: He said, What kind of deposit should I give you? And she said, Your seal, its cord, and the staff in your hand. He gave these to her, slept with her, and she became pregnant by him.
local: Yuda biae be: “Nu ka matsɔ ɖo awɔba na wò?” Eɖo eŋu be: “Wò ŋkɔsigɛ kple eŋuka kpakple atizɔti si le asiwò la.” Yuda tsɔ wo nɛ, eye wòmlɔ egbɔ, eye nyɔnua fɔ fu nɛ.

eng: Bela, Beors son, ruled in Edom; his citys name was Dinhabah.
local: Bela, Beor viŋutsu ɖu fia le Edom, eye eƒe fiadue nye Dinhaba.

eng: When they sat down to eat, they looked up and saw a caravan of Ishmaelites coming from Gilead, with camels carrying sweet resin, medicinal resin, and fragrant resin on their way down to Egypt.
local: Azɔ wobɔbɔ nɔ anyi be yewoaɖu nu, kasia Ismael asitsahawoe nye ekem tso Gilead gbɔna. Wodo agba na woƒe kposɔwo kple aŋe, atikemi kple kotoklobo yina Egipte.

eng: From the time he appointed Joseph head of his household and of everything he had, the LORD blessed the Egyptians household because of Joseph. The LORD blessed everything he had, both in the household and in the field.
local: Tso ɣe ma ɣi dzi la, Aƒetɔ yra Egiptetɔ la ƒe aƒe le Yosef ta. Eyra Potifar ƒe nunɔamesiwo katã le aƒeme kple agbleta siaa.

eng: Then she got up, left, and took off her veil, dressing once again in the clothing she wore as a widow.
local: Tamar yi aƒeme, ɖe motsyɔnu la da ɖi, eye wògatsɔ eƒe ahovɔ ta.

eng: After Bela died, Jobab son of Zerah from Bozrah became king.
local: Esi Bela ku la, Serax viŋutsu, Yobab tso Bozra, ɖu fia ɖe eteƒe.

eng: Judah said to his brothers, What do we gain if we kill our brother and hide his blood?.
local: Tete Yuda gblɔ na nɔviawo be: “Ne míewu míawo ŋutɔ nɔvi le bebeme ko hemia nu ɖe edzi ɖe, viɖe kae le eme na mí?.

eng: So he handed over everything he had to Joseph and didnt pay attention to anything except the food he ate. Now Joseph was well-built and handsome.
local: Potifar tsɔ eƒe nunɔamesiwo katã de Yosef si, eye megatsi dzi le eƒe nunɔamesi aɖeke ŋu o, negbe nu si wòaɖu la ko. Azɔ Yosef dze ɖeka ŋutɔ, eye wònyakpɔ.

eng: Judah sent the kid goat with his neighbor Hirah the Adullamite so he could take back the deposits from the woman, but he couldnt find her.
local: Emegbe Yuda tsɔ gbɔ̃tsu la de xɔlɔ̃a Adulamtɔ la si be netsɔ yi na nyɔnua ne wòaxɔ awɔbanuawo vɛ na ye, gake Xira mekpɔ nyɔnua o.

eng: After Jobab died, Husham from the land of the Temanites became king.
local: Esi Yobab ku la, Xusam tso Temantɔwo ƒe anyigba dzi ɖu fia ɖe eteƒe.

eng: Come on, lets sell him to the Ishmaelites. Lets not harm him because hes our brother; hes family. His brothers agreed.
local: Mina míadzrae na Ismael asitsala siawo boŋ. Ame aɖeke megawɔ nu vevii o, elabe mía nɔvi kpamkpam wònye; ʋu ɖeka me mí katã míedzɔ tso.” Nɔviawo lɔ̃ ɖe edzi.

eng: Some time later, his masters wife became attracted to Joseph and said, Sleep with me.
local: Le ɣeyiɣi aɖewo megbe la, eƒe aƒetɔ srɔ̃ va nɔ ahiãmoe ɖom ɖee, eye wògblɔ na Yosef be: “Va dɔ kplim.”.

eng: He asked the locals of that place, Wheres the consecrated worker who was at Enaim on the road? But they said, Theres no consecrated worker here.
local: Ebia dua me ŋutsuwo be: “Afi ka gbolo si nɔ mɔ to le Enaim le afii la le?” Ke woɖo eŋu be: “Gbolo aɖeke menɔ afi sia o.”.

eng: After Husham died, Hadad, Bedads son who defeated Midian in the countryside of Moab, became king; his citys name was Avith.
local: Esi Xusam ku la, Bedad viŋutsu, Hadad, ɖu fia ɖe eteƒe. Eya nye ame si ɖu Midiantɔwo dzi le Moab-nyigba dzi, eye eƒe fiadue nye Avit.

eng: When some Midianite traders passed by, they pulled Joseph up out of the cistern. They sold him to the Ishmaelites for twenty pieces of silver, and they brought Joseph to Egypt.
local: Ale esi Midian-sitsala aɖewo va yina la, woɖee le doa me hetsɔe dzra, klosaloga bla eve, na Ismaeltɔwo. Tete asitsalaawo kplɔe yi Egipte.

eng: He refused and said to his masters wife, With me here, my master doesnt pay attention to anything in his household; hes put everything he has under my supervision.
local: Yosef gbe hegblɔ na eƒe aƒetɔ srɔ̃ la be: “Kpɔ ɖa, nye aƒetɔ meganya aƒe sia me nu siwo le asinye me la o; etsɔ eƒe nunɔamesiwo katã de asi nam gɔ̃.

eng: So he went back to Judah and said, I couldnt find her. The locals even said, Theres no holy woman here.
local: Tete wòtrɔ yi Yuda gbɔ ɖagblɔ nɛ be: “Nyemekpɔe o. Dua me ŋutsuwo hã be gbolo aɖeke menɔ afi ma o.”.

eng: After Hadad died, Samlah from Masrekah became king.
local: Esi Hadad ku la, Samla tso Masreka, ɖu fia ɖe eteƒe.

eng: When Reuben returned to the cistern and found that Joseph wasnt in it, he tore his clothes.
local: Esi Ruben trɔ va doa gbɔ, eye wòkpɔ be Yosef mele eme o la, edze awu le eɖokui ŋu.

eng: No one is greater than I am in this household, and he hasnt denied me anything except you, since you are his wife. How could I do this terrible thing and sin against God.
local: Mete nanekem le aƒe sia me o, negbe wò dzaa ko, esi nènye srɔ̃a ta. Ale ke mawɔ nu vlo sia gbegbe awɔ nuvɔ̃ ɖe Mawu ŋuti?”.

eng: Judah said, Let her keep everything so we arent laughed at. I did send this kid goat, but you couldnt find her.
local: Yuda ɖo eŋu be: “Nuawo ate ŋu atsi esi faa, ne menye nenem o la, woava ko mí. Kpɔ ɖa, nye ya, meɖo gbɔ̃tsu la ɖee, ke mèkpɔe atsɔe nɛ o.”.

eng: After Samlah died, Shaul from Rehoboth on the river became king.
local: Esi Samla ku la, Saul tso Rexobot le tɔsisia nu, ɖu fia ɖe eteƒe.

eng: Then he returned to his brothers and said, The boys gone! And I—where can I go now.
local: Etrɔ va nɔviawo gbɔ eye wògblɔ be: “Ɖevia megali o! Nu ka wɔ ge mala?”.

eng: Every single day she tried to convince him, but he wouldnt agree to sleep with her or even to be with her.
local: Togbɔ be nyɔnua nɔ fu ɖem na Yosef kple nya sia gbe sia gbe hã la, Yosef melɔ̃ kpɔ be yeamlɔ egbɔ, ade asi eŋu o.

eng: About three months later, Judah was told, Your daughter-in-law Tamar has become a prostitute and is now pregnant because of it. And Judah said, Bring her out so that she may be burned.
local: Le ɣleti etɔ̃ lɔƒo megbe la, wova gblɔ na Yuda be: “Toyɔviwò Tamar wɔ ahasi hefɔ fu ɖe eme.” Tete Yuda ɖe gbe be: “Mikplɔe do goe ne miatɔ dzoe!”.

eng: After Shaul died, Baal-hanan, Achbors son, became king.
local: Esi Saul ku la, Axbor viŋutsu Baal-Xanan, ɖu fia ɖe eteƒe.

eng: His brothers took Josephs robe, slaughtered a male goat, and dipped the robe in the blood.
local: Emegbe wowu gbɔ̃tsu tsɔ Yosef ƒe awu la nyrɔ ɖe eƒe ʋu me.

eng: One day when Joseph arrived at the house to do his work, none of the households men were there.
local: Gbe ɖeka Yosef gava aƒea me, eƒe dɔ wɔ ge. Aƒea me tɔ bubu aɖeke meli o.

eng: When she was brought out, she sent this message to her father-in-law, Im pregnant by the man who owns these things. See if you recognize whose seal, cord, and staff these are.
local: Ke esi wokplɔ Tamar yinae la, edɔ ame ɖe toa gbɔ be: “Ŋutsu si do fum la ƒe nuwoe nye esiawo. Taflatsɛ, lé ŋku ɖe ŋkɔsigɛa, eŋukaa kple atizɔti la ŋu nyuie be ame ka tɔwoe mahã.”.

eng: After Baal-hanan, Achbors son, died, Hadar became king; his citys name was Pau and his wifes name was Mehetabel the daughter of Matred and granddaughter of Me-zahab.
local: Esi Baal-Xanan, Axbor viŋutsu ku la, Hadar ɖu fia ɖe eteƒe. Eƒe fiadue nye Pagu, eye srɔ̃a ŋkɔe nye Mehetabeel, Matred vinyɔnu kple Mesahab tɔgbuiyɔvi.

eng: They took the long robe, brought it to their father, and said, We found this. See if its your sons robe or not.
local: Wotsɔ awu ʋlaya la ɖo ɖe wo tɔ́, eye wogblɔ be: “Míefɔ awu ʋlaya sia. Lé ŋku ɖe eŋu be viwò tɔe hã.”.

eng: She grabbed his garment, saying, Lie down with me. But he left his garment in her hands and ran outside.
local: Tete nyɔnua lé Yosef ƒe awu, eye wògblɔ nɛ be: “Va mímlɔ anyi!” Ke Yosef gblẽ awua ɖe nyɔnua si me, hesí do go le aƒea me.

eng: Judah recognized them and said, Shes more righteous than I am, because I didnt allow her to marry my son Shelah. Judah never knew her intimately again.
local: Tete Yuda kpɔ nuawo, eye wògblɔ be: “Eƒe nya dzɔ wu tɔnye elabe nyee metsɔe na vinye Sela wòɖe o.” Tso ema dzi la, megade asi eŋu kpɔ o.

eng: These are the names of Esaus tribal chiefs according to their families, their locations, and their names: Chief Timna, Chief Alvah, Chief Jetheth.
local: Esiawo nye amegã siwo dzɔ tso Esau me la ƒe ŋkɔwo le woƒe ƒomewo, nɔƒewo kple ŋkɔwo nu: Timna, Alva, Yetet.

eng: He recognized it and said, Its my sons robe! A wild animal has devoured him. Joseph must have been torn to pieces.
local: Tɔ́a kpɔ awua dze sii eye wògblɔ be: “Ẽ, vinye ƒe awu ʋlaya ye! Lã wɔadã aɖe lée ɖu! Ɖikeke aɖeke mele eme o; evuvu Yosef keŋkeŋ!”.

eng: When she realized that he had left his garment in her hands and run outside.
local: Esi wòkpɔ be Yosef gblẽ eƒe awu ɖe ye si, sí do go la.

eng: When she gave birth, she discovered she had twins in her womb.
local: Eƒe vidziɣi ɖo, kasia wokpɔ be eve dzi ge wòala.

eng: Chief Oholibamah, Chief Elah, Chief Pinon.
local: Oholibama, Ela, Pinon.

eng: Some time later, both the wine steward and the baker for Egypts king offended their master, the king of Egypt.
local: Le ɣeyiɣi aɖe megbe la, Egipte-fia ƒe ahakula kple eƒe aboloƒola da vo ɖe woƒe aƒetɔ fia la ŋu.

eng: Then Jacob tore his clothes, put a simple mourning cloth around his waist, and mourned for his son for many days.
local: Tete Yakob dze awu le eɖokui ŋu, sa akpanya ɖe ali, eye wòfa via ŋkeke geɖe.

eng: she summoned the men of her house and said to them, Look, my husband brought us a Hebrew to ridicule us. He came to me to lie down with me, but I screamed.
local: eyɔ aƒea me tɔwo vɛ, eye wògblɔ na wo be: “Mikpɔ ɖa ko. Srɔ̃nye kplɔ Hebri-ŋutsu sia va de mía dome be wòaɖi gbɔ mí. Eva gbɔnye be yeadɔ kplim, ke medo ɣli sesĩe!.

eng: At birth, one boy put out his hand, and the midwife took it and tied a red thread on his hand, saying, This one came out first.
local: Esime wòle ku lém la, ɖeviawo dometɔ ɖeka do asi ɖa. Tete vixela la sa ka dzĩ ɖe asi ŋu nɛ hegblɔ be: “Esiae tre dodo.”.

eng: Chief Kenaz, Chief Teman, Chief Mibzar.
local: Kenaz, Teman, Mibzar.

eng: Pharaoh was angry with his two officers, the chief wine steward and the chief baker.
local: Fia la do dɔmedzoe ɖe eŋume eveawo ŋuti.

eng: All of his sons and daughters got up to comfort him, but he refused to be comforted, telling them, Ill go to my grave mourning for my son. And Josephs father wept for him.
local: Via ŋutsuwo kple via nyɔnuwo fa akɔ nɛ do kpoe. Egblɔ na wo be: “Matsɔ konyifafa ayi vinye gbɔe le tsiẽƒe boŋ.” Ale Yosef tɔ́ fae vevie.

eng: As soon as he pulled his hand back, his brother came out, and she said, Youve burst out on your own. So he was named Perez.
local: Gake ɖevi sia he asi yi eme, eye wodzi nɔvia gbã. Vixela la gblɔ na esi wodzi gbã la be: “E! Ale ke nèdze mɔ na ɖokuiwò ale?” Eya ta wòna ŋkɔe be Perez.

eng: When he heard me raise my voice and scream, he left his garment with me and ran outside.
local: Esi wòkpɔ be mele ɣli dom le avi fam sesĩe la, egblẽ eƒe awu ɖe gbɔnye, eye wòsí do go le aƒea me.”.

eng: Chief Magdiel, and Chief Iram. These are Edoms tribal chiefs according to their settlements in the land they possessed. This is Esau, the ancestor of the Edomites.
local: Magdiel kple Iram. Ame siawoe nye Edom-megãwo le woƒe nɔƒewo kple woawo ŋutɔ ƒe anyigbawo nu. Esau ye nye Edomtɔwo tɔgbui.

eng: and he put them under arrest with the commander of the royal guard in the same jail where Joseph was imprisoned.
local: eye wòtsɔ wo de gaxɔ me le fiaŋumewo ƒe amegã ƒe aƒeme; Yosef hã le gaxɔ me le afi ma.

eng: Afterward, his brother with the red thread on his hand came out, and he was named Zerah.
local: Emegbe nɔvia si ŋu ka dzĩ le la hã va do; eya ta wòna ŋkɔe be Serax.

eng: She kept his garment with her until Josephs master came home.
local: Etsɔ awu la da ɖe eɖokui gbɔ va se ɖe esime Yosef ƒe aƒetɔ gbɔ.

eng: Meanwhile the Midianites had sold Joseph to the Egyptians, to Potiphar, Pharaohs chief officer, commander of the royal guard.
local: Ke Midiantɔwo ɖadzra Yosef le Egipte na Potifar, Egipte Fiagã ƒe aƒedzikpɔla kple eŋutimewo ƒe amegã.

eng: The commander of the royal guard assigned Joseph to assist them. After they had been under arrest for some time.
local: Fiaŋumewo ƒe amegã la tsɔ wo de Yosef si, be wòakpɔ wo dzi. Wonɔ gaxɔa me eteƒe didi.

eng: and she told him the same thing: The Hebrew slave whom you brought to us, to ridicule me, came to me.
local: Egblɔ nya mawo ke nɛ be: “Kpɔ ɖa, Hebrivi kluvi ma, si nèkplɔ va mía dome la va gbɔnye be yeaɖi gbɔm.

eng: both of them—the wine steward and the baker for Egypts king who were imprisoned in the jail—had dreams one night, and each mans dream had its own meaning.
local: Gbe ɖeka ƒe zã me la, ahakula la kple aboloƒola la ku drɔ̃e wotɔxɛwotɔxɛe, eye ɖe sia ɖe tɔ ƒe gɔmeɖeɖe to vovo.

eng: but when I raised my voice and screamed, he left his garment with me and ran outside.
local: Esi medo ɣli ɖe eta la, esí do go le aƒea me gblẽ eƒe awu ɖe gbɔnye!”.

eng: When Joseph met them in the morning, he saw that they were upset.
local: Esi Yosef va wo gbɔ le ŋdi me la, ekpɔ be woli kpoo, nɔ nu xam.

eng: Two years later, Pharaoh dreamed that he was standing near the Nile.
local: Le ƒe eve megbe la, Egipte Fiagã ku drɔ̃e be yenɔ tsitre ɖe Nil-tɔsisi to.

eng: When Josephs master heard the thing that his wife told him, This is what your servant did to me, he was incensed.
local: Esi Yosef ƒe aƒetɔ se srɔ̃a ƒe nya be ale kple ale eƒe kluvi wɔ ɖe ye ŋu la, edo dɔmedzoe ŋutɔ.

eng: He asked the officers of Pharaoh who were under arrest with him in his masters house, Why do you look so distressed today.
local: Ebia wo be: “Ale ke wɔ egbe mielé blanui li kpoo ale?”.

eng: In front of him, seven healthy-looking, fattened cows climbed up out of the Nile and grazed on the reeds.
local: eye yekpɔ nyinɔ dami adrẽ siwo nyakpɔ ŋutɔ la wodo go tso tɔsisia me va le gbe ɖum le avawo me.

eng: Josephs master took him and threw him in jail, the place where the kings prisoners were held. While he was in jail.
local: Ale elé Yosef de gaxɔ si me fia ƒe gamenɔlawo nɔna la me.

eng: They answered, Weve both had dreams, but theres no one to interpret them. Joseph said to them, Dont interpretations belong to God? Describe your dreams to me.
local: Woɖo eŋu nɛ be: “Míeku drɔ̃e, gake ame meli aɖe egɔme na mí o.” Tete Yosef gblɔ be: “Menye Mawue naa drɔ̃e gɔmeɖeɖe oa? Milĩi nam.”.

eng: Just then, seven other cows, terrible-looking and scrawny, climbed up out of the Nile after them and stood beside them on the bank of the Nile.
local: Emegbe nyinɔ adrẽ bubuwo hã do va; woawo ya ɖi ku glàŋuu. Wova le tsitre ɖe nyinɔ kemɛawo gbɔ le tɔsisia to.

eng: The chief wine steward described his dream to Joseph: In my dream there was a vine right in front of me.
local: Ale ahakulawo ƒe amegã lĩ eƒe drɔ̃e na Yosef be: “Le nye drɔ̃ea me la, mekpɔ wainka aɖe le ŋkunye me.

eng: the LORD was with Joseph and remained loyal to him. He caused the jails commander to think highly of Joseph.
local: Ke Aƒetɔ nɔ kple Yosef, eye wòve enu hena be gaxɔdzikpɔla la kpɔ ŋudzedze le eŋu.

eng: The terrible-looking, scrawny cows devoured the seven healthy-looking, fattened cows. Then Pharaoh woke up.
local: Kasia ɖeko nyinɔ ɖikuawo lé damitɔwo mi! Tete fia la nyɔ.

eng: and on the vine were three branches. When it budded, its blossoms appeared, and its clusters ripened into grapes.
local: alɔ etɔ̃ le eŋu. Kaka aŋgbawo nado ko la, eƒo se, eye wòtse heɖi.

eng: The jails commander put all of the prisoners in the jail under Josephs supervision, and he was the one who determined everything that happened there.
local: Etsɔ Yosef ɖo gaxɔmenɔlawo katã nu, eye eyae wɔa ɖoɖo ɖe nu sia nu si wowɔna le gaxɔa me la ŋu.

eng: He went back to sleep and had a second dream, in which seven ears of grain, full and healthy, grew on a single stalk.
local: Egayi alɔ̃ me, eye wòku drɔ̃e zi evelia. Ekpɔ lutsetse adrẽ siwo ʋã nyuie, eye wonyakpɔ la le luti ɖeka ŋu.

eng: Pharaohs cup was in my hand, so I took the grapes, crushed them into Pharaohs cup, and put the cup in Pharaohs hand.
local: Fia ƒe kplu la nɔ asinye. Ale megbe kutsetseawo, fiãe ɖe kplu la me, hetsɔe de fia si.”.

eng: The jails commander paid no attention to anything under Josephs supervision, because the LORD was with him and made everything he did successful.
local: Gaxɔdzikpɔla la megatsia dzi le nu siwo le Yosef ƒe dzikpɔkpɔ te la ŋu o, elabe Aƒetɔ li kple Yosef, eye wòna nu sia nu si Yosef wɔna la dzea edzi.

eng: Just then, seven ears of grain, scrawny and scorched by the east wind, sprouted after them.
local: Emegbe lutsetse adrẽ siwo ɣedzeƒeya lé, eye wodze heteku la hã do.

eng: Joseph said to him, This is the dreams interpretation: The three branches are three days.
local: Tete Yosef gblɔ nɛ be: “Drɔ̃e la gɔmee nye esi. Alɔ etɔ̃awo nye ŋkeke etɔ̃.

eng: and the scrawny ears swallowed up the full and well-formed ears. Then Pharaoh woke up and realized it was a dream.
local: Kasia lutsetse míamiawo lé ʋaʋãtɔwo mi. Enyɔ, eye wòkpɔ be drɔ̃e kum yenɔ.

eng: After three days, Pharaoh will give you an audience and return you to your position. You will put Pharaohs cup in his hand, just the way things were before when you were his wine steward.
local: Etsɔ manɔmee la, fia la ana nàdo le gaxɔa me, eye wòagatsɔ wò aɖo wò dɔ nu, nàgaku aha nɛ abe tsã ene.

eng: In the morning, he was disturbed and summoned all of Egypts religious experts and all of its advisors. Pharaoh described his dreams to them, but they couldnt interpret them for Pharaoh.
local: Esi ŋu ke la, Egipte Fiagã la tɔtɔ, ale wòdɔ woyi ɖayɔ afakalawo kple nunyala siwo katã le Egipte-dukɔa me la vɛ. Elĩ drɔ̃eawo na wo, ke wo dometɔ aɖeke mete ŋu ɖe ɖeke gɔme nɛ o.

eng: But please, remember me when you are doing well and be loyal to me. Put in a good word for me to Pharaoh, so he sets me free from this prison.
local: Ke ne eme va nyo na wò la, nàɖo ŋku dzinye. Meɖe kuku, ve nunye nàɖo ŋku dzinye na Egipte Fiagã la be wòaɖem le afi sia.

eng: Then the chief wine steward spoke to Pharaoh: Today Ive just remembered my mistake.
local: Tete ahakulawo ƒe amegã la gblɔ na fia la be: “Meɖo ŋku vodada aɖe si mewɔ la dzi egbe.

eng: I was stolen from the land of the Hebrews, and here too Ive done nothing to be thrown into this dungeon.
local: Le nyateƒe me la, ɖe wofim dzoe le Hebritɔwo ƒe anyigba dzi, eye le afii gɔ̃ hã la, nyemewɔ naneke hafi wolém de gaxɔ me o.”.

eng: Pharaoh was angry with his servants and put me and the chief baker under arrest with the commander of the royal guard.
local: Esi nèdo dɔmedzoe ɖe nye kple aboloƒolawo ƒe amegã ŋu, eye nèna wolé mí de gaxɔ me le fiaŋumewo ƒe amegã ƒeme la.

eng: When the chief baker saw that the interpretation was favorable, he said to Joseph, It was the same for me. In my dream, there were three baskets of white bread on my head.
local: Esi aboloƒolawo ƒe amegã kpɔ be drɔ̃e la gɔme nyo la, egblɔ na Yosef be: “Nye hã meku drɔ̃e: metsɔ abolo kusi etɔ̃ ɖe ta.

eng: We both dreamed one night, he and I, and each of our dreams had its own interpretation.
local: nye kplii míeku drɔ̃e vovovo le zã ɖeka ma me. Drɔ̃eawo gɔmeɖeɖe le vovovo.

eng: In the basket on top there were baked goods for Pharaohs food, but birds were eating them out of the basket on my head.
local: Egipte Fiagã la ƒe abolo mème vovovowo le kusi edzitɔ me, ke xeviwo va nɔ wo ɖum le kusia me le tanye.”.

eng: A young Hebrew man, a servant of the commander of the royal guard, was with us. We described our dreams to him, and he interpreted our dreams for us, giving us an interpretation for each dream.
local: Hebri-ɖekakpui aɖe si nye fiaŋumewo ƒe amegã ƒe kluvi la nɔ mía gbɔ le afi ma. Míelĩ míaƒe drɔ̃eawo nɛ, eye wòɖe wo gɔme na mí.

eng: Joseph responded, This is the dreams interpretation: The three baskets are three days.
local: Yosef ɖo eŋu nɛ be: “Wò drɔ̃e la gɔmee nye esi. Kusi etɔ̃awo nye ŋkeke etɔ̃.

eng: His interpretations came true exactly: Pharaoh restored me to my position but hanged him.
local: Ale si wòɖe drɔ̃eawo gɔme na mí la, nenema tututu wova emee. Ègatsɔm ɖo nye dɔ nu, ke èna wode ka ve na aboloƒolawo ƒe amegã ya.”.

eng: After three days, Pharaoh will give you an audience and will hang you from a tree where birds will peck your flesh from you.
local: Etsɔ manɔmee la, fia ana woatso ta le nuwò, ahe wò kukua ɖe ati ŋu, eye xeviwo ava ɖu wò ŋutilã.”.

eng: So Pharaoh summoned Joseph, and they quickly brought him from the dungeon. He shaved, changed clothes, and appeared before Pharaoh.
local: Tete Egipte Fiagã la dɔ be woayɔ Yosef vɛ, eye woɖee le gaxɔa me enumake. Elũ ge, ɖɔli eƒe awuwo, eye wòva Fiagã la gbɔ.

eng: When Jacob learned that there was grain in Egypt, he said to his sons, Why are you staring blankly at each other?.
local: Esi Yakob se be bli le Egipte la, egblɔ na viawo be: “Ale ke wɔ miele afii le mia nɔewo ƒe mo kpɔm?.

eng: The third day was Pharaohs birthday, and he gave a party for all of his servants. Before all of his servants, he gave an audience to the chief wine steward and the chief baker.
local: Le ŋkeke etɔ̃a gbe la, fia la ɖu eƒe dzigbe. Ekpe eƒe dɔwɔlawo katã va nuɖuƒe, eye wòna woɖe ahakulawo ƒe amegã kple aboloƒolawo ƒe amegã le gaxɔa me. Wokplɔ wo va eya kple eƒe dɔwɔlawo gbɔe.

eng: Pharaoh said to Joseph, I had a dream, but no one could interpret it. Then I heard that when you hear a dream, you can interpret it.
local: Egipte Fiagã la gblɔ nɛ be: “Meku drɔ̃e, ame aɖeke mete ŋu ɖe egɔme nam o. Ke mese le ŋuwò be ne wolĩ drɔ̃e na wò la, ètea ŋu ɖea egɔme.”.

eng: Ive just heard that theres grain in Egypt. Go down there and buy some for us so that we can survive and not starve to death.
local: Mese be bli le Egipte, eya ta miyi ɖaƒle ɖe vɛ ne dɔ nagawu mí míaku o!”.

eng: He returned the chief wine steward to his position, and he placed the cup in Pharaohs hand.
local: Egatsɔ ahakulawo ƒe amegã la ɖo eƒe dɔ nu wògakua aha nɛ.

eng: Joseph answered Pharaoh, Its not me. God will give Pharaoh a favorable response.
local: Yosef ɖo eŋu be: “Mawu aɖe drɔ̃ea gɔme na fia wòadze eŋu; menye nye o.”.

eng: So Josephs ten brothers went down to buy grain in Egypt.
local: Ale Yosef nɔviwo dometɔ ewo dzra ɖo be yewoayi Egipte aɖaƒle blia.

eng: But the chief baker he hanged, just as Joseph had said would happen when he interpreted their dreams for them.
local: ke ena wode ka ve na aboloƒolawo ƒe amegã ya, abe ale si Yosef ɖe drɔ̃eawo gɔme na wo la ene.

eng: The famine was severe in the land.
local: Dɔwuame la nu va sẽ ɖe dzi ŋutɔ le anyigba la dzi.

eng: So Pharaoh said to Joseph, In my dream I was standing on the bank of the Nile.
local: Fiagã la lĩ drɔ̃ea be: “Meku drɔ̃e be mele tsitre ɖe Nil-tɔsisi to.

eng: However, Jacob didnt send Josephs brother Benjamin along with his brothers because he thought something bad might happen to him.
local: Gake Yakob meɖe mɔ na Benyamin, Yosef dadavi, ya be neyi kple wo o, elabe evɔ̃ be nu vevi ava wɔe.

eng: But the chief wine steward didnt remember Joseph; he forgot all about him.
local: Azɔ ahakulawo ƒe amegã la meɖo ŋku Yosef dzi o; eŋlɔe be.

eng: and when they had eaten all the grain that they brought from Egypt, their father said to them, Go back and buy us a little food.
local: Yakob kple eƒe ƒomea ɖu bli si woƒle tso Egipte vɛ la vɔ, ale Yakob gblɔ na viawo be: “Migayi ne miaɖaƒle nuɖuɖu vi aɖe vɛ na mí.”.

eng: In front of me, seven fattened, stout cows climbed up out of the Nile and grazed on the reeds.
local: Kasia mekpɔ nyinɔ dami adrẽ siwo nyakpɔ ŋutɔ la do tso tɔsisi la me va le gbe ɖum.

eng: Israels sons came to buy grain with others who also came since the famine had spread to the land of Canaan.
local: Tete Israelviwo va Egipte bli ƒle ge, abe ame bubuwo ene, elabe dɔ to le Kanaan-nyigba dzi.

eng: Judah said to him, The man was absolutely serious when he said, You may not see me again without your brother with you.
local: Yuda gblɔ nɛ be: “Ŋutsua te gbe ɖe edzi na mí vevie be, ne míekplɔ mía nɔvia vɛ o la, mígado ɖe ye ŋkume o.

eng: Just then, seven other cows, weak and frail and thin, climbed up after them. Ive never seen such awful cows in all the land of Egypt.
local: Woawo yome la, nyinɔ bubu adrẽ siwo ɖi ku glàŋuu la hã do. Nyemekpɔ nyinɔ siwo vlo abe emawo tɔgbe ene le Egipte-nyigba blibo la dzi kpɔ o.

eng: As for Joseph, he was the lands governor, and he was the one selling grain to all the lands people. When Josephs brothers arrived, they bowed down to him, their faces to the ground.
local: Le ɣe ma ɣi me la, Yosef nye mɔ́mefia le dukɔa me, eye eyae dzraa bli na ame siwo katã tso xexea me godoo va la. Yosef nɔviwo va, eye wodze klo de ta agu nɛ.

eng: If you agree to send our brother with us, then we will go down and buy you food.
local: Ne èlɔ̃ be mía nɔvia neyi kpli mí la, míayi aɖaƒle blia vɛ.

eng: Then the thin, frail cows devoured the first seven, fattened cows.
local: Enumake nyinɔ ɖiku vloawo lé nyinɔ dami gbãtɔ adrẽawo mi.

eng: When Joseph saw his brothers, he recognized them, but he acted like he didnt know them. He spoke to them with a harsh tone and said, Where have you come from? And they said, From the land of Canaan to buy food.
local: Esi Yosef kpɔ nɔviawo la, edze si wo, gake ewɔ ɖe wo ŋu abe ɖe menya wo kura o ene. Etɔ gbe wo be: “Afi ka mietso?” Woɖo eŋu be: “Míetso Kanaan-nyigba dzi, va bli ƒle ge.”.

eng: But if you dont agree to send him, then we cant go down because the man said to us, You may not see me again without your brother with you.
local: Ke ne mèlɔ̃ be neyi kpli mí o la, míayi o. Elabe ŋutsua te gbe ɖe edzi na mí be, ne míekplɔ mía nɔvia vɛ o la, mígado ɖe ye ŋkume o.”.

eng: But after they swallowed them whole, no one would have known it. They looked just as bad as they had before. Then I woke up.
local: Gake esi womi wo hã la, ɖeko wogale abe ɖe womemi naneke o ene; woƒe dzedzeme gavlo abe tsã ene. Kasia menyɔ.

eng: Joseph recognized his brothers, but they didnt recognize him.
local: Togbɔ be Yosef dze si nɔviawo hã la, woawo ya medze sii o.

eng: Israel said, Why have you caused me such pain by telling the man you had another brother.
local: Israel gblɔ na wo be: “Miewɔ nu vevim. Nu ka ta mieʋu nya me na ŋutsua be nɔvi gale yewo si?”.

eng: I went to sleep again and saw in my dream seven full and healthy ears of grain growing on one stalk.
local: Emegbe megaku drɔ̃e bubu. Mekpɔ lutsetse adrẽ siwo ʋã nyuie, eye wonyakpɔ la le luti ɖeka ŋu.

eng: Joseph remembered the dreams he had dreamed about them, and said to them, You are spies. Youve come to look for the countrys weaknesses.
local: Eɖo ŋku drɔ̃e si wòku le wo ŋu la dzi, eye wògblɔ na wo be: “Ŋkutsalawo mienye. Ɖe mieva be yewoadi mɔnu le míaƒe anyigba la ŋu.”.

eng: They said, The man asked us pointedly about our family: Is your father still alive? Do you have a brother? So we told him just what weve said. How were we to know hed say, Bring your brother down here.
local: Woɖo eŋu be: “Ŋutsua bia gbe mí tsitotsito tso míawo ŋutɔ kple míaƒe ƒome la ŋu. Ebia be, ‘Mia tɔ́ gale agbea? Nɔvi bubu gale mia sia?’ Biabia siawo ŋue míeɖo nɛ. Ale ke míawɔ anya be agblɔ na mí be míkplɔ mía nɔvia vɛ?”.

eng: Just then, seven hard and thin ears of grain, scorched by the east wind, sprouted after them.
local: Le woawo yome la, lutsetse adrẽ siwo ɣedzeƒeya lé womía, eye wotsi to nu la do.

eng: They said to him, No, Master. Your servants have just come to buy food.
local: Woɖo eŋu be: “Gbeɖe, amegã, wò dɔlawo míenye; nuɖuɖu ko ƒle ge míeva.

eng: Judah said to his father Israel, Send the young man with me. Lets get ready to leave so that we can stay alive and not die—we, you, and our children.
local: Yuda gblɔ na tɔ́a be: “Ɖe asi le ɖevia ŋu nam ne makplɔe yii ne wò ŋutɔ, míawo kple mía viwo nagaku o.

eng: We are all sons of one man. We are honest men. Your servants arent spies.
local: Ŋutsu ɖeka viwo mí katã míenye. Menye ŋkutsalawo míenye o, amegã, ke boŋ nuteƒewɔlawo míenye.”.

eng: and the thin ears swallowed up the healthy ears. I told the religious experts, but they couldnt explain it to me.
local: Kasia lutsetse míamiawo lé ʋaʋãtɔwo mi. Melĩ drɔ̃e siawo na afakalawo, gake wo dometɔ aɖeke mete ŋu ɖe egɔme nam o.”.

eng: I will guarantee his safety; you can hold me responsible. If I dont bring him back to you and place him here in front of you, it will be my fault forever.
local: Eƒe nya ɖe sia ɖe kpɔ ŋkunye me; ele asinye me. Ne nyemete ŋu kplɔe gbɔe na wò o la, bu fɔm tegbee.

eng: Joseph said to Pharaoh, Pharaoh has actually had one dream. God has announced to Pharaoh what he is about to do.
local: Tete Yosef gblɔ na Fiagã la be: “Fiagã bubutɔ, gɔmeɖeɖe ɖekae li na wò drɔ̃e eveawo. Nu si Mawu ɖo be yeawɔ la, eyae wòɖe fia wò.

eng: He said to them, No. Youve come to look for the countrys weaknesses.
local: Yosef ɖo eŋu na wo be: “Kpao, mɔnu di ge mieva le míaƒe anyigba la ŋu!”.

eng: If we hadnt waited so long, we wouldve returned twice by now.
local: Ne menye ɖe míele hehem ɖe megbe o la, anye ne míede gbɔ zi eve xoxo.”.

eng: The seven healthy cows are seven years, and the seven healthy ears of grain are seven years. Its actually one dream.
local: Nyinɔ dami adrẽawo nye adrẽ, eye lutsetse ʋaʋã adrẽawo hã nye ƒè adrẽ. Drɔ̃e eveawo sɔ.

eng: They said, We, your servants, are twelve brothers, sons of one man in the land of Canaan. The youngest is now with our father, but one is gone.
local: Woxɔ edzi be: “Nɔvi wui eve míenye henye ŋutsu ɖeka viwo le Kanaan-nyigba dzi. Mía nɔvi ɖeka megali o, eye mía nɔvi ɖevitɔ ya tsi mía tɔ́a gbɔ.”.

eng: Their father Israel said to them, If it has to be, then do this. Take in your bags some of the lands choice produce, and bring it down to the man as a gift: a little medicinal resin, a little honey, gum, resin, pistachios, and almonds.
local: Tete wo tɔ́a, Israel, gblɔ na wo be: “Ekema mitsɔ mía de nu nyui aɖewo ɖe asi yi ɖana ŋutsua. Mitsɔ atikemi, anyitsi vi aɖe, aŋe, kotoklobo, ayedee kple yevuzi.

eng: The seven thin and frail cows, climbing up after them, are seven years. The seven thin ears of grain, scorched by the east wind, are seven years of famine.
local: Nyinɔ ɖiku vlo adrẽ siwo do evelia la nye ƒè adrẽ. Lutsetse adrẽ siwo ɣedzeƒeya lé wotsi to nu la hã nye ƒè adrẽ, siwo me dɔ ato le.

eng: Joseph said to them, Its just as Ive said to you. You are spies!.
local: Yosef ɖo eŋu na wo be: “Eya ko megblɔ be ŋkutsalawo mienye la!.

eng: Take twice as much silver with you, and take back the silver returned in the top of your sacks. It might have been a mistake.
local: Mitsɔ nuƒlega ƒe teƒe eve kple ga si wogbugbɔ de kotokuawo me la hã ɖe asi. Ɖewohĩ anye vodadae wowɔ.

eng: Its just as I told Pharaoh: God has shown Pharaoh what he is about to do.
local: Abe ale si megblɔ na wò va yi ene la, nu si wɔ ge Mawu le lae wòɖe fia wò.

eng: But here is how to prove yourselves: As Pharaoh lives, you wont leave here until your youngest brother arrives.
local: Mele mia do ge kpɔ ale: Metà Egipte Fiagã la! Miele dzodzo ge le afii akpɔ o, negbe ɖeko mia nɔvi ɖevitɔ la va afii hafi!.

eng: And take your brother, get ready, and go back to the man.
local: Azɔ mikplɔ mia nɔvia ne miayi.

eng: Seven years of great abundance are now coming throughout the entire land of Egypt.
local: Ƒè adrẽ gbɔna, eye nuɖuɖu abɔ ŋutɔ ɖe Egipte-nyigba blibo la dzi.

eng: Send one of you to get your brother, but the rest of you will stay in prison. We will find out if your words are true. If not, as Pharaoh lives, you are certainly spies.
local: Midɔ mia dometɔ ɖeka ɖa ne wòaɖakplɔe vɛ. Ke mi ame mamlɛawo ya la, mianɔ ga me va se ɖe esime manya kɔtɛe be nyateƒe tom miele. Evɔ ne menye nenem o la, metà fia, ŋkutsalawo mienye.”.

eng: May God Almighty make the man compassionate toward you so that he may send back your other brother and Benjamin with you. But me, if Im left childless, then Im left childless.
local: Mawu Ŋusẽkatãtɔ la neve mia nu le ŋutsua gbɔ ale be wòaɖe asi le mia nɔvia kple Benyamin ŋuti na mi. Ke nye ya la, ne vi gaku nam la, eku nam.”.

eng: After them, seven years of famine will appear, and all of the abundance in the land of Egypt will be forgotten. The famine will devastate the land.
local: Emegbe dɔ ato ƒè adrẽ, eye amewo aŋlɔ ale si nuɖuɖu bɔ ɖe Egipte-nyigba dzi kpɔ la be keŋ, elabe dɔŋɔli la agblẽ nu le anyigba la ŋu vevie.

eng: He put them all in prison for three days.
local: Yosef lé wo de ga me ŋkeke etɔ̃.

eng: So the men took this gift. They took twice as much silver with them, together with Benjamin. They left, traveled down to Egypt, and received an audience with Joseph.
local: Ale nɔviŋutsuawo tsɔ nunanaawo kple ga homea ƒe teƒe eve ɖe asi, kplɔ Benyamin hedze mɔ yi Egipte. Wova ɖo Yosef gbɔ.

eng: On the third day, Joseph said to them, Do this and you will live, for Im a God-fearing man.
local: Le ŋkeke etɔ̃a gbe la, Yosef gblɔ na wo be: “Mawuvɔ̃la menye, eya ta ne miedi be yewoatsi agbe la, miwɔ nya si gblɔ ge mala na mi la dzi.

eng: No one will remember the abundance in the land because the famine that follows will be so very severe.
local: Dɔ la nu asẽ ale gbegbe be amewo maganya kura be nuɖuɖu bɔ ɖe anyigba la dzi kpɔ o.

eng: When Joseph saw Benjamin with them, he said to the manager of his household, Bring the men to the house and slaughter an animal and prepare it because the men will have dinner with me at noon.
local: Esi Yosef kpɔ be Benyamin le wo dome la, egblɔ na eƒe aƒedzikpɔla be: “Kplɔ ŋutsu siawo yi nye aƒemee. Wole nu ɖu ge kplim egbe ŋdɔ, eya ta wu lã nàɖa nu na mí.”.

eng: The dream occurred to Pharaoh twice because God has determined to do it, and God will make it happen soon.
local: Azɔ la, Mawu na nèku drɔ̃e la zi eve elabe eɖo nu si wɔ ge wòle la kplikpaa; ele nàna ge wòava eme kaba.

eng: If you are honest men, let one of your brothers stay in prison, and the rest of you, go, take grain back to those in your households who are hungry.
local: Nenye nyateƒe tom miele la, ekema mina mia nɔvi ɖeka natsi ga me le afii ne miadzo. Mi ame mamlɛawo mitsɔ blia yi na mia tɔwo le aƒea ne woatsɔ lé dɔwuame la nu.

eng: The man did as Joseph told him and brought the men to Josephs house.
local: Aƒedzikpɔla la wɔ Yosef ƒe gbe dzi, eye wòkplɔ ŋutsuawo yi Yosef ƒemee.

eng: Now Pharaoh should find an intelligent, wise man and give him authority over the land of Egypt.
local: “Eya ta azɔ la, di nunyala aɖaŋudzela aɖe ɖo Egipte-nyigba la nu.

eng: But bring your youngest brother back to me so that your words will prove true and you wont die. So they prepared to do this.
local: Ne mieyi la, mikplɔ mia nɔvi ɖevitɔ la vɛ nam bene manya be nyateƒe tom miele, ne nyemagawu mi o.” Wolɔ̃ ɖe nya sia dzi.

eng: When they were brought to Josephs house, the men were frightened and said, Weve been brought here because of the silver put back in our sacks on our first trip so he can overpower us, capture us, make slaves of us, and take our donkeys.
local: Vɔvɔ̃ ɖo wo elabe wokplɔ wo yi Yosef ƒeme, eye wogblɔ be: “Ga si wogbugbɔ de míaƒe kotokuawo me esi míeva zi gbãtɔ la tae wokplɔ mí vɛ ɖo. Wole mía dzi dze ge, axɔ míaƒe tedziwo le mía si sesẽe, eye woawɔ mí kluviwoe.”.

eng: Then Pharaoh should appoint administrators over the land and take one-fifth of all the produce of the land of Egypt during the seven years of abundance.
local: Di dɔdzikpɔlawo hã ɖo anyigba la dzi kaba, ale be ne nuɖuɖu bɔ fũu le ƒè adrẽ me la, woadzɔ nuawo ƒe akpa atɔ̃lia le dukɔa me.

eng: The brothers said to each other, We are clearly guilty for what we did to our brother when we saw his life in danger and when he begged us for mercy, but we didnt listen. Thats why were in this danger now.
local: eye wogblɔ na wo nɔewo be: “Le nyateƒe me la, nu si míewɔ mía nɔvia tae míeɖo xaxa sia me ɖo. Togbɔ be míekpɔ be dzidzi ƒo mía nɔvia, eye wòɖe kuku na mí vevie ʋuu hã la, ɖeko míedo tokui! Eya tae míeɖo xaxa sia me fifia.”.

eng: They approached the man who was Josephs household manager and spoke to him at the houses entrance.
local: Wote ɖe Yosef ƒe aƒedzikpɔla la ŋu le xɔa ƒe ʋɔtru nu, eye wogblɔ nɛ be:.

eng: During the good years that are coming, they should collect all such food and store the grain under Pharaohs control, protecting the food in the cities.
local: Azɔ na ŋusẽ dɔdzikpɔla siawo be woaƒo ƒu nuɖuɖu le ƒè nyui siwo me nuɖuɖu le bɔbɔ ge la me, woatu nuɖuɖudzraɖoƒewo ɖe duwo me, eye woanɔ wo dzi kpɔm na wò.

eng: Reuben responded to them, Didnt I tell you, Dont do anything wrong to the boy? But you wouldnt listen. So now this is payback for his death.
local: Ruben gblɔ na wo be: “Megblɔ na mi be migawɔ vɔ̃ aɖekee o, gake miegbe. Azɔ la, wole to hem na mí ɖe eƒe ku ta.”.

eng: Please, Master, we came down the first time just to buy food.
local: “Taflatsɛ, amegã, míeva afii zi gbãtɔ va ƒle bli.

eng: This food will be reserved for the seven years of famine to follow in the land of Egypt so that the land wont be ravaged by the famine.
local: Nuɖuɖua ava zu megbedenu na dukɔ la, ale be ne dɔ to le Egipte-nyigba la dzi ƒè adrẽ la, woava wɔ eŋudɔ, bene dɔwuame nagatsrɔ̃ ame le dukɔa me o.”.

eng: They didnt know that Joseph was listening to them because they were using an interpreter.
local: Ke womenya be Yosef se nyaawo o, elabe gbesela dzie woƒo nu toe.

eng: but when we stopped to spend the night and opened our sacks, there was the exact amount of each mans silver at the top of his sack. Weve brought it back with us.
local: Esi míeɖo agbadroƒe, eye míetu míaƒe kotokuawo la, ame sia ame kpɔ eƒe ga si wòtsɔ xe fee la le eƒe bli dzi le kotokua me. Kpɔ ɖa, eyae nye esi míetrɔ vɛ.

eng: This advice seemed wise to Pharaoh and all his servants.
local: Nya sia dze fia la kple eŋumewo katã ŋu.

eng: He stepped away from them and wept. When he returned, he spoke with them again. Then he took Simeon from them and tied him up in front of them.
local: Tete Yosef dzo le wo gbɔ ɖafa avi. Egatrɔ va wo gbɔ, eye wòƒo nu kple wo. Eɖe Simeon le wo dome, eye wòna woblae le wo katã ŋkume.

eng: and weve brought down with us additional silver to buy food. We dont know who put our silver in our sacks.
local: Míetsɔ ga bubu ɖe asi vɛ be míatsɔ agaƒle bli. Míenya ame si trɔ míaƒe ga de míaƒe kotokuwo me o.”.

eng: and Pharaoh said to his servants, Can we find a man with more God-given gifts than this one.
local: Tete fia la bia eŋumeawo be: “Ɖe míate ŋu akpɔ ame aɖe si me Mawu ƒe gbɔgbɔ la le abe ame sia enea?”.

eng: Then Joseph gave orders to fill their bags with grain, to put back each mans silver into his own sack, and to give them provisions for their trip, and it was done.
local: Emegbe la, Yosef ɖe gbe be woade bli nɔviawo ƒe kotokuawo me woayɔ, woatsɔ ame sia ame ƒe ga ade eƒe kotokua me, eye woana nuɖuɖu wo hena mɔzɔzɔ. Ameawo wɔ ɖe edzi.

eng: He said, You are fine. Dont be afraid. Your God and your fathers God must have hidden a treasure in your sacks. I received your money. Then he brought Simeon out to them.
local: Ŋutsua ɖo eŋu na wo be: “Miaƒe dzi nedze eme. Migavɔ̃ o. Mia Mawu la, mia tɔ́ ƒe Mawu lae tsɔ ga la de miaƒe kotokuwo me. Wotsɔ miaƒe ga si miexe la nam.” Tete wòkplɔ Simeon vɛ na wo.

eng: Joseph gave commands to his household manager: Fill the mens sacks with as much food as theyll hold, and put each mans silver at the top of his sack.
local: Azɔ Yosef gblɔ na eƒe aƒedzikpɔla be: “De nuɖuɖu ŋutsuawo ƒe kotokuawo me wòasɔ gbɔ ale sinu anyatsɔ na wo la, eye nàtsɔ ame sia ame ƒe ga ada ɖe edzi le eƒe kotokua me.

eng: They loaded their grain onto their donkeys, and they set out.
local: Wodo bligba na woƒe tedziwo, eye wodze mɔ.

eng: Then Pharaoh said to Joseph, Since God has made all this known to you, no one is as intelligent and wise as you are.
local: Ale Fiagã la gblɔ na Yosef be: “Esi Mawu ɖe nu siawo katã fia wò ta la, efia be ame nyanu aɖaŋudzela aɖeke meli abe wò ene o.

eng: The manager brought the men into Josephs house and gave them water to wash their feet and feed for their donkeys.
local: Azɔ la, aƒedzikpɔla la kplɔ ŋutsuawo ge ɖe Yosef ƒe aƒeme. Eku tsi na wo woklɔ afɔ, eye wòna nuɖuɖu woƒe tedziwo.

eng: Put my cup, the silver cup, on top of the youngest brothers sack, together with the silver for his grain. So he did just as Joseph told him to do.
local: Tsɔ nye klosalokplu de ɖevitɔ ƒe kotoku me kpe ɖe ga si wòtsɔ va nu ƒle ge la ŋu.” Ewɔ ɖe nya si Yosef gblɔ nɛ la dzi.

eng: Joseph could no longer control himself in front of all his attendants, so he declared, Everyone, leave now! So no one stayed with him when he revealed his identity to his brothers.
local: Azɔ Yosef magate ŋu alé eɖokui le ame siwo le egbɔ la ŋkume o. Eɖe gbe be: “Ame sia ame nedo go!” Ame bubu aɖeke menɔ wo gbɔ hafi wòɖe eɖokui fia nɔviawo o.

eng: When they stopped to spend the night, one of them opened his sack to feed his donkey, and he saw his silver at the top of his sack.
local: Esi woɖo afi si woatsi dɔ la, wo dometɔ ɖeka ʋu eƒe kotokua nu be yeana nuɖuɖu yeƒe tedzi. Kasia ekpɔ ga si wòtsɔ xe fee la le blia dzi.

eng: You will be in charge of my kingdom, and all my people will obey your command. Only as the enthroned king will I be greater than you.
local: Eya ta wò koe matsɔ ɖo nye dukɔ nu. Dukɔ blibo la aɖo to wò. Nye, ame si le fiazikpui la dzi, koe anye ta na wò.”.

eng: They prepared the gift, anticipating Josephs arrival at noon, since they had heard that they would have a meal there.
local: Woɖo nunanaawo ɖi be ne Yosef va le ŋdɔ me la, yewoatsɔ wo nɛ elabe wose be yewole nu ɖu ge kplii le afi ma.

eng: At dawn, the men and their donkeys were sent off.
local: Le ŋufɔke ŋdi kanya la, wodo mɔ ŋutsuawo wodzo kple woƒe tedziwo.

eng: He wept so loudly that the Egyptians and Pharaohs household heard him.
local: Yosef wó avi doo, do ɣli ale be Egiptetɔawo kple fiaƒemetɔwo hã see.

eng: He said to his brothers, My silvers been returned. Its right here in my sack. Their hearts stopped. Terrified, they said to each other, What has God done to us.
local: Eyɔ nɔviawo gblɔ na wo be: “Wogbugbɔ nye ga nam! Eyae nye esi le nye kotokua me!” Tete dzika tso wo ƒo, eye wodzo nyànyanya hebia wo nɔewo be: “Nu kae nye esi Mawu wɔ mí?”.

eng: Pharaoh said to Joseph, Know this: Ive given you authority over the entire land of Egypt.
local: Fia la gagblɔ be: “Kpɔ ɖa, metsɔ wò ɖo Egipte-nyigba blibo la nu.”.

eng: They had left the city but hadnt gone far when Joseph said to his household manager, Get ready, go after the men and catch up with them! Ask them, Why have you repaid hospitality with ingratitude?.
local: Esi wodo go le dua me yina sẽe la, Yosef gblɔ na eƒe aƒedzikpɔla be: “Tso nàti ŋutsuawo yome nàɖatu wo. Bia wo se be: ‘Nu ka ta mietsɔ vɔ̃ ɖo nyui teƒe? Nu ka ta miefi nye aƒetɔ ƒe klosalokplu ɖo?.

eng: When Joseph came into the house, they presented him the gift they had brought with them into the house, and they bowed low in front of him.
local: Esi Yosef va aƒea me la, wotsɔ nunanaawo nɛ, eye wode ta agu nɛ.

eng: Joseph said to his brothers, Im Joseph! Is my father really still alive? His brothers couldnt respond because they were terrified before him.
local: Yosef gblɔ na nɔviawo be: “Nyee nye Yosef! Tɔ́nye gale agbea?” Ke esi nɔviawo se nyaa la, wolulu, eye gbɔgblɔ bu ɖe wo.

eng: Israel packed up everything he owned and traveled to Beer-sheba. There he offered sacrifices to his father Isaacs God.
local: Ale Yakob ƒo ƒu eƒe nuwo katã eye wòdze mɔ yi Beer-Seba. Esa vɔ na tɔ́a Isak ƒe Mawu la le afi ma.

eng: When they got back to their father Jacob in the land of Canaan, they described to him everything that had happened to them.
local: Esi wogbɔ va wo tɔ́ Yakob gbɔ le Kanaan-nyigba dzi la, wogblɔ nya siwo katã dzɔ ɖe wo dzi la nɛ. Wogblɔ be:.

eng: Pharaoh took his signet ring from his hand and put it on Josephs hand, he dressed him in linen clothes, and he put a gold necklace around his neck.
local: Tete Egipte Fiagã la ɖe eƒe ŋkɔsigɛ le asi do na Yosef, do aklala biɖibiɖi wu nɛ, eye wòtsɔ sikakɔsɔkɔsɔ de kɔ nɛ.

eng: Isnt this the cup my master drinks from and uses to discover Gods plans? What youve done is despicable.
local: Eyae wòtsɔna noa nui. Eya mee wokpɔa nu ɣaɣlawo le gblɔna. Miewɔ busunu!’ ”.

eng: He asked them how they were and said, How is your elderly father, about whom you spoke? Is he still alive.
local: Le gbelɔlɔ̃ kplii me la, ebia wo be: “Mia tɔ́ amegãɖeɖi, si ŋu mieƒo nu le nam la ɖe, egale agbea? Eli sesĩea?”.

eng: Joseph said to his brothers, Come closer to me, and they moved closer. He said, Im your brother Joseph! The one you sold to Egypt.
local: Yosef gblɔ na wo be: “Kpɔ ɖa, mite va gbɔnye.” Esi wote ɖe eŋu la, egblɔ na wo be: “Nyee nye mia nɔvi Yosef. Nyee miedzra ɖo ɖe Egipte.

eng: God said to Israel in a vision at night, Jacob! Jacob! and he said, Im here.
local: Mawu ƒo nu nɛ le ŋutega me le zã me. Eyɔe be: “Yakob, Yakob!” Etɔ be: “Nyee nye esi!”.

eng: The man, the countrys governor, spoke to us with a harsh tone and accused us of being spies in the country.
local: “Anyigba la dziɖula he nya ɖe mía ŋu vevie. Ebu mí ŋkutsalawoe.

eng: He put Joseph on the chariot of his second-in-command, and everyone in front of him cried out, Attention! So Pharaoh installed him over the entire land of Egypt.
local: Ena wotsɔ eƒe tasiaɖam evelia kɔ Yosef hetsae, eye gbeƒãɖelawo le eŋgɔ le ɣli dom be: “Mide ta agu nɛ! Mide ta agu nɛ!” Ale wòtsɔ Yosef ɖo Egipte-nyigba la katã nu.

eng: When he caught up to them, he repeated these words.
local: Esi aƒedzikpɔla la va tu wo la, egblɔ nya siawo na wo tututu.

eng: They said, Your servant our father is fine. Hes still alive. And they bowed down again with deep respect.
local: Woɖo eŋu be: “Mía tɔ́, wò subɔla, gale agbe, eye wòli sesĩe.” Tete wodze klo tsyɔ mo anyi ɖe eŋgɔ.

eng: Now, dont be upset and dont be angry with yourselves that you sold me here. Actually, God sent me before you to save lives.
local: Gake migaxa nu o, eye megave mi kura be yewoe dzram, wokplɔ va afii o, elabe Mawu ɖe wòɖom ɖe mia ŋgɔ be mava xɔ amewo ɖe agbe.

eng: He said, I am El, your fathers God. Dont be afraid to go down to Egypt because I will make a great nation of you there.
local: Tete Mawu gblɔ nɛ be: “Nyee nye Mawu, tɔ́wò ƒe Mawu la. Mègavɔ̃ Egipte yiyi o, elabe mawɔ wò nàzu dukɔ gã aɖe le afi ma.

eng: We told him, Were honest men, not spies.
local: Ke míegblɔ nɛ be menye ŋkutsalawo míenye o; ke boŋ nuteƒewɔlawo míenye.

eng: Pharaoh said to Joseph, I am Pharaoh; no one will do anything or go anywhere in all the land of Egypt without your permission.
local: Fiagã la gblɔ na Yosef be: “Nyee nye Egipte Fiagã; gake ne wò, mèɖe mɔ o la, ame aɖeke mekpɔ mɔ aɖe afɔ alo anye abɔ le Egipte-nyigba blibo la dzi o.”.

eng: Joseph looked up and saw his brother Benjamin, his own mothers son, and he said, Is this your youngest brother whom you told me about? God be gracious to you, my son.
local: Yosef fɔ ta dzi eye wòkpɔ Benyamin, dadavia. Ebia be: “Mia nɔvi ɖevitɔ si ŋu mieƒo nu le nam lae nye esia? Vinye, Mawu neve nuwò.”.

eng: They replied, Why does my master talk to us like this? Your servants would never do such a thing.
local: Woɖo eŋu nɛ be: “Nu ka, amegã, nya kae nye esi gblɔm nèle? Gbeɖe, míate ŋu awɔ nu sia tɔgbe akpɔ o!.

eng: Weve already had two years of famine in the land, and there are five years left without planting or harvesting.
local: Azɔ la, ƒè eve koe nye esi dɔ to le anyigba dzi; egasusɔ ƒè atɔ̃ sɔŋ si me amewo mate ŋu agade agble alo aŋe nu o.

eng: I will go down to Egypt with you, and I promise to bring you out again. Joseph will close your eyes when you die.
local: Nye ŋutɔ mayi Egipte kpli wò, eye nye ŋutɔ magakplɔ wò agbɔe. Yosef akpɔ wò kuƒe eye wòalé wò ŋkuyiwo akpe.”.

eng: We are twelve brothers, all our fathers sons. One of us is gone, but the youngest is right now with our father in the land of Canaan.
local: Nɔvi wui eve siwo nye ŋutsu ɖeka viwo míenye. Mía nɔvi ɖeka megali o, eye mía nɔvi ɖevitɔ ya le mía tɔ́ gbɔ ɖa le Kanaan-nyigba dzi.

eng: Pharaoh renamed Joseph, Zaphenath-paneah, and married him to Asenath, the daughter of Potiphera the priest of Heliopolis. Then Joseph assumed control of the land of Egypt.
local: Etsɔ Egipte ŋkɔ na Yosef be Zefnat Paneax. Etsɔ Asnat, Potifera si nye On nunɔla vinyɔnu nɛ wòɖe. Yosef yi ɖatsa le Egipte-nyigba blibo la dzi.

eng: The silver that we found at the top of our sacks, weve just brought back to you from the land of Canaan. We didnt steal silver or gold from your masters house.
local: Kpɔ ɖa, esi míeɖo Kanaan-nyigba dzi, kpɔ ga le blia dzi le miaƒe kotokuawo me la, míetrɔe vɛ na wòe. Ale ke wɔ míafi klosalo alo sika le wò aƒetɔ ƒeme adzoe azɔ?.

eng: Josephs feelings for his brother were so strong he was about to weep, so he rushed to another room and wept there.
local: Tete Yosef dzudzɔ eƒe nuƒoa elabe eƒe dɔme trɔ ɖe nɔvia ŋu, eye wòdi be yeafa avi. Ale wòyi ɖe eƒe xɔgãme ɖafa avi le afi ma.

eng: God sent me before you to make sure youd survive and to rescue your lives in this amazing way.
local: Ke Mawu dɔm ɖe mia ŋgɔ, be ame ʋɛ aɖe natsi agbe le anyigba sia dzi na mi, eye ame geɖe natsi agbe na mi.

eng: Then Jacob left Beer-sheba. Israels sons put their father Jacob, their children, and their wives on the wagons Pharaoh had sent to carry him.
local: Azɔ Yakob dze mɔ tso Beer-Seba. Via ŋutsuawo tsɔ eya, wo viwo kple wo srɔ̃wo de tasiaɖam siwo Egipte Fiagã ɖo ɖa la me.

eng: The man, the countrys governor, told us, This is how I will know you are honest men: Leave one of your brothers with me, take grain for those in your households who are hungry, and go.
local: Tete dziɖula la gblɔ na mí be: ‘Nu si matsɔ ado mi kpɔ be nyateƒetolawo mienye lae nye esi: Migblẽ mia nɔvi ɖeka ɖe gbɔnye, eye mi ame mamlɛawo miatsɔ blia ayi na miaƒe ƒomeawo bene woatsɔ alé dɔwuame la nu.

eng: Joseph was years old when he began to serve Pharaoh, Egypts king, when he left Pharaohs court and traveled through the entire land of Egypt.
local: Yosef xɔ ƒe bla etɔ̃ esime wòdze dɔwɔwɔ gɔme na Egipte Fiagã la. Esi wòdzo le fia la ŋkume la, eyi ɖatsa le anyigba blibo la dzi.

eng: Whoever of your servants is found with it will be put to death, and well be my masters slaves.
local: Amegã, mía dometɔ si gbɔ nàkpɔe le la dze na ku, eye mí ame mamlɛawo la míazu wò kluviwo.”.

eng: He washed his face, came back, pulled himself together, and said, Set out the dinner.
local: Efu mo, lé eɖokui nyuie, eye wòdo go va. Egblɔ be: “Mima nuɖuɖua!”.

eng: You didnt send me here; it was God who made me a father to Pharaoh, master of his entire household, and ruler of the whole land of Egypt.
local: Menye miawoe dɔm ɖe afii o; ke boŋ Mawue. Eyae tsɔm wɔ fia la ƒe adaŋudela vevi, eye nyee kpɔa eƒe aƒe dzi, ganye Egipte-nyigba blibo la dziɖula.

eng: They took their livestock and their possessions that they had acquired in the land of Canaan, and arrived in Egypt, Jacob and all of his children with him.
local: Wokplɔ woƒe lãwo, eye wotsɔ woƒe kesinɔnu siwo katã wokpɔ le Kanaan-nyigba dzi, eye woyi Egipte. Yakob kplɔ eƒe dzidzimeviwo katã hã yii.

eng: But bring back your youngest brother to me. Then I will know that you are not spies but honest men. I will give your brother back to you, and you may travel throughout the country.
local: Mikplɔ mia nɔvi ɖevitɔ la vɛ nam, ne manya be menye ŋkutsalawo mienye o, ke boŋ nyateƒe tom miele; ekema maɖe asi le mia nɔvi si le asinye me la ŋu na mi, eye miate ŋu anɔ anyigba sia dzi awɔ dɔ.’ ”.

eng: Joseph went to inform Pharaoh and said, My father and brothers with their flocks, herds, and everything they own have come from the land of Canaan and are now in the land of Goshen.
local: Ale Yosef yi ɖagblɔ na Fiagã la be: “Tɔ́nye kple nɔvinyewo tso Kanaan-nyigba dzi va kple woƒe alẽwo kple nunɔamesiwo katã. Wole Gosen-nutome.”.

eng: During the seven years of abundance, the land produced plentifully.
local: Azɔ la, agble me nuku ʋã le anyigba la dzi ŋutɔ ƒè adrẽ.

eng: He said, Fine. Well do just as youve said. Whoever is found with it will be my slave, and the rest of you will go free.
local: Egblɔ be: “Melɔ̃ ɖe edzi. Ɖeko ame si gbɔ woakpɔe le la lazu nye kluvi, mi ame mamlɛawo ya miadzo ayi faa.”.

eng: So they set out his food by himself, their food by themselves, and the Egyptians who ate with him by themselves because Egyptians dont allow themselves to eat with Hebrews; the Egyptians think it beneath their dignity.
local: Wotsɔ Yosef tɔ nɛ vovo, eye wotsɔ nɔviawo hã tɔ na wo vovo. Nenema ke wotsɔ Egiptetɔ siwo ɖua nu kplii la hã tɔ na wo vovo. Egiptetɔwo meɖua nu kple Hebritɔwo o, elabe wobunɛ be enye ŋunyɔnu.

eng: Hurry! Go back to your father. Tell him this is what your son Joseph says: God has made me master of all of Egypt. Come down to me. Dont delay.
local: Eya ta netsɔ miayi tɔ́nye gbɔ aɖagblɔ nɛ be nye via Yosef mebe: ‘Mawu wɔm mezu Egipte-nyigba la katã dziɖula. Va gbɔnye kaba; mègahe ɖe megbe o.

eng: His sons and grandsons, his daughters and his granddaughters—all of his descendants he brought with him to Egypt.
local: Ekplɔ via ŋutsuwo kple eƒe tɔgbuiyɔviŋutsuwo, via nyɔnuwo kple eƒe tɔgbuiyɔvinyɔnuwo katã yi Egipte.

eng: When they opened their sacks, each man found a pouch of his silver in his sack. When they and their father saw their pouches of silver, they were afraid.
local: Azɔ esi wotrɔ blia le kotokuawo me la, wo dometɔ ɖe sia ɖe kpɔ be yeƒe ga le kotokua me. Esi woawo kple wo tɔ́ kpɔ gaa la, vɔvɔ̃ ɖo wo ŋutɔ.

eng: From all of his brothers, he selected five men and presented them before Pharaoh.
local: Ekplɔ nɔvia atɔ̃ yi ɖaɖe fia fia la.

eng: He collected all of the food during the seven years of abundance in the land of Egypt, and stored the food in cities. In each city, he stored the food from the fields surrounding it.
local: Ale Yosef na woƒo ƒè adrẽ nuawo nu ƒu ɖe duawo me. Ena wodzra agble ɖe sia ɖe me tɔ ɖo ɖe du si te ɖe eŋu la me.

eng: Everyone quickly lowered their sacks down to the ground and each opened his sack.
local: Enumake ame sia ame ɖiɖi eƒe kotoku ɖe anyigba, eye wòtui.

eng: They were seated in front of him from the oldest to the youngest in their exact birth order, and the men looked at each other with amazement.
local: Wona wonɔ anyi dze ŋgɔ Yosef le nua ɖuƒe. Woɖo wo ɖe tsitsi nu, tso tsitsitɔ dzi va se ɖe ɖevitɔ dzi. Esi nɔviawo de dzesi ale si wòna wonɔ anyii la, woƒe nu ku, eye wole wo nɔewo kpɔm dũu.

eng: These are the names of the Israelites who went to Egypt, including Jacob and his sons. Jacobs oldest son was Reuben.
local: Yakob vi siwo yi Egipte kplii la woe nye: eƒe ŋgɔgbevi Ruben.

eng: Their father Jacob said to them, Youve taken my children from me. Josephs gone. Simeons gone. And you are taking Benjamin. All this cant really be happening to me.
local: Tete wo tɔ́ Yakob gblɔ na wo be: “Miena vinyewo ku nam vɔ! Yosef megali o; Simeon hã megali o. Azɔ miedi be yewoagaxɔ Benyamin hã. Nye ɖeka ko dzie nya siawo katã le dzɔdzɔm ɖo.”.

eng: You may live in the land of Goshen, so you will be near me, your children, your grandchildren, your flocks, your herds, and everyone with you.
local: Wò ŋutɔ, viwò ŋutsuwo kple wò tɔgbuiyɔviwo, wò alẽwo kple nyiwo kpakple nu siwo katã le asiwò la, mianɔ Gosen-nutome ale be miate ɖe ŋunye.

eng: Pharaoh said to Josephs brothers, What do you do? They said to Pharaoh, Your servants are shepherds, both we and our ancestors.
local: Fia la bia Yosef nɔviwo be: “Dɔ ka wɔlawo mienye?” Woɖo eŋu be: “Lãnyilawo míenye abe mía tɔgbuiwo ke ene.”.

eng: Joseph amassed grain like the sand of the sea. There was so much that he stopped trying to measure it because it was beyond measuring.
local: Bli si woƒo ƒui la sɔ gbɔ abe ƒutake ene. Wodzidzee ʋuu wòglo, ale womegadzidzee yi dzi o.

eng: He searched the oldest first and the youngest last, and the cup was found in Benjamins sack.
local: Aƒedzikpɔla la de asi kplua didi me. Edze egɔme tso tsitsitɔ dzi va wu nu ɖe ɖevitɔ dzi. Kasia wokpɔ kplu la le Benyamin ƒe agba me.

eng: Portions of food from Josephs table were brought to them, but Benjamins portion was five times as large as theirs. So they drank together and were at ease.
local: Yosef na woka nuɖuɖu si le ekɔme la na nɔviawo, eye wòna Benyamin tɔ sɔ gbɔ wu nɔviawo katã tɔ zigbɔzi atɔ̃. Woɖu nu, no nu, eye wokpɔ dzidzɔ kplii.

eng: Reubens sons were Hanoch, Pallu, Hezron, and Carmi.
local: Ruben viŋutsuwoe nye: Henox, Palu, Xezron kple Karmi.

eng: Reuben said to his father, You may put both of my sons to death if I dont bring him back to you. Make him my responsibility, and I will make sure he returns to you.
local: Ruben gblɔ na tɔ́a be: “Ne nyemekplɔ Benyamin gbɔe na wò o la, àte ŋu awu vinye ŋutsuvi eveawo faa. Wò ya tsɔe de asi nam ko; makplɔe agbɔe na wò.”.

eng: I will support you there, so you, your household, and everyone with you wont starve, since the famine will still last five years.
local: Makpɔ dziwò le afi ma elabe esusɔ ƒè atɔ̃ sɔŋ ne dɔa nake. Ne menye nenem o la, hiã atu wò kple wò aƒekɔ kple nu siwo katã le asiwò la.’.

eng: They continued, Weve come to the land as immigrants because the famine is so severe in the land of Canaan that there are no more pastures for your servants flocks. Please allow your servants to settle in the land of Goshen.
local: Woyi dzi gblɔ nɛ be: “Taflatsɛ, míeva be míanɔ anyigba sia dzi elabe míaƒe lãhawo megakpɔ gbe aɖu o eye dɔwuame la nu sẽ le Kanaan-nyigba dzi ale gbegbe. Eya ta míeɖe kuku na wò, ɖe mɔ na mí, wò dɔlawo, ne míanɔ Gosen-nutoa me.”.

eng: Before the years of famine arrived, Asenath the daughter of Potiphera, priest of Heliopolis, gave birth to two sons for Joseph.
local: Hafi dɔa nava to la, Asnat, Potifera si nye On nunɔla vinyɔnu la, dzi viŋutsu eve na Yosef.

eng: At this, they tore their clothing. Then everyone loaded their donkeys, and they returned to the city.
local: Nɔviŋutsuawo dze woƒe awuwo kple veve. Wo dometɔ ɖe sia ɖe gado agba na eƒe tedzi, eye wogbugbɔ yi dua me.

eng: Simeons sons were Jemuel, Jamin, Ohad, Jachin, Zohar, and Shaul, whose mother was a Canaanite.
local: Simeon viŋutsuwoe nye: Yemuel, Tamin, Ohad, Yaxin, Zoxar kple Saul, Kanaan-nyɔnu ƒe vi.

eng: But Jacob said to him, My son wont go down with you because his brothers dead and hes been left all alone. If anything were to happen to him on the trip you are taking, you would send me—old as I am—to my grave in grief.
local: Ke Yakob ɖo eŋu be: “Vinye la mayi kpli mi gbeɖe o; dadavia ku, eye eya ɖeka koe susɔ ɖi. Ne dzɔgbevɔ̃e aɖe dzɔ ɖe edzi le mɔa dzi la, miana matsɔ ta ɣi ayi tsiẽƒe kple nuxaxa.”.

eng: You and my brother Benjamin have seen with your own eyes that Im speaking to you.
local: Yosef yi eƒe nuƒoa dzi be: Azɔ la, miawo ŋutɔ miele ekpɔm kple ŋku; nɔvinye Benyamin hã le ekpɔm be nye ŋutɔe le nu ƒom na mi.

eng: Pharaoh said to Joseph, Since your father and brothers have arrived.
local: Tete Fiagã la gblɔ na Yosef be: “Tɔ́wò kple nɔviwòwo va ɖo.

eng: Joseph named the oldest son Manasseh, because, he said, God has helped me forget all of my troubles and everyone in my fathers household.
local: Yosef gblɔ be: “Mawu na meŋlɔ nye fukpekpewo kple nye ƒometɔwo be,” eya ta wòna ŋkɔ eƒe ŋgɔgbevia be Manase.

eng: When Judah and his brothers arrived at Josephs house, he was still there, and they fell to the ground in front of him.
local: Esi Yuda kple nɔviawo va Yosef ƒeme la, Yosef gale afi ma. Wodze klo de ta agu nɛ.

eng: Levis sons were Gershon, Kohath, and Merari.
local: Levi viŋutsuwoe nye: Gerson, Kahat kple Merari.

eng: Tell my father about my power in Egypt and about everything youve seen. Hurry and bring my father down here.
local: Miɖagblɔ bubu si gbegbe wotsɔ nam le Egipte kple nu siwo katã miekpɔ la na tɔ́nye. Miwɔ kaba ne miakplɔe va afii.”.

eng: the land of Egypt is available to you. Settle your father and brothers in the lands best location. Let them live in the land of Goshen. And if you know capable men among them, put them in charge of my own livestock.
local: Egipte-nyigba la le asiwò me. Àte ŋu ada wo ɖe Gosen-nutome elabe anyigba nyo le afi ma ŋutɔ. Ne ènya kutrikula aɖewo le wo dome la, tsɔ nye lãhawo de wo si ne woakpɔ wo dzi.”.

eng: He named the second Ephraim, because, he said, God has given me children in the land where Ive been treated harshly.
local: Egagblɔ hã be: “Mawu na medzi viwo le anyigba si dzi mekpe fu le la dzi,” eya ta wòna ŋkɔ via evelia be Efrayim.

eng: Joseph said to them, Whats this youve done? Didnt you know someone like me can discover Gods plans.
local: eye Yosef bia wo se be: “Nu ka nye esi miewɔ? Ɖe mienyae be, ame abe nye ene, mekaa nu oa?”.

eng: Judahs sons were Er, Onan, Shelah, Perez, and Zerah. Er and Onan both died in the land of Canaan. Perezs sons were Hezron and Hamul.
local: Yuda viŋutsuwoe nye: Er, Onan, Sela, Perez kple Serax. Er kple Onan ya ku le Kanaan-nyigba dzi. Perez viŋutsuwoe nye Xezron kple Xamul.

eng: He threw his arms around his brother Benjamins neck and wept, and Benjamin wept on his shoulder.
local: Tete wòkpla asi kɔ na nɔvia Benyamin, eye wòwó avi; Benyamin hã kpla asi kɔ nɛ kple avi.

eng: Joseph brought his father Jacob and gave him an audience with Pharaoh. Jacob blessed Pharaoh.
local: Tete Yosef kplɔ tɔ́a va Fiagã la gbɔe, eye Yakob yra fia la.

eng: The seven years of abundance in the land of Egypt came to an end.
local: Ƒè adrẽ siwo me nuku ʋã le le Egipte-nyigba dzi la va yi.

eng: Judah replied, What can we say to my master? What words can we use? How can we prove we are innocent? God has found your servants guilty. We are now your slaves, all of us, including the one found with the cup.
local: Yuda ɖo eŋu be: “Amegã, nya kae le mía si míate ŋu agblɔ na wò? Ale ke míaʋli nya siae? Ale ke míaɖe mía ɖokui nui? Mawu klo nu le wò dɔlawo ƒe nuvɔ̃ dzi. Kpɔ ɖa, menye ame si gbɔ wokpɔ wò kplua le la koe lanye wò kluvi o; mí katã tim míezu wò kluviwo.”.

eng: Issachars sons were Tola, Puvah, Iob, and Shimron.
local: Isaxar viŋutsuwoe nye: Tola, Puva, Yob kple Simron.

eng: He kissed all of his brothers and wept, embracing them. After that, his brothers were finally able to talk to him.
local: Yosef gbugbɔ nu na nɔviawo katã kple avi. Esia yome la, nɔviawo de asi dzeɖoɖo me kplii.

eng: and Pharaoh said to Jacob, How old are you.
local: Fiagã la biae be: “Ƒe nenie nèxɔ?”.

eng: and the seven years of famine began, just as Joseph had said. The famine struck every country, but the entire land of Egypt had bread.
local: eye ƒè adrẽ siwo me dɔ to le la dze egɔme, abe ale si Yosef gblɔe ene. Dɔ to le dukɔwo katã me, gake nuɖuɖu nɔ Egipte-nyigba ya dzi.

eng: Joseph said, Id never do such a thing. Only the man found with the cup will be my slave. As for the rest of you, you are free to go back to your father.
local: Yosef ɖo eŋu na wo be: “Gbeɖe! Nyemawɔ nenema akpɔ o. Ame si gbɔ wokpɔ nye kplua le la, eya koe anye nye kluvi. Mi ame mamlɛawo mitrɔ yi mia tɔ́a gbɔ le ŋutifafa me.”.

eng: Zebuluns sons were Sered, Elon, and Jahleel.
local: Zebulon viŋutsuwoe nye: Sered, Elon kple Yaxleel.

eng: When Pharaohs household heard the message Josephs brothers have arrived, both Pharaoh and his servants were pleased.
