local: Esi Egipte Fiagã la se be “Yosef nɔviwo va” la, dzi dzɔ eya kple eƒe dɔlawo ŋutɔ.

eng: Jacob said to Pharaoh, Ive been a traveler for years. My years have been few and difficult. They dont come close to the years my ancestors lived during their travels.
local: Yakob ɖo eŋu be: “Nye agbe mɔzɔƒè nye alafa ɖeka bla etɔ̃. Nye agbenɔƒèwo mesɔ gbɔ o, mekpe hiã geɖe le wo me, eye womede tɔ́nyewo ƒe mɔzɔƒè nu o.”.

eng: When the famine ravaged the entire land of Egypt and the people pleaded to Pharaoh for bread, Pharaoh said to all of the Egyptians, Go to Joseph. Do whatever he tells you.
local: Mlɔeba dɔwuame la va Egipte-nyigba hã dzi, eye dukɔa me tɔwo bia fia la be wòana nuɖuɖu yewo. Eɖo wo ɖe Yosef gbɔ, gblɔ na wo be: “Nu sia nu si wògblɔ na mi la, miwɔe.”.

eng: Judah approached him and said, Please, my master, allow your servant to say something to my master without getting angry with your servant since you are like Pharaoh himself.
local: Tete Yuda te ɖe Yosef ŋu gblɔ be: “Amegã, taflatsɛ, na maƒo nu na wò faa. Dzi megaku wò ɖe ŋunye o; èle abe Fiagã ŋutɔ ene.

eng: These are the sons Leah bore to Jacob in Paddan-aram. Her daughter was Dinah. All of these persons, including his sons and daughters, totaled .
local: Viŋutsuvi siwo Lea dzi na Yakob le Padan-Aram hekpe ɖe via nyɔnu Dina ŋu lae nye esiawo. Via ŋutsuwo kple via nyɔnuwo katã le ame bla etɔ̃ vɔ etɔ̃.

eng: Jacob blessed Pharaoh and left Pharaohs presence.
local: Emegbe Yakob gayra Fiagã la, eye wòtrɔ dzo.

eng: Pharaoh said to Joseph, Give your brothers these instructions: Load your pack animals and go back to the land of Canaan.
local: Fiagã la gblɔ na Yosef be: “Gblɔ na nɔviwòawo be: ‘Mido agba na miaƒe lãwo ne miagbugbɔ yi Kanaan-nyigba dzi.

eng: The famine covered every part of the land, and Joseph opened all of the granaries and sold grain to the Egyptians. In the land of Egypt, the famine became more and more severe.
local: Dɔwuame la nu sẽ ɖe edzi le afi sia afi le dukɔa me. Tete Yosef ʋu nuɖuɖudzraɖoƒewo katã nu, eye wòdzra bli na Egiptetɔwo.

eng: My master asked his servants, Do you have a father or brother.
local: Amegã, èbia mí se be, ‘Ɖe tɔ́ alo nɔviŋutsu aɖe gale mia sia?’.

eng: Gads sons were Ziphion, Haggi, Shuni, Ezbon, Eri, Arodi, and Areli.
local: Gad viŋutsuwoe nye: Zifyon, Hagi, Suni, Ezbon, Eri, Arodi kple Areli.

eng: Joseph settled his father and brothers and gave them property in the land of Egypt, in the best location in the land of Rameses, just as Pharaoh had ordered.
local: Azɔ Yosef fia teƒe si tɔ́a kple nɔviawo anɔ le Egipte-nyigba dzi la wo. Ena anyigba nyuitɔ wo ɖe Ramses-nutome, abe ale si Fiagã la gblɔ nɛ ene.

eng: Get your father and your households and come back to me. Let me provide you with good things from the land of Egypt so that you may eat the lands best food.
local: Mikplɔ mia tɔ́a kple miaƒe ƒometɔwo ne miatrɔ va gbɔnye. Mana anyigba nyuitɔ mi le Egipte eye miaɖu anyigba la dzi nu nyuiwo aɖi ƒo.’.

eng: And we said to my master, Yes, we have an elderly father and a young brother, born when he was old. His brother is dead and hes his mothers only child. But his father loves him.
local: Míeɖo eŋu be: ‘Mía tɔ́ amegãɖeɖi li, eye míaƒe dɔmlɔea ŋutsuvi hã li. Wodzi eya na mía tɔ́ le eƒe tsitsime. Ke dadavia ku, dadavi aɖeke megale esi o; tɔ́a lɔ̃nɛ vevie.’.

eng: Every country came to Egypt to buy grain from Joseph, because in every country the famine had also become more severe.
local: Amewo tso xexeme ƒe afi sia afi va Egipte be yewoaƒle bli le Yosef gbɔ, elabe dɔwuame la nu sẽ ŋutɔ le xexe blibo la me.

eng: Ashers sons were Imnah, Ishvah, Ishvi, Beriah, and their sister Serah. Beriahs sons were Heber and Malchiel.
local: Aser viŋutsuwoe nye: Yimna, Yisva, Yisvi, Beria kple wo nɔvinyɔnu Serax. Beria viŋutsuwo nye Xeeber kple Malkiel.

eng: Joseph provided food for his father, his brothers, and his fathers entire household, in proportion to the number of children.
local: Yosef kpɔ gbɔ be nuɖuɖu nɔ tɔ́a, nɔviawo kple tɔ́a ƒemetɔwo katã, ame sia ame kple via, si ɣe sia ɣi.

eng: Give them these instructions too: Take wagons from the land of Egypt for your children and wives, and pick up your father and come back.
local: De se na wo be: ‘Miwɔ ale: mitsɔ tasiaɖamwo tso Egipte yii, ne mia viwo kple mia srɔ̃wo kpakple mia tɔ́a naɖo ava.

eng: You told your servants, Bring him down to me so I can see him.
local: Azɔ ègblɔ na mí be míkplɔe vɛ na ye ne yeakpɔ.

eng: After this happened, Joseph was told, Your father is getting weaker, so he took his two sons Manasseh and Ephraim with him.
local: Esiawo megbe la, wogblɔ na Yosef be: “Kpɔ ɖa, tɔ́wò le dɔ lém.” Ale wòkplɔ via ŋutsu eveawo, Manase kple Efrayim, woyi egbɔ.

eng: These are the sons of Zilpah, whom Laban gave to his daughter Leah. She bore these to Jacob, a total of persons.
local: Silpa, kosi si Laban na via nyɔnu Lea viŋutsu siwo wòdzi na Yakob lae nye esiawo. Wo katã le ame wui ade.

eng: There was no food in the land because the famine was so severe. The land of Egypt and the land of Canaan dried up from the famine.
local: Dɔwuame la nu va sẽ ale gbegbe; nuɖuɖu aɖeke megale afi aɖeke o. Nu té amewo ŋu eye Egipte kple Kanaan-nyigba dzi ƒu kplákplakpla.

eng: Dont worry about your possessions because you will have good things from the entire land of Egypt.
local: Miɖe susu ɖa le miaƒe nunɔamesiwo ŋu, elabe nu nyuitɔwo katã le Egipte-nyigba dzi lanye mia tɔ.”.

eng: And we said to my master, The young man cant leave his father. If he leaves, his father will die.
local: Ke míeɖo eŋu be ɖevia mate ŋu adzo le tɔ́a gbɔ o; ne edzo la, tɔ́a aku.

eng: When Jacob was informed, Your son Joseph is here now, he pulled himself together and sat up in bed.
local: Esi wogblɔ na Yakob be: “Viwò Yosef va be yeakpɔ wò ɖa” la, Israel do ŋusẽ eɖokui fɔ, henɔ aba la dzi.

eng: The sons of Jacobs wife Rachel were Joseph and Benjamin.
local: Yakob srɔ̃ Raxel viŋutsuwoe nye Yosef kple Benyamin.

eng: Joseph collected all of the silver to be found in the land of Egypt and in the land of Canaan for the grain, which people came to buy, and he deposited it in Pharaohs treasury.
local: Yosef ƒo ƒu ga si Egiptetɔwo kple Kanaantɔwo tsɔ va ƒle bli le egbɔ la nu ƒu hetsɔ yi Egipte Fiagã ƒemee.

eng: So Israels sons did that. Joseph gave them wagons as Pharaoh instructed, and he gave them provisions for the road.
local: Yakob viwo wɔ nu si wogblɔ na wo la dzi. Yosef na tasiaɖamwo wo abe ale si fia la ɖe gbe ene, eye wòna nuɖuɖu hã wo na mɔzɔzɔa.

eng: You said to your servants, If your youngest brother doesnt come down with you, youll never see my face again.
local: Tete nègblɔ na mí be, ne míekplɔ mía nɔvi ɖevitɔ la vɛ o la, mígado ɖe ye ŋkume o.

eng: Jacob said to Joseph, God Almighty appeared to me in Luz in the land of Canaan. He blessed me.
local: Yakob gblɔ na Yosef be: “Mawu Ŋusẽkatãtɔ la ɖe eɖokui fiam le Luz le Kanaan-nyigba dzi eye wòyram.

eng: To Joseph, in the land of Egypt, were born Manasseh and Ephraim. Asenath daughter of Potiphera, priest of Heliopolis, bore them to him.
local: Viŋutsu siwo On nunɔla, Potifera ƒe vinyɔnu Asnat dzi na Yosef le Egipte-nyigba dzi lae nye Manase kple Efrayim.

eng: The silver from the land of Egypt and from the land of Canaan had been spent, and all of the Egyptians came to Joseph and said, Give us food. Why should we die before your eyes, just because the silver is gone.
local: Esi ga vɔ le Egipte kple Kanaan-nyigba dzi la, Egiptetɔwo katã va Yosef gbɔ be: “Na nuɖuɖu mí! Ga vɔ le mía si keŋkeŋ. Ànɔ mía kpɔm ale, míaku le ŋkuwò mea?”.

eng: To all of them he gave a change of clothing, but to Benjamin he gave three hundred pieces of silver and five changes of clothing.
local: Ena awu wo dometɔ ɖe sia ɖe; ke ena klosaloga alafa etɔ̃ kple awu dodo atɔ̃ Benyamin ya.

eng: When we went back to my father your servant, we told him what you said.
local: Esi míetrɔ yi mía tɔ́a gbɔ la, míegblɔ wò nya la nɛ.

eng: and said to me, I am about to give you many children, to increase your numbers, and to make you a large group of peoples. I will give this land to your descendants following you as an enduring possession.
local: Egblɔ nam be: ‘Mana nàdzi fũu, eye wò dzidzimeviwo asɔ gbɔ zã, eye mana woazu dukɔ geɖewo. Matsɔ anyigba sia ana wò dzidzimeviwo be wòanye wo tɔ tegbee.’.

eng: Benjamins sons were Bela, Becher, Ashbel, Gera, Naaman, Ehi, Rosh, Muppim, Huppim, and Ard.
local: Benyamin viŋutsuwoe nye Bela, Bexer, Asbel, Gera, Naeman, Exi, Ros, Muspim, Xupim kple Ard.

eng: Joseph said, Give me your livestock, and I will give you food for your livestock if the silver is gone.
local: Yosef ɖo eŋu be: “Ne ga vɔ le mia si la, mikplɔ miaƒe lãhawo vɛ. Maxɔ wo ana nuɖuɖu mi ɖe wo teƒe.”.

eng: To his father he sent ten male donkeys carrying goods from Egypt, ten female donkeys carrying grain and bread, and rations for his father for the road.
local: Edo agba na tedzi ewo kple Egipte nu nyuiwo, eye wògado agba na tedzinɔ ewo hã kple bli, abolo kpakple nuɖuɖu bubu siwo woaɖu le mɔ dzi, ne wotrɔ gbɔna la, ɖo ɖe tɔ́a.

eng: Our father told us, Go back and buy for us a little food.
local: Emegbe mía tɔ́a gblɔ na mí be migatrɔ va ƒle nuɖuɖu vɛ.

eng: Now, your two sons born to you in the land of Egypt before I arrived in Egypt are my own. Ephraim and Manasseh are just like Reuben and Simeon to me.
local: Azɔ viŋutsu eve siwo wodzi na wò le Egipte hafi meva gbɔwò la zu vinyewo. Efrayim kple Manase le nam pɛ abe Ruben kple Simeon ene. Woazu tɔnye.

eng: These are Rachels sons who were born to Jacob, a total of persons.
local: Raxel viŋutsu siwo wòdzi na Yakob lae nye esiawo. Wo katã le ame wui eve.

eng: So they brought their livestock to Joseph, and Joseph gave them food for the horses, flocks, cattle, and donkeys. He got them through that year with food in exchange for all of their livestock.
local: Tete wokplɔ woƒe lãhawo vɛ na Yosef eye wòtsɔ bli na wo ɖe woƒe sɔwo, alẽhawo, nyihawo kple tedziwo teƒe. Le ƒè ma me la, wotsɔ lãhawo ɖɔli bli le Yosef si.

eng: He sent his brothers off; and as they were leaving, he told them, Dont be worried about the trip.
local: Azɔ edo mɔ nɔviawo. Esi wole mɔ dzem la, exlɔ̃ nu wo be: “Migawɔ dzre le mɔ dzi o lo.”.

eng: But we said, We cant go down. We will go down only if our youngest brother is with us. We wont be able to gain an audience with the man without our youngest brother with us.
local: Míeɖo eŋu be: ‘Míate ŋu ayi o. Ne míawo ɖeɖe míeyi, eye mía nɔvi ɖevitɔ la mekpe ɖe mía ŋu o la, ŋutsua maɖe mɔ míado ɖe eŋkume o. Ne mía nɔvi ɖevitɔ la le mía dome ko hafi míawo hã míayi.’.

eng: Dans son was Hushim.
local: Dan viŋutsue nye Xusim.

eng: Your family who is born to you after them are yours, but their inheritance will be determined under their brothers names.
local: Ke vi siwo nèdzi ɖe wo yome la nanye tɔwò. Woawo akpɔ woƒe domenyinuwo tso wo nɔvi Efrayim kple Manase ƒe domenyinuwo me.

eng: When that year was over, they came to him the next year and said to him, We cant hide from my master that the silver is spent and that weve given the livestock to my master. All thats left for my master is our corpses and our farmland.
local: Esi ƒè trɔ la, wogava egbɔ be: “Amegã, míate ŋu aɣla nya aɖeke ɖe wò o. Nya lae nye be ga vɔ le mía si keŋkeŋ eye míaƒe lãhawo hã va zu tɔwò. Naneke megale mía si míatsɔ na wò wu míawo ŋutɔ kple míaƒe anyigbawo o.

eng: Your servant my father said to us, You know that my wife gave birth to two sons for me.
local: Tete mía tɔ́a gblɔ na mí be: ‘Mienyae be viŋutsu eve ko srɔ̃nye Raxel dzi nam.

eng: So they left Egypt and returned to their father Jacob in the land of Canaan.
local: Ale wodzo le Egipte, va ɖo wo tɔ́, Yakob, gbɔ le Kanaan-nyigba dzi.

eng: Naphtalis sons were Jahzeel, Guni, Jezer, and Shillem.
local: Naftali viŋutsuwoe nye: Yaxzeel, Guni, Yezer kple Silem.

eng: When I came back from Paddan-aram, Rachel died, to my sorrow, on the road in the land of Canaan, with some distance yet to go to Ephrathah, so I buried her there near the road to Ephrathah, which is Bethlehem.
local: Esi metrɔ tso Padan-Aram gbɔna ɖe Kanaan, eye wòsusɔ vie maɖo Efrat la, nɔwò Raxel ku le mɔ dzi. Meɖii ɖe mɔ to le Efrat.” Woyɔa Efrat fifia be Betlehem.

eng: Why should we die before your eyes, we and our farmland too? Buy us and our farms for food, and we and our farms will be under Pharaohs control. Give us seed so that we can stay alive and not die, and so that our farmland wont become unproductive.
local: Ànɔ mí kple anyigba kpɔm ale, míatsrɔ̃ le ŋkuwò mea? Mègana míaƒe agbledeƒewo natsi anyi o. Xɔ mí kple míaƒe agbledenyigbawo, ne nàna nuɖuɖu mí ɖe eteƒe. Míelɔ̃ be míanye fia la ƒe kluviwo, eye míaƒe anyigbawo nazu etɔ. Na nuku mí míaƒã, ne míaƒe agblewo nagazu afuti o.”.

eng: One disappeared and I said, He must have been torn up by a wild animal, and I havent seen him since.
local: Ɖeka ku le gbɔnye. Megblɔ be lã wɔadã aɖe kokokoe vuvui, elabe va se ɖe fifi la, nyemekpɔe kpɔ o.

eng: They announced to him, Josephs still alive! Hes actually ruler of all the land of Egypt! Jacobs heart nearly failed, and he didnt believe them.
local: Wogblɔ nɛ be: “Yosef gale agbe! Eyae le Egipte-nyigba katã dzi ɖum!” Yakob lulu, eye mexɔ nya la dzi se o.

eng: These are the sons of Bilhah, whom Laban gave to his daughter Rachel. She bore these to Jacob, a total of persons.
local: Bilha, kosi si Laban na via nyɔnu Raxel la ƒe viŋutsu siwo wòdzi na Yakob lae nye esiawo. Wo katã le ame adrẽ.

eng: When Israel saw Josephs sons, he said, Who are these.
local: Esi Israel kpɔ Yosef viwo la, ebia be: “Ame kawoe nye esiawo?”.

eng: So Joseph bought all of Egypts farmland for Pharaoh because every Egyptian sold his field when the famine worsened. So the land became Pharaohs.
local: Ale Yosef ƒle agbledeƒe siwo katã le Egipte-nyigba dzi la na fia la. Egiptetɔ ɖe sia ɖe dzra eƒe agbledeƒe elabe dɔwuame la nu sẽ ŋutɔ. Agbledeƒewo katã va zu fia tɔ.

eng: And if you take this one from me too, something terrible will happen to him, and you will send me—old as I am—to my grave in despair.
local: Ne miekplɔ evelia sia hã yii, eye nane dzɔ ɖe edzi la, mafa konyi ʋuu ayi yɔ me kple nye ta ɣia.’.

eng: When they told him everything Joseph had said to them, and when he saw the wagons Joseph had sent to carry him, Jacob recovered.
local: Ke esi woto nya siwo katã Yosef gblɔ ɖo ɖee la nɛ, eye wòkpɔ tasiaɖam siwo Yosef ɖo ɖa be wòaɖo ava la, egaɖo eɖokui me.

eng: All of the persons going to Egypt with Jacob—his own children, excluding Jacobs sons wives—totaled persons.
local: Yakob ƒe dzidzimevi ŋutɔŋutɔ siwo ʋu yi Egipte kplii la le ame bla ade vɔ ade; via ŋutsuawo srɔ̃wo mele eme o.

eng: Joseph told his father, Theyre my sons, whom God gave me here. Israel said, Bring them to me and I will bless them.
local: Yosef ɖo eŋu na tɔ́a be: “Viŋutsu siwo Mawu nam le afii la woe.” Yakob gblɔ be: “Kplɔ wo vɛ nam ne mayra wo.”.

eng: He moved the people to the cities from one end of Egypt to the other.
local: Azɔ dukɔmetɔwo va zu kluviwo le Egipte-nyigba bliboa dzi.

eng: When I now go back to your servant my father without the young man—whose life is so bound up with his.
local: Azɔ ne meɖaɖo wò dɔla, tɔ́nye gbɔ, eye ɖevi si wòlɔ̃ vevie la mele mía dome o la.

eng: Then Israel said, This is too much! My son Joseph is still alive! Let me go and see him before I die.
local: Israel gblɔ be: “Esia sɔ gbɔ nam. Vinye Yosef gale agbe! Mayi aɖakpɔe hafi aku!”.

eng: Josephs sons born to him in Egypt were persons. Thus, all of the persons in Jacobs household going to Egypt totaled .
local: Wodzi viŋutsu eve na Yosef le Egipte. Yakob ƒe ƒometɔ siwo katã va Egipte la le ame bla adrẽ.

eng: Because Israels eyesight had failed from old age and he wasnt able to see, Joseph brought them close to him, and he kissed and embraced them.
local: Israel tsi, eye eƒe ŋku le tsitsim ale be megate ŋu le nu kpɔm nyuie o. Esi Yosef kplɔ via eveawo te ɖe eŋu la, Yakob kpla asi kɔ na wo hegbugbɔ nu na wo.

eng: However, he didnt buy the farmland of the priests because Pharaoh allowed the priests a subsidy, and they were able to eat from the subsidy Pharaoh gave them. Therefore, they didnt have to sell their farmland.
local: Ke Yosef meƒle nunɔlawo ƒe anyigba ya o, elabe fia la naa dzikpɔga wo, eye wokpɔa woƒe nuɖuɖu tso egbɔ. Eya ta nunɔlawo medzra woƒe anyigba o.

eng: and when he sees that the young man isnt with us, he will die, and your servants will have sent our father your servant—old as he is—to his grave in grief.
local: ekema mía tɔ́ aku ne mekpɔ ɖevia le mía dome o. Ale wò dɔlawo ana wò dɔla, mía tɔ́ amegãɖeɖi taɣi la, natsɔ nuxaxa ayi tsiẽƒee.

eng: Israel had sent Judah ahead to Joseph so that Joseph could explain the way to Goshen. Then they arrived in the land of Goshen.
local: Yakob dɔ Yuda ɖe Yosef gbɔ do ŋgɔ be wòava kpe yewo le Gosen. Esi wòɖaɖo la.

eng: Israel said to Joseph, I didnt expect Id see your face, but now God has shown me your children too.
local: Tete Israel gblɔ na Yosef be: “Nyemebui be magakpɔ wò ŋkume gbeɖe o, ke azɔ Mawu wɔe meva kpɔ viwòwo gɔ̃ hã.”.

eng: Joseph said to the people, Since Ive now purchased you and your farmland for Pharaoh, heres seed for you. Plant the seed on the land.
local: Tete Yosef gblɔ na dukɔmetɔwo be: “Mikpɔ ɖa, tso egbe dzi la, meƒle mi kple miaƒe agbledeƒewo siaa na fia. Nukuwo nye esi; mixɔ wo yi ɖaƒã.

eng: I, your servant, guaranteed the young mans safety to my father, telling him, If I dont bring him back to you, it will be my fault forever.
local: Gawu la, metsɔ ɖokuinye dè megbe na ɖevia le tɔ́nye gbɔ. Medo ŋugbe nɛ be, ne nyemekplɔ ɖevia gbɔe o la, meɖi fɔ le egbɔ tegbee.

eng: Joseph hitched up his chariot and went to meet his father Israel in Goshen. When he arrived, he threw his arms around his neck and wept, embracing him for a long time.
local: Yosef dzra ɖo, eye wòɖo eƒe tasiaɖam la yi tɔ́a Israel kpe ge le Gosen. Esi wòdo go tɔ́a la, ekpla asi kɔ nɛ, eye wòfa avi ɖe edzi ʋuu.

eng: Then Joseph took them from Israels knees, and he bowed low with his face to the ground.
local: Emegbe Yosef kɔ ɖeviawo ɖa le tɔ́a ƒe ata ta, eye wòdze klo, de ta agu ɖe tɔ́a ŋkume.

eng: When the crop comes in, you must give one-fifth to Pharaoh. You may keep four-fifths for yourselves, for planting fields, and for feeding yourselves, those in your households, and your children.
local: Ne mieŋe nu la, mima nua ɖe akpa atɔ̃ me: Akpa ɖeka nanye fia tɔ, eye akpa ene mamlɛawo nanye miawo ŋutɔ tɔ. Miaƒã ɖe, eye esi susɔ nanye nuɖuɖu na miawo ŋutɔ, mia viwo kple mia ƒemetɔwo.”.

eng: Now, please let your servant stay as your slave instead of the young man so that he can go back with his brothers.
local: Eya ta amegã, meɖe kuku, nye la, matsi afii azu wò kluvi ɖe ɖevia teƒe, ne eya nagbugbɔ ayi aƒe kple nɔviawo.

eng: Israel said to Joseph, I can die now after seeing your face. You are really still alive.
local: Israel gblɔ na Yosef be: “Mate ŋu aku azɔ faa, elabe mekpɔ wò nègale agbe.”.

eng: Joseph took both of them, Ephraim in his right hand at Israels left hand, and Manasseh in his left hand at Israels right hand, and brought them close to him.
local: Azɔ Yosef lé via eveawo ɖe asi. Efrayim le eƒe ɖusime, si nye Israel ƒe miame, eye Manase le eƒe miame, si nye Israel ƒe ɖusime. Ekplɔ wo te ɖe tɔ́a ŋu.

eng: The people said, Youve saved our lives. If you wish, we will be Pharaohs slaves.
local: Woɖo eŋu be: “Wòe xɔ mí ɖe agbe! Ne edze nye aƒetɔ ŋu la, míanye Egipte Fiagã ƒe kluviwo.”.

eng: How can I go back to my father without the young man? I couldnt bear to see how badly my father would be hurt.
local: Ale ke mate ŋu atrɔ ayi tɔ́nye gbɔ, ɖevia manɔmee? Kpao! Nyemate ŋu akpɔ dzɔgbevɔ̃e gã si ava edzi la teƒe kple ŋku o.”.

eng: But Israel put out his right hand and placed it on the head of Ephraim, the younger one, and his left hand on Manassehs head, crossing his hands because Manasseh was the oldest son.
local: Ke Israel tso ga eƒe abɔwo, eye wòda eƒe nuɖusi ɖe Efrayim ƒe tame, togbɔ be eyae nye ɖevitɔ hã. Eda eƒe miasi ɖe Manase si nye tsitsitɔ la ƒe tame.

eng: Joseph said to his brothers and to his fathers household, Let me go up and inform Pharaoh and tell him, My brothers and my fathers household who were in the land of Canaan have arrived.
local: Tete Yosef gblɔ na nɔviawo kple tɔ́a ƒemetɔwo be: “Mayi aɖagblɔe na fia la be: ‘Nɔvinye kple tɔ́nye ƒometɔ siwo le Kanaan-nyigba dzi la va.

eng: So Joseph made a law that still exists today: Pharaoh receives one-fifth from Egypts farmland. Only the priests farmland didnt become Pharaohs.
local: Ale Yosef de se le Egipte-nyigba dzi be, ne woŋe nu la, nukuawo ƒe akpa atɔ̃lia nanye fia tɔ. Se sia gali va se ɖe egbe. Nunɔlawo ƒe anyigbawo koe menye fia tɔ o.

eng: He blessed them and said, May the God before whom my fathers Abraham and Isaac walked, may the God who was my shepherd from the beginning until this day.
local: Tete wòyra Yosef gblɔ be: “Mawu si ƒe ŋkume tɔ́nyewo Abraham kple Isak zɔ le, Mawu si kplɔm le nye agbe me ŋkekewo katã me va ɖo egbe.

eng: The men are shepherds, because they own livestock. Theyve brought with them their flocks and herds and everything they own.
local: Lãnyilawo wonye, eye wova kple woƒe lãhawo: alẽ, nyi kple woƒe nunɔamesiwo katã.’.

eng: Israel lived in the land of Egypt, in the land of Goshen. They settled in it, had many children, and became numerous.
local: Israel nɔ Gosen-nutome le Egipte-nyigba dzi. Wokpɔ kesinɔnu geɖe, eye wodzi, gadzi fũu ŋutɔ.

eng: may the divine messenger who protected me from all harm, bless the young men. Through them may my name be kept alive and the names of my fathers Abraham and Isaac. May they grow into a great multitude throughout the land.
local: mawudɔla si ɖem tso vɔ̃wo katã me la, neyra ɖevi siawo! Nye ŋkɔ, tɔ́nyewo Abraham kple Isak ŋkɔ, nenɔ ɖevi siawo ŋu ɖaa. Wonedzi ɖe edzi le anyigba la dzi.”.

eng: When Pharaoh summons you and says, What do you do.
local: Ne fia la yɔ mi, eye wòbia be: ‘Dɔ ka wɔlawo mienye?’ hã la.

eng: After Jacob had lived in the land of Egypt for seventeen years, and after he had lived a total of years.
local: Yakob nɔ Egipte-nyigba dzi ƒe wui adrẽ, eye eƒe agbenɔƒewo va le alafa ɖeka bla ene vɔ adrẽ.

eng: When Joseph saw that his father had placed his right hand on Ephraims head, he was upset and grasped his fathers hand to move it from Ephraims head to Manassehs head.
local: Esi Yosef kpɔ be ye tɔ́ da eƒe nuɖusi ɖe Efrayim ƒe tame la, medze eŋu o, eya ta elé tɔ́a ƒe asi be yeaɖee ɖa le Efrayim ƒe tame ada ɖe Manase tɔ.

eng: say, Your servants have owned livestock since we were young, both we and our ancestors, so that you will be able to settle in the land of Goshen, since Egyptians think all shepherds are beneath their dignity.
local: miɖo eŋu nɛ be: ‘Lãnyilawo wò dɔlawo nye tso ɖevime ke abe mía tɔgbuiwo ene.’ Ne miegblɔ nenema la, aɖe mɔ na mi mianɔ Gosen-nutome.” Elabe alẽkplɔlawo nye ŋunyɔnu na Egiptetɔwo.

eng: Israels death approached. He summoned his son Joseph and said to him, If you would be so kind, lay your hand under my thigh, and be loyal and true to me. Dont bury me in Egypt.
local: Esi Israel ƒe kuɣi ɖo la, ena woyɔ via Yosef vɛ, eye wògblɔ nɛ be: “Taflatsɛ, ne nye nya dze ŋuwò la, tsɔ asi de nye atame, eye nàdo ŋugbe be yeawɔ nuteƒe, ato nyateƒe nam. Mègaɖim ɖe Egipte-nyigba dzi gbeɖe o.

eng: Joseph said to his father, No, my father! This is the oldest son. Put your right hand on his head.
local: Yosef gblɔ na tɔ́a be: “Tɔ́nye, menye nenemae o. Ŋgɔgbetɔe nye esi; tsɔ wò nuɖusi da ɖe eya ƒe tame.”.

eng: When I lie down with my fathers, carry me from Egypt and bury me in their grave. Joseph said, I will do just as you say.
local: Medi be nàɖim ɖe tɔ́nyewo gbɔ. Kɔm dzoe le Egipte, eye nàɖaɖim ɖe woƒe ɖiƒe.” Yosef ɖo eŋu be: “Mawɔ nu si nègblɔ la.”.

eng: But his father refused and said, I know, my son, I know. Hell become a people too, and hell also be great. But his younger brother will be greater than he will, and his descendants will become many nations.
local: Ke tɔ́a gbe hegblɔ be: “Menya, vinye, menya! Eya hã ƒe dzidzimeviwo hã ava zu dukɔ gã aɖe. Gake nɔvia ɖevitɔ ade ŋgɔ wui, eye eƒe dzidzimeviwo ava zu dukɔ gãwo.”.

eng: Israel said, Give me your word! and Joseph gave his word. Then Israel slumped down at the head of the bed.
local: Egblɔ nɛ be: “Ka atam ɖe edzi nam!” Yosef ka atam nɛ. Israel bɔbɔ ta gbedoɖatɔe ɖe eƒe abati ƒe tade.

eng: Israel blessed them that day, saying, Through you, Israel will pronounce blessings, saying, May God make you like Ephraim and Manasseh. So Israel put Ephraim before Manasseh.
local: Ale wòyra wo gbe ma gbe gblɔ be: “Miaƒe ŋkɔ Israelviwo atsɔ ayra amee, agblɔ be: ‘Mawu neɖi na wò abe Efrayim kple Manase ene!’ ” To esia me la, Yakob trɔ Efrayim wòzu tsitsia na Manase.

eng: Then Israel said to Joseph, Im about to die. God will be with you and return you to the land of your fathers.
local: Azɔ Yakob gblɔ na Yosef be: “Kpɔ ɖa, nye kuɣi ɖo. Mawu anɔ kpli mi, eye wòagbugbɔ mi ayi mia tɔgbuiwo ƒe anyigba la dzi.

eng: Im giving you one portion more than to your brothers, a portion that I took from the Amorites with my sword and my bow.
local: Gawu la, metsɔ tonyigba si mexɔ le Amoritɔwo si kple nye ŋutɔ nye yi kple dati la na wò boŋ ɖe nɔviwòwo teƒe.”.

eng: Jacob summoned his sons and said, Gather around so that I can tell you what will happen to you in the coming days.
local: Yakob yɔ viawo vɛ, eye wògblɔ na wo be: “Miƒo ƒu, ne magblɔ nu si le dzɔdzɔ ge ɖe mia dzi le ɣeyiɣi siwo gbɔna me la na mi.

eng: Assemble yourselves and listen, sons of Jacob; listen to Israel your father.
local: “Yakob viwo, mite va ƒo ƒu, eye miɖo to. Mise nye, mia tɔ́, Israel, ƒe nya.”.

eng: Joseph fell across his fathers body, wept over him, and kissed him.
local: Tete Yosef dze tɔ́a dzi kple avi, eye wògbugbɔ nu nɛ.

eng: Reuben, you are my oldest son, my strength and my first contender, superior in status and superior in might.
local: “Ruben, nye ŋgɔgbevi, wòe nye nye ŋusẽ kple nye ɖekakpuimevi gbãtɔ. Èdze bubu, eye èsesẽ wu vinyewo katã.

eng: Joseph then ordered the physicians in his service to embalm his father, and the physicians embalmed Israel.
local: Emegbe Yosef ɖe gbe na eƒe dɔla atikewɔlawo be woasi atike na ye tɔ́. Ale atikewɔlawo si atike na Israel.

eng: As wild as the waters, you wont endure, for you went up to your fathers bed, you went up and violated my couch.
local: Ke èle abe tsi fiefie si gbagba la ene; màganye vi vevitɔ nam akpɔ o. Elabe ède tɔ́wò ƒe aba dzi, eye nèdo gu aba la. Ẽ, nye ŋutɔ nye aba dzi nède gbe ma gbe!”.

eng: They mourned for him forty days because that is the period required for embalming. Then the Egyptians mourned him for seventy days.
local: Atikesisi la xɔ ŋkeke bla ene elabe nu geɖe hiã nɛ. Egiptetɔwo fa nɛ ŋkeke bla adrẽ.

eng: Simeon and Levi are brothers, weapons of violence their stock in trade.
local: “Simeon kple Levi: nɔviŋutsuwo, wotsɔ woƒe lãnuwo do hlɔ̃e.

eng: After the period of mourning had passed, Joseph spoke to Pharaohs household: If you approve my request, give Pharaoh this message.
local: Le konyifaɣi la megbe la, Yosef gblɔ na Egipte Fiagã la ŋutimewo be: “Meɖe kuku, mive nunye ne miayi ɖagblɔ na fia be.

eng: May I myself never enter their council. May my honor never be linked to their group; for when they were angry, they killed men, and whenever they wished, they maimed oxen.
local: Nye luʋɔ, megade adaŋu kpli wo o. Nye gbɔgbɔ, megade ha kpli wo hã o, elabe wowua ame le dziku me, eye le woƒe movidzɔdzɔ me la, wotso drika na nyitsuwo.

eng: My father made me promise, telling me, Im about to die. You must bury me in the tomb I dug for myself in the land of Canaan. Now, let me leave and let me bury my father, and then I will return.
local: hafi tɔ́nye naku la, ena meka atam nɛ be ne yeku la, maɖi ye ɖe Kanaan-nyigba dzi, ɖe yɔdo si ye ŋutɔ yeɖe da ɖi la me. Eya ta meɖe kuku, miɖe mɔ nam ne mayi aɖaɖii ɖe afi ma agatrɔ agbɔ.”.

eng: Cursed be their anger; it is violent, their rage; it is relentless. Ill divide them up within Jacob and disperse them within Israel.
local: Woƒe dziku neɖi wo ŋu, elabe ŋɔdzi le eŋu. Woƒe dɔmedzoe neɖi wo ŋu, elabe evɔ̃ɖi. Mama wo me ɖe Yakob ƒe anyigba dzi, eye maka wo ahlẽ ɖe Israel-nyigba dzi.”.

eng: Pharaoh replied, Go, bury your father as you promised.
local: Egipte Fiagã ɖo eŋu be: “Yi ɖaɖi tɔ́wò abe ale si wòna nèka atam nɛ la ene.”.

eng: These are the names of the Israelites who came to Egypt with Jacob along with their households.
local: Israel viŋutsu siwo yi Egipte kple Yakob la ŋkɔwoe nye esiwo gbɔna. Ɖe sia ɖe yi kple eƒemetɔwo:.

eng: Judah, you are the one your brothers will honor; your hand will be on the neck of your enemies; your fathers sons will bow down to you.
local: “Yuda, nɔviwò ŋutsuwo akafu wò. Àlé vehlo na wò futɔwo; tɔ́viwòwo abɔbɔ ta na wò.

eng: So Joseph left to bury his father. All of Pharaohs servants went with him, together with the elder statesmen in his household and all of the elder statesmen in the land of Egypt.
local: Ale Yosef dze mɔ be yeaɖaɖi ye tɔ́. Egipte Fiagã ƒe subɔlawo katã, eŋutimewo kple dukɔmemegãwo katã yi kplii.

eng: Reuben, Simeon, Levi, and Judah.
local: Ruben, Simeon, Levi, Yuda.

eng: Judah is a lions cub; from the prey, my son, you rise up. He lies down and crouches like a lion; like a lioness—who dares disturb him?.
local: Dzatavie nye Yuda. Le nuléle ta, vinye, ànɔ to dzi. Emlɔ anyi tsyɔ akɔ abe dzatatsu ene, eye abe dzatanɔ ene; ame ka ate ŋu aʋuʋui?.

eng: Josephs entire household, his brothers, and his fathers household. Only the children, flocks, and cattle remained in the land of Goshen.
local: Yosef ƒe ƒometɔwo katã, nɔviawo kple tɔ́a ƒe ƒometɔwo katã hã yi kplii. Wo vi suewo, alẽwo kple gbɔ̃wo kpakple nyiwo ko wogblẽ ɖe megbea le Gosen-nyigba dzi.

eng: Issachar, Zebulun, and Benjamin.
local: Isaxar, Zebulon, Benyamin.

eng: The scepter wont depart from Judah, nor the rulers staff from among his banners. Gifts will be brought to him; people will obey him.
local: Atamkayi mavɔ le Yuda si o, eye sewɔtikplɔ maɖe ɖa le ekɔme akpɔ o, va se ɖe esime woava xe adzɔ nɛ, eye dukɔwo aɖo toe.

eng: Even chariots and horsemen went with him; it was a huge collection of people.
local: Amewo le tasiaɖamwo me, eye ɖewo le sɔwo dzi kplɔe yii. Ameha si yi la sɔ gbɔ ŋutɔ.

eng: Dan and Naphtali, Gad and Asher.
local: Dan, Naftali, Gad kple Aser.

eng: He ties his male donkey to the vine, the colt of his female donkey to the vines branches. He washes his clothes in wine, his garments in the blood of grapes.
local: Ade ka eƒe tedzivi ɖe wainka ŋu, eye wòade ka eƒe tedzinɔ ƒe vi ɖe wainka nyui ƒe alɔdzewo ŋu. Anya eƒe avɔ le wain me, eye wòanya eƒe awu le waintsetse me tsi me.

eng: When they arrived at the threshing floor of Atad on the other side of the Jordan River, they observed a solemn, deeply sorrowful period of mourning. He grieved seven days for his father.
local: Esi wova ɖo lugbɔƒe le Atad le Yordan ƒe ɣedzeƒe gome la, wofa Yakob vevie kple ɣli. Wofa na tɔ́a ŋkeke adrẽ.

eng: The total number in Jacobs family was seventy. Joseph was already in Egypt.
local: Yakob vi kple tɔgbuiyɔvi siawo katã ƒe xexlẽme de bla adrẽ. Via Yosef ya nɔ Egipte xoxo.

eng: His eyes are darker than wine, and his teeth whiter than milk.
local: Wain ana eƒe ŋku nabiã hẽe; notsi ana eƒe aɖu nafu tititi.”.

eng: When the Canaanites who lived in the land saw the observance of grief on Atads threshing floor, they said, This is a solemn observance of grief by the Egyptians. Therefore, its name is Abel-mizraim. It is on the other side of the Jordan River.
local: Kanaantɔ siwo nye anyigba la dzi tɔwo la kpɔ kunua teƒe le Atad, eye wogblɔ be: “Egiptetɔwo va le kunu tɔxɛ aɖe wɔm.” Eya ta woyɔa teƒe ma be Abel-Mizraim.

eng: Eventually, Joseph, his brothers, and everyone in his generation died.
local: Yosef kple nɔvia ŋutsuawo kpakple dzidzime ma katã va ku.

eng: Zebulun will live at the seashore; hell live at the harbor of ships, his border will be at Sidon.
local: “Zebulon anɔ ƒutanyigba dzi. Ʋudzeƒewo anɔ eƒe ƒu nu, eye eƒe anyigba akeke ade keke Sidon ke.”.

eng: Israels sons did for him just as he had ordered.
local: Ale Yakob viŋutsuawo wɔ nu si wògblɔ be wonewɔ na ye la nɛ.

eng: But the Israelites were fertile and became populous. They multiplied and grew dramatically, filling the whole land.
local: Ke Israelviwo va dzi fũu. Wova sɔ gbɔ hekpɔ ŋusẽ ale gbegbe, eye wokaka ɖe anyigba la ƒe afi sia afi.

eng: Issachar is a sturdy donkey, bedding down beside the village hearths.
local: “Isaxar nye tedzi sesẽ. Etsyɔ akɔ anyi ɖe lãkpɔwo dome.

eng: His sons carried him to the land of Canaan and buried him in the cave in the field of Machpelah near Mamre, which Abraham had purchased as burial property from Ephron the Hittite.
local: Esi woɖo Kanaan la, woɖii ɖe kpeto si le Maxpela, le Mamre ƒe ɣedzeƒe lɔƒo la me. Kpeto sia le agble si Abraham ƒle le Efron, Xititɔ la si be wòanye yeƒe ameɖiƒe la me.

eng: Now a new king came to power in Egypt who didnt know Joseph.
local: Eva eme be fia yeye aɖe si menya naneke le Yosef ŋu o la va zi dzi le Egipte.

eng: He saw that a resting place was good and that the land was pleasant. He lowered his shoulder to haul loads and joined the work gangs.
local: Ekpɔ be dzudzɔƒe la dze eme na ye, eye anyigba la nyo. Ale wòbi abɔta be yeatsɔ agba, eye wozi edzi wòzu kluvi agbatsɔla.”.

eng: Then Joseph returned to Egypt, he, his brothers, and everyone who left with him to bury his father.
local: Esi Yosef ɖi tɔ́a vɔ la, eya kple nɔviawo kpakple ame siwo katã kpe ɖe eŋu yi amea ɖiƒe la trɔ gbɔ va Egipte.

eng: He said to his people, The Israelite people are now larger in number and stronger than we are.
local: Eya gblɔ na eƒe dukɔ la be: “Mikpɔ ɖa, Israelviwo va sɔ gbɔ wu mí. Wole ŋusẽ hã kpɔm wu mí.

eng: Dan will settle disputes for his people, as one of Israels tribes.
local: “Dan akplɔ eƒe dukɔ abe Israel-to bubuwo ke ene.

eng: When Josephs brothers realized that their father was now dead, they said, What if Joseph bears a grudge against us, and wants to pay us back seriously for all of the terrible things we did to him.
local: Esi Yosef nɔviwo kpɔ be yewo tɔ́ ku la, wogblɔ be: “Ɖewohĩ Yosef alé fu mí, eye wòaɖo nu vlo siwo katã míewɔ ɖe eŋu la teƒe na mí.”.

eng: Come on, lets be smart and deal with them. Otherwise, they will only grow in number. And if war breaks out, they will join our enemies, fight against us, and then escape from the land.
local: Mina míadze aye ɖe wo ŋu, be womagadzi fũu ɖe edzi o. Ne menye nenema o, ne aʋa dzɔ la, woava de míaƒe futɔwo dzi ɖe mía ŋu, akpɔ mɔnu así dzo le míaƒe anyigba la dzi.”.

eng: Dan will be a snake on the road, a serpent on the path, biting a horses heels, so its rider falls backward.
local: Dan anye da tsomɔ, dzakpata le afɔmɔ dzi. Eɖua sɔ ƒe afɔkpodzi, eye sɔdola dzea anyi megbemegbee.

eng: So they approached Joseph and said, Your father gave orders before he died, telling us.
local: Ale wodɔ ame ɖo ɖe Yosef gbɔ be: “Tɔ́wò gblɔ nya sia na mi hafi ku:.

eng: Now a man from Levis household married a Levite woman.
local: Eva eme be ŋutsu aɖe tso Levi-to la me ɖaɖe Levi-nyɔnu aɖe.

eng: As a result, the Egyptians put foremen of forced work gangs over the Israelites to harass them with hard work. They had to build storage cities named Pithom and Rameses for Pharaoh.
local: Ale Egiptetɔwo tia dɔdzikpɔlawo ɖo Israelviwo nu be woawɔ funyafunya wo, aɖo dɔ sesẽ na wo. Wona Israelviwo tsò nudzraɖodu na Egipte Fiagã la. Du siawoe nye Pitom kple Ramses.

eng: I long for your victory, LORD.
local: O Aƒetɔ, wò xɔname la ko lalam mele!”.

eng: This is what you should say to Joseph. Please, forgive your brothers sins and misdeeds, for they did terrible things to you. Now, please forgive the sins of the servants of your fathers God. Joseph wept when they spoke to him.
local: ‘Migblɔ na Yosef be: Taflatsɛ, tsɔ nɔviwòwo ƒe vodada kple nuvɔ̃ ke wo, elabe wowɔ vɔ̃ ɖe ŋuwò.’ Azɔ míeɖe kuku na wò, tsɔ tɔ́wò ƒe Mawu la ƒe dɔlawo ƒe vodada ke wo.” Yosef fa avi esi woƒo nu kplii.

eng: The woman became pregnant and gave birth to a son. She saw that the baby was healthy and beautiful, so she hid him for three months.
local: Nyɔnua fɔ fu eye wòdzi ŋutsuvi. Ekpɔ be ɖevia dze ɖeka ŋutɔ. Ale wòɣlae ɣleti etɔ̃ sɔŋ.

eng: But the more they were oppressed, the more they grew and spread, so much so that the Egyptians started to look at the Israelites with disgust and dread.
local: Gake zi geɖe ale si Egiptetɔwo le Israelviwo tem ɖe anyi la, nenema ke wole dzidzim ɖe edzi, le ta kekem wu. Ale Egiptetɔwo de asi vɔvɔ̃ me na Israelviawo.

eng: Gad will be attacked by attackers, but hell attack their back.
local: “Adzohawo ava dze Gad dzi; ke ayɔ̀ wo ɖe nu, aha wo.”.

eng: His brothers wept too, fell down in front of him, and said, Were here as your slaves.
local: Azɔ nɔviawo ŋutɔ yi egbɔ. Wodze klo, de ta agu nɛ, eye wogblɔ be: “Kpɔ ɖa, míawoe nye esi. Wò kluviwo míenye.”.

eng: So the Egyptians enslaved the Israelites.
local: Esia dɔ Egiptetɔwo be wogazi Israelviwo dzi wu, eye wona wowɔ dɔ kutɔkutɔe.

eng: When she couldnt hide him any longer, she took a reed basket and sealed it up with black tar. She put the child in the basket and set the basket among the reeds at the riverbank.
local: Esi megate ŋu le ɖevia ɣlam o la, edi kusi si wolɔ̃ kple aɖa eye wotree kple aŋɔ la, tsɔ ɖevi la de eme, eye wòtsɔe yi ɖada ɖe aƒlawo dome le tɔsisia to.

eng: Asher grows fine foods, and he will supply the kings delicacies.
local: “Aser ƒe nuɖuɖu ada mi, eye fia ƒe nuɖuɖu viviwo atso egbɔ.”.

eng: They made their lives miserable with hard labor, making mortar and bricks, doing field work, and by forcing them to do all kinds of other cruel work.
local: Wote wo ɖe anyi kple dɔ sesẽ wɔwɔ ale gbegbe be agbenɔnɔ ve helihelĩ le woƒe ve me. Egiptetɔwo na Israelviwo nya anyi tsɔ me anyikpee. Wo zi wo dzi wowɔ agbledɔ vovovowo hã. Wona wowɔ dɔ siawo nublanuimakpɔmakpɔtɔe.

eng: The babys older sister stood watch nearby to see what would happen to him.
local: Ɖevia nɔvinyɔnu bè ɖe adzɔge be yeakpɔ ɖa be nu kae adzɔ ɖe edzi mahã.

eng: Naphtali is a wild doe that gives birth to beautiful fawns.
local: “Naftali nye zinɔ le eɖokui si. Edzia vi nyakpɔwo.”.

eng: The king of Egypt spoke to two Hebrew midwives named Shiphrah and Puah.
local: Tete Egipte Fiagã la gblɔ na Hebri-vixela siwo ƒe ŋkɔwoe nye Sifra kple Pua la be:.

eng: Moses was taking care of the flock for his father-in-law Jethro, Midians priest. He led his flock out to the edge of the desert, and he came to Gods mountain called Horeb.
local: Mose nɔ toa Yetro si nye Midiantɔwo ƒe nunɔla la ƒe lãwo dzi kpɔm. Gbe ɖeka esi wòkplɔ lãawo yi gbedzi la, eva do ɖe Xoreb, Mawu ƒe to la gbɔ.

eng: Pharaohs daughter came down to bathe in the river, while her women servants walked along beside the river. She saw the basket among the reeds, and she sent one of her servants to bring it to her.
local: Egipte Fiagã vinyɔnu va tsi le ge le tɔsisia me. Enɔ tsia lem eye eƒe subɔlawo nɔ tsa ɖim le tɔa to. Kasia eƒe ŋku kle kusia le aƒlaawo me ɖaa, eye wòna eƒe dɔlanyɔnu yi ɖatsɔe vɛ.

eng: Joseph is a young bull, a young bull by a spring, who strides with oxen.
local: “Yosef nye atilɔ tseku le tsidzɔƒe. Eɖe alɔ flɔ gli ta.

eng: When you are helping the Hebrew women give birth and you see the baby being born, if its a boy, kill him. But if its a girl, you can let her live.
local: Ne miele vi xem na Hebri-nyɔnu aɖe, eye miekpɔ be ŋutsu dzim wòle la, miwu ɖevia; ke nenye nyɔnu dzim wòle la, mina ɖevia natsi agbe.”.

eng: When she opened it, she saw the child. The boy was crying, and she felt sorry for him. She said, This must be one of the Hebrews children.
local: Esi wòʋui la, ekpɔ vidzĩ le eme. Ŋutsuvi ye; enɔ avi fam. Eƒe nu wɔ nublanui nɛ eye wògblɔ be: “Hebritɔwo ƒe viwo dometɔ ɖekae nye esia.”.

eng: The LORDs messenger appeared to him in a flame of fire in the middle of a bush. Moses saw that the bush was in flames, but it didnt burn up.
local: Tete Aƒetɔ ƒe dɔla ɖe eɖokui fiae le dzobibi me le ŋugbe aɖe me. Esi wòɖe ŋku ɖe eŋu la, ekpɔ be dzoa le bibim gake gbea mele fiafiãm o.

eng: They attacked him fiercely and fired arrows; the archers attacked him furiously.
local: Eƒe futɔ aŋutrɔdalawo dze edzi, tee, eye woɖe fu nɛ vevie.

eng: Now the two midwives respected God so they didnt obey the Egyptian kings order. Instead, they let the baby boys live.
local: Gake vixelaawo vɔ̃a Mawu, eya ta womewɔa fia la ƒe sea dzi o; ke boŋ wonana be ŋutsuviawo tsia agbe.

eng: Then the babys sister said to Pharaohs daughter, Would you like me to go and find one of the Hebrew women to nurse the child for you.
local: Tete nɔvia nyɔnu la te va. Ebia fiavinyɔnua be: “Mayi ɖadi Hebri-nyɔnu vi nɔ aɖe vɛ, ne wòana no ɖevia na wòa?”.

eng: Then Moses said to himself, Let me check out this amazing sight and find out why the bush isnt burning up.
local: Mose gblɔ be: “Mayi aɖakpɔ nu dziŋɔ sia ɖa: nu si ta gbea mele fiafiãm o!”.

eng: But his bow stayed strong, and his forearms were nimble, by the hands of the strong one of Jacob, by the name of the shepherd, the rock of Israel.
local: Ke eƒe dati meʋã o. Yakob ƒe Mawu ŋusẽtɔ la, wɔ eƒe asi wòle tsɔtsɔm to Alẽkplɔla, Israel ƒe Agakpe la ŋkɔ me.

eng: Then Moses replied, But what if they dont believe me or pay attention to me? They might say to me, The LORD didnt appear to you.
local: Tete Mose ɖo eŋu be: “Ke ne womexɔ dzinye se o, alo womese nye gbe hã o, ke wogblɔ be: ‘Aƒetɔ meɖe eɖokui fia wò fɔ̃e o ɖe.’ ”.

eng: So the king of Egypt called the two midwives and said to them, Why are you doing this? Why are you letting the baby boys live.
local: Ale Egipte Fiagã yɔ vixelaawo, eye wòbia wo be: “Nu ka ta miena ŋutsuviawo le agbe tsim?”.

eng: Pharaohs daughter agreed, Yes, do that. So the girl went and called the childs mother.
local: Egipte Fiagã vinyɔnua ɖo eŋu be: “Yi.” Nyɔnuvia yi ɖayɔ vidzĩ la dada vɛ.

eng: When the LORD saw that he was coming to look, God called to him out of the bush, Moses, Moses! Moses said, Im here.
local: Esi Aƒetɔ kpɔ be edze be yeakpɔ nua ɖa la, eyɔe tso ŋugbea me be: “Mose, Mose!” Mose tɔ be: “Nyee nye esi.”.

eng: by God, your father, who supports you, by the Almighty who blesses you with blessings from the skies above and blessings from the deep sea below, blessings from breasts and womb.
local: To tɔ́wò ƒe Mawu, ame si akpe ɖe ŋuwò, to Mawu Ŋusẽkatãtɔ, ame si ayra wò kple yayra tso dziƒo, yayra tso agume ke, yayra tso no nu kple vidzidɔ me, la dzi.

eng: The LORD said to him, Whats that in your hand? Moses replied, A shepherds rod.
local: Aƒetɔ biae be: “Nu kae nye ema le asiwò?” Eɖo eŋu be: “Atizɔti ye.”.

eng: The two midwives said to Pharaoh, Because Hebrew women arent like Egyptian women. Theyre much stronger and give birth before any midwives can get to them.
local: Vixelaawo ɖo eŋu na Egipte Fiagã be: “Hebri-nyɔnuwo mele abe Egipte-nyɔnuwo ene o. Ŋusẽ le wo ŋu ŋutɔ, ale be kasia vixela naɖo Hebri-nyɔnu si le ku lém la gbɔ la, eya ŋutɔ dzi via xoxo.”.

eng: Pharaohs daughter said to her, Take this child and nurse it for me, and Ill pay you for your work. So the woman took the child and nursed it.
local: Fiavinyɔnua gblɔ nɛ be: “Xɔ ɖevi sia nàna noe nam. Maxe fe na wò.” Nyɔnua kɔ vidzĩa dzoe, eye.

eng: Then the LORD said, Dont come any closer! Take off your sandals, because you are standing on holy ground.
local: Aƒetɔ gblɔ nɛ be: “Mègate va afii o. Ɖe wò atokota le afɔ, elabe anyigba si dzi nètsi tsitre ɖo la, anyigba kɔkɔe ye.”.

eng: The blessings of your father exceed the blessings of the eternal mountains, the wealth of the everlasting hills. May they all rest on Josephs head, on the forehead of the one set apart from his brothers.
local: Tɔ́wò ƒe yayra gbɔ to mavɔwo tɔ kple togbɛ mavɔwo ƒe nu gbogboawo ŋu. Na nenɔ Yosef ƒe ta dzi, eye wòanɔ ame si wosa ɖe aga la ƒe ŋgonu.”.

eng: The LORD said, Throw it down on the ground. So Moses threw it on the ground, and it turned into a snake. Moses jumped back from it.
local: Aƒetɔ gblɔ nɛ be: “Dae ɖe anyigba.” Edae ɖe anyigba eye atia trɔ zu da. Mose sí le enu.

eng: So God treated the midwives well, and the people kept on multiplying and became very strong.
local: Ale Mawu wɔ nyui na vixelaawo. Dukɔ la dzi ɖe edzi hesẽ ŋu hã.

eng: After the child had grown up, she brought him back to Pharaohs daughter, who adopted him as her son. She named him Moses, because, she said, I pulled him out of the water.
local: wòɖakpɔ edzi. Esi ɖevia tsi la, ekplɔe vɛ na Egipte Fiagã vinyɔnu la. Exɔe wɔ viae, eye wòtsɔ ŋkɔ nɛ be Mose hegblɔ be: “Elabe tɔ mee meɖee tsoe.

eng: Afterward, Moses and Aaron went to Pharaoh and said, This is what the LORD, Israels God, says: Let my people go so that they can hold a festival for me in the desert.
local: Emegbe Mose kple Aron woyi Egipte Fiagã la gbɔ ɖagblɔ nɛ be: “Aƒetɔ, Israel Mawu be: “Ɖe asi le nye dukɔ ŋu ne woaɖaɖu ŋkekenyui nam le gbedadaƒo.”.

eng: He continued, I am the God of your father, Abrahams God, Isaacs God, and Jacobs God. Moses hid his face because he was afraid to look at God.
local: Egagblɔ be: “Nyee nye tɔgbuiwòwo ƒe Mawu, Abraham ƒe Mawu, Isak ƒe Mawu kple Yakob ƒe Mawu.” Tete Mose tsyɔ nu ŋkume elabe enɔ vɔvɔ̃m be yeakpɔ Mawu.

eng: Benjamin is a wolf who hunts: in the morning he devours the prey; in the evening he divides the plunder.
local: “Benyamin nye amegãxi nuvuvula. Le ŋdi la, eɖua nu si wòlé la; fiẽ la, emaa aboyonuwo.”.

eng: Then the LORD said to Moses, Reach out and grab the snake by the tail. So Moses reached out and grabbed it, and it turned back into a rod in his hand.
local: Aƒetɔ gblɔ nɛ be: “Do asi ɖa nàlé eƒe asikɛ.” Mose do asi ɖa lée. Tete daa gazu atizɔti ɖe esi.

eng: And because the midwives respected God, God gave them households of their own.
local: Esi vixelaawo vɔ̃a Mawu ta la, ena woƒe ƒomewo li ke.

eng: One day after Moses had become an adult, he went out among his people and he saw their forced labor. He saw an Egyptian beating a Hebrew, one of his own people.
local: Esi Mose va tsi la, eɖi tsa yi nɔvia Hebritɔwo gbɔ gbe ɖeka, eye wòkpɔ wo wole woƒe dzizizidɔawo wɔm. Tete wòkpɔ Egiptetɔ aɖe wòle Hebritɔ aɖe ƒom.

eng: But Pharaoh said, Who is this LORD whom Im supposed to obey by letting Israel go? I dont know this LORD, and I certainly wont let Israel go.
local: Ke Egipte Fiagã la ɖo eŋu be: “Ame kae nye Aƒetɔ ta maɖo toe, aɖe asi le Israelviwo ŋu woayi? Nyemenya Aƒetɔ la o, eye nyemaɖe asi le Israelviwo ŋu woayi o.”.

eng: Then the LORD said, Ive clearly seen my people oppressed in Egypt. Ive heard their cry of injustice because of their slave masters. I know about their pain.
local: Aƒetɔ gblɔ nɛ be: “Mekpɔ ale si wole funyafunya wɔm nye dukɔ le Egipte. Mese ɣli si dom wole be woaɖe yewo tso dɔdzikpɔla siwo te wo ɖe anyi la si me.

eng: These are the twelve tribes of Israel, and this is what their father said to them. He blessed them by giving each man his own particular blessing.
local: Israel-to wui eveawoe nye esiawo, eye nya mamlɛtɔ siwo wo tɔ́a gblɔ na wo dome ɖe sia ɖe tsɔ yrae abe ale si dze nɛ ene lae nye esiawo.

eng: Do this so that they will believe that the LORD, the God of their ancestors, Abrahams God, Isaacs God, and Jacobs God has in fact appeared to you.
local: Aƒetɔ gblɔ nɛ be: “Wɔ nu sia ekema Israelviwo axɔe se be nye Aƒetɔ, wo tɔgbuiwo ƒe Mawu: Abraham ƒe Mawu, Isak ƒe Mawu kple Yakob ƒe Mawu la, meɖe ɖokuinye fia wò vavã.”.

eng: Then Pharaoh gave an order to all his people: Throw every baby boy born to the Hebrews into the Nile River, but you can let all the girls live.
local: Tete Egipte Fiagã de se na eƒe dukɔ blibo la be: “Ne Hebri-nyɔnu dzi ŋutsuvi la, mitsɔ ɖevia ƒu gbe ɖe Nil-tɔsisi me; ke ne edzi nyɔnuvi la, mina ɖevia natsi agbe.”.

eng: He looked around to make sure no one else was there. Then he killed the Egyptian and hid him in the sand.
local: Mose tsa ŋku, tsa ŋku, eye esi mekpɔ ame aɖeke o la, eƒo Egiptetɔ la wu, eye wòlɔ ke ƒo ɖe edzi.

eng: Jacob ordered them, I am soon to join my people. Bury me with my ancestors in the cave thats in the field of Ephron the Hittite.
local: Tete Yakob gblɔ na viawo be: “Fifia la, mele dzodzo ge ayi tɔgbuinyewo gbɔ. Miɖim ɖe wo xa le kpeto si le Efron, Xititɔ la ƒe agble me la me.

eng: Then they said, The Hebrews God has appeared to us. Let us go on a three-day journey into the desert so we can offer sacrifices to the LORD our God. Otherwise, the LORD will give us a deadly disease or violence.
local: Mose kple Aron gagblɔ nɛ be: “Hebritɔwo ƒe Mawu la ɖe eɖokui fia mí. Ɖe mɔ na mí ne míazɔ ŋkeke etɔ̃ ƒe mɔ ayi gbedadaƒo aɖasa vɔ na Aƒetɔ, mía Mawu la, ne menye nenema o la, adze mía dzi kple dɔ vɔ̃ loo, alo awu mí kple yi.”.

eng: Ive come down to rescue them from the Egyptians in order to take them out of that land and bring them to a good and broad land, a land thats full of milk and honey, a place where the Canaanites, the Hittites, the Amorites, the Perizzites, the Hivites, and the Jebusites all live.
local: Ẽ, menya woƒe fukpekpewo eya ta meɖi va be maɖe wo tso Egiptetɔwo si me, eye makplɔ wo ayi anyigba nyui aɖe dzi. Anyigba sia keke, eye notsi kple anyitsi bɔ ɖe edzi. Afi ma Kanaanitɔwo, Xititɔwo, Amoritɔwo Perizitɔwo, Xivitɔwo kple Yebusitɔwo le.

eng: Again, the LORD said to Moses, Put your hand inside your coat. So Moses put his hand inside his coat. When he took his hand out, his hand had a skin disease flaky like snow.
local: Aƒetɔ gagblɔ nɛ be: “Ƒo asi ɖe wò awu ƒe akɔta.” Eƒoe ɖee. Esi wohee ɖe go la, dɔ ƒo ɖe asia ŋu wòfu kpii abe tsikpe ene.

eng: The LORD replied to Moses, Now you will see what Ill do to Pharaoh. In fact, hell be so eager to let them go that hell drive them out of his land by force.
local: Gake Aƒetɔ gblɔ na Mose be: “Azɔ àkpɔ nu si mele Egipte Fiagã la wɔ ge. Elabe alɔ sesẽe wòatsɔ nyà wo do goe, ẽ atsɔ alɔ sesẽ anyà wo ɖa le eƒe anyigba dzii.”.

eng: When Moses went out the next day, he saw two Hebrew men fighting with each other. Moses said to the one who had started the fight, Why are you abusing your fellow Hebrew.
local: Evea gbe la, Mose gayi nɔviawo gbɔ. Kasia Hebritɔ evee nye esiwo le avu wɔm. Ebia ame si tɔ medzɔ o la be: “Nu ka ŋuti nèle nɔviwò ƒom?”.

eng: in the cave thats in the field of Machpelah near Mamre in the land of Canaan that Abraham bought from Ephron the Hittite as a burial property.
local: Kpeto sia le Maxpela, le Mamre ƒe ɣedzeƒe lɔƒo le Kanaan-nyigba dzi. Tɔgbuinye Abraham ƒle agblea kple kpetoa le Efron si be wòanye yeƒe ameɖiƒe.

eng: The king of Egypt said to them, Moses and Aaron, why are you making the people slack off from their work? Do the hard work yourselves.
local: Ke Egipte-fia la ɖo eŋu na wo be: “Mose kple Aron, nu ka ŋuti miele didim be yewoana Israelviwo nagblẽ woƒe dɔdeasiwo ɖi adzo? Mitrɔ yi miaƒe dɔ sesẽ la wɔwɔ dzi.”.

eng: Now the Israelites cries of injustice have reached me. Ive seen just how much the Egyptians have oppressed them.
local: Israelviwo ƒe ɣli ɖi mese eye mekpɔ ale si Egiptetɔwo le wo tem ɖe anyii.

eng: Then God said, Put your hand back inside your coat. So Moses put his hand back inside his coat. When he took it back out again, the skin of his hand had returned to normal.
local: Tete Mawu be: “Gaƒo asi ɖe awua me.” Egaƒoe ɖee, eye esi wògaɖee la, asia ŋu gakɔ keŋkeŋ abe ale si wònɔ tsã ene.

eng: But Joseph said to them, Dont be afraid. Am I God?.
local: Ke Yosef gblɔ na wo be: “Migavɔ̃ o. Mawu teƒe melea?.

eng: God also said to Moses: I am the LORD.
local: Mawu gblɔ na Mose be: “Nyee nye Aƒetɔ.

eng: He replied, Who made you a boss or judge over us? Are you planning to kill me like you killed the Egyptian? Then Moses was afraid when he realized: They obviously know what I did.
local: Eɖo eŋu na Mose be: “Ame kae ɖo wò amegã kple ʋɔnudrɔ̃la ɖe mía nu? Ɖe nèbe yeawu nye hã abe ale si nèwu Egiptetɔ ma enea?” Mose gblɔ be: “Nya la ɖi; amewo see!” Ale vɔvɔ̃ ɖoe.

eng: Pharaoh continued, The lands people are now numerous. Yet you want them to stop their hard work.
local: Egipte Fiagã la gblɔ be: “Azɔ esi ameawo sɔ gbɔ ɖe edzi le anyigbaa dzi ale hafi miebe woadzudzɔ woƒe dɔ sesẽa wɔwɔa?”.

eng: That is where Abraham and his wife Sarah are buried, and where Isaac and his wife Rebekah are buried, and where I buried Leah.
local: Afi mae woɖi Abraham ŋutɔ kple srɔ̃a Sara ɖo. Afi ma kee woɖi Isak kple srɔ̃a Rebeka ɖo, eye eyae nye hã meɖi Lea ɖo.

eng: So get going. Im sending you to Pharaoh to bring my people, the Israelites, out of Egypt.
local: Eya ta va, madɔ wò ɖe Egipte Fiagã gbɔ be nàɖe nye dukɔ Israelviwo tso esi me, eye nàkplɔ wo dzoe le Egipte.”.

eng: If they wont believe you or pay attention to the first sign, they may believe the second sign.
local: Aƒetɔ gblɔ nɛ be: “Ne womexɔ wò dzi se o, eye womebu dzesi gbãtɔa o la, woaxɔ evelia ya dzi ase.

eng: You planned something bad for me, but God produced something good from it, in order to save the lives of many people, just as hes doing today.
local: Miawo mieɖoe be yewoawɔ vɔ̃m, ke Mawu trɔe wòzu nyui, be wòaxɔ ame geɖe ɖe agbe abe ale si wòdzɔ miele kpɔkpɔm egbe ene.

eng: I appeared to Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob as God Almighty, but I didnt reveal myself to them by my name The LORD.
local: Nyee ɖe ɖokuinye fia Abraham, Isak kple Yakob abe Mawu Ŋusẽkatãtɔ ene. Gake nyemena wonya be ŋkɔnyee nye Aƒetɔ o.

eng: When Pharaoh heard about it, he tried to kill Moses. But Moses ran away from Pharaoh and settled down in the land of Midian. One day Moses was sitting by a well.
local: Esi Egipte Fiagã va se nyaa la, edi Mose be yeawu. Tete Mose sí le enu yi Midian-nyigba dzi. Eɖanɔ vudo aɖe to.

eng: It is the field and the cave in it that belonged to the Hittites.
local: Ɖe woƒle agblea kple kpeto si le eme la le Xiti viwo si. Miɖi nye hã ɖe afi ma.”.

eng: On the very same day Pharaoh commanded the peoples slave masters and supervisors.
local: Gbe ma gbe ke la, fia la de se na kluviwo dzikpɔlawo kple wo nunɔlawo be:.

eng: But Moses said to God, Who am I to go to Pharaoh and to bring the Israelites out of Egypt.
local: Ke Mose gblɔ na Mawu be: “Ame ka menye be maɖado ɖe Egipte Fiagã ŋkume eye be makplɔ Israelviwo adzoe le Egipte?”.

eng: If they wont believe even these two signs or pay attention to you, then take some water from the Nile River and pour it out on dry ground. The water that you take from the Nile will turn into blood on the dry ground.
local: Ne womexɔ dzesi eveawo dzi se o, eye womexɔ wò hã dzi se o la, ekema ku tsi le Nil-tɔsisia me nàkɔ ɖe anyigba ƒuƒui; tsia atrɔ zu ʋu ɖe anyigba.”.

eng: Now, dont be afraid. I will take care of you and your children. So he put them at ease and spoke reassuringly to them.
local: Eya ta migavɔ̃ o. Malé be na mi kple mia viwo siaa.” Ale Yosef fa akɔ na wo, eye wòde dzi ƒo na wo.

eng: I also set up my covenant with them to give them the land of Canaan where they lived as immigrants.
local: Mebla nu kpli wo hã be matsɔ Kanaan-nyigba, anyigba si dzi wonɔ abe amedzrowo ene la ana wo wòazu wo tɔ.

eng: Now there was a Midianite priest who had seven daughters. The daughters came to draw water and fill the troughs so that their fathers flock could drink.
local: Midian nunɔla vinyɔnu adrẽ wova tsi du ge akɔ ɖe lãwo ƒe tsinonuwo me na wo tɔ́ ƒe lãwo.

eng: After he finished giving orders to his sons, he put his feet up on the bed, took his last breath, and joined his people.
local: Esi Yakob gblɔ nya siawo na viawo vɔ la, eƒo ƒu ata ɖe aba dzi, mia nu, eye wòyi tɔgbuiawo gbɔ.

eng: God said, Ill be with you. And this will show you that Im the one who sent you. After you bring the people out of Egypt, you will come back here and worship God on this mountain.
local: Mawu ɖo eŋu nɛ be: “Manɔ kpli wò; eye dzesi si nàkpɔ anya be nyee dɔ ye ɖa lae nye be: ne èkplɔ dukɔ la do goe tso Egipte la, miasubɔm le to sia dzi.”.

eng: Dont supply the people with the straw they need to make bricks like you did before. Let them go out and gather the straw for themselves.
local: “Tso azɔ heyi, migana gbe Israelviwo woade anyi me amè anyikpeawoe o. Woawo ŋutɔ naɖadi gbe amè anyikpe lae.

eng: But Moses said to the LORD, My Lord, Ive never been able to speak well, not yesterday, not the day before, and certainly not now since youve been talking to your servant. I have a slow mouth and a thick tongue.
local: Gake Mose gagblɔ na Aƒetɔ be: “Taflatsɛ, nye Aƒetɔ, nu menya ƒona nam o; menye egbe o, menye etsɔ o, eye menye tso esime nèdze nuƒoƒo gɔme nam hã o. Nye aɖe metrɔna ɖe nya ŋu o.”.

eng: Thus Joseph lived in Egypt, he and his fathers household. Joseph lived years.
local: Yosef ganɔ Egipte kple tɔ́a ƒemetɔwo. Exɔ ƒe alafa ɖeka kple ewo.

eng: Ive also heard the cry of grief of the Israelites, whom the Egyptians have turned into slaves, and Ive remembered my covenant.
local: Gawu la, mese konyi si fam Israelviwo, ame siwo Egiptetɔwo te ɖe anyi la, eye meɖo ŋku nu, si mebla kpli wo la dzi.

eng: But some shepherds came along and rudely chased them away. Moses got up, rescued the women, and gave their flock water to drink.
local: Lãkplɔla aɖewo va nyà wo ɖa. Mose tso ʋli wo ta eye wòku tsi na wo tɔ́ ƒe lãawo wono.

eng: But still make sure that they produce the same number of bricks as they made before. Dont reduce the number! They are weak and lazy, and thats why they cry, Lets go and offer sacrifices to our God.
local: Ke kpe ale sinu womèna tsã la ke koe wòle be woamè. Migaɖe le edzi kura o. Kuvia wɔm wole ta wole ɣli dzi be: ‘Na míayi aɖasa vɔ na mía Mawu.’.

eng: But Moses said to God, If I now come to the Israelites and say to them, The God of your ancestors has sent me to you, they are going to ask me, Whats this Gods name? What am I supposed to say to them.
local: Mose bia Mawu be: “Ne meyi Israelviwo gbɔ, eye megblɔ na wo be: ‘Mia tɔgbuiwo ƒe Mawu lae dɔm ɖe mia gbɔ,’ eye wobiam be: ‘Eŋkɔ ɖe?’ hã la, ale ke maɖo eŋu na wo?”.

eng: Then the LORD said to him, Who gives people the ability to speak? Whos responsible for making them unable to speak or hard of hearing, sighted or blind? Isnt it I, the LORD?.
local: Tete Aƒetɔ biae be: “Ame kae wɔ nu na ame? Ame kae nana be ame ƒe to kuna alo ame ƒe aɖe tuna? Ame kae nana be ame kpɔa nu alo nyea ŋkuagbãtɔ? Ɖe menye nye, Aƒetɔ ye oa?.

eng: and saw Ephraims grandchildren. The children of Machir, Manassehs son, were also born on Josephs knees.
local: Enɔ agbe tsò Efrayim ƒe tɔgbuitɔgbuiyɔviwo ɖo, eye wònɔ anyi xɔ Maxir, Manase vi, hã viwo ɖe ata ta.

eng: Therefore, say to the Israelites, I am the LORD. Ill bring you out from Egyptian forced labor. Ill rescue you from your slavery to them. Ill set you free with great power and with momentous events of justice.
local: Eya ta gblɔ na Israel-dukɔ be: ‘Nyee nye Aƒetɔ. Maɖe mi le Egiptetɔwo ƒe kɔkuti te. Maɖe mi le woƒe kluvinyenye me, eye maɖe mi tso wo si me ale be miaganye kluviwo na wo o. Mamlo nye abɔ me atsɔ nye ŋusẽ triakɔ la atu nu kpli wo.

eng: When they went back home to their father Reuel, he asked, How were you able to come back home so soon today.
local: Esi wotrɔ yi aƒeme la, wo tɔ́ si ŋkɔe nye Reguel la, bia wo be: “Ale ke wɔ miegbɔ kaba egbe ya ale?”.

eng: God said to Moses, I Am Who I Am. So say to the Israelites, I Am has sent me to you.
local: Mawu gblɔ na Mose be: “AME SI MENYE LAE MENYE.” Eyi edzi be: “Gblɔ na Israelviwo be: ‘MENYE ye dɔm ɖe mia gbɔ.’ ”.

eng: Make the mens work so hard that its all they can do, and they cant focus on these empty lies.
local: Mizi wo dzi eye mite wo ɖe anyi ne woagawɔ dɔ sesĩe, ekema womaɖo to alakpanya mawo o.”.

eng: Now go! Ill help you speak, and Ill teach you what you should say.
local: Eya ta heyi ko; makpe ɖe ŋuwò nàƒo nu eye mafia nya si nàgblɔ la wò.”.

eng: Joseph said to his brothers, Im about to die. God will certainly take care of you and bring you out of this land to the land he promised to Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob.
local: Gbe ɖeka Yosef gblɔ na nɔviawo be: “Nye kuɣi ɖo; Mawu aɖo ŋku mia dzi, eye wòakplɔ mi tso anyigba sia dzi ayi anyigba si ŋugbe wòdo na Abraham, Isak kple Yakob la dzi.”.

eng: Ill take you as my people, and Ill be your God. You will know that I, the LORD, am your God, who has freed you from Egyptian forced labor.
local: Mawɔ mi mianye nye ŋutɔ nye dukɔ, eye manye miaƒe Mawu. Mianyae be nyee nye Aƒetɔ, mia Mawu, si ɖe mi le Egiptetɔwo ƒe agba kpekpe la te.

eng: They replied, An Egyptian man rescued us from a bunch of shepherds. Afterward, he even helped us draw water to let the flock drink.
local: Woɖo eŋu be: “Egipte-ŋutsu aɖee ʋli mía ta le lãkplɔlawo gbɔ, eye wòdu tsi na míaƒe alẽwo wono gɔ̃ hã.”.

eng: So the peoples slave masters and supervisors came out and spoke to the people, This is what Pharaoh says, Im not giving you straw anymore.
local: Tete kluviwo dzikpɔlaawo kple woƒe dɔnunɔlawo do yi ɖagblɔ na Israelviwo be: “Egipte Fiagã la be yemegale gbe na ge mi miatsɔ amè anyikpe la azɔ o.

eng: God continued, Say to the Israelites, The LORD, the God of your ancestors, Abrahams God, Isaacs God, and Jacobs God, has sent me to you. This is my name forever; this is how all generations will remember me.
local: Mawu gagblɔ na Mose be: “Gblɔ na Israelviwo be: “Aƒetɔ, mia tɔgbuiwo ƒe Mawu: Abraham ƒe Mawu, Isak ƒe Mawu kple Yakob ƒe Mawue dɔm ɖe mia gbɔ. Esiae nye nye ŋkɔ tegbee. Ŋkɔ sia nye nye ŋkɔ tso dzidzime yi dzidzime.

eng: But Moses said, Please, my Lord, just send someone else.
local: Tete Mose gabe: “Gake, ao, nye Aƒetɔ, meɖe kuku, di ame bubu nàdɔ.”.

eng: Joseph made Israels sons promise, When God takes care of you, you must bring up my bones out of here.
local: Tete Yosef na Israelviwo ka atam nɛ be: “Ne Mawu va mia kplɔ ge la, mitsɔ nye ƒuwo tso afii yii.”.

eng: Ill bring you into the land that I promised to give to Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob. Ill give it to you as your possession. I am the LORD.
local: Makplɔ mi ayi anyigba si meka atam be matsɔ na Abraham, Isak kple Yakob la dzi, eye matsɔe ana mi wòanye miawo ŋutɔ tɔ. Nyee nye Aƒetɔ.’ ”.

eng: Reuel said to his daughters, So where is he? Why did you leave this man? Invite him to eat a meal with us.
local: Ebia via nyɔnuawo be: “Ŋutsua ɖe? Ale ke wɔ miegblẽe ɖe afi ma? Miyi ɖayɔe ne wòava ɖu nu.”.

eng: Go and get the straw on your own, wherever you can find it. But your work wont be reduced at all.
local: Miawo ŋutɔ miyi ɖadi gbe le afi sia afi si miakpɔe le la amèe. Ke kpe ale sinu miemèna tsã la ke koe miagamè. Womaɖe ɖeke le dzi o.”.

eng: Go and get Israels elders together and say to them, The LORD, the God of your ancestors, the God of Abraham, of Isaac, and of Jacob, has appeared to me. The LORD said, Ive been paying close attention to you and to what has been done to you in Egypt.
local: Yi nàƒo Israelviwo ƒe ame tsitsiwo nu ƒu, eye nàgblɔ be nye Aƒetɔ, mia tɔgbuiwo ƒe Mawu, Abraham ƒe Mawu, Isak ƒe Mawu kple Yakob ƒe Mawu, ɖe eɖokui fiam, gblɔ be, ‘Nye ŋku le mia ŋu, eye menya nu si wole mia wɔm le Egipte.

eng: Then the LORD got angry at Moses and said, What about your brother Aaron the Levite? I know he can speak very well. Hes on his way out to meet you now, and hes looking forward to seeing you.
local: Aƒetɔ do dziku ɖe eŋu azɔ, eye wògblɔ be: “Nɔviwò Aron Levitɔ la meli oa? Menya be eƒoa nu nyuie. Egbɔna gɔ̃ be yeava kpe wò. Ne eva ke ɖe ŋuwò la, dzi adzɔe ŋutɔ.

eng: Joseph died when he was years old. They embalmed him and placed him in a coffin in Egypt.
local: Yosef ku le Egipte esi wòxɔ ƒe alafa ɖeka kple ewo. Wosi atike na eƒe kukua, eye wokɔe de aɖaka me le Egipte.

eng: Moses told this to the Israelites. But they didnt listen to Moses, because of their complete exhaustion and their hard labor.
local: Mose gblɔ nya siawo na Israelviwo; ke womeɖo to se eƒe nyaawo o, le ale si gbegbe woƒe dzi gbã kple fu si gbegbe kpem wole le woƒe kluvinyenye la me la ta.

eng: Moses agreed to come and live with the man, who gave his daughter Zipporah to Moses as his wife.
local: Mose lɔ̃ be yeatsi ŋutsua gbɔ koŋ, eye ŋutsua tsɔ via nyɔnu Zipora nɛ wòɖe.

eng: So the people spread out all through the land of Egypt to gather stubble for straw.
local: Ale Israel-dukɔ blibo la kaka ɖe Egipte-nyigba la katã dzi hele gbe dim.

eng: Ive decided to take you away from the harassment in Egypt to the land of the Canaanites, the Hittites, the Amorites, the Perizzites, the Hivites, and the Jebusites, a land full of milk and honey.
local: Eya ta medo ŋugbe be maɖe mi tso Egiptetɔwo ƒe fuwɔame la me, eye makplɔ mi ayi Kanaanitɔwo, Xititɔwo, Amoritɔwo, Xivitɔwo kple Yebusitɔwo ƒe anyigba dzi; anyigba si dzi notsi kple anyitsi bɔ ɖo la.’.

eng: Speak to him and tell him what hes supposed to say. Ill help both of you speak, and Ill teach both of you what to do.
local: Ƒo nu kplii ne nàtsɔ nya de enu. Nye la, makpe ɖe mia ŋu miaƒo nu nyuie, eye mafia nu si miawɔ la mi.

eng: Then the LORD said to Moses.
local: Aƒetɔ gblɔ na Mose be:.

eng: She gave birth to a son, and Moses named him Gershom, because, he said, Ive been an immigrant living in a foreign land.
local: Zipora dzi viŋutsuvi nɛ. Mose na ŋkɔe be Gersom hegblɔ be: “Amedzro menye le dzronyigba dzi”.

eng: The slave masters drove them hard and said, Make sure you make the same daily quota as when you had the straw.
local: Ke kluviwo dzikpɔlaawo yɔa ʋu ɖe wo te gblɔna be: “Dɔa netsɔ! Gbe sia gbe ƒe dɔ si miewɔna tsã, esi wonaa gbe mi la kee wòle be miawɔ.”.

eng: Aaron will speak for you to the people. Hell be a spokesperson for you, and you will be like God for him.
local: Eya naƒo nu kple dukɔ la ɖe nuwò. Anye wò nu, eye wò la, nànɔ Mawu teƒe nɛ.

eng: They will accept what you say to them. Then you and Israels elders will go to Egypts king and say to him, The LORD, the Hebrews God, has met with us. So now let us go on a three-day journey into the desert so that we can offer sacrifices to the LORD our God.
local: Dukɔ la aɖo to wò. Ekema wò kple Israelviwo ƒe ame tsitsiwo miayi Egipte-fia la gbɔ, eye miagblɔ nɛ be: ‘Aƒetɔ, Hebritɔwo ƒe Mawu la ɖe eɖokui fia mí; eya ta ɖe mɔ na mí míazɔ ŋkeke etɔ̃ ƒe mɔ ayi gbedzi, aɖasa vɔ na Aƒetɔ, mía Mawu la.’.

eng: Go and tell Pharaoh, Egypts king, to let the Israelites out of his land.
local: “Yi ɖagblɔ na Egipte Fiagã la, be neɖe asi le Israelviwo ŋu ne woadzo le eƒe anyigba dzi.”.

eng: A long time passed, and the Egyptian king died. The Israelites were still groaning because of their hard work. They cried out, and their cry to be rescued from the hard work rose up to God.
local: Le ƒè geɖee megbe la, Egipte-fia va ku. Israelviwo nɔ ŋèŋem le woƒe kluvikɔkuti sesẽ la te. Wodo ɣli, eye Mawu se woƒe ɣlidodo la.

eng: The Israelite supervisors, whom Pharaohs slave masters had set over them, were also beaten and asked, Why didnt you produce the same number of bricks yesterday and today as you did before.
local: Ne anyikpeawo mede o la, Egipte Fiagã ƒe dɔdzikpɔlawo ƒoa Israelvi siwo nye dɔnunɔlawo eye wobiaa wo be: “Nu ka ta anyikpeawo mede xexlẽme xoxoa nu egbe o?”.

eng: Take this shepherds rod with you too so that you can do the signs.
local: Nàlé atizɔti sia ɖe asi elabe eyae nèle tsɔtsɔ ge awɔ dzesiawoe.”.

eng: However, I know that Egypts king wont let you go unless hes forced to do it.
local: Menyae be Egipte-fia la maɖe mɔ na mi miayi o, negbe ɖeko woazi edzi hafi.

eng: But Moses said to the LORD, The Israelites havent even listened to me. How can I expect Pharaoh to listen to me, especially since Im not a very good speaker.
local: Ke Mose gblɔ na Aƒetɔ be: “Kpɔ ɖa, esi Israelviwo gɔ̃ hã meɖo tom o ɖe, ale ke wɔ Egipte Fiagã ya boŋ laɖo to nye, ame si nu menyaƒona na kura o la?”.

eng: God heard their cry of grief, and God remembered his covenant with Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.
local: Mawu se woƒe ŋèŋe eye wòɖo ŋku nu, si wòbla kple Abraham, Isak kple Yakob la dzi.

eng: Then the Israelite supervisors came and pleaded to Pharaoh, Why do you treat your servants like this?.
local: Azɔ Israelvi dɔnunɔlawo yi ɖafa konyi na Egipte Fiagã gblɔ be: “Nu ka ta nèle mí, wò dɔlawo wɔm ale?.

eng: Moses went back to his father-in-law Jethro and said to him, Please let me go back to my family in Egypt and see whether or not they are still living. Jethro said to Moses, Go in peace.
local: Tete Mose trɔ gbɔ va toa Yetro gbɔ va gblɔ nɛ be: “Taflatsɛ, ɖe mɔ nam ne matrɔ yi nɔvinyewo gbɔ le Egipte aɖakpɔ ɖa be wogale agbe mahã.” Yetro ɖo eŋu be: “Heyi le ŋutifafa me.”.

eng: So Ill use my strength and hit Egypt with dramatic displays of my power. After that, hell let you go.
local: Ale matsɔ nye ŋusẽ kple nukunu siwo katã mawɔ le wo dome la, atu nu kple Egipte, eye eya megbe la, ana miadzo.

eng: Nevertheless, the LORD spoke to Moses and Aaron about the Israelites and Pharaoh, Egypts king, giving them orders to let the Israelites go from the land of Egypt.
local: Tete Aƒetɔ ƒo nu na Mose kple Aron, eye wòdɔ wo ɖe Israel-dukɔ la kple Egipte Fiagã la gbɔ be woakplɔ Israelviwo dzoe le Egipte-nyigba dzi.

eng: God looked at the Israelites, and God understood.
local: Mawu trɔ kɔ kpɔ Israelviwo eye wònya nu si nɔ dzɔdzɔm ɖe wo dzi.

eng: No straw is supplied to your servants, yet they say to us, Make bricks! Look at how your servants are being beaten! Your own people are to blame.
local: Womegale gbe na mí o, gake ɣe sia ɣi ko wole gbɔgblɔm nam mí be ‘Mimè anyikpe! Mimè anyikpe’ eye woƒoa mí gɔ̃, evɔ wò amewo gbɔ boŋue vodada la tso.”.

eng: The LORD said to Moses in Midian, Go back to Egypt because everyone there who wanted to kill you has died.
local: Aƒetɔ gblɔ na Mose le Midian be: “Gbugbɔ yi Egipte, elabe ame siwo katã nɔ didim be yewoawu wò la ku.”.

eng: Ill make it so that when you leave Egypt, the Egyptians will be kind to you and you wont go away empty-handed.
local: Mana gɔ̃ hã be Egiptetɔwo ƒe dɔme atrɔ ɖe dukɔ sia ŋu ale be ne miele dzodzom la, miayi asi ƒuƒlu o.

eng: These were the leaders of their households. The descendants of Reuben, Israels oldest son: Hanoch, Pallu, Hezron, and Carmi. These were Reubens clans.
local: Wo tɔgbuiwo ƒe aƒekɔtatɔwoe nye esiawo: Ruben, Israel ƒe ŋgɔgbeviŋutsu viŋutsuwoe nye: Henox, Palu, Xezron kple Karmi. Woawo mee Ruben-to la dzɔ tso.

eng: Pharaoh replied, You are lazy bums, nothing but lazy bums. Thats why you say, Let us go and offer sacrifices to the LORD.
local: Ke fia la ɖo eŋu na wo be: “Kuviatɔwo! Alɔgblɔdɔwɔlawo mienye, eya ta miele biabiam be: ‘Ɖe mɔ na mí ne míayi aɖasa vɔ na Aƒetɔ.’.

eng: So Moses took his wife and his children, put them on a donkey, and went back to the land of Egypt. Moses also carried the shepherds rod from God in his hand.
local: Mose kɔ srɔ̃a kple via ŋutsuwo ɖo tedzi dzi, eye wòtrɔ yi Egipte. Etsɔ Mawu ƒe atizɔti la ɖe asi.

eng: Every woman will ask her neighbor along with the immigrant in her household for their silver and their gold jewelry as well as their clothing. Then you will put it on your sons and daughters, and you will rob the Egyptians.
local: Israel-nyɔnu ɖe sia ɖe nabia klosalotsyɔ̃ɖonu, sikanuwo kple nudodowo le eƒe aƒelika kple amedzro si le eƒeme la si. Miado nu siawo na mia viŋutsuwo kple mia vinyɔnuwo adzoe. To esia me, miaha Egiptetɔwo.”.

eng: The Simeonites: Jemuel, Jamin, Ohad, Jachin, Zohar, and Shaul, a Canaanite womans son. These were Simeons clans.
local: Simeon viŋutsuwoe nye: Yemuel, Yamin, Ohad, Yaxin, Zoxar kple Saul, ame si nye Kanaan-nyɔnu ƒe vi. Woawo mee Simeon-to la dzɔ tso.

eng: Go and get back to work! No straw will be given to you, but you still need to make the same number of bricks.
local: Midzo yi kaba miaɖawɔ dɔ! Womana gbe aɖeke mi o. Ke ele be miamè anyikpe xexlẽme si woɖo na mi la kokoko.”.

eng: The LORD said to Moses, When you go back to Egypt, make sure that you appear before Pharaoh and do all the amazing acts that Ive given you the power to do. But Ill make him stubborn so that he wont let the people go.
local: Aƒetɔ gblɔ na Mose be: “Ne èɖo Egipte la, ɖo ŋku edzi nàwɔ nukunu siwo katã metsɔ de asi na wò la le Egipte Fiagã la ŋkume. Mana eƒe dzime nasẽ ale be maɖe asi le dukɔ la ŋu woadzo o.

eng: These were the Levites names by their generations: Gershon, Kohath, and Merari. Levi lived years.
local: Levi viŋutsuwo ƒe ŋkɔwoe nye: Gerson, Kahat kple Merari. Levi nɔ agbe ƒe alafa ɖeka bla etɔ̃ vɔ adrẽ.

eng: The Israelite supervisors saw how impossible their situation was when they were commanded, Dont reduce your daily quota of bricks.
local: Esesẽ ŋutɔ na Israelvi dɔdzikpɔlawo be woaɖagblɔ na wo tɔwo be womekpɔ mɔ aɖe le anyikpe xexlẽme si woɖo na wo be woamè gbe sia gbe la dzi o.

eng: Then say to Pharaoh, This is what the LORD says: Israel is my oldest son.
local: Ekema nàgblɔ na Egipte Fiagã la be: ‘Ale Aƒetɔ gblɔ nye esi: Israel la vinye ŋutsu ye; nye ŋgɔgbevi wònye.

eng: The Gershonites: Libni and Shimei and their clans.
local: Gerson viŋutsuwoe nye Libni kple Simei. Dzidzimeviwo nɔ ɖe sia ɖe si.

eng: When they left Pharaoh, they met Moses and Aaron, who were waiting for them.
local: Esime wodzo le Egipte Fiagã la gbɔ la, wodo go Mose kple Aron siwo nɔ wo lalam.

eng: I said to you, Let my son go so he could worship me. But you refused to let him go. As a result, now Im going to kill your oldest son.
local: Megblɔ na wò be: ‘Ɖe asi le vinye ŋu ne woaɖasubɔm.’ Gake ègbe mèɖe mɔnɛ o; eya ta mawu wò ŋgɔgbeviŋutsu.’ ”.

eng: The Kohathites: Amram, Izhar, Hebron, and Uzziel. Kohath lived years.
local: Kahat viŋutsuwoe nye: Amram, Yizhar, Xebron kple Uziel. Kahat nɔ agbe ƒe alafa ɖeka bla etɔ̃ vɔ etɔ̃.

eng: The supervisors said to them, Let the LORD see and judge what youve done! Youve made us stink in the opinion of Pharaoh and his servants. Youve given them a reason to kill us.
local: Wogblɔ na wo be: “Aƒetɔ nelé ŋku ɖe mia ŋu ne wòadrɔ̃ ʋɔnu kpli mi, elabe miena Egipte Fiagã la kple eŋumewo lé fu mí vevie. Miede yi asi na wo be woawu mí.”.

eng: During their journey, as they camped overnight, the LORD met Moses and tried to kill him.
local: Esi wole mɔa dzi va ɖo dzeƒe aɖe la, Aƒetɔ va, eye wòdi be yeawu Mose.

eng: The Merarites: Mahli and Mushi. These were the Levite clans by their generations.
local: Merari viŋtsuwoe nye Maxeli kple Masi. Ame siawo mee Levi ƒome la dzɔ tso.

eng: Then Moses turned to the LORD and said, My Lord, why have you abused this people? Why did you send me for this?.
local: Tete Mose gatrɔ ɖe Aƒetɔ ŋu eye wò gblɔ be: “Aƒetɔ, nu ka ta nèhe vɔ̃ va dukɔ sia dzi? Nu ka koŋ ta nèdɔm ɖa ya?.

eng: Amram married Jochebed, his fathers sister. She gave birth to Aaron and Moses. Amram lived years.
local: Amram ɖe tɔ́a nɔvinyɔnu Yoxebed, eye wòdzi Aron kple Mose nɛ. Amram nɔ agbe ƒe alafa ɖeka bla etɔ̃ vɔ adrẽ.

eng: But Zipporah took a sharp-edged flint stone and cut off her sons foreskin. Then she touched Moses genitals with it, and she said, You are my bridegroom because of bloodshed.
local: Tete Zipora tsɔ kpe ɖaɖɛ lã via ŋutsu ƒe aʋanuyi. Etsɔ aʋanuyi la tɔ eƒe afɔ, eye wògblɔ nɛ be: “Ʋusrɔ̃ nènye nam baa!”.

eng: Ever since I first came to Pharaoh to speak in your name, he has abused this people. And youve done absolutely nothing to rescue your people.
local: Elabe tso esime meva Egipte Fiagã la gbɔ va ƒo nu le wò ŋkɔ me la, ɖeko wògale ŋuta sẽm le wò dukɔ la ŋu wu. Ke wò la, mèɖe wò dukɔ la kura o.”.

eng: So the LORD let him alone. At that time, she announced, A bridegroom because of bloodshed by circumcision.
local: Tete Aƒetɔ megawɔ nu vevi Mose o. Le aʋatsotso la ta, Zipora da ŋkɔ ɖe srɔ̃a dzi be, “Ʋuŋugbetɔsrɔ̃.”.

eng: The Izharites: Korah, Nepheg, and Zichri.
local: Yizhar viŋtsuwoe nye: Korax, Nefeg kple Sixri.

eng: The LORD said to Aaron, Go into the desert to meet Moses. So he went, and Aaron met him at Gods mountain and greeted him with a kiss.
local: Azɔ Aƒetɔ gblɔ na Aron be: “Yi gbedzi nàɖakpe Mose.” Aron yi eye wòɖakpe Mose le Mawu ƒe to la gbɔ. Egbugbɔ nu nɛ.

eng: The Uzzielites: Mishael, Elzaphan, and Sithri.
local: Uziel viŋutsuwoe nye: Misael, Elzafan kple Sitri.

eng: Aaron married Elisheba, Amminadabs daughter and Nahshons sister. She gave birth to Nadab, Abihu, Eleazar, and Ithamar.
local: Aron ɖe Eliseba, Aminadab vinyɔnu, si nye Naxason nɔvinyɔnu eye wòdzi Nadab, Abihu, Eleazar kple Itamar nɛ.

eng: Moses told Aaron what the LORD had said about his mission and all the signs that the LORD had told him to do.
local: eye Mose gblɔ dɔ si gbe Aƒetɔ dɔe ɖo, nya siwo katã wòto nɛ kple dzesi siwo keŋ wòna ŋusẽe be wòawɔ la na Aron.

eng: The Korahites: Assir, Elkanah, and Abiasaph. These were the Korahite clans.
local: Korax viŋutsuwoe nye: Asir, Elkana kple Abiasaf. Woawoe nye Korax ƒome la ƒe tɔgbuitɔgbuiwo.

eng: Then Moses and Aaron called together all the Israelite elders.
local: Tete Mose kple Aron woyi Egipte, eye woyɔ Israelviwo ƒe ame tsitsiwo katã ƒo ƒu.

eng: Aarons son Eleazar married one of Putiels daughters. She gave birth to Phinehas. These were the leaders of Levite households by their clans.
local: Eleazar, Aron vi ɖe Putiel vinyɔnuwo dometɔ ɖeka, ame si dzi Pinehas nɛ. Ame siawo nye Levi-to la ƒe tɔgbuitɔgbuiwo kple woƒe ƒomeawo.

eng: Aaron told them everything that the LORD had told to Moses, and he performed the signs in front of the people.
local: Aron gblɔ nya siwo katã Aƒetɔ gblɔ na Mose la na wo, eye Mose wɔ dzesiawo hã wokpɔ.

eng: It was this same Aaron and Moses whom the LORD commanded, Bring the Israelites out of the land of Egypt in military formation.
local: Aron kple Mosee Aƒetɔ gblɔ na be: “Mikplɔ Israelviwo ƒe toawo dzoe le Egipte-nyigba dzi.”.

eng: The people believed. When they heard that the LORD had paid attention to the Israelites and had seen their oppression, they bowed down and worshipped.
local: Ameawo xɔ Mose kple Aron dzi se, eye esi wose be Aƒetɔ va Israelviwo gbɔ, eye wòkpɔ ale si Egiptetɔwo le fu wɔm wo la, wode ta agu hesubɔe.

eng: It was also this same Moses and Aaron who spoke to Pharaoh king of Egypt to bring the Israelites out of Egypt.
local: Woawoe yi ɖagblɔ na Egipte Fiagã, Egipte-fia la be neɖe mɔ ne Israelviwo nadzo le eƒe anyigba dzi. Mose sia kple Aron sia ye.

eng: At the time the LORD spoke to Moses in the land of Egypt.
local: Esi Aƒetɔ ƒo nu kple Mose le Egipte-nyigba dzi la.

eng: the LORD said to him, I am the LORD. Tell Pharaoh, Egypts king, everything that Ive said to you.
local: egblɔ nɛ be: “Nyee nye Aƒetɔ. Yi ɖagblɔ nya siwo katã megblɔ na wò la na Egipte Fiagã, Egipte-fia la.”.

eng: But Moses replied to the LORD, Look, Im not a very good speaker. How is Pharaoh ever going to listen to me.
local: Ke Mose gblɔ na Aƒetɔ be: “Kpɔ ɖa, wò ŋutɔ ènya be nu menyaƒona nam o. Ale ke wɔ Egipte Fiagã la aɖo tomee?”.

eng: The LORD said to Moses, See, Ive made you like God to Pharaoh, and your brother Aaron will be your prophet.
local: Aƒetɔ gblɔ na Mose be: “Kpɔ ɖa, mewɔ wò abe Mawu ene ɖe Egipte Fiagã dzi, eye mana nɔviwò Aron nanye wò gbeƒãɖela.

eng: You will say everything that I command you, and your brother Aaron will tell Pharaoh to let the Israelites out of his land.
local: Gblɔ gbe siwo katã mede asiwò la na Aron, eye eya negblɔ na Egipte Fiagã be nena mɔ Israelviwo be woadzo le eƒe anyigba dzi.

eng: But Ill make Pharaoh stubborn, and Ill perform many of my signs and amazing acts in the land of Egypt.
local: Ke mana Egipte Fiagã ƒe dzime nasẽ eye mawɔ dzesi kple nukunu geɖe le Egipte-nyigba dzi.

eng: When Pharaoh refuses to listen to you, then Ill act against Egypt and Ill bring my people the Israelites out of the land of Egypt in military formation by momentous events of justice.
local: ke Fiagã la maɖo to mi o. Maɖo asi Egipte dzi akplɔ nye aʋakɔ, nye dukɔ Israelviwo, ado goe tso Egipte-nyigba dzi to nu gã siwo mawɔ atsɔ ahe toe na wo la me.

eng: The Egyptians will come to know that I am the LORD, when I act against Egypt and bring the Israelites out from among them.
local: Ne meho kple ŋusẽ ɖe Egipte ŋu, eye mekplɔ Israelviwo dzoe la, ekema Egiptetɔwo adze sii be nyee nye Aƒetɔ.”.

eng: Then the LORD said to Moses, Go to Pharaoh and tell him: This is what the LORD says: Let my people go so that they can worship me.
