local: Azɔ Aƒetɔ gblɔ na Mose be: “Yi Egipte Fiagã la gbɔ nàɖagblɔ nɛ be Aƒetɔ be: ‘Ɖe asi le nye dukɔ ŋu ne woayi aɖasubɔm.

eng: Moses and Aaron did just as the LORD commanded them.
local: Mose kple Aron wɔ nu si Aƒetɔ gblɔ na wo be woawɔ la dzi pɛpɛpɛ.

eng: If you refuse to let them go, then Ill send a plague of frogs over your whole country.
local: Ne ègbe la, mana akpɔkplɔ nadzi ɖe anyigba la katã dzi abe dɔléle ene.

eng: Moses was years old and Aaron was when they spoke to Pharaoh.
local: Ɣesiɣi woƒo nu kple Egipte Fiagã la la, Mose xɔ ƒe bla enyi, eye Aron xɔ ƒe bla enyi vɔ etɔ̃.

eng: The Nile will overflow with frogs. Theyll get into your palace, into your bedroom and onto your bed, into your officials houses, and among all your people, and even into your ovens and bread pans.
local: Nil-tɔsisi azu akpɔkplɔ ɖeɖe, eye woaté bìbibi ava wò fiasã me, woage ɖe wò xɔdɔme me, adzo ɖe wò abati dzi. Woabɔ ɖe wò subɔlawo kple ŋuwòmewo ƒe aƒewo me. Woage ɖe wò abolokpowo kple abolodugugbawo me.

eng: The LORD said to Moses and Aaron.
local: Aƒetɔ gblɔ na Mose kple Aron be:.

eng: The frogs will crawl up on you, your people, and all your officials.
local: Akpɔkplɔawo adzo ɖe wò ŋutɔ dziwò, wò dumeviwo kple wò subɔlawo katã dzi, ado kplamatsɛ na mi atraɖii.’ ”.

eng: When Pharaoh says to you, Do one of your amazing acts, then say to Aaron, Take your shepherds rod and throw it down in front of Pharaoh, and it will turn into a cobra.
local: “Ne Egipte Fiagã la gblɔ na mi be: ‘Miwɔ nukunu aɖe míakpɔ’ la, ekema nàgblɔ na Aron be: ‘Tsɔ wò atizɔti da ɖe anyigba le Egipte Fiagã la ŋkume ne wòazu da.’ ”.

eng: And the LORD said to Moses, Tell Aaron, Stretch out your hand with your shepherds rod over the rivers, the canals, and the marshes, and make the frogs crawl up all over the land of Egypt.
local: Aƒetɔ gblɔ na Mose be: “Gblɔ na Aron be: ‘Tsɔ wò atikplɔ la nado asi ɖe tɔsisiwo, tɔʋuwo kple táwo dzi, ne akpɔkplɔwo nava Egipte-nyigba la dzi.”.

eng: So Moses and Aaron went to Pharaoh and did just as the LORD commanded. Aaron threw down his shepherds rod in front of Pharaoh and his officials, and it turned into a cobra.
local: Ale Mose kple Aron woyi Egipte Fiagã la gbɔ, eye wowɔ nu si Aƒetɔ gblɔ na wo be woawɔ la: Aron ɖe asi le eƒe atizɔti la ŋu da ɖe anyigba le Fiagã la kple eŋumewo ŋkume, eye wòtrɔ zu da.

eng: Then the LORD said to Moses, Go to Pharaoh and say to him, This is what the LORD, the Hebrews God, says: Let my people go so that they can worship me.
local: Azɔ Aƒetɔ gblɔ na Mose be: “Yi Egipte Fiagã la gbɔ, ne nàgblɔ nɛ be Aƒetɔ, Hebritɔwo ƒe Mawu la be: ‘Ɖe asi le nye dukɔ la ŋu, ne woaɖasubɔm.

eng: So Aaron stretched out his hand over the waters of Egypt. The frogs crawled up and covered the land of Egypt.
local: Aron do atizɔtia ɖe Egipte-nyigba dzi tsiwo dzi, eye akpɔkplɔwo do va kɔ ɖe anyigba bliboa dzi fũu.

eng: Then Pharaoh called together his wise men and wizards, and Egypts religious experts did the same thing by using their secret knowledge.
local: Tete fiagã la yɔ Egiptetɔwo ƒe nunyalawo kple dzosalawo vɛ, eye wotsɔ woƒe vivimeŋusẽwo wɔ nukunu ma tututu:.

eng: If you refuse to let them go and you continue to hold them back.
local: Ne ègbe asiɖeɖe le wo ŋu eye nègalé wo ɖi la.

eng: However, the Egyptian religious experts were able to do the same thing by their secret knowledge. They too made frogs crawl up onto the land of Egypt.
local: Ke Egipte-dzotɔwo hã tsɔ woƒe vivimeŋusẽwo he akpɔkplɔwo va Egipte-nyigba la dzii.

eng: Each one threw down his rod, and they turned into cobras. But then Aarons rod swallowed up each of their rods.
local: wo dometɔ ɖe sia ɖe tsɔ eƒe atizɔti da ɖe anyigba eye wotrɔ zu da, ke Aron ƒe atizɔti la lé wo tɔwo mi.

eng: the LORD will send a very deadly disease on your livestock in the field: on horses, donkeys, camels, cattle, and flocks.
local: kpɔ ɖa, nye Aƒetɔ, matsrɔ̃ wò lãhawo le gbedzi. Mana dɔléle dziŋɔ nadze wò lãwo katã dzi. Adze sɔwo, tedziwo, kposɔwo, nyihawo, gbɔ̃ kple alẽwo dzi.

eng: Then Pharaoh called for Moses and Aaron, and said, If you pray to the LORD to get rid of the frogs from me and my people, then Ill let the people go so that they can offer sacrifices to the LORD.
local: Tete Egipte Fiagã la yɔ Mose kple Aron, eye wògblɔ na wo be: “Mido gbe ɖa na Aƒetɔ ne wòaɖe akpɔkplɔawo ɖa le nye kple nye dukɔmetɔwo gbɔ, ekema maɖe asi le miaƒe dukɔ la ŋu woayi aɖasa vɔ na Aƒetɔ.”.

eng: However, Pharaoh remained stubborn. He wouldnt listen to them, just as the LORD had said.
local: Gake Egipte Fiagã la ƒe dzime gasẽ ko, eye abe ale si Aƒetɔ la gblɔe ene la, egbe be yemaɖo to wo o.

eng: But the LORD will distinguish Israels livestock from Egypts livestock so that not one that belongs to the Israelites will die.
local: Ke made vovototo Israelviwo ƒe lãwo kple Egiptetɔwo tɔwo dome ale be Israelviwo ƒe lã aɖeke maku o.’ ”.

eng: Moses said to Pharaoh, Have it your way. When should I pray for you and your officials and your people to remove the frogs from your houses, courtyards, and fields? Theyll stay only in the Nile.
local: Mose ɖo eŋu be: “Ele asiwò me. Ɣe ka ɣie maɖe kukua ɖe wò ŋutɔ, ŋuwòmewo kple wò dukɔ la ta, ne akpɔkplɔawo nadzo le ŋuwò kple miaƒe aƒewo me, eye woanɔ Nil-tɔsisia ɖeɖe ko me ya?”.

eng: Then the LORD said to Moses, Pharaoh is stubborn. He still refuses to let the people go.
local: Aƒetɔ gblɔ na Mose be: “Egipte Fiagã la ƒe dzime sẽ ŋutɔ; egbe be yemaɖe asi le dukɔa ŋu woadzo o.

eng: The LORD set a time and said, Tomorrow the LORD will do this in the land.
local: Aƒetɔ ɖo ɣeyiɣi gblɔ be: “Etsɔ nye, Aƒetɔ mawɔ nu sia le anyigba sia dzi.”.

eng: Pharaoh said, Tomorrow! Moses said, Just as you say! That way you will know that there is no one like the LORD our God.
local: Fiagã la xɔ edzi be: “Doe ɖa etsɔ.” Mose lɔ̃ be: “Yoo. Mawɔ wò gbe dzi ne nànyae be ame aɖeke meli abe Aƒetɔ, mía Mawu la ene o.

eng: Then the LORD said to Moses, Go to Pharaoh. Ive made him and his officials stubborn so that I can show them my signs.
local: Eyome Aƒetɔ gblɔ na Mose be: “Yi Egipte Fiagã la gbɔ. Nyee na eya ŋutɔ kple eŋumewo ƒe dzime sẽ, bene mawɔ dzesi siawo le Egipte-dukɔ la me.

eng: Go to Pharaoh in the morning. As he is going out to the water, make sure you stand at the bank of the Nile River so you will run into him. Bring along the shepherds rod that turned into a snake.
local: Eya ta etsɔ ŋdi kanya la, yi nàɖado goe le Nil-tɔsisi la to. Ne èyina la, tsɔ wò atizɔti si trɔ zu da la ɖe asi.

eng: And the next day the LORD did it. All of the Egyptian livestock died, but not one animal that belonged to the Israelites died.
local: Esi ŋu ke la, Aƒetɔ wɔ nu si wòbe yeawɔ la. Egiptetɔwo ƒe lãwo katã ku gake Israelviwo tɔ ya ƒe ɖeka gɔ̃ hã meku o.

eng: The frogs will leave you, your houses, your officials, and your people. Theyll stay only in the Nile.
local: Wò ŋutɔ kple wò aƒe, ŋuwòmewo kple wò dukɔmeviwo siaa miakpɔ gbɔdzɔe tso akpɔkplɔawo si me. Nil me ko woasusɔ ɖo.”.

eng: and so that you can tell your children and grandchildren how I overpowered the Egyptians with the signs I did among them. You will know that I am the LORD.
local: To esia me nàva xlẽ nu siwo mewɔ Egiptetɔwo le woƒe anyigba dzi la afia viwòwo kple wò tɔgbuiyɔviwo, eye mi katã mianyae be nyee nye Aƒetɔ.”.

eng: Say to him, The LORD, the Hebrews God, has sent me to you with this message: Let my people go so that they can worship me in the desert. Up to now you still havent listened.
local: Gblɔ nɛ be: ‘Aƒetɔ, Hebritɔwo ƒe Mawu la dɔm ɖe gbɔwò be magblɔ na wò be: Ɖe asi le nye dukɔ ŋu ne woayi aɖasubɔm le gbedadaƒo. Gake ègbe va se ɖe fifi.

eng: Pharaoh asked around and found out that not one of Israels livestock had died. But Pharaoh was stubborn, and he wouldnt let the people go.
local: Egipte Fiagã la dɔ ame ɖa; ke wokpɔ be Israelviwo ƒe lãwo ya ɖeka gɔ̃ hã meku o. Gake Egipte Fiagã la gasẽ dzime, eye meɖe asi le dukɔ la ŋu o.

eng: The LORD said to Moses, Ill bring one more disaster on Pharaoh and on Egypt. After that, hell let you go from here. In fact, when he lets you go, hell eagerly chase you out of here.
local: Aƒetɔ gblɔ na Mose be: “Esusɔ fukpekpe ɖeka mahe va Egipte Fiagã la kple Egipte dzi. Emegbe la, Fiagã la aɖe mɔ na mi miadzo le afi sia. Ke menye asi ko wòaɖe le mia ŋu o; mia nyà ge wòala miadzo le afi sia.

eng: After Moses and Aaron had left Pharaoh, Moses cried out to the LORD about the frogs that the LORD had brought on Pharaoh.
local: Mose kple Aron dzo le Fiagã la gbɔ. Mose yi ɖado gbe ɖa na Aƒetɔ le akpɔkplɔ siwo va bɔ̀ ɖee la ŋuti.

eng: So Moses and Aaron went to Pharaoh and said to him, This is what the LORD, the Hebrews God, says: How long will you refuse to respect me? Let my people go so that they can worship me.
local: Ale Mose kple Aron yi Egipte Fiagã la gbɔ ɖagblɔ nɛ be: “Aƒetɔ, Hebritɔwo ƒe Mawu la be: ‘Ɣe ka ɣi nàgbe ɖokuiwò bɔbɔ ɖi nam aɖase ɖo? Ɖe mɔ na nye dukɔ la ne woayi aɖasubɔm.

eng: This is what the LORD says: By this you will know that I am the LORD. Im now going to hit the water of the Nile River with this rod in my hand, and it will turn into blood.
local: Esia nàtsɔ anyae be nyee nye Aƒetɔ nye esi: Kpɔ ɖa, mele atizɔti si le asinye la tsɔ ge aƒo Nil-tɔsisi la dzi, eye tsia atrɔ zu ʋu.

eng: Then the LORD said to Moses and Aaron, Take handfuls of ashes from a furnace and have Moses throw it up in the air in front of Pharaoh.
local: Aƒetɔ gblɔ na Mose kple Aron be: “Miɖe kpomedzɔble ɖe asiƒome eye mina Mose nahlẽe ɖe ya me le Egipte Fiagã la ŋkume.

eng: Tell every man to ask his neighbor and every woman to ask her neighbor for all their silver and gold jewelry.
local: Gblɔ na dukɔ la be ŋutsu sia ŋutsu kple nyɔnu sia nyɔnu nebia klosalo kple sika nuwo le eƒe aƒelika Egiptetɔ gbɔ.”.

eng: The LORD did as Moses asked. The frogs died inside the houses, out in the yards, and in the fields.
local: Aƒetɔ se eƒe gbedodoɖa. Akpɔkplɔawo ku le aƒewo me, xɔxɔnuwo kple agblewo me.

eng: Otherwise, if you refuse to let my people go, Im going to bring locusts into your country tomorrow.
local: Ne ègagbe la, mahe ʋetsuviwo ava wò anyigba dzi etsɔ.

eng: The fish in the Nile are going to die, the Nile will stink, and the Egyptians wont be able to drink water from the Nile.
local: Lã siwo le eme la aku, eye tɔsisia anɔ ʋeʋẽm ale gbegbe be Egiptetɔwo mate ŋu ano emetsi o.’ ”.

eng: The ashes will turn to soot over the whole land of Egypt. It will cause skin sores that will break out in blisters on people and animals in the whole land of Egypt.
local: Azu abe ʋuʋudedi ene akaka ɖe Egipte-nyigba blibo la dzi, eye wòana ƒoƒoe si me dɔ vɔ̃ le la nate amewo kple lãwo siaa le Egipte-nyigba blibo la dzi.”.

eng: The LORD made sure that the Egyptians were kind to the Hebrew people. In addition, Pharaohs officials and the Egyptian people even came to honor Moses as a great and important man in the land.
local: Aƒetɔ na Egiptetɔwo ve Israelviwo nu, eye Mose ŋutɔ hã nye bubume gã le Egipte Fiagã la ŋumewo kple dukɔ la ŋkume.

eng: They gathered them together in big piles, and the land began to stink.
local: Woli kɔ wo fũu ɖi, eye anyigba la katã dzi nɔ ʋeʋẽm kũu.

eng: They will cover the landscape so that you wont be able to see the ground. They will eat the last bit of vegetation that was left after the hail. They will eat all your trees growing in the fields.
local: Woanyè tsyɔ anyigba blibo la katã dzi ale gbegbe be anyigba magadze ame nakpɔ o. Woaɖu nu si susɔ si kpetsia megblẽ o la, eye woaɖu ati siwo gato gbɔna la hã keŋkeŋ.

eng: The LORD said to Moses, Say to Aaron, Take your shepherds rod and stretch out your hand over Egypts waters—over their rivers, their canals, their marshes, and all their bodies of water—so that they turn into blood. There will be blood all over the land of Egypt, even in wooden and stone containers.
local: Aƒetɔ gblɔ na Mose be: “Gblɔ na Aron be: ‘Tsɔ wò atizɔti eye nàdoe ɖe Egipte-nyigba dzi tsiwo, tɔsisiwo, tɔʋuwo, táwo kple tsiteƒewo katã dzi bene woatrɔ zu ʋu, eye ʋu nanɔ Egipte-nyigba blibo la katã dzi; atigowo kple kpezewo gɔ̃ hã ʋu anɔ wo me.’ ”.

eng: So they took ashes from the furnace, and they stood in front of Pharaoh. Moses threw the ash up in the air, and it caused skin sores and blisters to break out on people and animals.
local: Woku kpomedzɔble ɖe asiƒome tsɔ va tsi tsitre ɖe Egipte Fiagã la ŋkume. Mose hlẽe ɖe ya me eye wòtrɔ zu ƒoƒoedɔ vɔ̃ te amewo kple lãwo siaa le Egipte-nyigba blibo la dzi.

eng: Moses said, This is what the LORD says: At midnight Ill go throughout Egypt.
local: Mose gblɔ na Egipte Fiagã la be: “Aƒetɔ be: ‘Le zã titina la, mele Egipte-nyigba me to ge ava yi.

eng: But when Pharaoh saw that the disaster was over, he became stubborn again and wouldnt listen to them, just as the LORD had said.
local: Gake esi Egipte Fiagã la kpɔ be yekpɔ gbɔdzɔe tso akpɔkplɔawo si me la, egasẽ dzime eye abe ale si Aƒetɔ gblɔ ene la, egbe toɖoɖo Mose kple Aron.

eng: The locusts will fill your houses and all your officials houses and all the Egyptians houses. Your parents and even your grandparents have never seen anything like it during their entire lifetimes in this fertile land. Then Moses turned and left Pharaoh.
local: Woage ɖe wò xɔwo me, atra ɖe ŋuwòmewo kple Egiptetɔwo katã ƒe xɔwo me, ayɔ wo taŋtaŋ. Tso esime tɔ́wòwo kple tɔgbuiwòwo va anyigba sia dzi va se ɖe egbe kpa, womekpɔ esia tɔgbe kpɔ o.’ ” Tete wotrɔ dzo le Egipte Fiagã la gbɔ.

eng: Moses and Aaron did just as the LORD commanded. He raised the shepherds rod and hit the water in the Nile in front of Pharaoh and his officials, and all the water in the Nile turned into blood.
local: Mose kple Aron wɔ nu si Aƒetɔ gblɔ na wo la. Ekɔ atizɔti la dzi le Egipte Fiagã kple eŋumewo ŋkume, eye wòƒo tsia dzi. Tete Nil-tɔsisi me tsi la katã trɔ zu ʋu.

eng: The religious experts couldnt stand up to Moses because of the skin sores, because there were skin sores on the religious experts as well as on all the Egyptians.
local: Ƒoƒoea te Egiptetɔwo ƒe dzotɔwo hã abe Egiptetɔ bubuawo ke ene, eye le esia ta la, womete ŋu nɔ te ɖe Mose nu o.

eng: Every oldest child in the land of Egypt will die, from the oldest child of Pharaoh who sits on his throne to the oldest child of the servant woman by the millstones, and all the first offspring of the animals.
local: Ŋgɔgbevi ɖe sia ɖe le anyigba la dzi aku; tso Egipte Fiagã la vi gbãtɔ si ava zi dzi ɖe tɔ́a yome dzi va se ɖe kosi si le teƒo la ƒe ŋgɔgbevi dzi. Nenema ke lãwo ƒe ŋgɔgbeviwo katã hã aku.

eng: Then the LORD said to Moses, Tell Aaron, Stretch out your shepherds rod and hit the lands dirt so that lice appear in the whole land of Egypt.
local: Aƒetɔ gagblɔ na Mose be: “Gblɔ na Aron be: ‘Tsɔ wò atizɔti ƒo ke me le anyigba ne wòatrɔ zu muwo le Egipte-nyigba la katã dzi.’ ”.

eng: Pharaohs officials said to him, How long will this man trap us in a corner like this? Let the people go so that they can worship the LORD their God. Dont you get it? Egypt is being destroyed.
local: Egipte Fiagã la ŋumewo gblɔ nɛ be: “Va se ɖe ɣe ka ɣi koŋ ame sia anye mɔ̀ anɔ mía ɖem? Esi Egipte-dukɔ la gbã vɔ alea ɖe, mèkpɔe haɖe oa? Ɖe asi le wo ŋu bene woayi aɖasubɔ Aƒetɔ, woƒe Mawu la. Wò ŋutɔ kpɔ ale si Egipte dome gblẽ vɔ keŋkeŋ lae ɖa!”.

eng: The fish in the Nile died, and the Nile began to stink so that the Egyptians couldnt drink water from the Nile. There was blood all over the land of Egypt.
local: Lã siwo le eme la katã ku. Tɔa de asi ʋeʋẽ me ale gbegbe be Egiptetɔwo megate ŋu no emetsi o. Ʋu xɔ afi sia afi le Egipte-nyigba dzi.

eng: But the LORD made Pharaoh stubborn, and Pharaoh wouldnt listen to them, just as the LORD had said to Moses.
local: Ke Aƒetɔ na Egipte Fiagã la ƒe dzime gasẽ kokoko, eye abe ale si wògblɔ na Mose ene la, Fiagã la meɖo to wo o.

eng: Then a terrible cry of agony will echo through the whole land of Egypt unlike any heard before or that ever will be again.
local: Aviɣli aɖi le Egipte-nyigba blibo la dzi. Ɣli sia tɔgbe meɖi kpɔ o, eye magaɖi akpɔ hã o.

eng: They did this. Aaron stretched out his hand with his shepherds rod, hit the lands dirt, and lice appeared on both people and animals. All the lands dirt turned into lice throughout the whole land of Egypt.
local: Aron tsɔ atizɔtia ƒo anyigba. Tete ke trɔ zu mu sɔŋ bɔ̀ ɖe amewo kple lãwo siaa yome le Egipte-nyigba blibo la dzi.

eng: So Moses and Aaron were brought back to Pharaoh, and he said to them, Go! Worship the LORD your God! But who exactly is going with you.
local: Tete wogayɔ Mose kple Aron va Egipte Fiagã la gbɔ, eye wògblɔ na wo be: “Miyi ɖasubɔ Aƒetɔ, mia Mawu la. Evɔ ɖe, ame ka kple ame kawoe le yiyim?”.

eng: The LORD said to Moses and Aaron in the land of Egypt.
local: Aƒetɔ gblɔ na Mose kple Aron le Egipte-nyigba dzi be:.

eng: But the Egyptian religious experts did the same thing with their secret knowledge. As a result, Pharaoh remained stubborn, and he wouldnt listen to them, just as the LORD had said.
local: Ke Egiptetɔwo ƒe nukunuwɔlawo hã to woƒe ayemɔwo dzi wɔ nukunu siawo ke. Ale abe ale si Aƒetɔ la gblɔe ɖi ene la, Egipte Fiagã la ƒe dzime gasẽ ko, eye wògbe be yemaɖo to Mose kple Aron ƒe gbe o.

eng: Then the LORD said to Moses, Get up early in the morning and confront Pharaoh. Say to him, This is what the LORD, the God of the Hebrews, says: Let my people go so that they can worship me.
local: Aƒetɔ gagblɔ na Mose be: “Fɔ yi Fiagã la gbɔ etsɔ ŋdi kanya nàɖatsi tsitre ɖe eŋkume agblɔ nɛ be: ‘Aƒetɔ, Hebritɔwo ƒe Mawu la be: Ɖe asi le nye dukɔ ŋu ne woayi aɖasubɔm.

eng: The religious experts tried to produce lice by their secret knowledge, but they werent able to do it. There were lice on people and animals.
local: Muawo nye tsyɔ amegbetɔ kple lãwo dzi. Woƒe dzotɔwo hã to vivimemɔwo dzi be yewoawɔ muwo gake womete ŋui o.

eng: But as for the Israelites, not even a dog will growl at them, at the people, or at their animals. By this, you will know that the LORD makes a distinction between Egypt and Israel.
local: Ke le Israelviwo gbɔ la, ame o alo lã o, avu gɔ̃ hã mate gbe ɖe wo ŋu o. To esia me miadze sii be Aƒetɔ dea vovototo Egiptetɔwo kple Israelviwo dome.

eng: Moses said, Well go with our young and old, with our sons and daughters, and with our flocks and herds, because we all must observe the LORDs festival.
local: Mose ɖo eŋu be: “Mí katã tim ye le yiyi ge: ɖeviwo, ame tsitsiwo, mía viŋutsuwo, mia vinyɔnuwo, míaƒe alẽwo, gbɔ̃wo kple nyiwo, elabe ele na mí be míayi ɖaɖu ŋkekenyui na Aƒetɔ.”.

eng: This month will be the first month; it will be the first month of the year for you.
local: “Ɣleti sia nanye ɣleti vevitɔ na mi le ƒèa me. Nezu ɣleti gbãtɔ na mi le ƒèa me.

eng: This time Im going to send all my plagues on you, your officials, and your people so that you will know that there is no one like me in the whole world.
local: Ne ègasẽ to azɔ ya la, mawɔ fu wò ŋutɔ, ŋuwòmewo kple wò dukɔ la vevie bene nànyae be ame aɖeke meli abe nye ene le anyigba blibo la dzi o.

eng: Pharaoh turned and went back to his palace. He wasnt impressed even by this.
local: Fiagã la trɔ dzo yi aƒeme, eye esia meʋã dzi nɛ vie gɔ̃ hã o.

eng: The religious experts said to Pharaoh, This is something only God could do! But Pharaoh was stubborn, and he wouldnt listen to them, just as the LORD had said.
local: Wogblɔ na fia la be: “Esia ya la, Mawu ƒe nuwɔwɔ ye.” Ke Egipte Fiagã la ƒe dzime gasẽ ko eye abe ale si Aƒetɔ la gblɔe ene la, egbe toɖoɖo.

eng: Then all your officials will come down to me, bow to me, and say, Get out, you and all your followers! After that Ill leave. Then Moses, furious, left Pharaoh.
local: Ekema ŋuwòme siawo katã ava gbɔnye, ade ta agu nam, agblɔ be: ‘Wò ŋutɔ kple wò ameawo katã midzo yi!’ Emegbe madzo.” Mose dzo le Egipte Fiagã la ŋkume kple dziku gã ŋutɔ.

eng: Pharaoh said to them, Yes, the LORD will be with you, all right, especially if I let your children go with you! Obviously, you are plotting some evil scheme.
local: Fiaa xɔ edzi be: “Aƒetɔ aɖi na mi baa! Nye, mana mi kple mia viwo miadzo ayi? Nyateƒe, mieɖo vɔ̃ ɖe tame.

eng: Tell the whole Israelite community: On the tenth day of this month they must take a lamb for each household, a lamb per house.
local: Migblɔ na Israel-dukɔ blibo la be le ɣleti sia ƒe ŋkeke ewolia dzi la, ŋutsu sia ŋutsu nalé alẽvi ɖeka; aƒe ɖeka, alẽvi ɖeka.

eng: By now I could have used my power to strike you and your people with a deadly disease so that you would have disappeared from the earth.
local: Elabe mate ŋu atsɔ dɔ vɔ̃ atsrɔ̃ wò ŋutɔ kple wò dukɔ la ɖa le anyigba dzi xoxoxo hafi.

eng: Meanwhile, all the Egyptians had to dig for drinking water along the banks of the Nile River, because they couldnt drink the water of the Nile itself.
local: Egiptetɔwo ku do ɖe Nil-tɔsisi la to be tsi nadzi ɖe wo me yewoano elabe Nil-tɔsisi me tsi la meganyo ne woano o.

eng: The LORD said to Moses, Get up early in the morning and confront Pharaoh as he goes out to the water. Say to him, This is what the LORD says: Let my people go so that they can worship me.
local: Aƒetɔ gblɔ na Mose be: “Fɔ etsɔ ŋdi kanya naɖalala Egipte Fiagã la. Egbɔna tɔsisi la to. Nàgblɔ nɛ be Aƒetɔ be: ‘Ɖe asi le nye dukɔ la ŋu ne woayi aɖasubɔm.

eng: The LORD said to Moses, Pharaoh wont listen to you so that I can perform even more amazing acts in the land of Egypt.
local: Tete Aƒetɔ gblɔ na Mose be: “Egipte Fiagã la gale to sẽ ge kokoko, ale be to ema me la, magawɔ nukunu geɖe wu le Egipte.”.

eng: No way! Only your men can go and worship the LORD, because thats what you asked for. Then Pharaoh had them chased out of his presence.
local: Kpao! Mia kple ŋutsuawo ɖeɖe miyi ɖasubɔ Aƒetɔ elabe eya miedi.” Tete wonyà wo dzoe le Egipte Fiagã la ŋkume.

eng: But Ive left you standing for this reason: in order to show you my power and in order to make my name known in the whole world.
local: Ke mena nètsi agbe be maɖe nye ŋusẽ afia wò, eye nye ŋkɔ naɖi le anyigba ƒe afi sia afi.

eng: If a household is too small for a lamb, it should share one with a neighbor nearby. You should divide the lamb in proportion to the number of people who will be eating it.
local: Ne aƒe aɖe me tɔwo mesɔ gbɔ ate ŋu aɖu alẽvi ɖeka o la, woaƒo ƒu kple woƒe aƒelika be woasɔ gbɔ, eye alẽvi ɖeka nade wo nu.

eng: Seven days went by after the LORD had struck the Nile River.
local: Aƒetɔ gblẽ Nil me tsi la ŋkeke adrẽ sɔŋ.

eng: If you refuse to let my people go, Ill send swarms of insects on you, your officials, your people, and your houses. All Egyptian houses will be filled with swarms of insects and also the ground that they cover.
local: Ne ègbe la, mana togbatowo nava ɖe fu na wò ŋutɔ, ŋuwòmewo kple wò dukɔ la katã. Togbatowo age ɖe miaƒe aƒewo me, eye Egiptetɔwo ƒe aƒewo me ayɔ fũu kple togbato ale be ame makpɔ afɔɖoƒe teti hã o.

eng: Now Moses and Aaron did all these amazing acts in front of Pharaoh, but the LORD made Pharaoh stubborn so that he didnt let the Israelites go from his land.
local: Mose kple Aron wɔ nukunu siawo katã le Egipte Fiagã ŋkume. Ke Aƒetɔ na fiagã la ƒe dzime gasẽ ale be wògbe meɖe asi le Israelviwo ŋu wodzo le eƒe anyigba dzi o.

eng: Then the LORD said to Moses: Stretch out your hand over the land of Egypt so that the locusts will swarm over the land of Egypt and eat all of the lands grain and everything that the hail left.
local: Aƒetɔ gblɔ na Mose be: “Do asi ɖe Egipte-nyigba la dzi, ne ʋetsuviwo nava. Woava ɖu nu miemie siwo katã kpetsi la megblẽ o la.

eng: You are still abusing your power against my people, and you refuse to let them go.
local: Ke ègale dàdam hegbe be yemaɖe asi le nye dukɔ ŋu woadzo o.

eng: Your lamb should be a flawless year-old male. You may take it from the sheep or from the goats.
local: Lã la nanye atsui, nexɔ ƒe ɖeka eye naneke nagawɔ afi aɖeke nɛ o. Ate ŋu anye alẽ alo gbɔ̃.

eng: But on that day Ill set apart the land of Goshen, where my people live. No swarms of insects will come there so you will know that I, the LORD, am in this land.
local: Ke maɖe Gosen-nyigba, afi si nye dukɔa le la le eme. Togbatoawo made afi ma ya o, ne nànya be nyee nye Aƒetɔ le anyigba dzi.

eng: So Moses stretched out his shepherds rod over the land of Egypt, and the LORD made an east wind blow over the land all that day and all that night. When morning came, the east wind had carried in the locusts.
local: Mose do eƒe atizɔti la ɖe Egipte-nyigba la dzi, eye Aƒetɔ na ya ƒo tso ɣedzeƒe va anyigba la dzi ŋkeke blibo kple zã blibo. Kaka ŋu nake la, ya la lɔ ʋetsuviwo va kɔ ɖi.

eng: Tomorrow at this time Ill cause the heaviest hail to fall on Egypt that has ever fallen from the day Egypt was founded until now.
local: Eya ta etsɔ ɣe alewo ɣi la, mana tsikpe sesẽ nadza vevie. Tsikpe sia tɔgbe medza kpɔ le Egipte-nyigba dzi tso esime woɖo dukɔa gɔme anyi o.

eng: You should keep close watch over it until the fourteenth day of this month. At twilight on that day, the whole assembled Israelite community should slaughter their lambs.
local: Woaɖee ɖe aga va se ɖe ɣletia ƒe ŋkeke wui enelia gbe, eye le gbe ma gbe fiẽ la, Israelviwo ƒe ha bliboa katã le afi sia afi nawui.

eng: The LORD said to Moses.
local: Aƒetɔ gblɔ na Mose be:.

eng: Ill put a barrier between my people and your people. This sign will happen tomorrow.
local: Made vovototo nye dukɔ kple wò dukɔ dome. Etsɔ tututu dzesi sia lava eme.’ ”.

eng: The locusts swarmed over the whole land of Egypt and settled on the whole country. Such a huge swarming of locusts had never happened before and would never happen ever again.
local: Ʋetsuviawo va le hatsotso gã gãwo me, eye wotsyɔ anyigba la dzi taŋtaŋ. Ʋetsuviwo ƒe agbɔsɔsɔ sia ƒomevi meva kpɔ gbeɖe o, eye magava akpɔ hã o.

eng: So bring under shelter your livestock and all that belongs to you that is out in the open. Every person or animal that is out in the open field and isnt brought inside will die when the hail rains down on them.
local: Eya ta na woadi beɖoƒe na wò lãhawo kple wò nu siwo katã le agbleta la. Ame kple lã si tsi gbedzi, eye womele beɖoƒe o la, tsikpe aƒo wo, eye wòaku.’ ”.

eng: They should take some of the blood and smear it on the two doorposts and on the beam over the door of the houses in which they are eating.
local: Woatsɔ eƒe ʋu asisi ɖe ʋɔtrutiawo kple eve kple wo tametɔ la ŋu.

eng: Dedicate to me all your oldest children. Each first offspring from any Israelite womb belongs to me, whether human or animal.
local: “Ɖe ŋgɔgbeviwo katã ɖe aga nam. Nu sia nu si aʋu dɔ nu na nɔa le Israelviwo dome la, tɔnye ye. Eɖanye ame alo lã o, tɔnyee.”.

eng: The LORD did this. Great swarms of insects came into the houses of Pharaoh and his officials and into the whole land of Egypt. The land was ruined by the insects.
local: Aƒetɔ wɔ esia. Togbatowo ƒe ha gãwo va kɔ ɖi. Wogba ɖe Egipte Fiagã la ƒe fiasã me, eŋumewo ƒe aƒewo me, woƒe xɔwo me kple Egipte-nyigba blibo la katã dzi, eye togbatowo gblẽ anyigba la dzi.

eng: They covered the whole landscape so that the land turned black with them. They ate all of the lands grain and all of the orchards fruit that the hail had left. Nothing green was left in any orchard or in any grain field in the whole land of Egypt.
local: Wonyè tsyɔ anyigba la katã dzi ale gbegbe be afi sia afi biã hẽe. Woɖu nu miemíe kple kutsetse siwo katã kpetsi la tso ɖi la, ale be aŋgba kple gbe mumu aɖeke megasusɔ ɖe Egipte-nyigba blibo la katã dzi o.

eng: Some of Pharaohs officials who took the LORDs word seriously rushed to bring their servants and livestock inside for shelter.
local: Egipte Fiagã la ŋume siwo Aƒetɔ ƒe nya la tso dzika ƒo na la na woƒe kluviwo kple woƒe lãhawo yi ɖe bebeƒe.

eng: That same night they should eat the meat roasted over the fire. They should eat it along with unleavened bread and bitter herbs.
local: Woamè lã la, eye woaɖui le zã ma me kple amɔwɔ maʋamaʋã bolo kpakple ama veve.

eng: Moses said to the people, Remember this day which is the day that you came out of Egypt, out of the place you were slaves, because the LORD acted with power to bring you out of there. No leavened bread may be eaten.
local: Mose gblɔ na dukɔ la be: “Miaƒe ŋku nenɔ ŋkeke sia, si dzi miedzo le Egipte, afi si mienye kluviwo le la dzi, elabe ŋusẽ triakɔ ŋutɔe Aƒetɔ tsɔ kplɔ mi dzoe le afi ma. Migaɖu amɔwɔ ʋaʋã bolo le ŋkeke sia dzi o.

eng: Then Pharaoh called in Moses and Aaron and said, Go, offer sacrifices to your God within the land.
local: Tete Egipte Fiagã la yɔ Mose kple Aron, eye wògblɔ na wo be: “Enyo, miyi ɖasa vɔ na mia Mawu la le anyigba sia dzi.”.

eng: Pharaoh called urgently for Moses and Aaron and said, Ive sinned against the LORD your God and against you.
local: Tete Egipte Fiagã la na woyɔ Mose kple Aron vɛ kaba, eye wògblɔ na wo be: “Meda vo ɖe Aƒetɔ, mia Mawu la ŋu, eye meda vo ɖe miawo hã mia ŋu.

eng: Others who didnt take the LORDs word to heart left their servants and livestock out in the open field.
local: Ke esiwo nya la metso dzika ƒo na o la gblẽ wo tɔwo ɖe gbedzi.

eng: Dont eat any of it raw or boiled in water, but roasted over fire with its head, legs, and internal organs.
local: Migaɖui mumu alo aɖae le tsi me o, ke boŋ mimèe le dzo me. Ele be wòanɔ blibo nenema, ta, afɔ kple dɔmenuawo nanɔ eŋu hafi miame aɖu.

eng: Today, in the month of Abib, you are going to leave.
local: Miele dzodzom le Egipte egbe sia, le Abib, si nye nuŋeɣleti la me.

eng: Moses replied, It wouldnt be right to do that, because the sacrifices that we offer to the LORD our God will offend Egyptians. If we openly offer sacrifices that offend Egyptians, wont they stone us to death?.
local: Mose ɖo eŋu nɛ be: “Manyo be míawɔ nenema o, elabe nu siwo míele tsɔtsɔ ge asa vɔ na Aƒetɔ, mía Mawu la nye busunu na Egiptetɔwo. Ne míetsɔ nu siwo nye busunu na Egiptetɔwo va le vɔ sam le wo ŋkume ɖe, womaƒu kpe mí oa?.

eng: Please forgive my sin this time. Pray to the LORD your God just to take this deathly disaster away from me.
local: Eya ta, meɖe kuku na mi, mitsɔ nye vodada la kem fifia hã, eye miɖe kuku na Aƒetɔ, mia Mawu la, be wòaɖe ku sia ɖa le dzinye.”.

eng: The LORD said to Moses, Raise your hand toward the sky so that hail will fall on the whole land of Egypt, on people and animals and all the grain in the fields in the land of Egypt.
local: Aƒetɔ gblɔ na Mose be: “Do asi ɖe dziƒo, ne kpetsi nadza ɖe Egipte-nyigba blibo la katã dzi. Nedza ɖe amewo, lãwo kple agblemenuku siwo katã le Egipte-nyigba dzi la dzi.”.

eng: Dont let any of it remain until morning, and burn any of it left over in the morning.
local: Migasusɔ ɖeke ɖi ŋu nake ɖe edzi o. Ne ɖe tsi anyi ŋu ke la, miatɔ dzoe.

eng: The LORD will bring you to the land of the Canaanites, the Hittites, the Amorites, the Hivites, and the Jebusites. It is the land that the LORD promised your ancestors to give to you, a land full of milk and honey. You should perform this ritual in this month.
local: Aƒetɔ ka atam na mia tɔgbuiwo be yeatsɔ Kanaanitɔwo, Xititɔwo, Amoritɔwo, Xivitɔwo kple Yebusitɔwo ƒe anyigba si dzi notsi kple anyitsi bɔ ɖo la ana mi. Ne wòkplɔ mi va anyigba sia dzi la, miaɖu ŋkekenyui sia le ɣleti sia me.

eng: We need to go for a three-day journey into the desert to offer sacrifices to the LORD our God as he has ordered us.
local: Ehiã be míazɔ ŋkeke etɔ̃ ƒe mɔ ayi gbedadaƒo aɖasa vɔ na Aƒetɔ, mia Mawu la, abe ale si wòafia mí lae ene.”.

eng: So Moses left Pharaoh and prayed to the LORD.
local: Ale Mose dzo le Egipte Fiagã la gbɔ, eye wòyi ɖaƒo koko na Aƒetɔ.

eng: Then the LORD said to Moses.
local: Azɔ Aƒetɔ gblɔ na Mose be:.

eng: Then Moses raised his shepherds rod toward the sky, and the LORD sent thunder and hail, and lightning struck the earth. The LORD rained hail on the land of Egypt.
local: Mose do eƒe atizɔti la ɖe dziƒo, eye Aƒetɔ na dziɖegbe, tsikpe kple dzo kɔ ɖe anyigba dzi. Aƒetɔ na kpetsi la dza ɖe Egipte-nyigba blibo la dzi.

eng: You must eat unleavened bread for seven days. The seventh day is a festival to the LORD.
local: Miaɖu amɔwɔ maʋamaʋã bolo ŋkeke adrẽ, eye le ŋkeke adrẽa gbe la, miaɖu ŋkekenyui na Aƒetɔ.

eng: This is how you should eat it. You should be dressed, with your sandals on your feet and your walking stick in your hand. You should eat the meal in a hurry. It is the Passover of the LORD.
local: Ale mianɔ aɖui nye esi: Mibla ali dzi, eye mido afɔkpawo ɖe afɔ. Milé atizɔti ɖe asi, eye miaɖui kabakaba. Aƒetɔ ƒe Paska wònye.

eng: So Pharaoh said, Ill let you go to offer sacrifices to the LORD your God in the desert, provided you dont go too far away and you pray for me.
local: Ale Egipte Fiagã la be: “Maɖe asi le mia ŋu miayi aɖasa vɔ na Aƒetɔ, mia Mawu la le gbedadaƒo. Ke migayi didiƒe akpa o. Miado gbe ɖa ɖe tanye.”.

eng: The LORD turned the wind into a very strong west wind that lifted the locusts and drove them into the Reed Sea. Not a single locust was left in the whole country of Egypt.
local: Aƒetɔ na ya sesẽ aɖe tso ɣetoɖoƒe lɔ ʋetsuviawo ɖe nu yi ɖakɔ ɖe Aƒlaƒu la me. Ɖeka pɛ gɔ̃ ha metsi Egipte-nyigba la ƒe afi aɖeke o.

eng: The hail and the lightning flashing in the middle of the hail were so severe that there had been nothing like it in the entire land of Egypt since it first became a nation.
local: Dzi le gbe ɖem ɖe tsikpe la me atraɖii. Esia tɔgbe medzɔ kpɔ tso esime woɖo Egipte-dukɔ la gɔme anyi o.

eng: Tell the Israelites to turn back and set up camp in front of Pi-hahiroth, between Migdol and the sea in front of Baal-zephon. You should set up camp in front of it by the sea.
local: “Gblɔ na Israelviwo be woatrɔ va ƒu asaɖa anyi ɖe Pi-Haxirot gbɔ le Migdol kple ƒu la dome le Baal-Zefon kasa ɖe ƒua nu.

eng: Only unleavened bread should be eaten for seven days. No leavened bread and no yeast should be seen among you in your whole country.
local: Le ŋkeke adrẽ siawo me la, miaɖu amɔwɔ maʋamaʋã bolo ko. Womakpɔ amɔwɔ ʋaʋã bolo alo amɔwɔʋãnu aɖeke le mia si le miaƒe anyigba blibo la dzi o.

eng: Moses said, Ill leave you now, and Ill pray to the LORD. Tomorrow the swarms of insects will leave Pharaoh, his officials, and his people. Just dont let Pharaoh lie to us again and not let the people go to offer sacrifices to the LORD.
local: Woxɔ edzi nɛ be: “Ne metrɔ le gbɔwò ko maɖe kuku na Aƒetɔ be wòana togbatoawo nadzo etsɔ ɖeka bene Egipte Fiagã la kple eŋumewo kpakple dukɔa me tɔwo navo tso wo si me. Ɖeko Fiagã la negablem eye wòagagbe be yemaɖe asi le dukɔ la ŋu ne woaɖasa wɔ na Aƒetɔ o.”.

eng: Ill pass through the land of Egypt that night, and Ill strike down every oldest child in the land of Egypt, both humans and animals. Ill impose judgments on all the gods of Egypt. I am the LORD.
local: Elabe mele Egipte-nyigba la me to ge ava yi le zã ma me, eye mawu ŋgɔgbevi ɖe sia ɖe le Egipte-nyigba la dzi, eɖanye ame o, eɖanye aƒemelã o. Matso nya me na Egipte-mawuwo katã. Nyee nye Aƒetɔ.

eng: But the LORD made Pharaoh stubborn so that he wouldnt let the Israelites go.
local: Ke Aƒetɔ na Egipte Fiagã la ƒe dzime gasẽ ale be wògbe meɖe asi le Israelviwo ŋu wodzo o.

eng: The hail beat down everything that was in the open field throughout the entire land of Egypt, both people and animals. The hail also beat down all the grain in the fields, and it shattered every tree out in the field.
local: Kpetsi la gblẽ nu le Egipte-nyigba blibo la katã dzi. Egblẽ nu sia nu le gbedzi, eye wòwu ame kple lãwo siaa. Egblẽ agblemenukuwo eye wòfe gbemetiwo katã.

eng: Pharaoh will think to himself, The Israelites are lost and confused in the land. The desert has trapped them.
local: Ekema Egipte Fiagã la agblɔ le mi, Israelviwo ŋu be, ‘Wobu mɔ eye woble ɖe gbedadaƒo.’.

eng: You should explain to your child on that day, Its because of what the LORD did for me when I came out of Egypt.
local: Gbe ma gbe la, miagblɔ na mia viŋutsuwo be: ‘Nu si Aƒetɔ wɔ na mí esime míeʋu tso Egipte la tae míele ŋkeke sia ɖum ɖo.’.

eng: So Moses left Pharaoh and prayed to the LORD.
local: Mose dzo le Egipte Fiagã la gbɔ, eye wòɖado gbe ɖa na Aƒetɔ.

eng: The blood will be your sign on the houses where you live. Whenever I see the blood, Ill pass over you. No plague will destroy you when I strike the land of Egypt.
local: Ʋu si anɔ aƒe siwo me miele ƒe ʋɔtrutiwo ŋuti la ade dzesi mi, ale be ne mekpɔ ʋua ko la, mato mia ŋu ayi, eye fukpekpe si ava Egiptetɔwo dzi la matsrɔ̃ miawo ya o.

eng: Then the LORD said to Moses, Raise your hand toward the sky so that darkness spreads over the land of Egypt, a darkness that you can feel.
local: Aƒetɔ gblɔ na Mose be: “Do asi ɖe dziƒo ne viviti tsiɖitsiɖi si anyɔ abe ɖe woate ŋu ali asi eŋu ene la nado ɖe Egipte-nyigba dzi.”.

eng: The only place where hail didnt fall was in the land of Goshen where the Israelites lived.
local: Gake le Gosen-nutome, afi si Israelviwo le ya la, kpetsi aɖeke medza ɖee o.

eng: Ill make Pharaoh stubborn, and hell chase them. Ill gain honor at the expense of Pharaoh and all his army, and the Egyptians will know that I am the LORD. And they did exactly that.
local: Mana Egipte Fiagã la ƒe dzime nasẽ, eye wòakplɔ wo ɖo. Ekema maɖe nye ŋusẽ afia ɖe Egipte Fiagã la kple eƒe aʋakɔ blibo la ŋuti be Egiptetɔwo nadze sii be nyee nye Aƒetɔ” eye wowɔ nenema.

eng: It will be a sign on your hand and a reminder on your forehead so that you will often discuss the LORDs Instruction, for the LORD brought you out of Egypt with great power.
local: Ŋkeke nyui sia nanye ŋkuɖodzinu le miaƒe ŋgo nu, bene Aƒetɔ ƒe se la nanɔ mia nu elabe ŋusẽ triakɔ ŋutɔe wòtsɔ kplɔ mi dzoe le Egipte.

eng: The LORD did as Moses asked and removed the swarms of insects from Pharaoh, from his officials, and from his people. Not one insect remained.
local: Aƒetɔ wɔ Mose ƒe nya dzi, eye wòɖe togbatoawo ɖa le fiagã la kple eŋumewo kpakple dukɔa dome. Ɖeka pɛ hã mesusɔ o.

eng: This day will be a day of remembering for you. You will observe it as a festival to the LORD. You will observe it in every generation as a regulation for all time.
local: Ŋkeke sia nanye ŋkuɖodziŋkeke na Aƒetɔ tso dzidzime yi dzidzime; nezu ɖoɖo na mi tegbee.”.

eng: Then Pharaoh sent for Moses and Aaron and said to them, This time Ive sinned. The LORD is right, and I and my people are wrong.
local: Tete Egipte Fiagã la na woɖayɔ Mose kple Aron vɛ, eye wògblɔ na wo be: “Azɔ ya la, meda vo. Aƒetɔ tɔ dzɔ. Ke nye kple nye dukɔ la tɔ medzɔ o.

eng: So Moses raised his hand toward the sky, and an intense darkness fell on the whole land of Egypt for three days.
local: Mose do asi ɖe dzi. Tete viviti tsiɖitsiɖi do ɖe anyigba blibo la dzi. Edo nenema ŋkeke etɔ̃.

eng: When Egypts king was told that the people had run away, Pharaoh and his officials changed their minds about the people. They said, What have we done, letting Israel go free from their slavery to us.
local: Esi wogblɔ na Egipte-fia be Israel-dukɔ la sí dzo la, eya ŋutɔ kple eŋumewo ƒe susu trɔ eye wobia wo ɖokui be: “Nu kae nye esi míewɔ? Míeɖe asi le Israelvi siwo subɔa mí la ŋu wodzo!”.

eng: But Pharaoh was stubborn once again, and he wouldnt let the people go.
local: Gake Egipte Fiagã la gasẽ dzime fifia hã, egbe meɖe asi le dukɔ la ŋu wodzo o.

eng: So you should follow this regulation at its appointed time every year.
local: Miwɔ ɖoɖo sia dzi, ƒè sia ƒè, le ɣeyiɣi si woɖo ɖi la dzi.”.

eng: You will eat unleavened bread for seven days. On the first day you must remove yeast from your houses because anyone who eats leavened bread anytime during those seven days will be cut off from Israel.
local: “Miaɖu amɔwɔ maʋamaʋã bolo la ŋkeke adrẽ. Tso ŋkeke gbãtɔ dzi la, ele be miaɖe amɔwɔʋãnu ɖe sia ɖe ado goe le miaƒe aƒewo katã me. Ame sia ame si aɖu nane si me amɔwɔʋãnu le tso ŋkeke gbãtɔ yi ɖase ŋkeke adrẽlia dzi la, woaɖee ɖa le Israelviwo dome.

eng: Pray to the LORD! Enough of Gods thunder and hail! Im going to let you go. You dont need to stay here any longer.
local: Miɖaɖe kuku na Aƒetɔ ne wòana dziɖegbe kple kpetsia nu natso, ekema maɖe asi le mia ŋu miadzo; miaganɔ afi sia o.”.

eng: People couldnt see each other, and they couldnt go anywhere for three days. But the Israelites all had light where they lived.
local: Ame aɖeke megatea ŋu kpɔa ehavi o, eye ame aɖeke megatea ŋu dona le enɔƒe hã o. Ke Israelviwo gbɔ ya la, kekeli nɔe.

eng: So he sent for his chariot and took his army with him.
local: Fia la ɖe gbe wobla akpa na eƒe aʋatasiaɖam, eye wòkplɔ eƒe aʋakɔwo yii.

eng: When the LORD brings you into the land of the Canaanites and gives it to you as promised to you and your ancestors.
local: “Ne Aƒetɔ kplɔ mi va Kanaanitɔwo ƒe anyigba dzi abe ale si wòka atam na mi kple mia tɔgbuiwoe ene, eye wòtsɔe na mi la.

eng: Moses said to him, As soon as Ive left the city, Ill spread out my hands to the LORD. Then the thunder and the hail will stop and wont return so that you will know that the earth belongs to the LORD.
local: Mose ɖo eŋu nɛ be: “Ne menyado le dua me ko la, make nye abɔwo me, aƒo koko na Aƒetɔ. Ekema dziɖegbe la nu atso, eye kpetsia hã ake, ne nàtsɔ nya be Aƒetɔ tɔe nye xexea me.

eng: The first day and the seventh day will be a holy occasion for you. No work at all should be done on those days, except for preparing the food that everyone is going to eat. That is the only work you may do.
local: Le ŋkeke gbãtɔ dzi la, miwɔ subɔsubɔ takpekpe kɔkɔe, eye migawɔ nu bubu aɖeke le ŋkeke adrẽlia hã dzi o. Ame aɖeke mawɔ dɔ aɖeke le ŋkeke eve mawo dzi o, negbe nu si miaɖu gbe ma gbe ko miaɖa.

eng: Then Pharaoh called Moses and said, Go! Worship the LORD! Only your flocks and herds need to stay behind. Even your children can go with you.
local: Egipte Fiagã la yɔ Mose vɛ, eye wògblɔ nɛ be: “Miheyi ɖasubɔ Aƒetɔ la. Miate ŋu akplɔ mia viwo hã ɖe asi faa; ke migblẽ miaƒe alẽwo, gbɔ̃wo kple nyiwo ya ɖi.”.

eng: He took six hundred elite chariots and all of Egypts other chariots with captains on all of them.
local: Eho kple eƒe aʋatasiaɖam alafa ade siwo wotia kpakple Egipte-tasiaɖam bubuwo, eye aʋawɔla veviwo le wo katã nu.

eng: you should set aside for the LORD whatever comes out of the womb first. All of the first males born to your animal belong to the LORD.
local: ekema miaɖe nu sia nu si ʋu dɔ nu na nɔa la ɖe aga na Aƒetɔ; miaƒe lãwo ƒe ŋgɔgbevi siwo nye atsu la katã nanye Aƒetɔ tɔ.

eng: But I know that you and your officials still dont take the LORD God seriously. .
local: Ke wò ŋutɔ kple ŋuwòmewo ya la, menyae be miele Aƒetɔ Mawu la vɔ̃m haɖe o.”.

eng: You should observe the Festival of Unleavened Bread, because on this precise day I brought you out of the land of Egypt in military formation. You should observe this day in every generation as a regulation for all time.
local: Miɖo ŋku amɔ maʋamaʋã bolo ŋkeke sia dzi ɖaa, elabe ŋkeke ma dzie mekplɔ miaƒe dukɔ la ƒe amehawo dzoe le Egipte-nyigba la dzi. Anye ŋkekenyui na mi, mianɔ ɖuɖum ƒe sia ƒe tso dzidzime yi dzidzime.

eng: But Moses said, You need to let us have sacrifices and entirely burned offerings to present to the LORD our God.
local: Mose ɖo eŋu nɛ be: “Ele be nàɖe asi le lã siwo míatsɔ asa vɔe kple esiwo miame na Aƒetɔ, mía Mawu la ŋu na mí.

eng: The LORD made Pharaoh, Egypts king, stubborn, and he chased the Israelites, who were leaving confidently.
local: Aƒetɔ na Egipte Fiagã la ƒe dzime sẽ ale be wokplɔ Israelviawo ɖo, esime wodzo yina aglãdzetɔe.

eng: But every first male donkey you should ransom with a sheep. If you dont ransom it, you must break its neck. You should ransom every oldest male among your children.
local: Ke tedzinɔ ƒe ŋgɔgbevi ya la, miatsɔ alẽ aɖɔlii. Ne mieɖɔlii o la, ekema miŋe kɔ nɛ. Ke miɖɔli amewo ƒe ŋgɔgbeviwo katã.

eng: Now the flax and the barley were destroyed, because the barley had ears of grain and the flax had buds.
local: Ɖeti kple mɔli gblẽ le agble dzi elabe mɔli do ku eye ɖeti ƒo se ɣe ma ɣi.

eng: In the first month, from the evening of the fourteenth day until the evening of the twenty-first day, you should eat unleavened bread.
local: Tso ɣleti gbãtɔ ƒe ŋkeke wui enelia ƒe fiẽ la, miaɖu amɔwɔ maʋamaʋã bolo.

eng: So our livestock must go with us. Not one animal can be left behind. Well need some of them for worshipping the LORD our God. We wont know which to use to worship the LORD until we get there.
local: Ele be míakplɔ míaƒe lãwo hã ayii; ɖeke ƒe afɔkli teti hã matsi anyi o. Woawo dometɔ aɖewo tia ge míala atsɔ sa vɔe na Aƒetɔ, mía Mawu la, eye ɖe kokoko míaɖo afi ma hafi anya esiwo nyo míatsɔ awɔ vɔsaawoe.”.

eng: The Egyptians, including all of Pharaohs horse-drawn chariots, his cavalry, and his army, chased them and caught up with them as they were camped by the sea, by Pi-hahiroth in front of Baal-zephon.
local: Egiptetɔwo kple Egipte Fiagã la ƒe sɔwo, tasiaɖamwo, sɔdolawo kpakple aʋakɔawo katã ti Israelviwo yome va tu wo le woƒe asaɖa me le ƒua nu le Pi-Haxirot gbɔ le Baal-Zefon kasa.

eng: When in the future your child asks you, What does this mean? you should answer, The LORD brought us with great power out of Egypt, out of the place we were slaves.
local: Ne etsɔ megbe mia viŋutsuwo va bia mi be: ‘Nuwɔna sia gɔme ɖe?’ mahã la, miaɖo eŋu be: ‘Aƒetɔ tsɔ ŋusẽ triakɔ ŋutɔ kplɔ mí dzoe le Egipte, afi si míenye kluviwo le tsã la.

eng: But both durum and spelt wheat werent ruined, because they hadnt come up.
local: Ke naneke mewɔ lu ŋutɔ kple bli ya o, elabe woawo do megbe.

eng: For seven days no yeast should be found in your houses because whoever eats leavened bread will be cut off from the Israelite community, whether the person is an immigrant or a native of the land.
local: Mele be amɔwɔ ʋaʋã aɖeke nanɔ miaƒe aƒewo me le ŋkeke adrẽ mawo me o. Ne ame aɖe ɖu amɔwɔ ʋaʋã le ŋkeke mawo me la, woaɖee ɖa le Israelviwo dome. Eɖanye amedzro o, eɖanye Israelvi o, woaɖee ɖa.

eng: But the LORD made Pharaoh stubborn so that he wasnt willing to let them go.
local: Gake Aƒetɔ na Egipte Fiagã la ƒe dzime gasẽ, eye wògagbe be yemaɖe asi le Israelviwo ŋu woayi o.

eng: As Pharaoh drew closer, the Israelites looked back and saw the Egyptians marching toward them. The Israelites were terrified and cried out to the LORD.
local: Egipte Fiagã la gogo wo, eye Israelviwo fɔ mo dzi. Kasia Egiptetɔwo nye esi zɔ ɖe wo dzi gbɔna. Dzidzi ƒo wo eye wodo ɣli yɔ Aƒetɔ.

eng: When Pharaoh refused to let us go, the LORD killed all the oldest offspring in the land of Egypt, from the oldest sons to the oldest male animals. That is why I offer to the LORD as a sacrifice every male that first comes out of the womb. But I ransom my oldest sons.
local: Esi Egipte Fiagã la sẽ tame, gbe be yemaɖe asi le mía ŋu míadzo o la, Aƒetɔ wu ŋgɔgbeviwo katã le Egipte-nyigba dzi, amegbetɔwo ƒe ŋgɔgbeviwo kple lãwo tɔwo siaa. Eya ta míetsɔa atsu sia atsu si ʋu dɔ nu na nɔa la saa vɔe na Aƒetɔ, eye míeɖɔlia mía viŋutsuviwo ya kple alẽ.

eng: Moses left Pharaoh and the city, and spread out his hands to the LORD. Then the thunder and the hail stopped, and the rain stopped pouring down on the earth.
local: Mose dzo le Egipte Fiagã la gbɔ. Esi wòdo go le dua me la, eke eƒe abɔ me, do gbe ɖa na Aƒetɔ. Tete dziɖegbe la dzudzɔ, kpetsia hã nu tso, eye tsidzadza la megakɔ ɖi o.

eng: You should not eat anything made with yeast in all your settlements. You should eat only unleavened bread.
local: Miekpɔ mɔ aɖu naneke si me amɔwɔʋãnu le la o. Afi ka ke miele o, amɔwɔ maʋamaʋã bolo ko miaɖu.”.

eng: Pharaoh said to him, Get out of here! Make sure you never see my face again, because the next time you see my face you will die.
local: Eblu ɖe Mose ta be: Dzo le gbɔnye. Kpɔ nyuie. Nyemegale wò mo dim makpɔ gbeɖegbeɖe o. Gbe si gbe nye ŋku agakli wò la, àku!”.

eng: They said to Moses, Werent there enough graves in Egypt that you took us away to die in the desert? What have you done to us by bringing us out of Egypt like this?.
local: Wobia Mose be: “Yɔdoe mele Egipte o ta nèkplɔ mí dzo le afi ma be míava ku ɖe gbedadaƒoa? Nu kae nye esi nèkplɔ mí dzo le Egipte va wɔ mí?.

eng: It will be a sign on your hand and a symbol on your forehead that the LORD brought us out of Egypt with great power.
local: Esia nye dzesi anɔ míaƒe asi ta kple ŋgoblanu anɔ míaƒe ŋgo nu, be Aƒetɔ ye tsɔ eƒe ŋusẽ triakɔ la kplɔ mí dzoe le Egipte.’ ”.

eng: But when Pharaoh saw that the rain, hail, and thunder had stopped, he sinned again. Pharaoh and his officials became stubborn.
local: Esi Egipte Fiagã la kpɔ be tsidzadzaa, kpetsia kple dziɖegbe la dzudzɔ la, egayi nuvɔ̃ wɔwɔ dzi. Eya kple eŋumewo gasẽ dzime.

eng: Then Moses called together all of Israels elders and said to them, Go pick out one of the flock for your families, and slaughter the Passover lamb.
local: Mose yɔ Israelviwo ƒe ame tsitsiwo katã, eye wògblɔ na wo be: “Mia dome ame sia ame neyi ɖalé alẽvi ɖe eƒemetɔwo ƒe agbɔsɔsɔ nu, ne wòawui hena Paska la.

eng: Moses said, Youve said it! Ill never see your face again.
local: Mose ɖo eŋu nɛ be: “Yoo, mesee! Nyemagava do ɖe ŋkuwò me azɔ gbeɖe o!”.

eng: Didnt we tell you the same thing in Egypt? Leave us alone! Let us work for the Egyptians! It would have been better for us to work for the Egyptians than to die in the desert.
local: Esia ko gblɔm míenɔ na wò le Egipte be: ‘Ɖe asi le mía ŋu, ne míasubɔ Egiptetɔwo.’ Elabe ne míele Egiptetɔwo subɔm eye míele agbe la, enyo wu be míayi aɖaku ɖe gbedadaƒo.”.

eng: When Pharaoh let the people go, God didnt lead them by way of the land of the Philistines, even though that was the shorter route. God thought, If the people have to fight and face war, they will run back to Egypt.
local: Esi Egipte Fiagã la ɖe asi le Israelviwo ŋu wodzo la, Mawu mekplɔ wo to Filistitɔwo ƒe anyigba dzi mɔ o, togbɔ be eyae tsɔ kpuie hafi. Elabe Mawu gblɔ be: “Ne woho aʋa ɖe wo ŋu la, woƒe susu ava trɔ, eye woagbugbɔ ayi Egipte.”.

eng: Because of his stubbornness, Pharaoh refused to let the Israelites go, just as the LORD had told Moses.
local: Ale abe ale si Aƒetɔ gblɔ to Mose dzi ene la, egagbe be yemaɖe asi le Israelviwo ŋu woadzo o.

eng: Take a bunch of hyssop, dip it into the blood that is in the bowl, and touch the beam above the door and the two doorposts with the blood in the bowl. None of you should go out the door of your house until morning.
local: Mitsɔ hisɔptilɔ babla nyrɔ ɖe lãa ƒe ʋu me le agba si me ʋua le la me, eye miatsɔe asisi ɖe ʋɔtruti tsitrenutɔ eveawo kple wo tametɔ ŋuti. Ame aɖeke nagado go to ʋɔtrua nu o, va se ɖe esime ŋu nake.

eng: But Moses said to the people, Dont be afraid. Stand your ground, and watch the LORD rescue you today. The Egyptians you see today you will never ever see again.
local: Ke Mose gblɔ na dukɔ la be: “Migavɔ̃ o. Milé dzi ɖe ƒo ko ne miakpɔ ale si Aƒetɔ le xɔxɔ ge na mi egbe sia la ɖa; Egiptetɔ siwo kpɔm miele egbe la, miegale wo kpɔ ge akpɔ gbeɖe o.

eng: So God led the people by the roundabout way of the Reed Sea desert. The Israelites went up out of the land of Egypt ready for battle.
local: Eya ta ena woto gbedadaƒomɔ si ɖo ta Aƒlaƒu la gbɔ la dzi. Ke Israelviwo bla akpa ɖe aʋawɔwɔ ŋu hafi dze mɔ tso Egipte.

eng: When the LORD comes by to strike down the Egyptians and sees the blood on the beam above the door and on the two doorposts, the LORD will pass over that door. He wont let the destroyer enter your houses to strike you down.
local: elabe Aƒetɔ va le yiyi ge aɖatsrɔ̃ Egiptetɔwo, eye ne wòkpɔ ʋua le ʋɔtrutiawo kple wo tametɔ ŋu la, ato aƒea ŋu ayi, eye mana nugblẽla nage ɖe miaƒe aƒewo me awu mi o.

eng: The LORD will fight for you. You just keep still.
local: Miawo mizi kpi ko, Aƒetɔ le aʋa wɔ ge ɖe mia nu.”.

eng: Moses took with him Josephs bones just as Joseph had made Israels sons promise when he said to them, When God takes care of you, you must carry my bones out of here with you.
local: Azɔ Mose tsɔ Yosef ƒe ƒuwo ɖe asi elabe Yosef gblɔ na Israelviwo be: “Mika atam nam be, ne Mawu ve mia nu eye miedzo le afii yina la, miatsɔ nye ƒuwo ɖe asi ayii.”.

eng: You should observe this ritual as a regulation for all time for you and your children.
local: Miawo ŋutɔ kple miaƒe dzidzimeviwo miwɔ ɖoɖo sia dzi tegbee.

eng: Then the LORD said to Moses, Why do you cry out to me? Tell the Israelites to get moving.
local: Aƒetɔ bia Mose be: “Nu ka ta nèle ɣli dom le yɔyem? Gblɔ na Israelviwo be woaho aɖo ta ŋgɔ ko.

eng: They set out from Succoth and camped at Etham on the edge of the desert.
local: Israelviwo dze mɔ tso Sukot, eye woɖaƒu asaɖa anyi ɖe Etam le gbedadaƒo la to.

eng: When you enter the land that the LORD has promised to give you, be sure that you observe this ritual.
local: Ne mieva ɖo anyigba si Aƒetɔ le tsɔtsɔ ge na mi, abe ale si wòdo ŋugbe ene la dzi la, ele be mianɔ subɔsubɔ sia wɔm.

eng: As for you, lift your shepherds rod, stretch out your hand over the sea, and split it in two so that the Israelites can go into the sea on dry ground.
local: Wò la, tsɔ wò atizɔti, nàdoe ɖe ƒu la dzi ne nàma tsia ɖe eve bene Israelviwo nazɔ ƒuƒuiƒe ato ƒu la me ayi.

eng: The LORD went in front of them during the day in a column of cloud to guide them and at night in a column of lightning to give them light. This way they could travel during the day and at night.
local: Aƒetɔ nɔ wo ŋgɔ le alilikpo tsrala me le ŋkeke me, eye wòkplɔa wo. Le zã me la, enɔa dzobibi tsrala me, eye wònaa kekeli wo, ale be wote ŋu zɔ mɔ zã kple keli.

eng: And when your children ask you, What does this ritual mean to you.
local: Ne mia viwo abia be: ‘Miaƒe subɔsubɔ sia gɔme ɖe?’ la.

eng: But me, Ill make the Egyptians stubborn so that they will go in after them, and Ill gain honor at the expense of Pharaoh, all his army, his chariots, and his cavalry.
local: Mana Egiptetɔwo ƒe dzime nasẽ, eye woakplɔ wo ɖo bene maɖe nye ŋusẽ afia ɖe Egipte Fiagã la kple eƒe aʋakɔ blibo la, eƒe tasiaɖamwo kple sɔdolawo katã ŋu.

eng: The column of cloud during the day and the column of lightning at night never left its place in front of the people.
local: Alilikpo tsrala la medzona le dukɔ la gbɔ le ŋkeke me o, eye dzobibi tsrala la hã medzona le dukɔ la ŋgɔ le zã me o.

eng: you will say, It is the Passover sacrifice to the LORD, for the LORD passed over the houses of the Israelites in Egypt. When he struck down the Egyptians, he spared our houses. The people then bowed down and worshipped.
local: ekema miaɖo eŋu be: ‘Esiae nye ŋutitotovɔsa na Aƒetɔ, ame si to Israelviwo ƒe aƒewo ŋu yi le Egipte, eye wòɖawu Egiptetɔwo hetso míaƒe aƒewo ɖi la.’ ” Tete dukɔ blibo la de ta agu, eye wodo gbe ɖa.

eng: The Egyptians will know that I am the LORD, when I gain honor at the expense of Pharaoh, his chariots, and his cavalry.
local: Ne meɖe nye ŋusẽ fia ɖe Egipte Fiagã la, eƒe tasiaɖamwo kple sɔdolawo ŋu alea la, ekema adze sii be nyee nye Aƒetɔ.”.

eng: The Israelites went and did exactly what the LORD had commanded Moses and Aaron to do.
local: Tete Israelviwo ɖawɔ nu siwo Aƒetɔ ɖo na wo to Mose kple Aron dzi la dzi tututu.

eng: Gods messenger, who had been in front of Israels camp, moved and went behind them. The column of cloud moved from the front and took its place behind them.
local: Tete mawudɔla si le Israel ƒe aʋakɔ la ŋgɔ la dzo yi emegbe. Nenema ke alilikpo tsrala si le wo ŋgɔ la hã dzo yi wo megbe.

eng: At midnight the LORD struck down all the first offspring in the land of Egypt, from the oldest child of Pharaoh sitting on his throne to the oldest child of the prisoner in jail, and all the first offspring of the animals.
local: Le zã titina la, Aƒetɔ wu ŋgɔgbeviwo katã le Egipte-nyigba dzi. Ewu ŋgɔgbevi ɖe sia ɖe, tso Egipte Fiagã si le Egipte fiazikpui dzi la ƒe ŋgɔgbevi kple gaxɔmenɔla ƒe ŋgɔgbevi kpakple lãwo ƒe ŋgɔgbeviwo katã dzi.

eng: It stood between Egypts camp and Israels camp. The cloud remained there, and when darkness fell it lit up the night. They didnt come near each other all night.
local: heɖale Egiptetɔwo ƒe aʋakɔ kple Israelviwo ƒe aʋakɔ dome, eye alilikpo tsrala la do viviti ɖe Egiptetɔwo le zã la me, ke eklẽ na Israelviwo ya ale be wo dometɔ aɖeke mete ɖe nɔvia ŋu zã blibo la katã o.

eng: When Pharaoh, all his officials, and all the Egyptians got up that night, a terrible cry of agony rang out across Egypt because every house had someone in it who had died.
local: Tete Egipte Fiagã la, eŋumewo kple Egiptetɔwo katã fɔ le zã titina, eye aviɣli de dzi hoo le dukɔ bliboa me elabe Egiptetɔwo ƒe aƒe aɖeke meli si me ame meku le o.

eng: Then Moses stretched out his hand over the sea. The LORD pushed the sea back by a strong east wind all night, turning the sea into dry land. The waters were split into two.
local: Azɔ Mose do asi ɖe ƒua dzi. Tete Aƒetɔ na ɣedzeƒeya sesẽ ƒo zã blibo la katã, hetutu atsiaƒua ɖe megbe. Ƒuƒuiƒe do, eye tsia me ma.

eng: Then Pharaoh called Moses and Aaron that night and said, Get up! Get away from my people, both you and the Israelites! Go! Worship the LORD, as you said!.
local: Egipte Fiagã la yɔ Mose kple Aron vɛ le zã la me, eye wògblɔ na wo be: “Netsɔ! Midzo le nye dukɔ me, miawo ŋutɔ kple Israelviwo siaa. Miyi ne miaɖasubɔ Aƒetɔ abe ale si miegblɔ la ene.

eng: The Israelites walked into the sea on dry ground. The waters formed a wall for them on their right hand and on their left.
local: Ale Israelviwo zɔ ƒuƒuiƒe to ƒua me. Tsi la zu abe gli ene na wo le ɖusime kple miame.

eng: You can even take your flocks and herds, as you asked. Just go! And bring a blessing on me as well.
local: Mikplɔ miaƒe alẽ kple gbɔ̃hawo kpakple miaƒe nyihawo, abe ale si miedi ene, ne miadzo! Miabia yayra na nye hã!”.

eng: The Egyptians chased them and went into the sea after them, all of Pharaohs horses, chariots, and cavalry.
local: Egiptetɔawo ɖe wo ɖe nu, Egipte Fiagã la ƒe sɔwo, tasiaɖamwo kple sɔdolawo va ɖo ƒu la titina.

eng: The Egyptians urged the people to hurry and leave the land because they thought, Well all be dead.
local: Egiptetɔwo ƒo du ɖe Israelviwo nu be woadzo le anyigba la dzi kabakaba. Wole ɣli dzi be: “Mí katã míele kuku ge!”.

eng: As morning approached, the LORD looked down on the Egyptian camp from the column of lightning and cloud and threw the Egyptian camp into a panic.
local: Le fɔŋli me la, Aƒetɔ le dzobibi kple alilikpo tsrala me kpɔ Egiptetɔwo ƒe aʋakɔ la dzi ɖa, eye wòtɔtɔ wo.

eng: So the people picked up their bread dough before the yeast made it rise, with their bread pans wrapped in their robes on their shoulders.
local: Ale Israelviwo lɔ woƒe nuwo: amɔwɔ si mekpɔ ʋã haɖe o kple akpaku si me woblua nuɖuɖu le la hebla ɖe avɔ kple awu ʋlayawo me lé ɖe abɔta.

eng: The LORD jammed their chariot wheels so that they wouldnt turn easily. The Egyptians said, Lets get away from the Israelites, because the LORD is fighting for them against Egypt.
local: Ena woƒe tasiaɖamfɔtiwo gli, ŋgɔyiyi zu dɔ. Tete Egiptetɔwo gblɔ be: “Mina míasí dzo, elabe Aƒetɔ le Israel dzi tsi tre ɖe mía ŋu le aʋa wɔm na wo!”.

eng: The Israelites did as Moses had told them and asked the Egyptians for their silver and gold jewelry as well as their clothing.
local: Abe ale si Mose ɖo aɖaŋu na wo va yii ene la, wobia klosalo kple sika nuwo kpakple nudodowo Egiptetɔwo hexɔ le wo si.

eng: Then the LORD said to Moses, Stretch out your hand over the sea so that the water comes back and covers the Egyptians, their chariots, and their cavalry.
local: Fifia Aƒetɔ gblɔ na Mose be: “Do asi ɖe ƒu la dzi ne ƒua nagbugbɔ yi enɔƒe aɖaŋe ɖe Egiptetɔawo, woƒe tasiaɖamwo kple woƒe sɔdolawo dzi.”.

eng: The LORD made sure that the Egyptians were kind to the people so that they let them have whatever they asked for. And so they robbed the Egyptians.
local: Aƒetɔ na Egiptetɔwo ve Israelviwo nu tsɔ nuawo na wo faa. Esia wɔ be Israelviwo ha Egiptetɔwo tsɔ dzoe.

eng: So Moses stretched out his hand over the sea. At daybreak, the sea returned to its normal depth. The Egyptians were driving toward it, and the LORD tossed the Egyptians into the sea.
local: Mose do asi ɖe ƒua dzi, eye kaka ŋu nake la, ƒua gaɖɔ, goglo abe tsã ene. Esi Egiptetɔwo sí yina la, Aƒetɔ nyà wo de ƒu la me.

eng: The Israelites traveled from Rameses to Succoth. They numbered about six hundred thousand men on foot, besides children.
local: Ale Israelviwo ho tso Ramses ɖo ta Sukot. Ŋutsu siwo le afɔ dzi dze mɔ la ƒe xexlẽme anɔ ame akpe alafa ade, ɖeviwo mele eme o.

eng: The waters returned and covered the chariots and the cavalry, Pharaohs entire army that had followed them into the sea. Not one of them remained.
local: Tsia trɔ va tsyɔ tasiaɖamuawo, sɔdolaawo kple Egipte Fiagã la ƒe aʋakɔ si kplɔ Israelviwo ɖo yi ƒua me la katã dzi. Wo dometɔ ɖeka pɛ hã metsi agbe o.

eng: A diverse crowd also went up with them along with a huge number of livestock, both flocks and herds.
local: Amedzro geɖewo hã ho kple wo, eye wokplɔ lãha gã ŋutɔ ɖe asi: gbɔ̃wo, alẽwo kple nyiwo.

eng: The Israelites, however, walked on dry ground through the sea. The waters formed a wall for them on their right hand and on their left.
local: Ke Israelviwo ya zɔ ƒuƒuiƒe to ƒua me, eye tsia zu abe gli ene ɖe woƒe ɖusime kple miame.

eng: They baked unleavened cakes from the dough they had brought out of Egypt. The dough didnt rise because they were driven out of Egypt and they couldnt wait. In fact, they didnt have time to prepare any food for themselves.
local: Wotsɔ amɔwɔ maʋamaʋã si wotsɔ tso Egipte la me aboloe, ke amɔwɔ la meʋã o, elabe Egiptetɔwo nyà wo dzoe kpata, eye womete ŋu wɔ amɔwɔ ɖi wòʋã wotsɔ ɖe asi o. Nenema ke womekpɔ ɣeyiɣi di nuɖuɖu ɖe asi hafi tso o.

eng: The LORD rescued Israel from the Egyptians that day. Israel saw the Egyptians dead on the seashore.
local: Aleae Aƒetɔ ɖe Israelviwo tso Egiptetɔwo si me gbe ma gbe, eye Israelviwo kpɔ Egiptetɔwo ƒe kukuawo le ƒua nu.

eng: The length of time that the Israelites had lived in Egypt was four hundred thirty years.
local: Israelviwo ƒe Egipte nɔnɔ de ƒe alafa ene bla etɔ̃.

eng: Israel saw the amazing power of the LORD against the Egyptians. The people were in awe of the LORD, and they believed in the LORD and in his servant Moses.
local: Esi Israel kpɔ ŋusẽ triakɔ si Aƒetɔ tsɔ tu nu kple Egiptetɔwo la, dukɔ la vɔ̃ Aƒetɔ, eye woxɔ eyama kple eƒe dɔla Mose dzi se.

eng: At the end of four hundred thirty years, on that precise day, all the LORDs people in military formation left the land of Egypt.
local: Gbe si gbe ƒe alafa ene bla etɔ̃lia wu nu tututu lae Aƒetɔ ƒe dukɔ la katã ho dzo le Egipte.

eng: For the LORD, that was a night of intent watching, to bring them out of the land of Egypt. For all Israelites in every generation, this same night is a time of intent watching to honor the LORD.
local: Aƒetɔ nɔ ŋu zã blibo ma be yeakplɔ wo dzoe le Egipte-nyigba dzi. Zã ma va zu ŋudɔzã tɔxɛ si me Israelviwo katã dɔa ŋu tsɔna dea bubu Aƒetɔ ŋu, tso dzidzime yi dzidzime.

eng: The LORD said to Moses and Aaron: This is the regulation for the Passover. No foreigner may eat it.
local: Aƒetɔ gblɔ na Mose kple Aron be: Paska la ŋu ɖoɖowoe nye esiawo: ame si menye Israelvi o la mekpɔ mɔ aɖui o.

eng: However, any slave who has been bought may eat it after hes been circumcised.
local: Ke kluvi si woƒle vɛ la, ele be woatso aʋa nɛ hafi wòaɖui.

eng: No temporary foreign resident or day laborer may eat it.
local: Amedzro alo apaɖila aɖeke mekpɔ mɔ aɖui o.

eng: Then Moses and the Israelites sang this song to the LORD: I will sing to the LORD, for an overflowing victory! Horse and rider he threw into the sea!.
local: Ale Mose kple Israelviwo dzi ha sia na Aƒetɔ be: “Madzi ha na Aƒetɔ elabe ewɔ nu dzɔtsuwo; enyrɔ sɔ kple sɔdola ɖe ƒu me.

eng: The whole Israelite community set out from Elim and came to the Sin desert, which is located between Elim and Sinai. They set out on the fifteenth day of the second month after they had left the land of Egypt.
local: Eyome Israelviwo ƒe ha blibo la ho tso Elim va ɖo Sin-gbedadaƒo le Elim kple Sinai dome. Gbe ma gbee nye ɣleti evelia ƒe ŋkeke wui atɔ̃lia gbe tututu esi woʋu tso Egipte-nyigba dzi.

eng: It should be eaten in one house. You shouldnt take any of the meat outside the house, and you shouldnt break the bones.
local: Aƒe ɖeka me ko woaɖui le; womatsɔ lã la ƒe ɖeke adoe le aƒea me o, eye womagbã eƒe ƒu aɖeke hã o.

eng: The LORD is my strength and my power; he has become my salvation. This is my God, whom I will praise, the God of my ancestors, whom I will acclaim.
local: Aƒetɔ nye nye ŋusẽ kple nye ha; eya va zu xɔname nam. Nye Mawue, eya ta makafui. Tɔ́nye ƒe Mawue, eya ta madoe ɖe dzi.

eng: The whole Israelite community complained against Moses and Aaron in the desert.
local: Tete ameha blibo la de asi liʋiliʋĩlilĩ me le Mose kple Aron ŋu.

eng: The whole Israelite community should observe it.
local: Israelviwo ƒe ha blibo la neɖu ŋkekenyuia.

eng: The LORD is a warrior; the LORD is his name.
local: Aʋawɔlae nye Aƒetɔ; Aƒetɔ nye eŋkɔ.

eng: The Israelites said to them, Oh, how we wish that the LORD had just put us to death while we were still in the land of Egypt. There we could sit by the pots cooking meat and eat our fill of bread. Instead, youve brought us out into this desert to starve this whole assembly to death.
local: Wobe: “Ne Aƒetɔ ɖe wòwu mí le míaƒe lãzewo gɔme le Egipte, afi si míeɖua nu ɖia ƒo nyuie le la, ne menyo wu esia be miekplɔ mí ameha gã sia va gbedadaƒo afii, be dɔ newu mí, míaku oa?”.

eng: Pharaohs chariots and his army he hurled into the sea; his elite captains were sunk in the Reed Sea.
local: Etsɔ Egipte Fiagã la ƒe tasiaɖam kple aʋakɔ la ƒu gbe ɖe ƒu me, eye aʋakalẽtɔwo nyrɔ ɖe Aƒlaƒu la me.

eng: Then the LORD said to Moses, Im going to make bread rain down from the sky for you. The people will go out each day and gather just enough for that day. In this way, Ill test them to see whether or not they follow my Instruction.
local: Tete Aƒetɔ gblɔ na Mose be: “Kpɔ ɖa, mana abolo nadza tso dziƒo na mi ne ameawo ayi aɖafɔ gbe sia gbe. Gake abolo agbɔsɔsɔ sinu ame ɖeka ate ŋu aɖu gbe ɖeka la ko ame sia ame kpɔ mɔ afɔ le ŋkeke ɖeka dzi. Mawɔ esia atsɔ do wo kpɔ be woawɔ nye sewo dzi loo, alo womawɔ wo dzi o mahã.

eng: The deep sea covered them; they sank into the deep waters like a stone.
local: Tsiɖɔɖɔ gbagba tsyɔ wo dzi eye woyi to abe kpe ene.

eng: On the sixth day, when they measure out what they have collected, it will be twice as much as they collected on other days.
local: Le ŋkeke adelia dzi, kaka woaɖu nu si wofɔ vɛ vɔ la, adzi ɖe edzi ade agbɔsɔsɔ sinu wofɔna gbe sia gbe la ƒe teƒe eve.”.

eng: Your strong hand, LORD, is dominant in power; your strong hand, LORD, shatters the enemy!.
local: O Aƒetɔ, wò nuɖusibɔ ƒe ŋusẽ triakɔ la ɖua dzi. O Aƒetɔ, wò nuɖusibɔ sia nètsɔna gbãa futɔwoe gudugudu.

eng: So Moses and Aaron said to all the Israelites, This evening you will know that it was the LORD who brought you out of the land of Egypt.
local: Ale Mose kple Aron gblɔ na Israelviwo katã be: “Egbe fiẽ mianyae be Aƒetɔ ye kplɔ mi dzoe le Egipte vavã.

eng: With your great surge you overthrow your opponents; you send out your hot anger; it burns them up like straw.
local: Wò gãnyenye manyagblɔ nètsɔna hea ame siwo tsi tre ɖe ŋuwò la ƒua anyi. Èdɔ wò dɔmedzoe helihelĩ la ɖa wòfiã wo abe gbe ƒuƒu ene.

eng: And in the morning you will see the LORDs glorious presence, because your complaints against the LORD have been heard. Who are we? Why blame us.
local: Etsɔ ŋdi la, miele Aƒetɔ ƒe ŋutikɔkɔe kpɔ ge elabe Aƒetɔ se liʋiliʋĩ si lĩm miele le eŋu?”.

eng: With the breath of your nostrils the waters swelled up, the floods surged up in a great wave; the deep waters foamed in the depths of the sea.
local: Wò ŋɔti me gbɔgbɔ na tsia ƒo kɔ. Tsi tsatsa dzɔ ɖi abe gli ene; ƒu gɔme tsi bla zu kpe.

eng: Moses continued, The LORD will give you meat to eat in the evening and your fill of bread in the morning because the LORD heard the complaints you made against him. Who are we? Your complaints arent against us but against the LORD.
local: Mose yi edzi be: “Ne Aƒetɔ na lã mi mieɖu le fiẽ, eye mieɖu abolo ɖi ƒo le ŋdi, elabe Aƒetɔ se miaƒe liʋiliʋĩ si mielĩ le eŋu la, nu ka míawo míenye? Menye míawo ŋu mielĩ o, ke boŋ Aƒetɔ ŋue mielĩ.

eng: The enemy said, Ill pursue, Ill overtake, Ill divide the spoils of war. Ill be overfilled with them. Ill draw my sword; my hand will destroy them.
local: Futɔ ya be: “Mati wo aɖatu wo; mafiti wo, maha aboyonu hafi nye dzi nadze me. Malũ nye yi le aku me eye matsrɔ̃ wo!”.

eng: Then Moses said to Aaron, Say to the whole Israelite community, Come near to the LORD, because hes heard your complaints.
local: Mose gblɔ na Aron be: “Gblɔ na Israel ƒe ameha blibo la be: ‘Mite va Aƒetɔ ŋkume elabe ese miaƒe liʋiliʋĩlilĩ la.’ ”.

eng: You blew with your wind; the sea covered over them. They sank like lead in the towering waters.
local: Ke wò la, ƒuƒu ko nèɖo; tete ƒu la gbagba tsyɔ wo dzi; woyi to abe tsumi ene ɖe gogloƒe.

eng: As Aaron spoke to the whole Israelite community, they turned to look toward the desert, and just then the glorious presence of the LORD appeared in the cloud.
local: Esi Aron nɔ nu ƒom na Israel ƒe ameha bliboa la, wonɔ gbedadaƒo la lɔƒo kpɔm eye kpɔ ɖa, Aƒetɔ ƒe ŋutikɔkɔe do ɖe alilikpo me le wo ŋkume.

eng: Who is like you among the gods, LORD? Who is like you, foremost in holiness, worthy of highest praise, doing awesome deeds?.
local: O Aƒetɔ, ame kae de nuwò le mawuwo dome? Ame kae le abe wò ene, si wotsɔ gãnyenye ɖo atsyɔ̃ na, wòɖi vɔvɔ̃, dze kafukafu eye wòwɔa nukunuwo?.

eng: The LORD spoke to Moses.
local: Aƒetɔ gblɔ na Mose be:.

eng: You raised your strong hand; earth swallowed them up.
local: Ɖusi ko nèdo ɖa, tete anyigba mi wo.

eng: The whole Israelite community broke camp and set out from the Sin desert to continue their journey, as the LORD commanded. They set up their camp at Rephidim, but there was no water for the people to drink.
local: Israelviwo ƒe ameha blibo la ho tso Sin-gbedadaƒo ɖo ta ŋgɔ. Wotɔ ɖe teƒeteƒewo le Aƒetɔ ƒe gbeɖeɖe nu. Wova ɖo Refidim mlɔeba eye woƒu asaɖa anyi ɖe afi ma. Ke tsi aɖeke mele afi ma woano o.

eng: Ive heard the complaints of the Israelites. Tell them, At twilight you will eat meat. And in the morning you will have your fill of bread. Then you will know that I am the LORD your God.
local: “Mese Israelviwo ƒe liʋiliʋĩlilĩ la. Gblɔ na wo be: ‘Le fiẽ me la, lã si miaɖu la ava na mi, eye etsɔ ŋdi la, miakpɔ abolo aɖu wòasu mi nyuie ne mianyae be nyee nye Aƒetɔ, mia Mawu la.’ ”.

eng: With your great loyalty you led the people you rescued; with your power you guided them to your sanctuary.
local: Ève wò dukɔ la nu; nèkplɔ wo eye nèɖe wo. Etsɔ wò ŋusẽ kplɔ wo va ɖo teƒe kɔkɔe la.

eng: The people argued with Moses and said, Give us water to drink. Moses said to them, Why are you arguing with me? Why are you testing the LORD.
local: Eya ta wokpe dzre kple Mose gblɔ be: “Di tsi na mí ne míano.” Mose bia wo be: “Nu ka ta miele dzre wɔm kplim? Nu ka ta miele Aƒetɔ tem kpɔ?”.

eng: In the evening a flock of quail flew down and covered the camp. And in the morning there was a layer of dew all around the camp.
local: Esi fiẽ ɖo la, tegliwo va dze ɖe asaɖa la me hetsyɔ eƒe afi sia afi. Ŋufɔke ŋdi la, edzé be zãmu dza fũu ƒo xlã asaɖa la.

eng: The peoples heard, they shook in terror; horror grabbed hold of Philistias inhabitants.
local: Dukɔwo see eye dzidzi ƒo wo; Filistitɔwo le ƒoƒom tititi.

eng: But the people were very thirsty for water there, and they complained to Moses, Why did you bring us out of Egypt to kill us, our children, and our livestock with thirst.
local: Gake esi tsikɔ wu ameawo vevie le afi ma la, woƒo nu le Mose ŋu be: “Nu ka ta nèkplɔ mí dzoe le Egipte? Ɖe nèbe tsikɔ nawu míawo ŋutɔ kple mía viwo kpakple míaƒe lãwo ne míakua?”.

eng: When the layer of dew lifted, there on the desert surface were thin flakes, as thin as frost on the ground.
local: Kasia zãmu la naƒu vɔ la, nu suesue, gbabɛ, gotoe aɖewo le gbedadaƒo la le anyigba fũu abe kpetsi ene.

eng: If an immigrant who lives with you wants to observe the Passover to the LORD, then he and all his males should be circumcised. Then he may join in observing it. He should be regarded as a native of the land. But no uncircumcised person may eat it.
local: Ke ne amedzro aɖe le gbɔwò, si di be yeaɖu Paska la la, ele be woatso aʋa na ŋutsu ɖe sia ɖe si le esi la hafi wòava ɖui. Ekema woabui abe Israel vidzidzia ene. Ke bolobolotɔ aɖeke mekpɔ mɔ aɖu Paska la o.

eng: Then Edoms tribal chiefs were terrified; panic grabbed hold of Moabs rulers; all of Canaans inhabitants melted in fear.
local: Edom-fiawo ɖi vo; Moab-megãwo dzo nyànyanya; Kanaanitɔwo katã lulu.

eng: So Moses cried out to the LORD, What should I do with this people? They are getting ready to stone me.
local: Ale Mose do ɣli na Aƒetɔ be: “Nu ka mawɔ na dukɔ sia? Wole klalo be yewoaƒu kpem.”.

eng: When the Israelites saw it, they said to each other, What is it? They didnt know what it was. Moses said to them, This is the bread that the LORD has given you to eat.
local: Esi Israelviwo kpɔ wo la, wobia wo nɔewo be: “Nu kae nye esi?” Mose gblɔ na wo be: “Nu sia nye abolo si Aƒetɔ na mi be miaɖu.

eng: There will be one Instruction for the native and for the immigrant who lives with you.
local: Sedede ɖeka mae anɔ ŋkekea ŋu na aƒevi kple amedzro siwo le mia dome la siaa.

eng: Terror and fear came over them; because of your great power, they were as still as a stone until your people, LORD, passed by, until the people you made your own passed by.
local: Vɔvɔ̃ kple ŋɔdzi lé wo. Wò ŋusẽ triakɔ la na wodze ti abe kpe ene, va se ɖe esime wò dukɔ si nèxɔ ƒle la to wo dome yi!.

eng: The LORD said to Moses, Go on ahead of the people, and take some of Israels elders with you. Take in your hand the shepherds rod that you used to strike the Nile River, and go.
local: Aƒetɔ gblɔ na Mose be: “Dze ŋgɔ na dukɔ la, eye nàtia Israel-me tsitsi aɖewo akpe ɖe ɖokuiwò ŋu. Tsɔ atizɔti si nètsɔ ƒo Nil dzi la ɖe asi yii.

eng: This is what the LORD has commanded: Collect as much of it as each of you can eat, one omer per person. You may collect for the number of people in your household.
local: Se si Aƒetɔ de lae nye esi: ‘Ame sia ame nalɔ agbɔsɔsɔ si ade na eya ŋutɔ kple eƒe agbadɔmetɔwo le ŋkeke ɖeka me la ko, eye dzidzeme si wòaku ɖe ame ɖeka nu la nanye lita ene.’ ”.

eng: All the Israelites did just as the LORD had commanded Moses and Aaron.
local: Ale Israelviwo katã wɔ se si Aƒetɔ de na Mose kple Aron la dzi pɛpɛpɛ.

eng: You brought them in and planted them on your own mountain, the place, LORD, that you made your home, the sanctuary, LORD, that your hand created.
local: Àkplɔ wò dukɔ vɛ eye àtsɔ wo aɖo to si nye wò ŋutɔ tɔwò la dzi; O Aƒetɔ, teƒe si nèdzra ɖo be yeanɔ, O Aƒetɔ, kɔkɔeƒe si wò ŋutɔ nètsɔ wò asi ɖo anyii.

eng: Ill be standing there in front of you on the rock at Horeb. Hit the rock. Water will come out of it, and the people will be able to drink. Moses did so while Israels elders watched.
local: Kpɔ ɖa, matsi tsitre ɖe ŋkuwò me ɖe Xoreb-kpe la dzi. Ƒo kpe la ekema tsi ado tso eme be dukɔ la nano.” Mose wɔ nenema le Israel-me tsitsiawo ŋkume.

eng: The Israelites did as Moses said, some collecting more, some less.
local: Israelviwo yi ɖalɔ nuɖuɖu la. Ame aɖewo lɔe wòsɔ gbɔ wu ale si dze; ke ame aɖewo tɔ mede o.

eng: On that precise day, the LORD brought the Israelites out of the land of Egypt in military formation.
local: Gbe ma gbe ye Aƒetɔ kplɔ Israelviwo ƒe hatsotsoawo do goe le Egipte-nyigba dzi.

eng: The LORD will rule forever and always.
local: Aƒetɔ aɖu fia tegbetegbee.”.

eng: He called the place Massah and Meribah, because the Israelites argued with and tested the LORD, asking, Is the LORD really with us or not.
local: “Dzrewɔwɔ” Mose tsɔ ŋkɔ na teƒe ma be Masa kple Meriba elabe Israelviwo wɔ dzre eye wote Aƒetɔ kpɔ le afi ma bia be: “Ɖe Aƒetɔ le mía dome loo, alo meli o?”.

eng: But when they measured it out by the omer, the ones who had collected more had nothing left over, and the ones who had collected less had no shortage. Everyone collected just as much as they could eat.
local: Esi wodzidze wo kple omer vɔ la, eva dze be ame siwo tɔwo sɔ gbɔ akpa la megbɔ dzidzemea dzi akpa o, eye ame siwo tɔwo mede o hã mele sue akpa o; ame sia ame lɔ esinu wòate ŋu aɖu la.

eng: Jethro, Midians priest and Moses father-in-law, heard about everything that God had done for Moses and for Gods people Israel, how the LORD had brought Israel out of Egypt.
local: Yetro, Mose to, ame si nye nunɔla le Midian la, se nu siwo katã Mawu wɔ na Mose kple eƒe dukɔ Israel kpakple ale si Aƒetɔ kplɔ Israel dzoe le Egipte.

eng: When Pharaohs horses, chariots, and cavalry went into the sea, the LORD brought back the waters of the sea over them. But the Israelites walked through the sea on dry ground.
local: Elabe esi Egipte Fiagã ƒe sɔwo, eƒe tasiaɖamwo kple sɔdolawo ɖo ƒua me la, Aƒetɔ na tsi la tsyɔ wo dzi. Ke Israelviwo ya zɔ ƒuƒuiƒe to ƒua me do.

eng: On exactly the third-month anniversary of the Israelites leaving the land of Egypt, they came into the Sinai desert.
local: Esi Israelviwo ʋu tso Egipte-nyigba dzi, le ɣleti etɔ̃ megbe ƒe ŋkeke ma dzi tututu la, wova ɖo Sinai-gbedadaƒo.

eng: Amalek came and fought with Israel at Rephidim.
local: Esia megbe Amalekitɔwo wɔ aʋa kple Israelviwo le Refidim.

eng: Moses said to them, Dont keep any of it until morning.
local: Mose gblɔ na wo be: “Migana ame aɖeke nagblẽ ɖeke ɖi ŋu nake ɖe edzi o.”.

eng: Moses father-in-law Jethro took with him Zipporah, Moses wife whom he had sent away.
local: Tete Yetro, Mose to, kplɔ Mose srɔ̃ Zipora, ame si wòɖo ɖe aƒe.

eng: Then the prophet Miriam, Aarons sister, took a tambourine in her hand. All the women followed her playing tambourines and dancing.
local: Tete Aron nɔvinyɔnu Miryam, Mawu ƒe gbeƒãɖela tsɔ axatsɛ eye nyɔnuwo katã hã tsɔ axatsɛwo kplɔe ɖo le ɣe ɖum.

eng: They traveled from Rephidim, came into the Sinai desert, and set up camp there. Israel camped there in front of the mountain.
local: Esi woho tso Refidim la, wova Sinai-gbedadaƒo eye woƒu asaɖa anyi ɖe gbedadaƒo la, afi mae Israel ƒu asaɖa anyi ɖo le to la ŋgɔ.

eng: Moses said to Joshua, Choose some men for us and go fight with Amalek. Tomorrow Ill stand on top of the hill with the shepherds rod of God in my hand.
local: Tete Mose gblɔ na Yosua be: “Tia ŋutsu aɖewo na mí ne miayi aɖakpe aʋa kple Amalek etsɔ. Nye la, maɖatsi tsitre ɖe togbɛ la tame alé Mawu ƒe atizɔti la ɖe asi.”.

eng: But they didnt listen to Moses. Some kept part of it until morning, but it became infested with worms and stank. Moses got angry with them.
local: Gake womeɖo to Mose o, eye ame aɖewo gblẽe ɖi ŋu ke ɖe edzi; ke edze nyé eye wòƒaƒã. Mose do dɔmedzoe ɖe wo ŋu.

eng: along with her two sons. One was named Gershom because he said, I have been an immigrant living in a foreign land.
local: kple via ŋutsu eveawo ɖe asi. Ɖeka ŋkɔ nye Gersom elabe Mose gblɔ be: “Amedzro menye le dzronyigba dzi.”.

eng: Miriam sang the refrain back to them: Sing to the LORD, for an overflowing victory! Horse and rider he threw into the sea!.
local: Miryam do ha ɖa be: “Midzi ha na Aƒetɔ elabe eɖu dzi nukutɔe; ede sɔ kple sɔdola ƒu.”.

eng: while Moses went up to God. The LORD called to him from the mountain, This is what you should say to Jacobs household and declare to the Israelites.
local: Tete Mose lia to la yi Mawu gbɔ eye Aƒetɔ yɔe tso to la dzi gblɔ be: “Gblɔ na Yakob ƒe aƒe la eye nàɖe gbeƒãe na Israel-dukɔ la be:.

eng: Every morning they gathered it, as much as each person could eat. But when the sun grew hot, it melted away.
local: Ŋdi sia ŋdi la, woyina ɖalɔnɛ; ame sia ame ɖe ale si wòate ŋu aɖu gbe ma gbe ko nu. Ne ŋdɔ ʋu la, esi tsi anyi la lólona.

eng: So Joshua did as Moses told him. He fought with Amalek while Moses, Aaron, and Hur went up to the top of the hill.
local: Ale Yosua wɔ abe ale si Mose gblɔ nɛ la ene eye wowɔ aʋa kple Amalek. Ke Mose, Aron kple Xur ya yi ɖe togbɛ la tame.

eng: The other was named Eliezer because he said, The God of my ancestors was my helper who rescued me from Pharaohs sword.
local: Evelia ŋkɔ nye Eliezer elabe egblɔ be: “Tɔ́nye ƒe Mawu la kpe ɖe ŋunye, eye wòɖem tso Egipte Fiagã la ƒe yi nu.”.

eng: You saw what I did to the Egyptians, and how I lifted you up on eagles wings and brought you to me.
