local: Miawo ŋutɔ miekpɔ nu si mewɔ Egiptetɔwo eye mekplɔ mi abe ale si hɔ̃ kɔa viawo ɖe eƒe aʋalawo dzi ene la va gbɔnyee.

eng: Then Moses had Israel leave the Reed Sea and go out into the Shur desert. They traveled for three days in the desert and found no water.
local: Emegbe Mose na Israel zɔ mɔ tso Aƒlaƒu la nu yi Sur-gbedadaƒo. Wozɔ ŋkeke etɔ̃ ƒe mɔ le gbedadaƒo gake womekpɔ tsi aɖeke o.

eng: On the sixth day the people collected twice as much food as usual, two omers per person. All the chiefs of the community came and told Moses.
local: Le ŋkeke adea gbe la, wolɔ ŋkeke eve ƒe ɖuɖu, ame ɖeka lita ene. Ameha la ƒe amegãwo va gblɔe na Mose.

eng: Whenever Moses held up his hand, Israel would start winning the battle. Whenever Moses lowered his hand, Amalek would start winning.
local: Ne Mose kɔ asi dzi la, Israelviwo ƒe alɔ yia dzi, ne eɖiɖi asi ɖe anyi la, ekema Amalekitɔwo ƒe alɔ yia dzi.

eng: Jethro, Moses father-in-law, brought Moses sons and wife back to him in the desert where he had set up camp at Gods mountain.
local: Ale Yetro, Mose to, kple Mose viŋutsuawo kpakple srɔ̃a wova Mose gbɔ, le gbedadaƒo le Mawu ƒe to la gbɔ, afi si woƒu asaɖa anyi ɖo.

eng: So now, if you faithfully obey me and stay true to my covenant, you will be my most precious possession out of all the peoples, since the whole earth belongs to me.
local: Eya ta tso azɔ heyina, ne miase nye gbe eye mialé nye nubabla me ɖe asi la, ekema mianye tɔnye koŋ le dukɔwo katã dome elabe tɔnyee nye anyigba blibo la katã.

eng: When they came to Marah, they couldnt drink Marahs water because it was bitter. Thats why it was called Marah.
local: Wova ɖo Mara; ke tsi si le afi ma la vena, eya ta womete ŋu noe o. Eya ta woyɔa teƒea be “Mara”.

eng: He said to them, This is what the LORD has said, Tomorrow is a day of rest, a holy Sabbath to the LORD. Bake what you want to bake and boil what you want to boil. But you can set aside and keep all the leftovers until the next morning.
local: Eɖo eŋu na wo be: “Aƒetɔ be: ‘Etsɔ nye dzudzɔgbe, ŋkeke kɔkɔe na Aƒetɔ. Nuɖuɖua ƒe akpa si miatsɔ ƒo aboloe la, mitsɔe ƒoe, eye esi miaɖa la, miɖae. Ke midzra esi asusɔ la ɖo na etsɔ.’ ”.

eng: But Moses hands grew tired. So they took a stone and put it under Moses so he could sit down on it. Aaron and Hur held up his hands, one on each side of him so that his hands remained steady until sunset.
local: Ke abɔ va ku ɖe Mose ŋu, ale wotsɔ kpe va de ete, eye wòbɔbɔ nɔ anyi ɖe edzi. Aron kple Xur lé eƒe asiwo ɖe dzi; ame ɖeka le axa ɖeka dzi eye ame evelia le axa kemɛ dzi. Ale wolé eƒe asiwo ɖe te, va se ɖe esime ɣe ɖo to.

eng: He sent word to Moses: I, your father-in-law Jethro, am coming to you along with your wife and her two sons.
local: Ke ame aɖe ɖagblɔ na Mose be: “Kpɔ ɖa, towò Yetro kple srɔ̃wò kpakple viwò ŋutsu eveawo gbɔna ɖe gbɔwò”.

eng: Then God spoke all these words.
local: Mawu gblɔ nya siawo katã be:.

eng: The people complained against Moses, What will we drink.
local: Dukɔ la lĩ Mose ŋu be: “Afi ka míakpɔ tsi le ano?”.

eng: You will be a kingdom of priests for me and a holy nation. These are the words you should say to the Israelites.
local: Ke miawo mianye nunɔlawo ƒe fiaɖuƒe kple dukɔ kɔkɔe nam. Nya si nàgblɔ na Israelviwo la nye esia.”.

eng: So they set the leftovers aside until morning, as Moses had commanded. They didnt stink or become infested with worms.
local: Wodae ɖi ŋu ke ɖe edzi abe ale si Mose gblɔ na wo la ene, gake meƒaƒã o, eye medze nyé hã o.

eng: So Joshua defeated Amalek and his army with the sword.
local: Yosua fiti Amalek kple eƒe aʋakɔ la kple yi keŋkeŋ.

eng: Moses went out to meet his father-in-law, and he bowed down and kissed him. They asked each other how they were doing, and then they went into the tent.
local: Tete Mose do yi ɖakpe toa. Ede ta agu na toa eye wògbugbɔ nu nɛ. Wobia wo nɔewo ƒe dɔdɔ ta se, eye woge ɖe agbadɔ me.

eng: I am the LORD your God who brought you out of Egypt, out of the house of slavery.
local: “Nyee nye Aƒetɔ, wò Mawu, si kplɔ wò dzoe le Egipte-nyigba dzi, afi si nènye kluvi le kpɔ la.

eng: Moses cried out to the LORD, and the LORD pointed out a tree to him. He threw it into the water, and the water became sweet. The LORD made a regulation and a ruling there, and there he tested them.
local: Mose do ɣli yɔ Aƒetɔ, eye Aƒetɔ fia ati aɖee. Esi wòtsɔ ati la da ɖe tsia me la, tsia trɔ zu tsi nyui. Afi mae Aƒetɔ na se kple ɖoɖo Israelviwo le. Afi mae wòdo wo kpɔ le.

eng: So Moses came down, called together the peoples elders, and set before them all these words that the LORD had commanded him.
local: Ale Mose va eye woyɔ dukɔ la ƒe ame tsitsiwo, eye wògblɔ nya siwo katã Aƒetɔ de esi la na wo.

eng: The next day Moses said, Eat it today, because today is a Sabbath to the LORD. Today you wont find it out in the field.
local: Mose gblɔ na wo be: Miɖu eya egbe, elabe egbe nye dzudzɔgbe na Aƒetɔ; miele ɖeke kpɔ ge le gbe me alɔ o.

eng: Then the LORD said to Moses, Write this as a reminder on a scroll and read it to Joshua: I will completely wipe out the memory of Amalek under the sky.
local: Tete Aƒetɔ gblɔ na Mose be: “Ŋlɔ nu sia ɖe agbalẽ me hena ŋkuɖoɖo edzi, eye nàkui ɖe to me na Yosua, elabe matsrɔ̃ Amalek ŋkɔ ɖa le xexea me keŋkeŋ.

eng: Moses then told his father-in-law everything that the LORD had done to Pharaoh and to the Egyptians on Israels behalf, all the difficulty they had on their journey, and how the LORD had rescued them.
local: Mose gblɔ nu si katã Aƒetɔ wɔ Egipte Fiagã la kple Egiptetɔwo ɖe Israel ta la na toa. Nenema ke wòxlẽ nukpekeame siwo katã ɖe fu na wo le woƒe mɔzɔzɔa me kple ale si Aƒetɔ ɖe wo tso wo me la hã fia toa.

eng: You must have no other gods before me.
local: “Mawu bubu aɖeke meganɔ asiwò le ŋkunye me o.

eng: The people all responded with one voice: Everything that the LORD has said we will do. Moses reported to the LORD what the people said.
local: Dukɔ blibo la ɖo eŋu hoo be: “Nu sia nu si Aƒetɔ gblɔ ko la, míawɔe.” Mose yi ɖagblɔ woƒe ŋuɖoɖo sia na Aƒetɔ.

eng: Six days you will gather it. But on the seventh day, the Sabbath, there will be nothing to gather.
local: Le ŋkeke ade me la, nuɖuɖu sia anɔ anyi miayi aɖalɔ; ke le ŋkeke adrẽa gbe, si nye dzudzɔgbe la, miele ɖeke kpɔ ge o.”.

eng: The LORD said, If you are careful to obey the LORD your God, do what God thinks is right, pay attention to his commandments, and keep all of his regulations, then I wont bring on you any of the diseases that I brought on the Egyptians. I am the LORD who heals you.
local: Egblɔ be: “Ne èɖo to Aƒetɔ, wò Mawu la, eye nèwɔ nu si dzea Mawu ŋu, ne èlea fɔ ɖe eƒe sewo ŋu eye nèwɔa eƒe ɖoɖowo katã dzi la, nyemahe fukpekpe siwo katã mehe va Egiptetɔwo dzi la ƒe ɖeke ava dziwò o, elabe nyee nye Aƒetɔ, wò dɔyɔla.”.

eng: Moses built an altar there and called it, The LORD is my banner.
local: Mose ɖi vɔsamlekpui eye wòna ŋkɔe be “Aƒetɔ nye nye aflaga.

eng: Jethro was glad about all the good things that the LORD had done for Israel in saving them from the Egyptians power.
local: Yetro kpɔ dzidzɔ le nyui si Aƒetɔ wɔ na Israelviwo kple ale si wòɖe wo tso Egiptetɔwo si me la ta.

eng: Do not make an idol for yourself—no form whatsoever—of anything in the sky above or on the earth below or in the waters under the earth.
local: “Mègawɔ legba aɖeke na ɖokuiwò o. Mègawɔ nu siwo le dziƒo, alo nu siwo le anyigba dzi, alo nu siwo le tsi me le anyigba te la dometɔ aɖeke ƒe nɔnɔme o.

eng: Then the LORD said to Moses, Im about to come to you in a thick cloud in order that the people will hear me talking with you so that they will always trust you. Moses told the LORD what the people said.
local: Aƒetɔ gblɔ na Mose be: “Kpɔ ɖa, megbɔna gbɔwò le alilikpo dodo aɖe me, be ne mele nu ƒom kpli wò la, dukɔ la nasee, eye woawo hã naxɔ dziwò ase tegbee.” Ale Mose gblɔ dukɔ la ƒe nyawo na Aƒetɔ.

eng: Then they came to Elim, where there were twelve springs of water and seventy palm trees. They camped there by the water.
local: Emegbe wova ɖo Elim, teƒe si tsidzɔƒe wui eve kple deti bla adrẽ le, eye woƒu asaɖa anyi ɖe afi ma, le tsia gbɔ.

eng: On the seventh day some of the people went out to gather bread, but they found nothing.
local: Ke le ŋkeke adrẽa gbe la, ame aɖewo ya gayi be yewoaɖalɔ ɖe; womekpɔ ɖeke o.

eng: Jethro said, Bless the LORD who rescued you from the Egyptians power and from Pharaohs power, who rescued the people from Egypts oppressive power.
local: Yetro gblɔ be: “Woakafu Aƒetɔ, ame si ɖe mi tso Egiptetɔwo kple Egipte Fiagã la si me.

eng: He said, The power of the LORDs banner! The LORD is at war with Amalek in every generation.
local: hegblɔ be, “Asi le Aƒetɔ ƒe aflaga dzi! Aƒetɔ awɔ aʋa kple Amalek tso dzidzime yi dzidzime.”.

eng: Do not bow down to them or worship them, because I, the LORD your God, am a passionate God. I punish children for their parents sins even to the third and fourth generations of those who hate me.
local: Mègade ta agu na ɖeke alo asubɔ ɖeke o. Elabe nye Aƒetɔ, wò Mawu la, Mawu ʋãŋu menye. Mehea to na vi siwo léa fum, ɖe wo tɔ́wo ƒe vodada nu, va se ɖe dzidzime etɔ̃lia kple enelia dzi.

eng: and the LORD said to Moses: Go to the people and take today and tomorrow to make them holy. Have them wash their clothes.
local: Aƒetɔ gblɔ na Mose be: “Gbugbɔ yi dukɔ la gbɔ, eye nàgblɔ na wo be woatsɔ egbe kple etsɔ anya woƒe nuwo, awɔ wo ɖokui dzadzɛ.

eng: The LORD said to Moses, How long will you refuse to obey my commandments and instructions?.
local: Aƒetɔ bia Mose be: “Ɣe ka ɣi gɔ̃ miagbe toɖoɖo nye sewo kple ɖoɖowo ase ɖo?.

eng: Now I know that the LORD is greater than all the gods, because of what happened when the Egyptians plotted against them.
local: Fifi ko menyae be Aƒetɔ lolo wu mawuwo katã elabe eɖe dukɔ la tso Egiptetɔwo si me, esime Egiptetɔwo te wo ɖe anyi dàdatɔe la.”.

eng: But I am loyal and gracious to the thousandth generation of those who love me and keep my commandments.
local: Gake mevea ame akpe nane siwo lɔ̃am, eye wowɔa nye sewo dzi ya nu.

eng: Be ready for the third day, because on the third day the LORD will come down on Mount Sinai for all the people to see.
local: Na woanɔ klalo le ŋkeke etɔ̃a gbe, elabe gbe ma gbe la, maɖi va Sinai-to dzi le wo katã ŋkume.

eng: Look! The LORD has given you the Sabbath. Therefore, on the sixth day he gives you enough food for two days. Each of you should stay where you are and not leave your place on the seventh day.
local: Kpɔ ɖa, Aƒetɔ na dzudzɔgbe mi, eya tae wònaa ŋkeke eve ƒe nuɖuɖu mi le ŋkeke adelia dzi ɖo. Ame sia ame nenɔ aƒeme le ŋkeke adrẽa gbe. Ame aɖeke megado go o.”.

eng: Then Jethro, Moses father-in-law, brought an entirely burned offering and sacrifices to God. Aaron came with all of Israels elders to eat a meal with Moses father-in-law in Gods presence.
local: Yetro, Mose to, sa numemevɔ kple vɔsa bubuwo na Mawu. Aron kple Israel-me tsitsiwo katã hã va be woaɖu nu kple Mose to le Mawu ŋkume.

eng: Do not use the LORD your Gods name as if it were of no significance; the LORD wont forgive anyone who uses his name that way.
local: “Mègayɔ Aƒetɔ, wò Mawu ŋkɔ dzodzro o, elabe Aƒetɔ magbe tohehe na ame si yɔa eŋkɔ dzodzro la o.

eng: Set up a fence for the people all around and tell them, Be careful not to go up the mountain or to touch any part of it. Anyone who even touches the mountain must be put to death.
local: Ɖo seƒe na wo godoo ƒo xlã to la, eye nàgblɔ na wo be: ‘Ame aɖeke mekpɔ mɔ aflɔ liƒo ma o. Ame aɖeke mekpɔ mɔ alia to la alo agogoe gɔ̃ hã o. Ame sia ame si ka toa ŋu la, woawui.

eng: So the people rested on the seventh day.
local: Ale dukɔ la dzudzɔ eɖokui le ŋkeke adrẽa gbe.

eng: The next day Moses sat as a judge for the people, while the people stood around Moses from morning until evening.
local: Esi ŋu ke la, Mose bɔbɔ nɔ anyi be yeadrɔ̃ nya na dukɔ la, eye dukɔ la tsi tsitre ɖe egbɔ tso ŋdi va se ɖe fiẽ.”.

eng: Remember the Sabbath day and treat it as holy.
local: “Ɖo ŋku dzudzɔgbe la dzi, ne nàwɔe kɔkɔe.

eng: No one should touch anyone who has touched it, or they must be either stoned to death or shot with arrows. Whether an animal or a human being, they must not be allowed to live. Only when the rams horn sounds may they go up on the mountain.
local: Asi aɖeke negaka nenem me ma ŋu o; ne wòkae la, woaƒu kpee alo adae; eɖanye ame alo lã o, maganɔ agbe o.’ Ne woku lãdzokpẽ he eme didie la, eya ko hafi woalia to la.”.

eng: The Israelite people called it manna. It was like coriander seed, white, and tasted like honey wafers.
local: Azɔ Israelviwo tsɔ ŋkɔ na nuɖuɖu la be mana. Ele abe xé ƒe tsetse ene gake efu ɣie, eye wòvivina abe tatali si me wode anyitsii ene.

eng: When Moses father-in-law saw all that he was doing for the people, he said, Whats this that you are doing for the people? Why do you sit alone, while all the people are standing around you from morning until evening.
local: Esi toa kpɔ dɔ gbogbo siwo le edzi na dukɔa la, ebiae be: “Nu kae nye esi wɔm nèle? Nu ka ta nèlɔ dɔ gbogbo siawo kɔ ɖe ɖokuiwò dzi? Nu ka ta wòle be wò ɖeka nàbɔbɔ nɔ anyi eye dukɔ blibo la nava tsi tsitre anɔ lalawòm tso ŋdi va se ɖe fiẽ?”.
